family: History: War of the Austrian Succession

description: The Drinking Roulette contains 16 glasses for vodka which have their place around the roulette. Some of the glasses should be filled with vodka, some with water. Players bet red or black and those players who bet correctly drink one glass of vodka or water (they don't know). If a player bet 0 and guess, he/she drinks all the glasses. After drinking each player is allowed to leave the game. The last player in the game is declared the winner.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
Game of speed and observation.
Players draw cards in front of them and when two identical cards are stacked, the first player to cry as an animal of those cards wins all the cards drawn. There are rooster, duck, donkey, cow, pig, turkey etc. in this game. Last player in game is the winner.

Object of the game is to control the heroes from the cartoon and help them defeat X.A.N.A.

Each player can utilise all 5 protagonists, who are placed in the center of the table, to carry out specific actions and face certain dangers to score points. Victory points can also be earned by playing some cards.

The winner is the first player to "Return to the past" using Jeremy after Aelita has "Deactivated the tower" and reached 20 points.

There are two Starter Decks available: "Alert on Lyoko" and "The secret is in danger".

Matching game about colors and animals
In his turn player place a card in the middle of the table either with same animal or same color. Chameleons are like joker cards.

First player who gets rid of his cards first wins the game.

boardgame: The 50's: A Game for Your Generation
description:
The 50's 
a game of your generation

This is a Monopoly clone from Late from the Sky, a publisher that specializes in themed Monopoly games.  In The 50's game, Blocks of Time and Decades replace houses and hotels, Big Fun! and Contingency replace Chance and Community Chest cards, and all the properties and spaces on the board are 1950's themed.  The tokens are a guitar, a 50's car, a jukebox, a male figure modeled on Elvis Presley, a shoe (blue suede?) and a pair of cat-eye glasses.  As in all versions of monopoly, the last player left in the game wins.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
42 cards represents one third of an animal: head, body or
legs - 14 animals in total.

In his turn player plays sets of animals with the symbol at the top of the card or invent imaginary creatures.

The winner is the one who put together the most animals.

Rules to the Dagobert Edition in French, English and German. Rules to the France Cartes edition in English, French and Spanish.

"In the near future players compete in a strategic boardgame called Cubotrix, a holographic table where a board is displayed and challenge peoples mind to overcame their oponent for the control of board´s areas"

A two player abstract game where players compete in a board made by dices in five different colors. The objective is to control moust of the board dices and create line patterns to score. The board chances every round, with new numbers coming up and others disapearing as the dice change position and faces.  Each player needs to combine it´s own dices, hidden from the other player, and pair two numbers that match the same numbers on the board that are adjacent in order to ocupy both dices with one of it´s own control markers. Also players have a set of special actions they can perform once per game that bend the general rules of the game.

Game plays for 45 to 60 minutes.

"Deep in the Jungle the Ekebet, Mayan star messangers compete in the dangerous ritual race to bring glory to their city state. They must run day and night to reach the oposite city state before anyone else. With Kukulkan´s help, the great serpent god, they call for ancient power to reach victory".

An abstract game set in the Mayan culture for 2 to 4 players. Each player has a set of four runners that starts in their home city state and must run across to board, a jungle, to reach the oposite side, another city state. The player who reaches the oposite city state first with all his runners win the game. The pices movements are managed by a set of movement pieces. Each runner and each movement piece has two sides: day and night. When a runner moves, his piece change it´s side to a new status (day to night or night to day). Players need to manage their movement pieces in order to move their runners in the board according to their status each round. Some animals in the jungle block the runners path and each player can call for Kukulkan aid, special poker tokens that bend the general game rules.

Wong's Game is a 2 players abstract board game about stone placement and area influence invented by Gary Wong in 1999.

The game is played on an initial empty square board. Additionally each player should have a enough stones of their own color. Alternatively the author suggest that the game can be played with just paper and pencil.

The game is divided in a placement phase and a growing phase:


     Placement Phase - Each player, in turns, drops N-1 stones into empty cells (with a NxN board).



     Growing Phase - Each player continues to drop stones into empty cells, but now, each stone must have two or more adjacent friendly stones (by "adjacent" it should be understood "orthogonal or diagonal adjacent").


When both players are unable to move the one who has more stones in the board will be the winner.

Abstract game of memory and pattern recognition. Players have two pieces in their hands and each round have to play one piece on the table, creating the board and buy a new piece to their hand.

Each piece is made of 3 connected hexagon, with different colors and shapes. To place pieces on the board, players need to connect at least 2 different hexagons matching or the colors or the numbers, or a mix of both from their hand to pieces already on the table.

Pieces can be placed adjacent to other pieces in the same level or placed over other pieces. Higher a piece is placed, more score a player gets.

Game ends when no more pieces can be added to the board and the player with higher score wins.

boardgame: French Toast And Friends Fun Food Matching Game
description:
Can you help French Toast make the most pairs in this whimsical matching game for preschool-aged children?  Featuring fun food and characters from the French Toast and Friends world!

Players match the most pairs by flipping over tile cards to win.  If you don't make a match, tell everyone which turned tile card is your favorite, and why.  Once all the tile cards have been matched, the player with the most matches wins.

Includes rules for the bonus Friends vs. Food Game.  Players flip the cards over one at a time.  Friends beats food!  If you flip a friend tile card and you opponent flips a food, take both tile cards.  Player with the most tile cards at the end wins!

Components:
50 Matching tile cards
2 Bonus sticker sheets

This is a spin and move game by J. W. Spear & Sons.
Each player has a piece that is used to make your way around the board. The inside box cover which has the rules for the game calls the game "A trip on the continent". By rolling dice players land on different spaces that cause either good or bad things to happen. Spot 13 says  " after hurrying to the wrong station you lose a turn". Spot 25 says "sees the sights and lose a turn". #40 says "on the right road, advance to space 46". Many other things can happen while on your trip.

The object is to get to the finish line (#75) first.

The game was manufactured at the SPEAR WORKS in Bavaria
This is a Monopoly clone from Late from the Sky, a publisher that specializes in themed Monopoly games.  All the deeds, spaces on the board and cards feature locations and landmarks from Austin.
family: Series: ... in a Box (Late for the Sky)
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description: This is a Monopoly clone from Late from the Sky, a publisher that specializes in themed Monopoly games.  All the deeds, spaces on the board and cards feature locations and landmarks from Austin.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description: This is a Monopoly clone from Late from the Sky, a publisher that specializes in themed Monopoly games.  All the deeds, spaces on the board and cards feature locations and landmarks from Buffalo.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
MEGA Bloks Memory Builder is a memory and matching game using 20 plastic maxi Mega Bloks in 4 different colours with animal pictures on them. These blocks are hidden randomly in a 'field' - a cardboard insert within the game box. Players can't see the animals, only the colored tops of the Mega Bloks. Each player takes one of four double-sided 'barn' cards showing a set of animals (four animals on each side, in four different colours). The cards have insert holes for a white Mega Bloks stand.

Players then take turns at pulling an animal block from the field, trying to be the first to find the four animals on their card. The first player to match all four animals on their barn card wins.

The game components are easy for small children to manage and the game is fun and challenging enough to hold interest. The double-sided cards allow some variety and replayability.

boardgame: Revolver Expansion 1.1: Ambush on Gunshot Trail
description:
The mayor of Repentance Springs has fallen down a mine shaft. Undismayed, Ned McReady vows to keep the peace, by fair means or foul – his way or the road to Perdition. The only fly in this curly wolf's ointment is Jack Colty - a loose cannon, robbing banks, and shooting folks, and he don't care a continental – if you believe the stories. With a plan hatched behind the gospel mill, Ned sets out to put a spoke in Jack's wheel. Deals are struck. Ambushes are set.

In the end, only one side will claim victory. Will it be yours?

Revolver: Ambush on Gunshot Trail – the first in a series of expansions for Revolver – gives the Colonel McReady player greater strategic options through the placement of ambush cards underneath the battlefield cards. The Jack Colty player receives two new bandits, and new cards for the decks of both players give them the opportunity to customize their decks, bringing new variety and replayability to each game.

The Commonwealth has decided to send the galaxy into martial law, sending probes out to all neighboring planets to keep an eye on any illegal contraband or fuel cells being used for the hyperspace lanes, a comfort only allowed by the Commonwealth and their Inquisitor Probes.

You have a destiny. You must travel from planet to planet from your home world, finding work to upgrade your ship or to obtain fuel cells, all while trying to find that one clue, just that one clue to your goal. The goal you yourself must accomplish. Be careful though for Inquisitor Probes run up and down the hyperspace lanes looking for folks like you. The Syndicate aren't that much fun either, placing embargo ships on planets and playing "pay the toll". You can do it though. I know you can.

After Objectives have been handed out, players take turns moving their Ship Tokens between the Planets, searching at each Planet first for their Objective’s Clue card and, once found, for their Objective’s Goal card. The first player to find their Goal card and return their Ship Token back to their Home Planet, residing there for one full rotation of players, wins the game! It sounds easy but you’ll want to collect Fuel Cells along the way, upgrade your Ship, take the occasional long route to avoid the Inquisitor Probes roaming the hyperspace lanes, complete jobs to earn rewards, ward off the Syndicate, and constantly be alert to the danger of other players attacking you. It was never going to be easy!

Patent Pending is card game for three to five players that simulates patent prosecution (i.e., the process by which one obtains a patent). On a player’s turn, the player plays one of their cards as a pending patent application that will be worth points if the application survives examination and issues as a patent. The remaining players act as the patent office by (a) playing a card from their hand as prior art against the pending patent application, (b) playing a card as an event to influence the play flow, or (c) passing. If the pending application survives the examination by the opposing players, then the patent application issues to the activate player. If the patent application is rejected, all the cards played are discarded. At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

"La bola de fuego" (The Fireball) it's a simple roll-and-move game in wich the players, taking the role of explorers, must enter a volcanic island and race against each other to catch some diamonds, avoiding the dangers that lurk within the place (Slippery caves, swaying bridges, crocodiles, octopuses and, of course, fireballs), and trying to escape first.

A quite lackluster game, trying to capitalize on the success of the more well-known Fireball Island (even directly stealing its front box artwork), made by knock-off specialist Falomir Juegos.

Rise of Rebels is a 2 player territory control wargame. Players deploy units, research tech and battle for control cities in an effort to achieve their secret objectives. Each round players choose three action cards which constrains the actions they may take during the round.

When a player has acquired the requisite number of Victory Points by claiming successfully completed objective cards they win.

Included with the game is the Rise of Rebels: Legacy variant. The Legacy variant takes two players through a campaign that details the story of the rival factions represented in the game (the rival Rebel organizations) as they battle for control of the space station. This variant involves making permanent modifications to the game bard and faction components based on the outcome of each battle.

Ísland-opoly is a licenced Monopoly clone which theme is unsurprisingly Iceland. The properties are famous landmarks, tourist attractions, building and so on.

From the publisher:
How do we make a board game about the most fun country in the world?
It's impossible to put all the great places of Iceland in one board game so we have chosen fun places and landmarks all over the country. We start in the Hvalfjörður and travel all around the country that keeps surprising us with natural disasters and unexpected happenings.

How do you play Ísland-opoly?
You can buy famous places or landmarks all around the country, up the rent by buying houses and upgrade them to hotels. Sounds easy? We'll figure it out! Traffic jam, radar guns and the police can make it harder for us, but that's just more fun!

You pick a fun pawn and go to the starting place, perhaps you'll become the richest person in Iceland... or just stuck in a traffic jam because of the ash cloud!

Get ready for the game! This is our country... and it's your turn!
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
In Kolory, each player is trying to create as large a territory as possible through clever card play.

To set up the game, one card showing four white quadrants is placed on the table while each player holds ten cards, with each card having four quadrants of a particular color. On a turn, a player adds a card to the playing area, laying it adjacent to other cards already in play or covering one or more cards – but only one quadrant can be covered on each card.

Once all the cards have been played, the player with the largest area of orthogonally-connected quadrants scores one point per quadrant. The player with the most points after a certain number of rounds wins.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Erin the Ever-Changing
description:
When you first meet this soft-spoken young elf, you might be surprised to learn that she's an adept adventurer; even more so that she's often fighting on the front line. But then again, you don't run into very many shapeshifting druids at the Red Dragon Inn...


    The Good: Erin's always ready with a kind word and a healing chant to smooth ruffled feathers or mend wounded flesh.



    The Bad: When the drinks start flowing, Erin can turn into a tree. Have you ever tried out-drinking a tree? It's not pretty.


The Red Dragon Inn: Allies - Erin the Ever-Changing expands the party at the Red Dragon Inn with a brand new elven shapeshifting druid, introducing new mechanisms that allow her to take on multiple forms throughout the game. She has to carefully manage which form she is in for the situation as being caught in the wrong form at the wrong time can spell for dire consequences!

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies is a new series of expansions for any standalone The Red Dragon Inn game. Each Allies set includes a single Character Deck, plus components to add a brand new character to your game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Treasure-Hunters

On this occasion I propose to players searching for sunken treasure, in what once were large ships at some point in history, and for some reason or another, suffered from the punishment of the sea, until the scourge of pirates, buccaneers and privateers but today, we rescued who were sleeping sleep for centuries.

For this game we put ourselves in the skin of modern rescuers sunken treasure, and by a simple board that will help us through all the seas and oceans, trying to salvage the wreck treasures included.

To move around the board, we will have several boats that will be used to navigate until you get to the coordinates indicated and initiate searches galleons, wrecks, ships and all kinds of boats that sailed these waters for centuries.

If we can find the part, the spoils can be of incalculable amount and report if found some interesting benefits.

In terms of mechanical and manufacturing included two forms of the game printed on one side is the most expensive and luxurious yet what material is concerned, and the other which I find very interesting and can serve to make the game a minimum cost.

This can be manufactured in a single letter large amount of material needed and sheets of different colors and Parcheesi board game have almost ready to play.

When treasure hunting system also devote special care to prove a simple, effective but not without emotion that surrounds so that any discovery.

boardgame: The Human Race: A matter of life and death
description:
The Human Race is a game for people of all ages. As you play, you will see that each move is like life itself, with rules that allow for freedom of choice and ensuing consequences. Here you will witness the gravity of your own judgement and values, as well as that of others. What will be sacrificed to win the Human Race?

Unlike other games that endorse a win-at-any-cost moral, The Human Race demonstrates that employing such a moral is possible, but not likely to win the game. Points, intelligence, cunning or luck may win the game, but if you play with heart and conscience you will never lose.

Bitterness over loss has no role in The Human Race. For if you lose, it is totally your own doing. The freedom of choice is yours. Whether you play to teach your children or learn about yourself, The Human Race gives you hours of fun.

The rules are simple. The play, intricate. Downright soul-searching. The Human Race.

Playing the Game: First player rolls the white die and moves the number of squares indicated by the die moving in a clockwise direction. If a player lands on a square which points are indicated those points are added if it is a blue square (good)
and deducted from the score if it is a red square (evil). After the player has moved, they draw a blue card or red card determined by their final location, either adding (good) or deducting (evil) points from their score or following the instructions on the card.

Rolling the Life Dice: After following the instructions on the card the player rolls the three black "Life Dice". If all three dice show the same number then the player dies. If on a red square or has negative points the player is out of the game. If a player dies while having positive points and on a blue square, then even though the player is out, they still may win the game -- that is if all the other players do not reach the Final Resting Place with positive points or if the others die with fewer good points.

Final Resting Place: The game is won by the player to reach the Final Resting Place. If no player reaches the Final Resting Place, then the player who dies with the most good points is the winner.

Cia: Cyber Ops is a collectible card game played between two or more players each of whom are computer programmers trying to program their three Computers before their opponents.  Using cards like Operations, Programs, Viruses, Firewalls, and Special Agents each player tries to program their Computers, thwart their opponent’s attempts to do the same and protect against devastating and underhanded attacks.

CIA: Cyber Ops is marketed primarily towards Christian home school students in Michigan as a way of providing an alternative to Magic: The Gathering, while encouraging academic development in math skills.

The game can be purchased in main sets and boosters. The game is currently in it's fourth series of main sets.

From the game description:

Take to the Skies!

Fly a patrol over the trenches and tangle with enemy planes in this set collection card game! Conserve your ammo as you jockey for position, attempting to line up the perfect shot. Choose from 24 different historic aircraft, each with different performance attributes which suffer as the plane takes damage. You may wind up an ace, or go down in flames!


    


Dogfight is a World War I aerial combat flavored set collection card game in which players strive to collect then play sets of cards to line up a chance to damage their opponents.

The game features set collection, card drafting, and variable player powers.

Throughout the game, players manage their altitude and speed while trying to collect the cards necessary to attack each other.

Damage is recorded, not as hit points, but as degradation to the plane's unique performance attributes. Such damage could eventually cause the plane to be forced down, start on fire, go into an uncontrolled spin, or break up in midair. It is also possible to hit the opposing pilot with a machine gun bullet, killing him instantly.

It's dangerous up there, and Dogfight is not for the faint of heart!

Ice Age is a Sudoku of people interaction and strategy.

Ice Age is a 30 minute one to four player Sudoku game. The goal of the game is to complete a 4x5 Sudoku puzzle or a 5x5 puzzle in a single player game. In each round, players roll four dice simultaneously, and begin with the starting player, each player takes one action per turn to place, remove, or replace a number base on the number rolled. Player may also use other's dice, except number 6, the joker. When all dice are used or players passed, a round ends.

When a row of 1 to 5 is completed, player receives victor point based on the time of completion. When a player finishes the puzzle or 7th round ends, game ends, and the player with highest score wins the game.

boardgame: More Drums and Shakos: Additional Scenarios, Army Lists, and Rules for Songs of Drums and Shakos
description:
More Drums & Shakos is a supplement for the 2009 Origins Award-winning Napoleonic skirmish game Song of Drums and Shakos. It includes new rules, special rules, weapons, 4 scenarios and army lists for most Nations involved in the Napoleonic wars.


     Rules for extreme weather conditions, mixed nationality squads and former allies
     Fight the War of 1812, the Tyrolean rebellion of 1809 and the bitter struggle of the French and their allies against Guerrillas in Spain!
     New weapons such as tomahawk, javelins, pikes and daggers
     New special rules to better depict irregulars
     4 scenarios, with rules for artillery fire and a generic task system for sappers and engineers
     Army lists for 28+ Nations: from Anhalt to Wurttemberg, foreign regiments in French and allied service, Partisans and Native Americans.

An abstract strategy game for 2 players. It is played using a  wooden board, marked into 64 squares, and 12 'ancient' looking coins, 6 silver and 6 gold. Five of each set of coins show a Wheel on one side and a Foot on the other, and the sixth coin has a Flag on both sides. There is also a small metal die.

Each player begins the game with his 6 pieces positioned on opposite corners of the board. On the throw of the die a player may move up to 3 pieces corresponding to the number shown. A Flag, a Wheel and a Foot move differently on the board. If a Foot stops on the centre square it is turned over to become a Wheel. Specific movement rules come into play when a confrontation occurs. For example, a Foot piece must be removed from the game if it is confronted by two opposing Foot pieces.

The game may be won in 3 ways:
a) by surrounding your opponent's Flag with any 3 pieces;
b) by removing all of your opponent's pieces from the game; or
c) by capturing your opponent's undefended Flag with your Foot.

The components are good quality - 'ancient' playing pieces, a burgundy and gold wooden board, a velvet draw-string bag for the coins.

Designed and produced in Australia. This is obviously a quality, small production but unfortunately the rules aren't available anywhere on the internet and the publisher's website no longer exists.

boardgame: Revolver Expansion 1.2: Hunt the Man Down
description:
A vicious hardened convict is imprisoned within the walls of a brutal Texan prison. Locked inside his mind is the key to Colonel Ned McReady's downfall, and Jack Colty is the only cowpoke alive who can spring him out. Trouble is, Jack's already being pursued by the vengeful Colonel about another matter...

In the end, only one side will claim victory. Will it be yours?

Revolver: Hunt the Man Down – the second in a series of expansions for Revolver – introduces two new icons to the game; a new winning condition for the McReady player; and a new location, the prison, from which the Colty player may decide to free his compadre. The method for resolving the jailbreak is unique compared to the other battlefields and does not lengthen the game. Also included are new cards for both players which give them the opportunity to customize their decks, bringing new variety and replayability to each game.

boardgame: Revolver Expansion 1.3: Vengeance on the Frontier
description:
Greetings to ya. Let me take your coat and hat, pilgrim – then pull up a pew while I have fetch ya some sarsaparilla to chase away the bitin' cold, but mind, I don't take me any sass.

Shucks, I don't get me many visitors this far up the creek, especially when accounting for the "injuns". Could be that hereabouts I'm as popular as a wet dog at a parlour social. I've been up here in the mountains so long that I know all the bears by their first names.

So, you folks wanna hear me talk about this newfangled card game expansion? Must be crackers – I doubt you could cut a lame cow from a shade tree. You'll not live long in these wicked parts. Saying that, prolonged life has ruined more men than it ever made.

[Spits, just missing your boots]

Well, unless you is planning on joining me for a rendition of "Green Grow the Rashes", I suggest we get started.

Revolver: Vengeance on the Frontier – the third in a series of expansions for Revolver – gives both players the option of buying cards from a separate, neutral Frontier deck with dollar coin tokens earned during the main game. Many of these cards feature a choice of useful options. Others are threats which can be played by either player, such as "Old Three Toes", a monstrous grizzly bear that is particularly difficult to kill.

The expansion further elaborates on the new winning condition added for McReady in Revolver: Hunt the Man Down, and many of the cards are also useful to both players at the San Manzanillo prison location.

Revolver: Vengeance on the Frontier also includes new cards for both players that give them the opportunity to customize their decks, bringing new variety and replayability to each game.

boardgame: Adventure 5: Beyond Grin's Crag – Kellar's Keep 2 (fan expansion for HeroQuest)
description:
In these next set of four adventures we continue the stories that were created in the expansions series by Milton Bradley.
In this adventure the Heroes meet up with Captain Kenaron and help him fight his way to the general of Zargon's armies. If they can kill him then they may buy the Dwarf armies enough time to regroup and defend their bridge. Can you do it?
The pages for this adventure are available to download at "Dragonkingdoms.co.uk" and yahoo's group site "HeroQuest-Adventures" for free. You do need to join the group to access the file's section where they are stored. The photo section of this site will allow you to read the story and look at the maps to see if this game is something you would like to play. Your adventure awaits!

boardgame: Adventure 6: Resurrection – Return of the Witch Lord 2 (fan expansion for HeroQuest)
description:
In this second set of adventures, we continue the stories that were created in the expansions series created by Milton Bradley.
In this adventure Mentor discovers that Queen Kessandria and Skulmar are planing to resurrect the Witch Lord. Can you get there in time to stop them?
This adventure has new spells for both sides and monster cards were created for all the cool monsters that were introduced in the original story. There is a page for making new doors. This game will take a little work to create but is well worth the effort.
The pages for this adventure are available to download at "Dragonkingdoms.co.uk" and yahoo's group site "HeroQuest-Adventures" for free. You do need to join the group to access the file's section where they are stored. The photo section of this site will allow you to read the story and look at the maps to see if this game is something you would like to play. Your adventure awaits!

boardgame: Ego: The Adult Game of Communication
description:
The fun filled thought provoking discussion generated between players demonstrates the qualities of being unique and being human. To express your opinion of a subject's qualities (player being evaluated), each analyst (all remaining players) must dial their "opinionators" to display a most/least or yes/no answer. Characteristics shown on Ego cards require your opinion of what the player is most and least like. When a statement is read from an Alter Ego card, your yes or no opinion is required of what you think the subject will say. There are no intrinsically right or wrong answers, but answering gives everyone a chance to express their opinions.

Playing The Game: To select the first subject, an Ego card is drawn by each player. The player whose characteristics begin with the letter nearest the beginning of the alphabet becomes the first subject. The subject then collects all Ego cards drawn and places them face up in the Tripper so that each space contains at least one card. All remaining players and the subject then express their opinions of the subject by dialing their opinionators to mach the most/least color combination under the Ego cards in the tripper.

Example: The three Ego cards displayed read generous, expressive and shy. If a player perceives the subject as being more expressive than shy or generous then the player would dial their opinionator to reveal the word most in the color that matches the color under the expressive ego card in the tripper. If the player perceives the subject as being less shy than generous then the player would dial their opinionator to reveal the word least in the color matching the "shy" ego card under the tripper making sure that the Most color already is revealed at the same time. When all players are ready they turn over their opinionators to reveal their opinions.

How to Score: The subject moves forward one square for each analyst opinion that matches his, plus one of his own. Players whose opinions do not match any other player move back one square. Players cannot move further back than the beginner square. Player whose opinions match one or more players remain where they are and are not penalized even if they do not match the subject's opinion. After scoring the player on the subject's left becomes the next subject and the next session is played.

Winning the Game: Play continues with a new subject being evaluated after each session is complete. The player who reaches the winners circle first is the winner.

From the box:

¿Aquatic animals beginning with D or I? Being faster than the rest of the family, Kevin hits the beep and activates the sound & light referee. Then he have 3 seconds to give his answer: "Dolphin!" He wins! Kevin discards a card and the game continues

It's easy!. Simply find a word beginning with a particular letter on one of the 66 entertaining topics. Only your own quick thinking and coolness will allow you the victory. To increase the variety and interest of the game, we propose two levels: 33 cards with easy and distracting issues to Play with the children and most difficult 33 tokens for the adults.

boardgame: Glory of the Three Kingdoms: Guandu Core Set
description:
Glory of the Three Kingdoms is a living deck-building game (LDBG) in which each player takes a role leading an allegiance in the Three Kingdoms period and battles for total victory or for victory points.

As in most deck-building games, player acquire additional cards to add to their decks. In GOTK, player may acquire cards depicting heroes, military, strategy, items, etc., and some cards provide victory points for endgame scoring. Unlike other games of this type, GOTK has a battle phase during which each player secretly deploys military cards (foot soldiers, pikemen, calvary and archers) and battles for Glory. The victor takes a glory token from the defeated player and a free card in the neutral zone. The victor may also capture and recruit the defeated general – or have him beheaded.

When two out of four card decks are depleted, the game ends and the player with the most victory points wins. However, if one allegiance acquired six or more Tiger Tally or all of opponent's glory tokens, the game ends immediately and the allegiance wins.

The first battle theme of GOTK is Guandu, the first major battle in the Three Kingdoms period. The Guandu Core Set, a two-player game, comes with Cao Cao and Yuan Shao allegiances. Lu Bu and Liu Bei allegiances will be available in a future three- and four-player expansion.

family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description: This is a very simple game for kids 4 to 8. You simply spin the spinner and move your marble from the start line to the finish. If your spin causes you to land on another players marble you simply stop one space short of where your opponents marble is.

A game of mimic in which the players, playing in couples, must discern which movie his partner is representing. As they guess them correctly (or fail in doing so), they advance (or move back) their tokens, 'till someone reach the "best director" square to win the Oscar and the game. After that, all the players can vote who's been the best actor and award him other little Oscar.

In Core Worlds: Galactic Orders – the first expansion to Core Worlds, the deck-building card game of intergalactic conquest – players begin to forge alliances with the six Galactic Orders, powerful organizations that have maintained their independence in the midst of a crumbling empire. These Orders consist of the Galactic Senate, the Science Guild, the Merchant Alliance, the Mining Coalition, the Order of Knighthood, and the Mystic Brotherhood. The Galactic Orders expansion focuses on these six independent organizations and their influence upon a galaxy at war.

Each Galactic Order is represented on the table by a large Galactic Order card that specifies the special power associated with that Order, and each player starts with 20 Faction Tokens that match the Faction symbols on their Starting Decks. Whenever a player deploys a Unit or plays a Tactic with a Galactic Order icon on it, he gets to place his Faction Token onto the corresponding Galactic Order card. Players must choose between leaving their Faction Tokens on the Galactic Order cards to score points at the end of the game, or removing their Faction Tokens in order to use the unique special powers associated with each Order.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Octopunk is a tile placement exploration game set in the near future, transgenic octopi have been tasked with building a human habitat on Europa's deep ocean floor. Players pick one of five different uplifted octopi and search 36 exploration tiles looking for the resources to build the dome while dodging the very dangerous dragon jellyfish. the humans will arrive in only four years and if the mission is not complete everyone loses. The Game consists of twenty five eight sided Ocean Floor zones in the spaces between the octagonal zones square exploration tiles are placed face down, they may contain either resources or hazards for the players to discover.
 If the players have completed building the human habitat and surrounded it with four defense turrets (before the humans arrive) then points are scored by resources collected, structures built, and hazards defeated.

boardgame: Dogs of War: Legends of the Crushed Planes
description:
Dogs of War is a wargame using 22mm miniatures to form together into squads that take to the battlefield and wage war on each other. The game has a rich history and setting with unique races and models. During each round of play, a player can move his or her squads, strike and shoot at enemy squads and even cast magic designed to help you and hinder allies. Various scenarios exist in the game that will define goals for the game, time limits and other details. There is even, in development, a campaign set of rules that will be designed directly into the core set of rules. Each race has it's own set of special rules and squad lists with attributed points values to make the game a "fair fight."

Play as one of several races for dominion and survival in the crushed planes. Play as the Ng'Awuthii, otherwise known as the Ng to foreign races, an advanced race of humanoids who possess technology far surpassing the technology of all other races combined. Or play as the talwart Tuskers, tribes of wise and savage nobility who use their heavy weaponry, tough skin and rending tusks to demolish their foes. Play as these, or several other races including the beautiful Eduran people who wield powerful magic and fight with inhuman grace, or the infamous Nameless, insect kin who travel above-ground in a pupal state which appear kin to dwarves of mythology or even the Kingdoms of Man.

From the Introduction:

The Republic Army army book requires the Star Confrontation rulebook in order to play.  This army book expands the base game by adding one additional army for players to select and use in play.  Each army has its own special abilities that will make every game unique.

The Republic Army have highly skilled soldiers commanded by psionic soldiers known as Masters.  When these forces work together they become a deadly fighting force that is near impossible to stop!

This is a Monopoly clone from Late from the Sky, a publisher that specializes in themed Monopoly games. All the deeds, spaces on the board and cards feature locations and landmarks from Kansas City.

Players roll dice to determine how far they move.  Landing on a space gives the player the option of buying the property.  When a player collects all the properties in one color group, the properties can be improved by first building skylines, and then upgrading to keys to the city. When players land on a space owned by another player they must pay rent.  Rent is determined by the location of the property and the number of improvements on the space.  Some spaces instruct a player to draw a card which can be beneficial or harmful to the player. Players must avoid going bankrupt to stay in the game.  The winner is the last player left in the game.

publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
The game
The aim of the game is to become rich. To do that, as merchant, the player should pay attention in selling, to do intelligent loans of money and wise investments.
The player represents a young merchant which should demonstrate all his ability to become the richest merchant of Venice.

How to play
The game is dividet into four turns. Each turn is divided into four round.
The first round concerns the travel organization in Venice, after the auction sale of the rights to be in command of each "muda". The players then must play to load the ships.
The second round concerns the selling of the goods that they are loaded. The goods must be sell in the same order as they were loaded.
In the third round the player can buy products in Oltremare and in the final step return to Venice. Here the players can sell their products or store them in their warehouses. The aim to store goods is to load them next turn, to sell them in another destination more profitable.

I'm looking for interested publishers for printing

"Off with his Head!" is a part of the "Tudor - Life in Times of Treason & Treachery" history kit which includes:
- History book
- Board game and dice
- Stickers
- Mini felt pens
- Colouring activity
- Press-out ship

Is it your unlucky day? Are you on the way to the block on Tower Green? Or will you be the one to get a Royal Pardon? Take it in turns to roll one dice, and obey the instructions on the board. When you get towards the end, you must roll the exact number to finish on 100. So if, for example, you're on square 97 and throw a 5, you go forward 3 spaces to 100, then 2 back and land on 98. When you throw the exact number, you've finished. The last (!) person to reach square 100 gets the Royal Pardon and wins!

Colonial Space Wars (CSW) is a tactical game based on a futuristic universe of science fiction that recreates battles between two or more armies. Each army is composed of square game pieces measuring 1.2 x 1.2 inches which represent their units.

During the game, pieces move across a rectangular board divided into squares arranged in rows and columns. In a game of CSW, each player has a number of units in his army which is set depending on the faction they are playing.  We call this set of units a “Combat Sword. Two basic Combat Swords (Union of Terra and Alpha Centauri Brotherhood) are available for an introductory game of CSW.

Game pieces represent the units, troops, and vehicles of a fighting force. The pieces have two sides, Front (Illustration and Health arrow) and Back (Name, Attributes and Skills). A player has some pieces with the skill Leader (X). While these pieces are on the board, they grant X command points at the beginning of each turn. Each side alternates turns, making one or more moves depending on the number of Command points available.

Each unit requires a number of command points to be used that turn; this is referred to as “Activating ” the unit. When all command points are spent, the opponent’s turn begins. Each unit, upon being activated, can move and act in any order. The possible actions are “Move”, “Embark/disembark”, “Attack” and “Use active skills”. A typical game is a Sudden Death match, where the winner is the player that eliminates the ir opponent’s ability to activate units.

This is a Scrabble like math game where you use your tiles to form valid math equations. Players follow basic order of operations when playing their equations. The first player to play all of their tiles wins and is declared the "Math Whiz".

Integrates with:

Fracul8

A golf boardgame containing 9 courses. The courses are stylistic rather than realistic in their portrayal, containing colored fields and numbered circles. The players use two ordinary six sided dice and move their markers to circles with numbers that are the same as the sum of their throw. The distribution of the numbered circles is made so that bad shots are less likely to occur (ie. bunker, ruff etc.) than shots to fairway. Putting is made using a special dice. Every hole has a small rule set that makes unusual events possible, such as hole in one etc.

boardgame: I Signori dei Draghi: I Segreti di Mysthras
description:
The first expansion for "I Signori dei Draghi":
Hordes of angry Dragons crowd Mysthras again after a long exile.
Countless creatures, new powers and new magical artifacts make your challenges even more exciting.
In addition to a new role strongly related to dragons, make their appearance on Mysthras creatures of "Lx" level and "allies" that will shape and make each game more strategic.
The Dragons are back in Mysthras. You will be able to control their powers?

The game box includes:
- 52 cards
- 2 instruction cards

From the rules: This is a most interesting and instructive game. As many children can play as there are individual playing cards. Give each player a card and place all the filler pieces in the center of the table face down. The object of the game is to complete cards correctly. Players draw from the face down fillers and try to complete a figure. If he/she does so the player draws another filler card and tries to add it properly. He continues to pick fillers until he draws a filler card that does not belong on his card. When that happens he/she returns the filler card face down into the center. After having mixed it with the rest of the remaining filler cards play continues   in turn until someone completes their card and wins the game,

Game has 105 cards, each bearing a picture of some object connected with the Bible or Bible times and in most cases one or more Bible references. Shuffle the cards and deal 10 cards to each player. Each player tries to make books. Three cards of the same class of subjets make up a book. These completed books are placed aside till the end of the game when they are counted. Whoever has the most books wins.

Each player calls any player he wishes and asks for a card from any series - (Animals, Trees etc.) If successful then the player may continue asking for other cards from any player he wants until he asks for a card from a player and that player does not have one that he asked for, at which time the active player draws 3 cards from the deck and play passes to the next player to his left.

Game #4012

Contents:
104 cards (4 sets of 26 cards)
rules

Includes rules for three separate games.

Game #1: The "Grab it quick" game
The Knock Knock cards are shuffled and placed in a draw pile, while the Who's There cards are placed face up on the table. A player says "Knock Knock", the other players respond, "Who's there?" and then all players race to find the correct card from those face up on the table. The person who grabs it first keeps both cards, and the person with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Game #2: Advanced grab it
Same as Game #1, except the Who's There cards are placed face down, adding a memory element.

Game #3: The "Remember Where" game
Instead of a draw pile, each player is dealt 4 Knock Knock cards, and on their turn must match a Knock Knock card to the Who's There cards which are face down on the table. If a player makes a match, they keep the cards and get a second turn. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Game consist of 4 pieces representing fire department apparatus and a spining dial. The movment of the various pieces of apparatur to the location of the fire is beset with many obstacles such as traffic jams, open drawbridges over which they must pass, railroad crossing with gates down, detours, engine and tire troubles and collisions which must be overcome. The oject of the game is to see which player best moves his piece of apparatus to the location of the fire, go into action, limber up his piece, put out the fire and then return to his fire house returning to their station by exact count on the spinner,

Mix between Trivia game and Top Trumps

The 32 trump cards are mixed. Each player receives 16 cards in front of them. The target goal is set in the middle of the table. 
The first player draws his top card and choose one of the four category, reads it and its value. The enemy reads value of same category. The higher value wins. The losing player ask the question from the card. Winner of the duel can answer the question and wins if he answer correctly. Otherwise he loses to opponent.
Some cards are with word "target goal", then the duel winner may shoot the goal.
After 16 duels the player who now has fewer cards takes "the end of the game" card and puts it behind his stack of cards. 
Duels continues until this card.
The winner is the player who has more trump cards in his hand.

BATTLE THIS! is the ultimate two players (Ages 12 to Adult) strategic board game for those who are looking for a challenge.  The game consists of two military forces; Green and Brown. Each force has a total of 16 playing pieces: 8 Soldiers, 4 Tanks and 4 Fighter Planes. Each playing pieces move and capture a certain way and each playing pieces are worth a certain amount of points. The objective of the game is to try to outwit your opponent; eliminate all of his / her playing pieces while trying to score as many points as you can.

The Game
Fleets at War! is a tactical wargame played on the floor or a large table with cardboard counters representing starships, fighters, and other space craft, set in the same universe as the Imperium Chronicles Role Playing Game.
 The wargame consists of three major factions: the Imperium, the Magna Supremacy, and the Pirate Clans.

The IMP System
Fleets at War! uses the IMP System that reduces the number of die rolls necessary during play.  All other calculations are done by referencing a chart of two opposing attributes (ex. Damage vs. Armor) to find a result.  The goal of this system is to speed up play and allow for large-scale battles.

Game Scale
Each counter in the game represents a single starship or a flight of four to five fighters.  An inch equals approximately 0.2 miles, or a mile equals 5 inches.  Each game turn equals roughly 2 to 3 minutes.

Stat Cards
Each class of ship (i.e.: heavy cruiser, destroyer, frigate, etc.) has a one- or two-sided card listing all the stats for that class, including its offensive and defensive capabilities.

Promo station card for Paperclip Railways. The base game has three 'placeholder' station cards that can be cashed in for a promo of choice 'from the box'.

This Promo card was given to customers of Boardgameguru, a UK board game store

Promo station card for Paperclip Railways. The base game has three 'placeholder' station cards that can be cashed in for a promo of choice 'from the box'.

This Promo card was given to players at the London on Board games club.

The game consists of two phases.  In the first phase, a players assemble a large (100 cm x 70 cm) jigsaw puzzle map of Italy.  In the second phase, players roll numbered and colored dice to place their tokens on cities and to place the special tiles representing the special landmarks of the regional capitals.

Players seek to place all of their tokens on cities and to place all of the regional capitals from their hands on to the board.  The numbered die indicates the quantity of tokens that may be placed, while the colored die indicates who may play tokens or place a regional capital tile.

The first player to place all of her tokens and regional capital tiles wins the game.

Players first assemble the large jigsaw puzzle game board then roll and move to take a tour of Italy.

Each turn, players roll a die and advance along a marked route from the starting point at Rome.  Landing on a red spot ends the turn; landing on a green spot allows the player to advance two extra cities; landing on a yellow spot causes a player to go back one city.

The winner is the first player to complete the entire tour route and return to Rome by exact die roll.

description:
It is a new card game that 2 or more people compete to spell most number of words using the most number of cards. It is unique that anyone can capture ('Bite') others' words by creating new ones from the spelled ones at any time, which makes the game fast pace and fun.

The person who has the most vocabulary may not necessarily win because smart use of special cards can turn the tide very quickly.

boardgame: Battle of Kawagoe: Hojo Ujiyasu's gamble
description:
Issue game in Command Magazine Japan #104

Operational level wargame of the Kawagoe battle during the Warring States Period (戦国時代 Sengoku jidai) in Japan. The Kawagoe battle is one of the three major surprise attack in Japanese History.

Players control either Hojo clan or the Allied force of Uesugi clan and Ashikaga Shogunate. Each player manages command allocation, movement and combat. Both force has different characteristics. The Hojo clan has small but powerful trained units. They also have special activation chit for Hojo Tsunasige. He was commander of Kawagoe castle and made effective sortie. The allied force has large units many more than Hojo clan's units. Their command control was inefficient because their army was a patchwork of clans.

Winner determined by special sudden-death condition or victory point. Victory point earn by elimination of opponent units and condition of Kawagoe Castle.

Issue game in Command Magazine Japan #104

Campaign level wargame for the battle of Itsukushima. The battle of Itsukushima is one of three major surprise attack in Japanese history.

Player control either Mouri clan or Sue clan. Each player manage movement, combat and negotiation with minor lords. Each counter represent lord and their soldiers. Some lords may change the loyalty to another side. In this part, Mouri clan has the advantage by their intelligence capability.

Winner determined by control of provinces.

boardgame: Monogamy: An Intimate Kit of Sensual Essentials
description: Expansion for Monogamy including 25 additional game cards and various accessories including a bullet, ties, spanker, feather.

Alchemist's Quest is a game about alchemists (players) competing to create compounds that will help them achieve one or all of the three great goals in life: Health, Wealth, and Power. Each player takes turn gathering elements (Rolling Dice) to create higher and higher order compounds (Buying table cards). Some of the cards have effects such as making a player an Apprentice (Giving the player an extra die to roll). Gathering elements is not without risk, however, as rolling the element death can cause negative consequences (such as losing a turn).

For many years, the King’s Royal Zoo has housed an odd collection of exotic Critters from across the kingdom. Only now, the Zoo is empty. A Dragon has been sleeping for centuries beneath a nearby mountain and has recently awoken. Intrigued by the King’s menagerie, the Dragon has stolen all the Critters from the Zoo and taken them back to its lair!

You and your fellow adventurers have answered the King’s call for help and volunteered to enter the Dragon’s lair to rescue the Critters, with the promise of great rewards for your efforts. The more Critters you bring back, the greater the reward.

Carrying a Magical Cart for your journey, how many Critters can you rescue? Sneak around the sleeping Dragon, and be careful not to wake it as you attempt to capture the King’s Critters!

Game Data:

Complexity: 2.5 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 3 on a 9 scale
Scale: Each player plays a Hero attempting to rescue Critters from the clutches of the Dragon.

Roll-and-move board game for theater fans with cards that challenge players to sing, dance, or act out lines from famous Broadway shows. Similar to The Game of Life, but for the Broadway-bound. Players start out in acting school and work their way up to the top, buying pictures and resumes, earning an Equity card, getting an agent and publicist, going to auditions, getting cast in shows, and maybe even winning a Tony Award.  Players are awarded Fan cards during the game, and whoever has the most fans at the end wins. Created by Broadway producer Ken Davenport ("Godspell," "Altar Boyz").

Sixx is a quick game with Magical pyramids (18 pyramids of 6 colors and 3 sizes).

The goal of the game is to create out of these  pyramids your secret pattern you have on your goal-card.

Sixx is a simplified version of Pyramideto game played with six cards only instead of 36. When children master the Sixx game, they can easily switch to Pyramideto.

To buy: http://mwe.cz/cart/index.php?route=product/product&product_id=73
Rules: http://www.mwe.cz/images/gettingstartedwithpyramids.pdf

An expansion set for Soccer Replay.

The game consists of a selection of 16 European teams from the 1994/95 season.

boardgame: Pettersson und Findus: Ein Feuerwerk für den Fuchs
description: Reaction game after a childrens book of the same name. The fox has to be chased off from the chickens. Every player gets a differently colored wooden ball and a puzzle consisting of six puzzle pieces which show Pettersson and Findus, building a rocket to chase away the fox. If a fox token has been revealed in the game, everybody makes rocket noises and throws his wooden ball, representing the rocket, into a platic cup. The two fastest players may take and use one of their puzzle pieces. The player completing the puzzle first wins the game.

Rodeo Rummy features the "card deck" dice from Square Shooters in a new take on traditional rummy games. At the start of a player's turn, she spins to find out which type of rummy hand she needs to roll on the dice, with an additional bonus or penalty coming from the inner ring of the spinner. You could earn an extra roll, be penalized for hearts or spades, earn double points for jokers, and more.

Once you have your target, you have at most three rolls of the dice to make it and earn points.

Rodeo Rummy includes instructions for other rummy variations that can be played with the Square Shooters dice.

With its many tribal, political, religious, and cultural divisions, Africa has long been a continent at war - both with itself and with others. For much of the 20th century, there has been a near-constant state of military unrest, from Cold War proxy wars with Soviet and Western powers supporting their African allies in live-fire incarnations of this ideological struggle, and revolutions against established regimes, to foreign interventions under the banner of peacekeeping and mercenary operations alike. Bush Wars brings this hotbed of unrest to the wargaming table, with scenarios, orders of battle and background information.

Where would we be without the light bulb, telephones, the world wide web or even tea bags and sliced bread? BrainBox Inventions contains 54 beautifully-illustrated cards showing when, where and who invented these everyday items. BrainBox Inventions strikes a great balance between learning and fun as less serious inventions such as the Whoopee cushion and Lava Lamp have been included as well as the patently strange which never quite made it such as the weed cutting golf club and the lollistraw! The object of the game is to study a card for 10 seconds and then answer a question based on the roll of a die. If the question is answered correctly, the card is kept and the person with the most number of cards after 5 or 10 minutes is the winner. This simple concept can be played by all ages and in large groups as well as a 1 person game. Made from a minimum of 70% recycled material and designed in the UK, the BrainBox range is fabulous fun. Find out who the BrainBox is in your family – it may not be who you think it is!

Did you know that during the first moon landing the astronauts spent 21 hours on the surface of the moon? Or that the Berlin Wall was 87 Miles long? BrainBox World History contains 56 beautifully-illustrated cards highlighting some of the greatest and most significant historical events. The object of the game is to study a card for 10 seconds and then answer a question based on the roll of a die. If the question is answered correctly, the card is kept and the person with the most number of cards after 5 or 10 minutes is the winner. This simple concept can be played by all ages and in large groups as well as a 1 person game. Made from a minimum of 70% recycled material and designed in the UK, the BrainBox range is fabulous fun. Find out who the BrainBox is in your family – it may not be who you think it is!

Kryptos is a trivia card game.

The game consists of 50 cards, each with 3 hints.

Players simultaniously try to find the right solution. If a player can find it with only the first hint, he scores 3 points, after the second hint, he gets 2 points and after the 3rd one point.

The first hint is a sentence of numbers and letters, for example T7WW.

The second hint is a little more precous, like The 7 W of the W)

And the third hint is a sketch, for example a pyramid.

The solutions for this examples would be "The 7 Wonders of the World"

Buzzles is a trivia game about wordplay.
The game consists of 50 cards, each with 3 hints on a neologism created by the combination of two words that sound quite similar.

Players simultaneously try to find the right word. If a player can find the word with only the first hint, he scores 3 points, after the second hint, he gets 2 points and after the 3rd one point.

The first hint is a vague description of the new word (containing attributes of both original words).

The second hint is a synonym for one of the words.

And the third hint is a funny sketch, showing the combination of the words.

Game description from the publisher:

The Gears of War: Mission Pack 1 print-on-demand expansion adds two new missions to Gears of War: The Board Game. Search for an isolated Stranded in hostile territory and stand toe-to-toe with the most vicious leader of the Locust horde – General RAAM himself. Face six all-new enemies in two of the most challenging and exciting missions yet!

The two new scenarios in Mission Pack 1 deliver all the tense action fans have come to expect from Gears of War: The Board Game. In "Search for the Stranded", the COGs must work together to rescue an isolated scavenger while recovering his valuable cache of ammunition and supplies. Then, in "The Showdown", the COGs must overcome a savage Berserker before facing off against none other than General RAAM himself!

description:
You are an Overlord ruling over a small army of devotees. Your destiny is upon you - can you capture your opponent's Throne for yourself, or even better, capture the opposing Overlord? Each player gets nine pieces - Mages, Soldiers, and an Overlord - with which they must destroy or be destroyed!

Players' armies are set up on opposite sides of the circular board, pieces in position to defend the Overlord and the Throne space it sits on. To win the game, you must maneuver your pieces to the other side of the board and either capture your opposing Overlord, or move your Overlord into their throne.

The circular battlefield makes for unusual strategies and challenges even the most experienced strategy gamer!

It is the year 2592. After countless years of war and struggle, the Defense of United Colonies (DUC) has triumphed against the Galactic Ordered Terrorists (GOT). Humanity now has high hopes for this new Era, as everywhere people attempt to rebuild the devastated worlds of the UC. 
But its hopes were dashed to pieces as a new enemy, the Horde, rose through the depths of the planet, Telocto, ravishing its colonists, and breaching evacuation ships, got to new planets. In a matter of a few months, over half of the UC’s planets were infected. The only known way to defeat them is to glass infected planets, destroying any life support that planet has. On the planet known as Sow, a force of seasoned, grizzled veterans, known as the Condors, attempt one last stand, before they are exterminated.

Condors of Sow is a cooperative game for 1-4 players, as they attempt to complete each mission. Using a token and a pencil for each player you will have three actions on your turn: move, attack, pick up an item, revive player, reload, or overkill move.

After players move, The Horde move, by having players mark one dot at the end of a predetermined number lines closest to them.

All this game needs is a player sheet, a map, and a pencil and token per player.

Players have the use of multiple weapons and tactics. You must use cover, or you will die. Fight hard Condors! Humanity’s last hope is you.

This set contains 15 cards (11 Doors and 4 Treasures) for Munchkin, drawn by Mike Krahulik the artist for the Penny Arcade webcomic, and designed by Steve Jackson the creator of Munchkin. The cards reference characters and situations from Penny Arcade. The card mechanics, however, are different than the norm, so this set will add variety to any Munchkin game.

Part of the Munchkin series.

Game description from the publisher:

Remember when you just THOUGHT everyone you work with was a...ZOMBIE!

Well, as it turns out, you were right.

The CEO is now the ZEO. Your coworkers now moan in addition to the aimless shambling. Suddenly, the receptionist is very interested in you. And the lunch room... Let's just say, you don't want what everyone else is having!

Zombies!!! 11: Death Inc. simulates what would happen if you went to work one day and discovered that all your coworkers really were zombies! Of course, you being the "brown-noser" that you are, you decide that rescuing the surviving board members just might help your career. Unfortunately, you aren't the only one with this idea.

Zombies!!! 11: Death Inc., an expansion for Zombies!!!, uses the same game play as the base game: Players take turns laying tiles (to create the board), fending off zombies by rolling dice, scavenging for goods in the form of health (to allow re-rolls) and ammo (to improve rolls), and playing cards to help you or hurt the other players, all in an effort to be the first to the helipad tile to escape (or kill 25 Zombies).

description:
Game description from the publisher:

Times have been hard. To save on money, you've moved out of your old castle and into a luxurious ravine. You didn't like that castle anyway; it was always getting looted and never at a reasonable hour. And if it wasn't barbarians it was the plague, or sometimes both would come at once, and there wouldn't be enough chairs. The ravine is great; you get lots of sun, and you can just drop garbage wherever you want. In your free time you've taken up begging. Begging is brilliant conceptually, but tricky in practice since no one has any money. You beg twigs from the villagers, and they beg them back, but no one really seems to come out ahead. That's just how life is sometimes. You're quietly conquering people, minding your own business, when suddenly there's a plague, or barbarians, or everyone's illiterate, and it's all you can do to cling to some wreckage as the storm passes through. Still, you are sure that, as always, you will triumph over this adversity, or at least do slightly better than everyone else.

Dominion: Dark Ages is the seventh addition to the game of Dominion. It contains 500 cards but is not a standalone game. It adds 35 new Kingdom cards to Dominion, plus new bad cards you give to other players (Ruins), new cards to replace starting Estates (Shelters), and cards you can get only via specific other cards. The central themes are the trash and upgrading. There are cards that do something when trashed, cards that care about the trash, cards that upgrade themselves, and ways to upgrade other cards.

Part of the Dominion series.

boardgame: Martian Empires: Wargames Rules for the Wars Between Mars and Earth
description:
Martian Empires is a set of Fast Play rules for Victorian Science Fiction Games using 15mm figures. It has been written to accompany the Martian Empires range of 18mm figures now sold by Fighting 15s. The rules are in full colour throughout and run to 52 pages plus the cover. They include the full rules, army lists and five scenarios.

Each player commands an army of figures, depicting terrestrial or Martian troops, mounted onto stands (recommended frontage 40mm for 15/18mm figures). Ranges are determined by measurement (no grid), firing and close combat are resolved by dice rolls. Leaders are needed to order troops and vehicles to move, but soldiers sensibly fire at visible enemies on their own initiative. Victory or defeat conditions are scenario-based.

family: Books: The War of the Worlds

description:
Based on rules published by MindSports.nl:

Mephisto is a puzzle game played by virtually any number of players. It requires a double-six domino set, five uniquely distinguishable pawns, five corresponding dice, and a one-minute timer.

The playing area is made by laying all twenty-eight dominoes face up into rectangle of 7x8 squares. Other than the double-blank, no blanks can touch the edge of another blank. Pawns are set on any five of the blanks, dice are rolled, and the timer is flipped.

Players examine the board and call out bids. When the timer ends, the high bidder must move the pawns to spaces with a sum that meets the bid. (There is also a special bid for landing all five pawns on matching spaces.) Meeting the bid wins the stake. (The publisher suggests candy or beer, but scoring one point would suffice.) Failure pays an equal stake to every opponent!

Pawns move orthogonally the number of spaces indicated by their corresponding die. They are not allowed to cross the same space twice nor any occupied space.

With magical pyramids Tic-tac-toe changes into a three-dimensional challenge. The situation on the board is moving fast and simple rule forces the starting player to activity.

Playrs place their pyramids on 3x3 grid board to form a Troika.

TROIKA is any combination of your own pyramids in which the two basic parameters (color and size) occur either in the ratio 3:0:0 or 1:1:1 (either all the same or all different).

Draw is not possible.

Full Rules:

Preparation of the game

You will need one trio each of white, yellow, red, blue, green and black pyramids.
Players divide pyramids. One of the players has all nine pyramids of lighter colors (white, yellow, red), the second has all nine pyramids of darker colors (green, blue, black).
Start of the Game

The younger player rolls the die. The player with the color on the die starts. Players consecutively place one of their pyramids on the board.

Placing of Pyramids

You have to place one pyramid per turn. Once laid pyramids can not be moved during the rest of the game.

Pyramids can be placed on free fields, yours or your opponent's pyramids.

Attention! Pyramids can not be placed on pyramids of the same size nor on pyramids two size bigger/smaller.

When laying pyramids may therefore occur following four options. You can lay down:
any pyramid on a free field
small pyramid on any middle pyramid
middle pyramid on any small or large pyramid
large pyramid on any middle pyramid

Only top pyramid counts for the goal!

The goal

Players try to create a TROIKA of pyramids lying in one row or one column, or the two main diagonals on the 3x3 board.

TROIKA

TROIKA is any combination of your own pyramids in which the two basic parameters (color and size) occur either in the ratio 3:0:0 or 1:1:1 (either all the same or all different).

Examples:

Large red, medium red and small red do form a TROIKA, because their color scheme is 3:0:0 (three red, no white, no yellow) and size scheme is 1:1:1 (one large, one medium and one small pyramid).
Medium blue, medium green and medium black do form a TROIKA, because their color scheme is 1:1:1 (one blue, one green, one black) and size structure is 3:0:0 (three medium, no large and no small pyramid).
Large blue, medium blue and small green do not form a TROIKA, because their color scheme is 2:1:0 (two blue, one green and no black pyramid)

Troika Training

To explain the rules it always helps when you form several TROIKAS, especially if you play with school children. Take any two pyramids and challenge players to find the third pyramid required to form a Troika. Practice this several times.
End Game

The game ends when the first player creates TROIKA in a row or column or one of two main diagonals on the board. This player is the winner.

If a player has at least one of his pyramids, but cannot place any of them, he loses the game.
If both players run out of all their pyramids and no player created TROIKA, the player who did NOT start the game, wins.

(For this reason it is good to keep the die next to the game board.)

Rules of 5 games: http://www.mwe.cz/images/gettingstartedwithpyramids.pdf
To buy: http://mwe.cz/cart/index.php?route=product/product&product_id=73

This game one of the many games in the Angry Birds Series.

With the Angry Birds Mega Smash Board Game you can build, launch, and destroy with an exciting skill-and-action game based on the best-selling digital app Angry Birds. Players draw a mission card and build the structure shown before their opponents launch an Angry Bird in an attempt to knock it, and the pigs, down to win points. This larger version includes many of the pieces from 'Angry Birds Knock on Wood' and 'Angry Birds on Thin Ice' plus new characters and features, such as four sculpted pigs (including the mustachioed pig), the white bird and the blue bird. Exclusively at Toys R Us.

From the box: 
"Not only speed counts at the steeple-chase, it mainly demands skills. Only the one who jumps over the fences safely and manages to sidestep the other obstacles is the winner. A game of skills for 2-4 players where the horse tokens have to be flipped through the show jumping course."

A steeple-chase course is created using the 2 pieces of string supplied. 5 high obstacles, on bases, are placed at regular intervals around the course. 10 ground obstacles are arranged between these. 16 horse show tokens are placed in the middle of the course and the horse figure to the side. This is the starting point.

Each player takes 1 small and one large coloured horse token (with horse sticker on) and placing the large token on the supplied piece of felt, uses the small token to try and flick his horse around the course, following the flicking rules laid out. As soon as you successfully complete the course you may take a horse shoe token from the middle. First player to collect 3 horse shoe tokens is the winner.

Nice components. Neat, portable game.

The trivia game with all the answers.

All the Answers is an interactive race through 4 challenging trivia categories: Who, What, Where, and Whatever.  Be three first to capture all 4 category cards and return to the centre and you're the winner.  A unique blend of party game and trivia game, everyone has an opportunity to answer all the questions in 
All the Answers.

What makes this trivia game unique is that it asks questions with multiple answers and you have to provide as many of them as you can, your opponents can try if you don't say all of them.  It is best with 4-6 players.

Dragonfly is a Chess variant played on a 7x7 board using most of the pieces of orthodox Chess, but not a Queen. The setup position puts the king in the center of the first row, with the Bishops left and the Knights to the right. Pawns have no initial double move, all other pieces move as in Chess. Castling is allowed, too.

In practice, two sets of pieces are necessary to execute the game's Shogi-like drops: captured pieces are held in reserve and can re-enter later under the capturing player's control.

Pawns which reach the opposite side promote to a unit in the opponent's reserve (!), if there is one, essentially re-capturing the reserve unit. Captured pawns cannot be dropped and remain out of play.

Statistical American Football game. Incredibly fast to play, it just uses one or two dice rolls to produce a result. This fast method means that a whole season - every game - can be played in one evening.

Each team has a specific rating based on the statistical data for that season. These are compared in a match-up and the dice does the rest.

Originally designed by Jon H. Wight in 1978, Bob Valvano produced updated ratings and rereleased the game for the 1985 and 1986 seasons.

A trading card game based on the "Berserk" manga series by Kentaro Miura.

The goal of the game is to seize control of 3 of the 5 cities in the game including the capital city or to reduce the cards in your opponent's deck to 0.

This is done by playing character and effect cards at the cities' locations. All cards enter play face-down, even the cities, and you can flip only one card per turn face-up plus one card per battle. Cities are flipped face-up whenever a face-up character is at its location and flipped face-down again when the last face-up character leaves that location. Battles over cities are fought with characters with freely distributable damage in an order determined by the character's range.

Card costs are a mix of payment costs (discarding cards from your hand) and a threshold cost (the number of cities that are currently face-up).
