description:
This is an umpired miniatures wargame with true fog of war and hidden troop values. All combat systems are integrated in the game and have been computer tested. The game may be played at the divisional, corps, or army level depending on how many troops you might have and how much time you have to conclude the battle. Balanced armies may be attained by using the Historical Correction Table, thus offering the chance of a one-sided game.

There are interesting command, morale and terrain effects for each game. Artillery is well catered and very realistic results should be obtained.

Appendices include campaign suggestions.

Detailed, but very fast playing. All you need are the troops, the rules and one kind friend to act as the umpire.

Amazonas is a dice-roll based game for 2-4 players. The players score points by "shooting" animals on the way to the finish line. Everyone starts with 10 bullets and uses the 1-2-3 dice to move forward and the regular dice to shoot at the animals. When you arrive on a tile with an animal on it, you can decide if you want to shoot at that animal. This is done by the regular die. If you roll a number (1-6) that has an animal token on it, you "shot" the animal, you remove the token and keep it. Every shot costs one bullet. If you miss, you can try again by using another bullet. Obviously, if less animals remain, your chances to shoot one is smaller. Once you hit an animal, the next player's turn begins. If you arrive on an "Amazonas" tile, you can choose the animal you want to shoot at. Different animals give you different points (e.g. parrot one, lion six).
There are also obstacles on the way that players must cross. If they fail to cross them, they loose animals worth of 6 points. If you don't have enough animals to return, you can give back your bullets (1 bullet - 1 point).

SWAMP: In order to cross the swamp, you need to roll a number (with the regular dice) that corresponds with the value of a neighboring tile (except the one behind you). In this case, you can move to that tile, making your way across the swamp.
Once you make it through an obstacle (or even if you fail), you arrive in a village, where you can sell your animals for bullets according to the animal's value. (For a 6 point lion you get 6 bullets).

CHASM: In order to cross the chasm, you need to get 10 points out of 3 rolls.

COCONUT: Monkeys are throwing coconuts at you, and you must avoid them. You place your marker anywhere on the first row (there are four) and throw with the blue and red dice. The values of the colored dice show you where the monkeys are throwing the coconuts (red value counted from the left and blue from the right). If they miss, you go to the next row, if either of the hits you, you fall.

SUSPENSION BRIDGE: from 8 rolls you must get all the 6 values of the dice (1-6, in any order)

CROCODILES: You need to row through the water by using the green and white 1-2 dice (Right paddle green dice, left paddle white). Equal numbers: you go straight ahead 1 or 2 tiles. Green 1, white 2: you move one ahead and one left. If you hit a croc on the way, you fail.

ROPE: You have 8 rolls and you need to get to the other side by always rolling a bigger number than the previous.

Once you get to the finish line, you count the value of your animals.

Death Ride Salerno - 16th Panzer Expansion is the third, of four, title in the Death Ride Salerno game set.  This game expands the beachhead to the east and introduces British forces from the 10th Corps.  The main battle in this part of the bridgehead was for Battipaglia.  The British Guard Brigade managed to get into the outskirts of the town only to be thrown out.  The front in this area was fairly settled for the 6 days of the battle and the Germans did not have the stregnth here to push the British back.  The German counter-attack instead focused on the American forces and ultimately fell short.

Naval gunfire support is present in the British area along with significant air support.  A new Naval Chart is included in this game that helps to organize the naval gunfire ships off shore and even takes into account ships needing to come out of the support line to conduct resupply.

The game system rules are written along the Seven Battlefield Operating Systems of Maneuver, Command and Control, Fire Support, Intelligence, Air Defense, Mobility and Survivability, and Combat Service Support.  Enhancements to those plus an Amphibious Enhancement are planned for future release.  A Fire Support Artillery Enhancement has already been released and provides for artillery mission markers that restrict artillery support to Direct Support (DS), General Support (GS), and Reinforcing (R).

boardgame: Death Ride Salerno: Herman Goring/15th Panzer Grenadier
description:
This game is the fourth and last installment in the Death Ride Salerno game set.  It is set in the Salerno city area and the passes heading north toward Naples.  The Herman Goring Division moved south to help 16th Panzer as did 15th Panzer Grenadier and held the British back from moving north.  While they tried to push the British back the British were too well supplied with heavy weapons and naval gunfire support.  The British were funneled into the passes between the hills leading north and a heavy attack could not be sufficiently concentrated to be successful.

The rules are written using the Seven Battlefield Operating Systems as a framework.  They are Maneuver, Command and Control, Fire Support, Mobility and Survivability, Combat Service Support, Intelligence, and Air Defense.  They are not complex and make the game easier to understand than most large games.  Putting all 4 games together creates a monster of monster games.  All together having 4 Allied divisions at company level and 5 German divisions at platoon level.

Twisted Texting is a party game where players try to get the other players to say a word on a card by spelling the word with transparent letter tiles. The 'Texter' has a limited pool of letter tiles and many times must improvise with misspelling, similar sounding and resemblance to get the players to say the word.

Texters have a limited time to get players to say words and race against a sand timer to get as many words correctly guessed as they can. Each player takes turns as Texter. The game plays over 3 rounds with each round providing a new pool of letters. Each successive round provides a smaller pool of letters.

A Game of aerial Dragon Combat!  Use your dragon to fight others in 3-D aerial action.  Anywhere from two to ten players can engage.

The game takes place in the air surrounding a floating castle.  Teams of 5 dragons each (chromatic on one side, metallic on the other) square off in battle to decide who takes possession of the floating Castle and the immense treasure within!

The basic game starts with each dragon's player rolling a d10 for initiative.  Highest roll goes first, ties roll-off.

Movement then commences, allowing each dragon to move 18 inches, curving or turning at will.  Facing is not taken into account.

After movement, each dragon may attack (either with breath weapons or by melee if in range) or defend.

Attacking with breath weapons have a 9 inch range, and automatically hits any target within range.  The target takes the 1d8x10 hp, and may save for half damage (all of the dragon stats are listed on a stat sheet that was included).  Breath weapons may be used three times per dragon per game.

For melee attacks, the dragon must be within base contact.  The dragon rolls a d20, and hits on a six or better.  damage is recorded against the targets hp.

If a dragon is out of range, it may Defend, allowing it to strike any dragon that comes into attack range, even on another dragon's turn (used to counterstrike when an enemy dragon moves into range).

Dragons are defeated when their HP are reduced to zero.  The first team to destroy the other, wins.

Advanced rules allow for dragon individualization, age categories, distinct breath weapon types, better Thac0 or ht points, immunities, riders, etc.

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Allies – Pooky
description:
While the master's away, the familiar will play! Zot's turning in early for the night, but Pooky's not done with the party. With a stash of gold and a big chip on his wee shoulder, this strange little rabbit-thing is looking to prove he's more than just Zot's whipping bunny.


    The Good: In the dungeon, Pooky's psychotic behavior has gotten the party out of some pretty nasty jams.
    The Bad: That rabbit is even more insane without Zot's influence. Seriously.


The Red Dragon Inn: Allies - Pooky lets the little white rabbit wreck havoc all on his own! This forty-card deck isn't an expansion for Zot the Wizard from The Red Dragon Inn, but rather allows Pooky to be a fifth character at the table and party with The Party. Bringing all his nature and vices to the table, get ready to see Pooky start partying a bit too hard for the rest of the table.

The Red Dragon Inn: Allies is a new series of expansions for any standalone The Red Dragon Inn game. Each Allies set includes a single Character Deck, plus components to add a brand new character to your game.

description:
In his turn player rolls 2 big dice (d32): first dice with letters, second dice with topics. Player tries to form a word in rolled topic what start with letter from the other dice. Then He may advence his game-piece as many squares forward as many letters is in his word. There are many bonus squares with special action on the track when landed (another turn etc.)

Player who finishes first on the track is the winner.

POPULATION: 4 is a fast paced Survival-Horror Board RPG with an unique premise: Get it, play it, write your survival story and have it published on the game's site.

THE GAME
The game can be played up to 5 players (1 Zombie Controller and 4 Survivors, that's where "Population: 4" comes from), where the survivors are to get the neccesary items to escape the city while the zombies lurk the districts of the city searching for food. Cooperativism between survivors is essential to escape, although every player is free to choose if they are to cooperate, ignore or betray everybody else.

CITY, GUNS AND SURVIVORS
The city has several locations where different items can be found (eg. You won't find ammunition at the Train Station), so the search strategy must be smart and coordinated. There is a wide variety of guns, just to mention some the classic Crowbar and Chainsaw, to the imperative Shotgun and Sniper Rifle.
Survivors come in 8 classes: Soldier, Policeman (Riot), Fireman, Medic, Athlete, Explorer, Scavenger and Child). The way you use every class' talent and overcome it's weaknesses have real weight on the strategy development and use of items.

STORY AND STRESS
Each match carries an excellent and different story, it is highly recommended for those who get the game to write their stories and send them through the game's website to have them published. The interaction between players is unique and spectacular. The stress, strategy and decisions to make are realistic

Munchkin Zombies 3: Hideous Hideouts is a "Dungeon" expansion for Munchkin Zombies that's similar in nature to Munchkin 6: Demented Dungeons and Munchkin Cthulhu 4: Crazed Caverns. It comes with 16 standard-sized Portal cards such as "Breach the Perimeter" and "Walking in Circles" and 20 double-sized Dungeon cards (e.g., "Fort Deadly", "The Creepy Isolated Cabin of the Dead", and "ResurrectiCon") that warp the current rules.

Part of the Munchkin series.

The Sector Kadom Campaign is a free to print expansion for Frontiers. It was going to be released as official, boxed expansion but the publishers decided against it. It has been posted as free to print on the designers website.

This expansion adds some new units, terrain, weapon types and a campaign with multiple scenarios. The new units including exo-tanks, spiders, a sniper, virus-link and other new infantry and vehicle units. There is also more terrain, such as a garage, hills and more building pieces.

Be the first player to get rid of all your cards by making pairs to win! To play, you must first learn to speak 'cat'! "Meow" means "No!" and "Meow, Meow" means "Yes!"  If a player uses a language other than cat, you must ignore them.  You can only speak cat while responding to questions in this game!

Or play the optional rules for a matching game by matching the most pairs by flipping over cards to win.

Contents include:
50 Cat's Pajama's jumbo color and shape cards
2 sticker sheets
Easy to follow rules

Heroes of Feonora is an RPG Board Game designed for players of all ages. It begins in a bustling village alive with activities set in a colorful world of humor and mystery.  The inhabitants of this world have unique backgrounds and personalities.  Some of them might even be persuaded to join up with a player character, bringing valuable skills along with them.  In order to succeed the group will need to work together as they make preparations for the perilous journey ahead.  Earn Copper Pieces by taking on part-time work at the local bakery, helping townsfolk with their quirky troubles, fishing and more... Of course, those with a more daring personality will find there is plenty of coin to be made catching dastardly Criminals or seeking out fame and fortune in the Fighter's Fighting Guild.  Recruit Companions, find a trusty Mount and gather supplies.  But don't delay, for once the Town Timer runs out the group must set out at last ~ into dangerous wild lands of Feonora.  How well did the group prepare?  You'll soon find out as the party encounters hostile foes or interesting characters looking to barter and trade.  Search for hidden treasure and get swept into unplanned Side Quests.  Good teamwork is vital for those wishing to reach the ultimate, harrowing Final Encounter, which will require a different strategy altogether depending on the Story.  Should the group survive the journey and complete the adventure (together as one) they will enter a special phase of the game that will reveal who among them was most worthy of honor and prestige.

The Red Skulls Faction is a free to print expansion for Frontiers. It was going to be released as official, boxed expansion but the publishers decided against it. It has been posted as free to print on the designers website.

This expansion adds a entirely new faction, the communistic pirates, the Red Skulls. There are brand new units, cards, and artifacts. The new units include, Turrets, Infiltrators, Pirates, Assault squads and more!

The object of the game is to roll numbers 1-5 in sets of three to get right colored hen.

In his turn player rolls the 4 dice up to 3 times and may choose or reroll some of the dice. Player must collect three of a kind. The Hen shown on the dice count as a joker and can replace any number. If player succeed to roll f.e. 3 x three he gets a white hen.
Player who collet all 5 hens first is the winner.

There are some other game variant as well in this game to learn numbers and colors.

Children's card game that will help a little with their mental arithmetic.

Four cards are placed face up. Each player gets 6 cards. On their turn a player may play a card with the same value as one of the cards in the four face-up cards. He then takes both cards and starts a pile of won cards. Alternatively, he may play a card from his hand that is the total of two or more face-up cards:
Example. The four cards face up are 2, 3, 5, 7. The player uses an 8 card from his hand to take the 5 and the 3.

The face-up cards are replenished after every turn so that each player starts their turn with 4 cards in the middle.

If players run out of cards in their hand, they are dealt six more cards each and the game proceeds as before. When there are not enough cards to complete a later deal, the game ends and the one with the most won cards, wins.

Jigsaw puzzle with an educational value: 6 panels, each panel is shown in 6 designs, along with free fields for the matching image. On display is one place or activity - the train station, business, etc. -  and the matching image must be placed.

The pictures on the blackboard and the pictures of this place are numbered alike.

Rules in the lid.

More detailed Information:
In this game it's important to observe. Each card show a person. This person can be a glazier, a gardener, a firefighter or another profession. These people find their place during the game on the white areas on the panels next to a representative exercise: the glazier replace broken glass, the gardener pour the flowers, the firefighter extinguish the burning house ...

In the first step a moderator is to be chosen. The moderator distribute the panels under the players and places the cards face down on a pile. Now he takes one card after another, shows the person and calls them by name. Each player looks now, whether the person fits to one of the pictures on his panel. If matching the player calls "for me", takes the card and places it in the appropriate space on the panel.

The first to complete all white boxes on his panel is the winner.

Nickropolis is a grave robbing game, where you, as a thief, try to swipe as much loot from the necropolis as you can before you're sealed in forever. The hex card graveyard is laid out differently each game. Move from tomb to tomb, fighting the undead for their treasures, while avoiding traps and the other theives. One word of warning though. You'll want to race to get your goods to the surface. The necropolis locks itself down as you play, so you not only have to collect the loot, you must return to the center periodically and toss the loot up to score it. You can also pickpocket the other thieves if you don't want to fight the undead for it. Nickropolis is a quick, easy to learn, and rather brutal game. If you do join the undead in the tombs, you can still haunt the other thieves and win. Happy stealing!

The Story:

Your buddy Marley motions for you to join him at a dark table crammed in the corner of the bar. You and the other thieves go over and seat yourselves around him. He looks around nervously before he speaks.

"Got a job for you. You know they're planting Ol' Lady Elston tonight? Gonna do some spooky ceremony before they put her in that family necropolis. Listen up. Gatekeeper Willy is in charge of unlocking the mausoleum, and Willy likes his bottle. So I'm gonna make sure he has a nice full one tonight, because that's the kind of guy I am. Once he's hammered, you break in. Got a good map. I'll keep a lookout up top and sort out your loot. Just one thing, you got to be quick. That place has some kind of clockwork mechanism that will seal you down in there with all them dead Elston's, and you don't want that. They say some of them ain't quite as dead as they should be. Now whoever tosses up the most loot items will be known as the king of the thieves around here. You in?"

This expansion will include quite a few additions to the game:

Dropped/Deployed Weapons. Sometimes there's no better way to get away than to put something in your opponent's path, like oil, smoke, caltrops, mines, cinder blocks ... you get the idea - this expansion includes passive and deployed weapons with expanded rules to incorporate them. But that's not all ...

Auxiliary Weapons. Sometimes there's no better way to finish off your enemy than with something explosive, like rockets - This expansion brings with it new goodies that can take the place of your secondary weapon slots. So say goodbye to Mr. peashooter and say hello to Mr. flamethrower.

Contact Weapons. Who said auto-combat wasn't up close and personal? For those who prefer ramming to shooting, this deck will include a wide range of options of Rams, blades, snares ... if it's vaguely pointy, it's in here

While taking a stroll on the beach of a skull-shaped island, you catch sight of a strange piece of paper sticking out the stand. Your curiosity leads you to dig in the sand with your hands, and you discover a golden coin and parts of a map that you piece together. What if the map leads to Captain Willain Kidd's fabulous treasure...

In Skeleton Island, players try to piece together treasure maps and find gold coins by digging in the "sand" – more specifically, by digging in the sand-decorated cards. To set up the game, someone takes the deck of 49 sand cards, splits them in half, turns one half face-down, shuffles the two halves together, then swishes the combined deck around on the table to make a pile of sand.

Each turn, a player takes either a standard turn or a piracy turn. In a standard turn, the player takes one "free" card – that is, a card not covered at all by any other card – and looks at it. The player can keep the card, or return it to the edge of the sand pile and draw a second card, which she must keep. (If only one or two cards are free, the player can close her eyes and blow on the pile to spread the sand.)

Players are searching for gold coins, which are valuable on their own, or halves and quarters of a treasure map. The first player to hand in a complete map of one of the five colors collects the island card of this color, which is worth five coins.

On a piracy turn, the player removes any three cards she's collected from the game, then either takes two consecutive standard turns or swipes any card from an opposing player.

When all the islands have been claimed or all the sand cards removed, the game ends and the player with the most coins wins.

The object is to "infect" as many players as you can using the cards in your hand and those available from a 5-card draw pool before the draw deck in gone. Each STD has a point value. At the end of the hand, the lowest score wins.

There are 3 ways to "infect" another player:

1- creating a 3 of a kind and passing it to a player
2- after each turn, give 1 of the STDs from your hand to the player on your right 
3- using a special event card that allows you to give half of the STDs you've been "infected" with to another player.

Beware of the HIV card... if you end up with it at the end of the game your points double.

Cycling through the Paris. Game is similar to Game of Goose.
Players roll the dice, advance their pawns and who finishes first is the winner.
Of course there are some special squares on the track.

boardgame: Over the Top: The Dogs of War
description:
A battle game of feints and bluffs where players vie for control of No Man's Land.

Game play is comprised of selecting from a limited pool of resources, and committing them to action.  Players face off knowing only how much has been committed, but not what specifically is sitting before them.  When the wave is revealed, they battle and ground is gained or lost.

Currently this game is in production and testing is available on Vassal

Timeline is a trivia board game about an exciting journey through Switzerland.
Players answer the questions and if these are correct place that card in front of their teammates with a series of eight cards. The cards must be arranged so that the numbers increase from left to right annually. 
The game consists of important events in Swiss history, politics, entertainment, business, traffic, sports, personalities, and inventions.

boardgame: Dr. Playwell's Controlling Your Anger Card Game
description:
From the publisher's website:
Help kids take control of their anger before it takes control of them. This game teaches children how to recognize and change the things that make them angry, how to compromise in situations where there is a conflict, how to talk about their feelings rather than act on them, and how to self-monitor their emotions. The bonus activities in this game help children learn positive ways to handle frustration in their day-to-day lives.

Appropriate responses to the question or activity on the back of the card allow the player to collect cards, and the first player to collect two from each "suit" wins.

This card game is also part of the Dr. Playwell's Amazing Card Games Collection.

Word game in grid as Scrabble and other similar games.
In his turn player roll the dice and advance his pawn around the board and places the letters to same row or column he stand and scoring points by forming words.

Player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: Kangaroos & Koalas: The Aussie game of ups and downs
description:
There are 4 games and a double-sided playing board. The main game, Kangaroos and Koalas, is a Snakes and Ladders type of roll and move game, where the aim of the game is to reward positive character traits displayed by the kangaroos and to penalise negative character traits displayed by the koalas by moving playing pieces to higher or lower squares. For example, landing on 'Caution' moves a player up to Safety' and landing on 'Selfish' moves a player back to 'Lonely'.

The second game, 'Where's Kolo?' is a search and find game, involving luck as well as colour, number and picture recognition. Players race around the board, trying to find Kolo Koala underneath special gumnut tokens. Finding a "Here's Kolo" token allows a player to collect a piece of a jigsaw puzzle. The first to complete their puzzle is the winner.

Rules for two other games, using the same components, are included in the box.

Components: 1 double-sided game board, 1 die, 6 playing pieces, tokens, 4 jigsaws

This game brings the set, Incredible Courage at Austerlitz, to its completion.  This third game contains the French and Coalition forces that fought in the northern part of the battlefield.  General Bagration, given orders to move toward Brunn to the west, dutifully moves out around 10am and is given a nasty surprise.  While his forces, and the forces of Prince Lichtenstein (5,000+ cavalry!), are well equipped, they are outnumbered by the French Vth Corps under Marshall Lannes and the Cavalry Reserve under the trusted leadership of Marshall Murat.  Murat's forces contain a mix of heavy and light cavalry to the total of around 7,000.
Needless to say the action is hot and intense.

This game is an expansion and requires Telnitz to be played.  It contains a great added value feature not originally planned, but included at the same proposed release price, of a counter sheet with battalion counters.  These battalion counters can be used in lieu of the company counters and will speed up game play.  Rules for using them are fairly simple and short, allowing for fighting the battle, not the rules.

boardgame: Armored Knights North Africa: Operation Crusader
description:
The British offensive to relieve Tobruk / November 18 - December 8, 1941

Armored Knights North Afrika - Operation Crusader pits the numerically inferior but tactically superior Axis forces of Rommel against the British forces trying to open a route to Tobruk and free the trapped garrison there.  The game provides a framework for Operational level combat that highlights the interaction between the various combined arms elements present.  The game system uses a variable strength table that players use to determine how much benefit they get from the various different combat multiplying forces.  Engineers, Anti-Tank, Anti-Aircraft, Recon, Close Air Support, and Artillery all work together to enhance you chances of success.  And sometimes they don't do it right and don't work as well together as we would like.  This creates a great deal of excitement in the battle and makes you think twice.

The battle for Tobruk started on 18 November 1941 with the British moving 3 Armoured Brigades and a host of Infantry Divisions toward the port and perimeter.  Some were fast cruiser tanks and others were faster American built Stuart tanks.  The British would get a jump on the Germans and Rommel would be slow to realize there was a major offensive underway.  There would be a number of major engagements to include two battles for the Sidi Rezegh Airfield, one for Bir El Gubi, where the Italians turned in a good account of themselves, and Rommel's famous Dash to the Wire.  His dash was a failure, but he recovered in time to fight  successful second battle for Sidi Rezegh.

All units modeled as battalions, companies/batteries.
Hexes represent approximately 2km of terrain.
Each turn represents 2 hours of ff daylight or 2-4 hours of night.

This game comes with:
• Armored Knights North Afrika Rules 
•(5) 11x17 Scenario Cards 
• Numerous Player Aides and Examples 
• Game Turn Record Track 
• (16) 11 x 17 Full Color map sections 
• (4) Full Color Order of Battle Charts 
• Very Sturdy box
• Approximately 1200 double sided unit counters and markers

(from box and publisher's website)

description: Expansion map for China / Web of Power, part of Schacht's 1-year campaign of releasing a new expansion map every month. Available for online play at www.michaelschacht.net.

Players identified by a color, place two marker chips on the board. Similar to Craps, there are several types of options with various odds. In addition to single roll options, one section has two levels. Upon successfully winning Level 1, Level 2 (the second marker chip, if placed in Level 2) is activated and increases the potential for an exponentially high payoff on the next dice roll.

Although calibrated for casino-style wagering, the premiere edition is configured for the accumulation of points. All options on the board state the point values. Everyone plays concurrently and independently, utilizing the same dice rolls. Each player lists the points acquired and after (30) minutes of play, the scores are totaled. The player with the highest score wins the game.  In the event of a tie, play continues one dice roll at a time until the tie is broken.

Level 1 is the spark that can ignite Level 2 to Fuel an exponential payoff.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Clone Wars – AAT Attack
description:
Your mission is to seek out and destroy a Separatist Armored Assault Tank (AAT).  You will need to get into close quarters with the tank and blow it up with a blast from your Thermal Detonator.  Use the debris scattered around the battlefield for cover and look to the Jedi Anakin's Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi for aid.  But beware, the evil and powerful Asajj Ventress also lurks about the battlefield.

On a 3D hex filled board, using roll and move mechanics, maneuver your plastic pre-painted Clone trooper miniature around the board trying to stay out of the firing direction of the large plastic AAT.  Either through the use of cards or die rolls, your turn may also consist of firing red plastic discs with the AAT at the other plyers' Clone Troopers.

The game comes with a "basic" version of play using only a single 12 sided die as well as an "advanced" version using a 6 sided die and cards.

boardgame: Lotto spielen mit dem kleinen Bär
description:
The Lotto game has four busy picture boards and the object is for each player to be the first to collect all six picture cards that correspond to his picture board.

To play:
 1. Each player takes one of the four picture boards. The 24 cards are shuffled and placed on the table face-down.
 2. The first player takes one of the cards, flip it and tries to find the same picture on his board. If he has found the picture, he place the card on one of the six squares around his picture board.
 3. If the player don´t find the same picture on his Board, he place the card face-down again.
 4. The game continues until one player has filled all six squares on his picture board.

Players are archaeologists digging for treasures in the desert, working together to collect as many valuable treasures as possible.

A Desert Dig area is formed in the center of the table by creating a tableau of face-down cards in 4 rows of 6 overlapping cards (similar to a solitaire set-up).  Only the top card of each row is available for play action.  Players are then dealt a hand of action cards that allow them to move cards in the tableau, search the dig field, dig in one column of the field, or load cards onto the camel train (in a separate area).  "No Trespassing" cards will block digging in a particular column and force a player to play a (limited quantity) "Deal" card in order to access any valuable treasure locked above that card.

When the camel train contains 15 treasures, the game ends and the team of archaeologists determines the value of the treasures collected.  As this is a cooperative game, the goal is to obtain the greatest possible treasure score as a team (a perfect game being a score of 115).

From the box: "A game where the players in their travels around the Australian Continent learn historical and modern day facts relating to each town, city and the many interesting and informative places along the way. The many everyday hazards of travel add to the interest and excitement of playing the game."

The board is a map of Australia. Players move around the board, visiting each of the State capitals. The first player back to the starting town stops that round and receives $500. The player with the most money and Town Bonus cards wins.

boardgame: Plains Wars: Campaigns and Skirmish Scenarios
description:
Campaigns and Skirmish Scenarios

Tactical Supplement for WarPaint or any western wargame

Contents:
1. Campaigning the Plains Wars (Operations)
2. Plains Wars Scenarios (Skirmishes)
3. Warfare on the Plains (Tactics)

Softcover, 28 pp

Includes 11 scenarios in a 64 page full color softcover book. The scenarios are:
• Long Island (1776)
• Harlem Heights (1776)
• Princeton (1777)
• Bound Brook (1777)
• The Hudson Forts (1777)
• Indian Field (1778)
• New Garden Meeting House (1781)
• Guilford Court House (1781)
• Petersburg (1781)
• The Cavalry Action at Gloucester (1781)
• Fishguard - The Last Invasion of Britain (1797)
