It's trading card game in which players are building their own fleets and then , using different captains, weapons and maneuvers, trying to beat each other.

At the start of game each player build their own fleets. Your fleet value could not be higher than 8.Each ship has different abilities, attack, defense and value. So you can build big fleet with a lot of weaker ships, small fleet with powerful ships or medium fleet with medium ships.

At the player turn he do these steps:

1. Draw 1 card
2. Choose flagship ( one ship which will be participating in battle)
3. Attack ( at this step, player must put captain card on the ship ( if that ship doesn't have captain yet). Each captain has different abilities and attack, defense values. After that players can use spacial cards to change their own attack and defense values. Attacker can use weapons and maneuvers, defender can use just maneuvers. When player don't want to use anymore cards, attacker attack value and defender defense value is compered. Player who has bigger value destroys opponent flagship)
4.End of the turn ( if attacker lost, he must assign new flagship and all destroyed ships,killed captains, used maneuvers and weapons are discarded)

First player to destroy all opponent's ships is the winner

Portable lunchbox game. The Marvel Heroes are always equipped and ready to go and now you can follow suit. Have great time wherever your adventures take you. Heroes unite. Players 2-4.

Product Features
The Marvel Heroes are always equipped and ready to go and now you can follow suit
Have great time wherever your adventures take you
Heroes unite
For Ages 4+
Players 2-4

Players roll dice on their turns in order to match the superhero icons on the dice to the villains currently at large on the table. To capture a villain such as the Lizard requires one Spiderman icon. To capture Galactus requires one each of Captain America, Hulk and Spiderman. Players may reroll "misses" but once an "X" is rolled, that die cannot be rerolled so there is a slight press-your-luck element.

There are some wild cards that give players an icon of their choice. Some other special cards give players extra rerolls or force a player to give up one of their previously captured villains.

The winner is the player who has captured the most villains when the deck runs out.

There are a number of methods of play, but basically your are stacking wooden sticks with a square cross section for points. Different colored sticks score different number of points.

There is a certain twist in that the stack of sticks is made on top of a cone with a flat top. The cone is high enough to guarantee that a falling stack of sticks will create a spectacular collapse!

The game came packaged in a can with metal ends and the sides made of paper.

boardgame: Who's Afraid of the Big Bad Wolf
description:
Basically the game is very similar to the much better known game Pachisi. Each player is trying to move his pigs from his starting position around a race track and get them to the brick yard, which is "home" for the game.

There are some differences to the regular Pachisi game. There are only four safe spaces marked on the board. Also there are special spaces which tell a player to advance or to go back a certain number of spaces. Also there are only three pigs, not four.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
The game is a simple race game somewhat similar to the better known Chutes and Ladders game. To play you'd need two dice (or a tee totem according to the rules...), a distinct marker for each player,  and the game sheet. Presumably only the game sheet was sold and the player would have to supply the other components.

The 122 spaces on the game board are marked out along a snake. The start is "through" the snakes mouth which requires a 2 to pass. Space 122 is at the tail.

The game board directs forwards and backwards movements as in Chutes and ladders. However there are many differences to the standard Chutes and ladders game. The winner must get past 122 to win. Landing on some squares entitles the player to two throws, or causes him to lose a turn. A player unlucky enough to land on space 119 loses the whole game!

Bonus tile that comes for free with Plato Magazine no 45.

The background is purple and you replace one purple discovery with this tile.

At the end of the game, the owner of this tile gets 2 points for each settler (disc) that he has retreived, but hasn't placed on the board.

Promotional game for a house improvements company.

The board looks interesting, with a track around the edge of a house which has a wall taken away so that you can see workmen in every room.

The game itself is not so interesting, being a close relative of games like Ludo, Pachisi and Mensch. To spice it up, if you land on a brown square you get to draw a card and must follow the instructions.

The first player to get all four of their men home is the winner.

Football related card game. 56 cards featuring 21 different footballing terms.

Each player is dealt a hand of cards. The 'match' kicks off by the first player to have an Anstoß (Kick-Off) card. If no-one has such a card, each player in turn draws another card from the stack. This goes on until an Anstoß is drawn and played. Next player must play a card that is the logical action after the kick-off. A check list is provided so you can see which action card is acceptable. The game continues like this until one player runs out of cards. That player wins the round and the other players get a minus score depending on which cards they still have in their hand.

The player with the most points after an agreed number of rounds, wins.

A tactical level wargame based on the Japanese robotech animation "Panzer World Galient".
Two editions of the game were published, in Dual Magazine #11 and 12. Each edition uses the same basic rules, but with different map and units.

Players control one or more Panzer Soldiers (the mecha name in this universe).
Each turn, players secretly fill out the log for their action pattern and movement plan. Then, they simultaneously resolve movement and combat. The combat includes fire and close combat.

This game does not contain any scenarios. In the basic game, each player use one Panzer Soldier and battles to destroy the opponent. A skilled player can handle more units for large-scale battles.

The creators are back once again with another card expansion that adds more hilarity for us horrible people that like to play.

In this card game, players take turns being the Card Czar, which means they will read a black question card or a fill-in-the-blank statement card. The other players will select a white answer card from the 10 in their hand that they think best or most humorously fits the question/statement. The Card Czar will then shuffle the answers and choose the one they like best, with that answer's owner receiving the black card as his "awesome point" for winning the round.

Once a player has at least one awesome point, they can wager an awesome point when giving a white answer card in order to throw in a second answer card, giving that player a better chance to be chosen. The winner of the round will receive the original black card as well as any that are wagered in that round.

Play continues until a pre-determined score is reached, or until everyone is tired from laughing so hard.

This expansion adds 25 new black question/statement cards and 75 new white answer cards to the deck as well as 12 blank cards for players to come up with their own card ideas. And to top it all of, it's dolphin-safe!

description:
Topic: Animals, nature

The object is to be the first reaching target zone on the little game board by answering most questions correctly.
There are 2 question and 3 variants for each question on one side of the card. Currect player draws a card and hide the answers. Other players show answers simultaneously;
Players who answer correctly scores 1 point and move their pawns one point further on little game board. 
If leading player moves upon a 'specail' space on the board: Last and second last Player run a 'Rock,Paper,Scissors' competition to make a step ahead.
Who finishes first on the game board is the winner.

You and your friends are managers of a Formula Fun racing team.

The winner is the player who obtains the highest number of points over the various championship races in which he participates.

To take part in a race, one must have first employed drivers and signed contracts for the supply of an engine and chassis and manage the finances from the sponsorship cards.

The finishing order of the cars is determined by adding the value of the chassis, engine and driver cards of each car taking into account any effect shown on the Circuit card.

Your progress in the championship will be subject to various positive and negative Special cards for you and your rivals who will do all possible to put a spoke in your wheels.

Racing Manager is an evolved version of Racing Formule Fun which was published in French in 2010. The principal differences concern tha packaging which is bigger and which contains banknotes and tokens (the French version didn't) and there are no more texts on the cards. So some cards have been simplified.

Warlord is a small set of rules and 5 different piece types that are used in a series of scenarios. Each scenario is a structured multi-move abstract strategy game of battle using a unique board and setup.

Armies of 30 - 35 pieces are commanded by half a dozen leaders, any 4 of which may be active in a turn. Active leaders may move themselves or any friendly piece within 2 squares that has not already moved.

Each of the 5 pieces has a different simple short range move, and each captures an enemy piece by legally moving onto the enemy square, stopping, then removing the enemy piece.

Win by capturing half of your enemy's leaders before half of yours are taken.

boardgame: Komorów (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Wsiąść do Pociągu: Komorów is based on Ticket to Ride Märklin Edition rules, but it's a little bit expanded. Generally all rules are the same (like in the TTR:ME) with one exception. There are four special "WKD" routes. When claiming such route the player has to throw a D6 dice and draw the corresponding number of cards from the deck. Now analogously to the tunnels, the player has to discard from his hand the adequate number of cards.

This game is made available as a print and play expansion for Ticket to Ride and may not be produced for commercial reasons.

Taken from Kickstarter page

The dead have risen from their graves and their only desire is to devour brains of the living.  Sure, it's fun to tell your friends you killed a zombie.  But it's even better to add "Oh, you may have heard of him."  That's why these zombies are famous.  Will they keep coming back? Most definitely. Will humanity survive? Not even close. But if you can take out a few of the better know walking dead along the way, then you could win.

Game Play:

Players use cards to place player tokens on different spaces.  Placing them on the main board allows players to search for and use weapons to kill and drive back zombies.  The trap area allows your tokens to electrocute or blow up zombies that wander across the board.  Intel spaces allow players to draw additional cards.  The more cards that are drawn, the faster the zombies come.

Zombies are constantly springing up and slowly moving toward the brains on the main board.  When they reach them, players lose player tokens.  Players may regain player tokens by using cards to recruit new allies from the "Eaten" space.

Zombies will come in waves.  Sometimes they give you time to set up your strategy, and sometimes they emerge so quickly and in such large numbers that you must take action immediately.

Famous Zombies is a victory point based game that can be learned quickly and has enough choices to make strategy the most important factor in winning.

This small expansion contains 2 new Battle Maps and were designed and offered during D-Day Dice's 2011 Kickstarter campaign.

The Battle Maps are:
Riva Bella Casino
Sword Beach II

boardgame: Brink of Battle: Skirmish Gaming Through the Ages
description:
Brink of Battle: Skirmish Gaming Through the Ages is a miniature wargame rule set that allows players to recreate small combat engagements from any time in history. It is designed to allow players to play either one off games or a campaign that lasts over several battles.

Players can choose between three main historical periods, Ancient/Medieval (3000 BC to 1450 AD), Early Modern (1450-1880 AD), and Modern (1880 to present).  Forces are created through a point-buy system, allowing players to customize the combat attributes, equipment, and special abilities of their troops.

A typical force is 3 to 20 miniatures, the game is played on a 3'x3' to 4'x4' tabletop, and can be completed in 30-60 minutes.

Three mission modes are included:  Raid, in which the attacking force must capture an objective protected by the defenders; Recon, in which each opposing force attempts to cause the other to suffer casualties and flee; and Retrieval, in which both forces seek to be the first to find and secure an objective.

From the companies website: www.panzerbattles.com

Panzer Battles (ages 10+) is a card game based on World War II armor combat in a fast and fun system for 2-8 players. All players start the game with five cards form the tank deck (their active tank units) and five cards from the action deck. If you ever happen to run out of tanks, it is game over.

Winning the Game

The goal of the game is to destroy all enemy tanks and artillery before they destroy you! the players decide what the total score will be for victory (typically 150 points). This may result in playing multiple rounds until someone achieves the agreed points to win the game.

description:
Whispers traveled like thunder through both the choirs and legions, and even though the tones were different the message was exactly the same – it had been found. The end could now begin.

Set in an alternate but familiar world, Black and White pits two players against each other as they try to complete their agenda, namely to either prevent or begin the apocalypse.

In the game, players morally battle for the control of five key figures in the end time by either praising their virtues or exploiting their vices, while fighting to find and recover a holy artifact that holds the key to opening the first seal and bring the world to its end. This is achieved through the play of cards that affect either specific characters or less powerful cards that can target any character. At the end of each round of play, the first player rolls to take control of characters. This roll's success is determined through comparison to the morality score of each character. The player who takes control of each character moves it, trying to pick up and deliver the artifact to that player's own base.

The Earth is a dead husk. Having mined all of its resources, facing a population many times that which could be possibly sustained and slowly mutating it into a massive, cloying, choking city, we had no choice but to set our greedy eyes on the planets around us and conquer new lands.

After the Information War of the 21st century, the Corporations dissolved national borders and began a new war of their own: the Resource War. Like their European ancestors, who conquered and fought over new land, the corporations started to clash for control of the stars around Earth, as if they had claims to them from the moment of their conception.

Thus began the new arms race, but this time the lessons learned from past wars would be embraced and used to leverage as much advantage as possible. Research into more advanced abilities to control not only the battles, but the hearts and minds of the people who fight them would become key to victory.

Steel Stars is a game of intergalactic warfare amongst rival corporations in Earth's dark future with a near endless number of system configurations and multiple paths to victory – through the council, through research of technologies and advanced abilities, via the creation of popular utopian worlds, or by the might of your powerful fleets.

Each player steps into the powerful and all encompassing shoes of the head of one of the five corporations fighting for the control of the universe. You will need to use cunning, guile, diplomacy, deception, and tactical thinking to make the decisions that will hopefully bring victory, control, renown, and respect to your corporation.

In each round of the game, players select four actions to perform. Players in turn perform one of these. Whenever an action is performed, all other players may also perform that action. The available actions are: Fleet (move ships), Research (develop tech), Council (Create votes on new rules), Planetary (develop your civilization), Energy (generate resources) and Logistics (build new ships). Players score points for colonising worlds, developing tech, building colonies, passing votes and developing their civilisation. The game ends when one player has accumulated 15 points, but that player does not necesarily win, as there are bonus points scored for being the best player in each of several categories.

"Prehistoric Animals"

The nature quiz for children with 32 color printed cards held together by a hinge.

Whether the answer is correct player scores a point.
At the end of the game the player with the most points wins.

"Snakes, lizards & Co."

The nature quiz for children with 32 color printed cards held together by a hinge.

Whether the answer is correct player scores a point.
At the end of the game the player with the most points wins.

"Life on the coast"

The nature quiz for children with 32 color printed cards held together by a hinge.

Whether the answer is correct player scores a point.
At the end of the game the player with the most points wins.

"Life in the Sea"

The nature quiz for children with 32 color printed cards held together by a hinge.

Whether the answer is correct player scores a point.
At the end of the game the player with the most points wins.

"Children ask ... why?"

63 cards age-appropriate questions and answers are represented.
Answer correctly and win the card.
Player with the most points if the deck is exhausted is the winner.

"Animals of the World"

63 cards age-appropriate questions and answers are represented.
Answer correctly and win the card.
Player with the most points if the deck is exhausted is the winner.

"Football"

63 cards age-appropriate questions and answers are represented.
Answer correctly and win the card.
Player with the most points if the deck is exhausted is the winner.

"Learning English"

63 cards age-appropriate questions and answers are represented.
Answer correctly and win the card.
Player with the most points if the deck is exhausted is the winner.

"Farm"

63 cards age-appropriate questions and answers are represented.
Answer correctly and win the card.
Player with the most points if the deck is exhausted is the winner.

A quizgame for the youngest players. The cards are held by a screw and can be ideally taken on travels, 
More than 1,100 questions and answers - themes: nature, animals, seasons, shapes, colors, maps - ideal for travel -
the answers are always on the next page. The quiz can be played alone or by several players.

boardgame: Professional: Turn professional and make a million
description:
Professional is a dice rolling game where players go around the board "training" themselves in any of the four sports in the game: golf, snooker, tennis and track. It has a board resembling a Monopoly board with spaces with sports competitions, training, and both specified and random events. The game features five decks of cards: One for each sport and one for random events, much like the Chance cards in Monopoly. Also included is a variety of paper currency.

The goal of the game is to train oneself and compete in the various sports to gain a million dollars. The first player to obtain that amount wins. The tournaments are divided into minor and major competitions with varying prize amounts for the first two places, as well as a "walkover" prize which will be handed out if only one player were to enter the competition. Training cards can be obtained in the training spaces. These cards are not only necessary to gain access to competitions, but can also boost the "skills" of the player when dice are rolled. Throwing dice is done to move on the board as well as determine competition winners, with the highest rolls winning unless card modifiers are in play. Other spaces include sponsorship funding, income taxes, injuries, travel fees, sporting luck (chance cards) and the start square, which trains a player in all sports.

While the game cover says that it supports 2-6 players, the rules inside the box mention 3-6 players. While it can be player with only two players, the game works much better with more players. Even 3 is quite few and in order to really play well, at least 4 players should be present as the competition dynamics really require that many players.

It was made and printed in South Africa.

This is a small, pocket-sized trivia game. It contains 50 cards with 3 questions each, most of them with multiple choice options. The backside of each card contains the answers and offers additional information. Most of the cards also show small illustrations.

The ultimate knowledge-check! Test your skills on the subject of more brain training! Confusing and challenging puzzles to think and thinking around the corner. 50 exercises and solutions

Essence: Archetypes & Emotions, the first expansion for Essence: The Game of Insights, allows the players to shed light on their emotional realm and to discover the archetypes which prominently reflect in their being.

This is a sorting and matching game for young children.

The board is a 3-D toy shelf. Players are required to draw random tiles illustrated with pictures of toys. The tiles are crafted in geometric shapes, and certain cards require the placement of a triangle, a square, etc. The child must match the tile to the appropriate spot on one of the shelves. A squirrel named "Rascal" can appear on one of the cards to mess up the display. The first kid to complete a set is declared the winner.

Includes the 3-D shelf assembly, 12 shape cards, 13 toy cards, and the rules.

This is a small, pocket-sized trivia game. It contains 50 cards with confusing and challenging puzzles to think and thinking around the corner. The backside of each card contains the answers.

Solve the puzzle first and get the card as a point. Player with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

A brand new evolution of Pox: Save the People, ZOMBIEPOX challenges 1-4 players to stop an approaching zombie apocalypse by vaccinating humans against the ZOMBIEPOX. Cure those in the most dire situations and protect the vulnerable who cannot be immunized. (No one wants zombie babies!) Win the game if the deadly ZOMBIEPOX can no longer spread. Lose if too many people become full-blown zombies.

Not only is the game fun, but through play and the zombie themed narrative, players gain an understanding of group immunity, the need to vaccinate, and systems thinking. Casual and hardcore gamers from 12-65 and have enjoyed ZOMBIEPOX.

Players alternate drawing cards. Each card specifies how the ZOMBIEPOX spreads, and then which resources the player has to combat the outbreak. It's important to protect the vulnerable people on the board, because if they become infected, they immediately succumb to zombification.

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: South America / South Africa
description:
Age of Steam: South America / South Africa takes one map each from two different expansions for Age of Steam by the Steam Brothers and pairs them to create the southiest AoS expansion ever.

In this mounted, double-sided expansion, South Africa features a new Engineer action which allows a discount on track price, while Angola and Rhodesia are hexes that only produce goods. South America features El Presidente and a change in the Turn Order Pass action.

Reimplements:

    Age of Steam Expansion: China / South America
    Age of Steam Expansion: North Eastern USA / South Africa

Solitaire wargame for the battle of Tsushima in 1905.
This game published with Japanese history and military magazine "Rekishi Gunzo" #114 the Twenty Anniversary Issue.

Player control Imperial Japanese fleet and fight against Russian Baltic fleet.

The start of the game, player decide turning position against Russian fleet. Then repeat five bombard stage, fire between each other. Japanese bombard resolve with fire-power style combat result table. Russian bombard resolve by random damage chit.

Japanese National victory condition determined by condition of Russian fleet, but player's personal victory condition also affected by condition of Japanese fleet.

"Monstrum" (Monster) is a quite easy fantasy quest type game. Game objectives which players have to achieve to win are: 1) to reach place called "Kopalnia Zezere" (Zezere Mine) after: 2) killing at least one Monster, 3) gaining 30 points of strenght.

During the game players have to fight with hostile creatures. They can sometimes find special objects and gain spells which can be used in further fights or help in achieving game goal.

There is almost no interaction between players - which can be recognized as a big minus in gameplay.

The origins of the game are dated in 1985 when Jacek Ciesielski published its rules, cards and board in magazine "Razem" (Together). Rules and board for this earlier version were different. Graphics on game cards were simpler, but almost every player who had opportunity to play 1985 and 1990 versions claims that older version is better looking. Changes in 1990 probably gone too far ;)

Graphic design came from Polish comics artist - Witold Parzydło.

Statistical boxing game especially designed for the Finals at the Seoul Olympics in 1988. The designer felt that there was a need for a quick version of his Championship Boxing game. It plays the same but has less detail. Each boxer is rated in various skills with dice and charts deciding the outcome.

NinjitZoo is an action packed card game in which you assist a bunch of martial art animals - the Ninjanimals - escape their caged confines within an obstacle rigged zoo.

Three characters are randomly dealt out to each player at the start of the game. The first person to assist all their ninjanimals in escaping - wins! This is accomplished by using the escape routes, which are drawn from the deck each turn. However, the deck also contains obstacles that can block your ninjanimal from freedom.

But you'll have lots of help along the way. Don the Mysterious Mo to slip past a patrol of guards, or grab a Thickslick Rope to rendezvous on the roof. That Hovering Heli giving you trouble? Throw down a Smoke Bomb and Back Flip out of there!

And watch out for the robots, time portals, piranha infested moats, and all the lasers.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Briefcase: Government Bonus, a small expansion for Briefcase, includes eight new goals which increase competition between players, who now receive government rewards for being the first to achieve specific goals during the game. The package also includes two Rules cards.

boardgame: Rivals for Catan: Age of Enlightenment
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Rivals for Catan: Age of Enlightenment contains three expansion sets (125 Cards Total) for The Rivals for Catan (released in Germany as Die Fürsten von Catan). These theme sets – "The Era of Explorers", "The Era of Sages", and "The Era of Prosperity" – offer new variety for the base game and bring new elements into play.

• In The Era of Explorers, players set sail from Catan to explore the world and discover new lands. Your explorer harbor serves as the starting point for your exploration of nine adjacent sea cards. Pirates, islanders, and shipwrecks await across the waters. Embark on missions and try to be the best explorer. Ship tokens chart your position. Victory awaits the most skilled and intrepid sailor!

• In The Era of Sages, the wisdom of many and varied sages – a currency in its own way – gives you the opportunity to influence the strength and frequency of events. Fend off famines, deflect attacks, and promote peace. Connect with nature and balance prudence, power, foresight, and vigilance in your struggle for Catan!

• In The Era of Prosperity, you strive to be a good prince and govern wisely, in order to build schools and a hospital – and even promote the arts. Raise your people's spirits and spur expansion. But beware as discontent dooms prosperity!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Game description from the publisher:

What's in goat poop? Why do shoes stink? And what makes a hot air balloon rise? These and 1,197 other multiple-choice questions await you in Checker Can: Das Checker Quiz, based on the television show of the same name. For each correct answer, you advance on the scoring track, and the first player to lap the track wins.

Educational range of games. This one deals with the Human Body. It is a very simple card game. You get asked a question. If you get it right, you gain a point. If you get it wrong, you lose a point. When the questions run out, or an agreed time-limit has been reached, the player with the most points wins.

The game consists of 36 cards in four colors which show an item in that color plus a right or a left hand. There are 16 additional color cards in four colors, which are sorted and stacked on the table. 
The active player draws a card, looks at it and announces (for example): "Ketchup - left hand". The first player to hit the red stack with his/her left hand gets the red color card (but is not eligible if s/he already has a red card). The first player to collect a color card in each of the colors wins the game.

From the publisher's website:

CONQUEST, WAR AND FAMINE OH MY!

And don’t forget Death. Commonly referred to as the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse they rode through the land signaling the end of the world. What caused the end of mankind, the end game? Who knows? Who cares? The real question is…

WHAT HAPPENS AFTER THE HORSEMEN?
After the Horsemen takes place after mankind has broken down. How’d it happen? Who really cares? As one of the few people still alive it’s your goal to survive and thrive in this new world. While some view it as the end of the world a few think of it as a new beginning. Kind of like the glass being half empty or half full, it’s all how you look at it.

After the Horsemen includes:

     Three character classes for your character, Sheep, Pack Wolves or Lone Wolves, each with their own specific way to behave. After the Apocalypse things are so much simpler.
     Over forty Signatures and Skills that allows you to customize and differentiate your main character, your Star, you!
     A variety of items ranging from food to “luxury items, those things that make life worth living in these troubled times. Those things you have to collect to survive.
     Various weapons to insure that you get those items.
     A number of pre-generated Encounters and the how to info for designing your own.
     A light book keeping campaign system to link your adventures.


After the Horsemen can be played solo or cooperatively, with all players on the same side, against the game mechanics. It can also be played head to head against your friends. After the Horsemen is compatible with All Things Zombie – Better Dead Than Zed and other Two Hour Wargames products.


Your SUV has a full of tank of gas and you’ve got food for a week or two. Weapons? You cleaned them, again, last night before saying your good-byes. You’re not sure which way to go but you know that it’s time to go. You start the engine and adjust your sunglasses, you’re off.

AFTER THE HORSEMEN THE GAME OF THE APOCALYPSE

(1) After the Horsemen is strictly a human centric PA game. No zombies, no aliens, no mutants, etc., just humans versus humans. If you’re looking for that sort of thing try All Things Zombies and 5150: New Beginnings, both are fully compatible with After the Horsemen.

Orange Revolution  is a block CDG game that pits Viktor Yushchenko against Viktor Yanukovych. Orange Revolution puts you into the revolution with its tense, thematic gameplay. Whether campaigning, attempting assassinations, or drumming up protesters to the Maidan, the many varied options will ensure each game is different — and with five total turns, the game is playable in 90-120 minutes.

For those unfamiliar with the series, Orange Revolution is played on a game board depicting the many oblasts or regions that comprise Ukraine. Each region can have 1-11 influence points placed there. Cards are used to move blocks and leaders from region to region, add influence points, exploit events, and bring protestors and militia to the Maidan. Blocks — representing political forces such as Pora, avid supporters and government forces — battle for the hearts and minds of the Ukrainian people. Blocks need to be present in a region to add influence points or for a leader to hold a rally in a region.

The Maidan may be activated during the game, leading to a sub game of amassing supporters and militia. The blue forces will have to choose to crackdown on the orange forces or allow the dangerous, but historical peaceful protests to swell. Will you bring Tulia Tymoshenko or Yushchenko to the Maidan to draw more crowds? Or use them to rally across the nation?

Orange Revolution will immerse you in the events of its namesake with thematic mechanisms, events and graphics. The Orange Revolution has begun!

The newly discovered "Expanse" contains a wide variety of astronomical phenomenon in improbably close proximity.  The race is now on to unravel the mysteries of this new microcosm, and prestigious research institutions with deep pockets are clamoring to be the first to know.   New, highly experimental ships are commissioned that can quickly move between systems using powerful, volatile drives and reality altering engines. Can you pilot one to victory?

Players compete to be the first to explore eight of the nine phenomenon using cards drawn from a common deck.  Different cards can provide fuel or special abilities, be played as anomalies, or be used to explore the different locations in The Expanse.

boardgame: Empires of the Void: Pirates of Cidran
description:
Pirates of Cidran is an expansion to Empires of The Void consisting of an additional race with the same name.

This expansion was offered as a backer reward to those backing Empires of The Void on Kickstarter.

Welcome to Tycoon 2. Happy Industrializing!
This is the classic reiteration of the Monopoly so that it adds depth and fun to the game.. Happy Industrializing! Though it is a lot different than monopoly, but it is simple and elegant enough to understand the rules soon enough.

The goal of the game is to hold the maximum asset values at the end of specific rounds of the game. (a round is actually a month in the game, and so usually 6 - 12 rounds/ half -yearly or annual game)
 Players will take the role of tycoons trying to maximize their growth potential by acquiring membership of cities of the world, purchasing ownership of industries, advanced industries and various services, playing the casino and making black money to aid their fundings, and finally, dominating the board to become the greatest tycoon of all times.

It has been named Tycoon 2 since this is the reinvention of the beta one named: Tycoon - The game, which had fewer elements.

This board game is not publicly available, it is hand made at home, using paper money and other stuffs from other board games. The cards were printed and pasted on standard playing cards, and everything else was printed and published manually. =

boardgame: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
description:
A simple board game for small children, based on the award winning picture book of the same name. Players choose a character piece - one of the family members from the book, or even the family dog - and then spin and move around the board. There are two piles of cards on the board - the Uh-uh! cards and the What's that? cards. If a player lands on an Uh-uh! space on the board he/she turns over the top card in that pile. A green card allows him/her to spin again and move past the obstacle; a red card means he has to wait until his next turn before moving.

Two thirds of the way around the board a player will reach the rainbow space just before the cave. Tentatively he turns over the top card in that pile. If the card shows an empty cave he yells 'Quick' and speeds past the cave to the next space. But if there is a bear in the cave ... Yikes! wait another turn and hope to get past next time.

This really is a simple game but small children who know and love this wonderful picture book are likely to love the re-enactment of the story in this way.

In the board game players bearer squads consist of 20 soldiers and placement of the order is completely random. In total six different types of soldiers, among whom is an officer-bearer, who could command the whole ranks. The game used a system of ranks and strength of a warrior, depending on the state of health.

  Before the beginning party players are laid out face down tiles of soldiers under the original arrangement. After that, all tiles are revealed and the game begins.
For his turn, a player must move a warrior and a tile to tile a single attack of the enemy. Bearer can move itself, and can also move the entire vertical or horizontal column of soldiers who are with him.

The winner is the player who destroyed the first of three standard-bearers of the enemy.

"Crafters" - a game against the four urban planners fictional world of time, similar to the early Renaissance. Insatiable King sends us to the fertile plains to establish a town. All is good, but to motivate the king announces that at the end of the work will choose which one of the founders of the rise at the head of the city.

In its course the first thing a player produces one of the starting resources: plants, timber, coal or iron. Then he can buy another resource for their own gold, to hire additional workers to make the resources available from the goods, sell them to spend money to develop drawings of new buildings, to build these buildings and others to invest in the construction drawings (as often, especially at the end of the game , the opponents are beginning to keep them in order to "strangle" this or that branch of production).
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Chit Chat Girls' Night Out is a 'Girls only' game, revolving around 252 questions, "written by women for women", such as:


     What would you like to change about your partner?
     When cooking, do you follow the recipe, or do you improvise?
     Which friend from the past would you like to see again?
     Do you prefer large passion, or quiet love?


The questions are divided into different categories. Each round, one player (the gamemaster) rolls a die which determines the category. She draws a card from that category, which lists three questions, from which she aks one. Now, the other players have to answer the question. After all players have answer, the gamemaster gives one 'gemstone' from the stock to the player whose answer she liked best, for whatever reason.

The game ends when all gemstones have been handed out; the player who has collected the most gemstones wins the game.

A kind of "Musical pig" game. It consists of a fluffy pig. When you press the belly, music will start to play. The players stand or sit in a circle and someone presses the pig's belly, then tosses the pig to another player who has to catch it and throw it to the next player as fast as possible. The player who holds the pig when the music stops is out. Continue play until only one player remains. That player is the winner.

Objective: Using the detailed street map of Nürnberg each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.

Contents: 80 cards, 8 counters, 3 dice, rules and city information.

Objective: Using the detailed street map of Karlsruhe each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.

Contents: 80 cards, 8 counters, 3 dice, rules and city information.

Findus has birthday three times per year. And every time Petterson bakes him a pancake cake. But this time he is still lacking 5 eggs - who can get hold of them first?

Players choose a basket of eggs on the board and then search the eggs with matching color. If the underside of an egg shows the wrong color, it is returned back to the board without showing the color to the other players. The first player to have found his five eggs is the winner.

In the second variant a die is rolled, then an egg matching the rolled color is sought, and if both match, the egg is placed in the player's basket. If the die shows Petterson, one can put any egg from the board in his basket. If it shows Findus, one egg has to be returned from the basket to the board. The first player to fill his basket is the winner.

Prepare for awkwardness! Each round  in Awkward Moment puts 3-8 players in terrifically awkward social situations. Take turns being the Decider. Draw a moment Card to a reveal hysterical, embarrassing, or stressful situation. Use the Decider Card to determine the basis for a winning Reaction. Impress the Decider by submitting the best response from your hand!

Block 5: The Card Game consists of cards numbered 1-10, but without 5s and with a few marked 3+. The goal is to get rid of one's cards. At the start of the game, players lay out rows with the sum of five (1,2,2, for instance). When a player adds the fifth row, s starts a new block by placing an individual card on the table. This card shows the number which has to be added up to. A player who cannot place a row draws a card. A player who places a 3+ card draws three cards, keeps one and gives the other two to two other players. The first player to get rid of all her cards wins.

Mix Fix is a reaction game where you are trying to get rid of all your cards first.
In the beginning every player get six cards showing six symbols. There are 4 different symbols in this game (fish, ship, sheep, frog). In each round 4 dice are rolled. Each dice has one symbol on four sides plus two blank sides. The players must then quickly find a card which shows all rolled symbols. If a matching card is found the player has to put it quickly in the middle of the table and has to name all symbols. If all was correct the player gets rid of this card. If he has made a mistake (wrong card or misspelling) he has to take it back and gets a new card as penalty.

Prosper the best way orcs know how ... RAID!

Each player controls a village of orcs led by a Shaman, Chieftain and Warlord.  Each round, players take turns playing a secret number of orc grunts with one or more leaders in various regions.  Raiding the human mines, forest camps, farms and port city provide resources and honor points.  Attacking other orc villages provides honor points and mucks with the opponents' plans.  Placing in one's own village allows the building of defenses, increasing population or training your leaders.

The game ends when the resource decks run out.  The player with the most honor points wins.

In this game of magic and thievery you lead a small band of thieves that are roaming the streets of ancient Baghdad stealing fabled enchanted treasures. Show the prowess to lead your thieves and capture the most valued treasures, while driving your opponents into the guards. Do this and all the thieves will unite behind you as the leader of the Guild of Thieves!

The game is played on a grid of cards that players move their thieves over to select. These cards then become part of a players deck, which determines in what way their thieves are able to move. Eventually everyone cannot move any more, at which time the players count up their victory points to determine the winner.

Meltdown Express is a nuclear plant escape game for two players. It uses a die in a non-luck way.

In turns, each player can move one of his three workers to get one of the three hatchways on the other side of the modular board. Each hatchway has different victory points.

You have to run hard and strategic to achieve the objective of not dying and score most points.

This game was made for the II "Imprimir y Jugar" contest of the spanish blog "Jugando en Pareja" and got de second position.

German game for young children to enhance their early English language skills.

Six player boards are included and 36 tiles, each representing a common English word and the associated image.

Two games are described. The first is Englische Bildpaare suchen, a version of Pairs or Memory. You don't use the boards. Instead, turn all the tiles face down and lay them out on the table. Children must find the pairs, which are not two of the exact same image but instead, two tiles that 'go together'. Examples: Cow and Milk, Jacket and Trousers, and so on. If you 'match' a pair, you take the two tiles. When all the tiles are matched, the player with the most tiless, wins.

The second game uses the player boards and is played like Picture Bingo or Lotto. The first player to complete a line of three images is the winner.

Young children's memory game.

The first player lays out six tiles where all the players can see them. Then each tile is covered with a wooden block.
Now, a die with six coloured faces is rolled and the first player announces the tile they think is under the block with the same colour as the die roll. The block is lifted and the tile beneath is checked. If the player was correct, they can continue  with another turn. If the player was wrong, then the turn passes to the next player in a clockwise direction.

When all six tiles have been correctly named, six more tiles are revealed, studied and covered by the blocks again. When all 30 tiles have been collected, the player with the most tiles, wins.

The 25 tiles are laid out face-down. In a turn, a player rolls both the dice. Now he must choose one of the tiles which contains both colours that were rolled. Each card is a combination of two colours, the background color and the object colour.

If a player is correct, he takes the card. If not, he must replace it face down in the same position it was when he chose it.

When all the cards are taken, the player with the most tiles wins.

A variant for older children is included. In this game, the various tiles have a points value and the winner is the player with the most points, not neccessarily the player who has the most tiles.

boardgame: Das kleine Ich bin Ich: Memospiel
description:
The game consists of 16 book tiles which have text on one side and one to three illustrations from the children's classic on the other, and 42 picture tiles matching the illustrations on the book tiles.
The picture tiles are placed on the table, face down. The book tiles are sorted and the first one is flipped over. The players then take turns flipping over a picture tile. If it matches the illustration, the active player can keep it and goes again. If there is more than one illustration on the book card, though, the matching tile stays on the table until all illustrations are found - the player to find the last one can keep them all. The game ends when all illustrations have been found. The player with most tiles is the winner.

A filler type old-style card game where you draw 1, play 1 to claim steer cards. Whoever has the most steer cards when the deck (45 cards) is depleted and all cards are played out (from 3 card hands) wins. You can claim a steer card directly by playing a Bar X card, or claim someone's steer card / Bar X stack by playing a Rustler, or claim someone's steer card / Bar X / Rustler stack by playing a Deputy.

The rules say it's best played with 2, 3, or 4 players, but 5 or 6 may play if desired.

Players become the emperors/kings of empires/kingdoms of the ancient world. This may appear to be a wargame, but there are numerous roads to victory, as the use of trade and economic advancement is just as likely to prevail, in the end, as territorial expansion and conquest.

King of Kings includes 15 scenarios set from 1700 BC to 1200 AD, ranging from 1 to 5 players.   A ruler can try to increase his empire by conquest, increase wealth by trade, or invest in civilisatory improvements that may well be a better path to victory than warfare.  Trade is handled by caravans moving from resource areas back to the heart of your empire.  As the design notes say, "this is not a wargame".

If players desire, battles can be fought in detail, by using the rules from Good Industries' other game, Ancients.  Calls for Divine Intervention, as well as finding out the personal characteristics of your partner in Royal Marriages add to the colour of the game.    The game end is variable, depending on the death of monarchs, which can be sudden (although it is possible in principle to continue playing as one's heir).

In Undead jungle players will be taking turns fighting their way through the jungle, during a players turn they will spend one of their four action points to do a number of things including attacking other players or jungle terrors, climbing trees for safety, scouring the land for item cards, and racing toward Dr. Manvil's sword. In 1887 the McLoughlin Brothers introduced a successful  series of educational card games under the name "Grandmama's XXX Game." The series included card games on Arithmetic, Bible Studies, Geography, Riddles. and Useful Knowledge.

One player was designated the "preceptor" and the other players were "scholars." The preceptor would ask questions and go around the players until someone got the question right. Most right answers wins.

"Grandmama's Arithmetical Game" card game had 119 question cards, an instruction sheet, and an answer booklet which were packaged in a paper covered cardboard box.

Who will be the Breakfast-King? A game with a message for children - eat wisely.

The board features a pathway with white, yellow, orange and blue spaces. If you land on any colour other than white, you must draw a card from the deck of that colour. The cards either tell you something positive or something negative. Examples:
Blue card - Bad choice of meal, hamburger with Pommes Frites. This is 'not good' so you have to go back three spaces.
Orange card - various breakfasts you might have. A healthy one illustrated, might move you forward two spaces.
Yellow card - Cleaning your teeth after a meal - very good, go forward two spaces

In addition, some cards show an activity that must also be obeyed. This will be one of four instructions:
3 x three jumps on the spot.
1 x run round the table
1 x hop round the table
1 x stand on one leg and count to 10

The winner is the first player to reach the end of the track and a crown is included for the victor to wear.

The game was published for the Zentralverband des Deutschen Bäckerhandwerks, a kind of organisation to promote different German bakeries.

Benjamin Blümchen says "Shopping is fun".

Each player gets a board with 16 squares, all illustrated with things Benjamin needs at the supermarket. The item tiles are shuffled and placed in a face-down stack. A turn starts when the player reveals the top tile. If he thinks he needs it, he pays to the bank the cost (in Deutschmarks). If he doesn't want to buy it, the next player can, and at a saving of 50 Pfennigs (half a Deutschmark). If not wanted it is offered to all the other players, knocking off 50 Pfennigs each time. If it gets down to zero, that player will get it for free.

The object is to buy goods that will form four boxes in a row on your board, so four across, four down and four corner-to-corner are all acceptable choices. The first to do so, wins.

A few tiles are what you might call Event tiles. If one of these is drawn, it is read out and takes place if the drawer wants to make use of it. Example: You may take one item off the board of the player to your left.

description:
This is a trivia game with questions around the world.
The game includes 100 cards with 100 Questions and answers (on the back side) and four clips in four colours used as player markers.

Players have to estimate facts for questions. There are always 10 different possible answers. There is a clip for each player to mark their answers on the card. Each spot with an answer can only be taken by one player. When all players made their estimations the card is flipped to the backside with the answer. The player with the correct answer or is closest to it gets the card. If theres a tie the next question/card is worth two cards (the old tied card and the next new card).
The first player with 7 cards is the winner.

Morro is a game for two players on a square grid with white and black stones.

Definitions:
Row: any line of consecutively adjacent, like-colored stones. Rows may be oriented either orthogonally or diagonally. The length of a row is the number of stones in it.
Drop: to place a stone on any empty space.

Rules:

To start, White drops a single stone.
From then on, starting with Black, the players take turns. On your turn, you must drop a number of stones equal to the length of the longest row (of either color) on the board. If there aren’t enough empty spaces left to do so, just fill up the board.
When you create a row longer than any row created by your opponent up until that point, you become the leader.
The game ends when the board is full, and the leader at that time wins.

description:
Game description from the publisher:

Seekers of Knowledge, the third deluxe expansion for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game, populates the hallowed halls of Miskatonic University with nineteen different characters committed to the merits of higher education, the application of science, and a little dabbling in stranger and darker knowledge.

The host of new Miskatonic characters provides the faction dynamic new life in Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game. Many of the characters work well together, gaining greater efficiency the more of their allies enter play. Other cards provide Miskatonic University players reasons to focus solely upon the heroic investigators, rather than pairing them up with another faction and compromising their ideals. Altogether, Seekers of Knowledge looks to sound the bell upon a brand new school year full of optimism.

Finally, Seekers of Knowledge is more than another deluxe expansion; it's the first in a new release model for Call of Cthulhu: The Card Game. The game will shift away from monthly Asylum Packs to the release of a deluxe expansion (each with 165 cards, three copies each of fifty-five individual cards) once every four months.

description:
Rules from game itself:

Directions for playing the Great Suffragette game:
"HOW TO GET OUT OF GAOL"

The suffragettes (i.e. the card disks) are first placed on the inner circle. (Holloway Goal).

The players alternately throw the dice, and at each throw the player moves his Suffragette round the spiral course, the same number of spaces as the number thrown.

Should a number be thrown which would bring a suffragette on to a space marked with either of the undermentioned words, she must be move as directed, viz. :-

"OPEN DOOR" The suffragette must be placed on the adjoining space in the next circle further away from the goal.

"POLICEMAN" or "WALL" The suffragette must be placed on the adjoining space in the next circle nearer to the goal.

"WARDRESS" The suffragette must be put back in Holloway Gaol and recommence  her journey.

The player whose Suffragette first reaches the end of the spiral course wins the game. As a puzzle, the problem is to see what is the fewest number of throws you can get your Suffratette out of goal, following the same rules as for the game.

description:
The game is a contest of occupation featuring two players around a grid board representing the streets of Edwardian London. One player plays 21 green markers as the radical suffragettes, the other player plays 21 dark blue markers as the police constables. The objective of the suffragettes is to break through police lines and enter the House of Commons, while at the same time preventing the police from entering Albert Hall. The objective of the police is to disrupt the meeting of the suffragettes by entering Albert Hall, while at the same time preventing them from entering the House of Commons.

"Arrested" suffragettes are confined to the "prison" section of the board, whereas "disabled" constables are confined to the "hospital" section. The game is won by the first player who introduces six markers into the opponent's base.

Source: Suffragetto, https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Suffragetto&oldid=833508008 (last visited June 16, 2018).

Print and play at: https://www.playsuffragetto.org/print

From the box. A wild and frantic speed game that everyone plays at once. The game is described and a great party and family fun game based on the fast style of play. It is a race to get rid of all cards by playing onto a center stack. Teams or individuals play provides hours of exciting family & party fun. 3 decks of 168 cards numbered 1-12 in 3 colors. Game can be played in teams or individually. All 3 decks are shuffled individually then passed, 5 cards dealt up and 12 cards dealt down. Then panic begins. As each card face up is played, it is replaced by one of the 12 down card. This continues until all 17 cards are played and the first player out of cards yells panic. Game description from the publisher:

The fifth campaign expansion for Dust Tactics, Operation "Hades" introduces new rules for subterranean warfare, superhuman soldiers, new skills, and richly detailed information about the city and battleground of Zverograd. It also brings you thirty-one tiles, a Zverograd city poster, a map of the Zverograd metro, six new scenarios, and over two dozen objectives to serve as the basis of your own scenarios. Finally, Operation "Hades" marks the arrival of Winter Child, the game's first superhuman soldier.

Operation "Hades" introduces new rules for underground fighting, as well as rules more specifically designated for scenarios fought in Zverograd’s sewers and metro.

During underground combat, troops must cope with dim lighting and confined quarters. There is very little light beneath the city of Zverograd. Units can hear enemies coming, but they are hard to make out in the darkness. As a result, it's not uncommon for soldiers in such tense conditions to fire blindly into the shadows ahead of them when they hear enemies approaching. The darkness of the underground grants a unit increasing levels of cover the farther it is from an attacking unit, and units cannot target foes with four or more spaces between them.

The game is played in a traditional domino manner except you are matching male and female pairs, and you are able to add a young below a 'paired' male and female (in which case you are able to take an extra turn). The winner is the first player to get rid of all the cards in his hand, or if the pack becomes exhausted and no one can 'go' the player with the least cards wins.

The cards have realistic artwork depicting the birds in a style similar to a bird reference book.

There is also another game variant included in the rules, called 'Bridges' which is for two players, and involves players creating columns of matching pairs of the same species or families of the same species, and bridges between the two players columns. Points are awarded for creating 12 card columns, and for creating bridges between columns. Games continue until one player has scored 30 points.

This is a tower-building game using plastic building blocks in eight colors.

The blocks are retrieved from a box with 6 x 6 compartments. Only a few compartments are available at any time decided by dice rolling. As a part of game setup the building blocks are put in the box and the box is shaken upside down and then right side up to randomly divide the blocks in the compartments.

From a deck of "building orders" a row of four available orders are drawn and two rows above it forms a future market.

Each player gets a set of six action cards numbered 1,2,2,3,3,4, four special cards and a base plate in his color to build the tower on.

The game is played in six rounds. Each round starts with that each player secretly chooses one of his six action cards. Everyone reveals which card they chose and each player gets as many actions as the number on the card. The cards are discarded after each round and cannot be used again.

The players take turns performing one action at a time and does one of the following:

- Take blocks from one allowed department. 
- Choose a building order card and build (but you have to have the blocks first). When a card is chosen the cards above are moved down and the lower is made available. Add a card from the deck to the empty space on the top row. 
- Discard all available build order cards, move down the cards from the upper rows and refill the top row.
- Reroll the dice that decides which compartments are avaialble. 
- Pass (do nothing).

At the end of each round the dice are rerolled to descide which compartments are available next round. Discard all available build order cards, move down the cards from the upper rows and refill the top row.

Some of the build orders are special and are not built until the end of the game where they allow you to add blocks to your tower depending on how your current tower looks like (color patterns).

Some build orders uses blocks from your opponents supply, or even tower. Some changes the order of blocks in the tower or adds blocks in the middle of a tower. Some cards are used to manipulate actions and order of actions.

Highest tower wins!

boardgame: Pixel Patch: The Real Life RPG
description:
Ever want your playing cards to be used for something more than a poker hand? Ever want to turn those hearts into healing items and those clubs into cash? We’ll, now you can.

Pixel Patch: The Real Life RPG is a 2-6 player card game that turn a standard deck of 52 cards into a a fun 8-bit RPG-influenced battle system. Players will try to work their way past their opponent’s defense through the cards they draft and grow more powerful through the games they play.

Every round, you will choose cards to decide whether to attack, defend, heal or gain influence for special abilities. You and your opponent will then decide whether to perform your action in an aggressive or defensive fashion and then draw a varying number of cards to determine your success.

This entire game can be played with just a deck of cards and a pad of paper if necessary, but player aids and guide books are also provided in the downloadable PDF.

The game ends when a player’s health reaches 0. At this point, experience points are handed out, which allow you to level up, boosting your original stats from your entrance exam and making your card drafts more powerful. All these stats are recorded on the printable booklets that come with the game.

This game is a great head to head challenge as you work to figure out what your opponent will do next and of course is immensely repayable as you work to unlock and master 11 unique classes.

Players can also boost their stats by purchasing RPG Patches from our website (listed in the links section); each one of these patches gives a different permanent stat boost to your character, without breaking the game.

description:
Question and answer game where each player is trying to answer the most questions correctly. Similar to McLoughlin's Gandmama series of games.

This game had 199 question cards, an answer book, and a set of instructions in the box.

In the 1860's the McLoughlin Brothers introduced a successful series of educational card games under the name "Grandmama's XXX Game." The first series included card games on Arithmetic, Geography, Riddles. and Useful Knowledge.

One player was designated the "preceptor" and the other players were "scholars." The preceptor would ask questions and go around the players until someone got the question right. Most right answers wins.

"Grandmama's Old Testament Questions & Answers" card game had 100 question cards, an instruction sheet, and an answer booklet which were packaged in a paper covered cardboard box.

The game is basically a children's race game with no decisions. In this regard it is like Chutes and Ladders. The theme is riding your pony from start to the finish line.

There is a twist on the Chutes and Ladders type game. There are two card decks from which a player may get a move card on his turn. However the player must flip a supplied "coin"  to see if he gets a card from the Johnny stack or the pony stack. having each player flip a coin would thus make "individual" luck come into play as opposed to both players just playing against a randomized deck.

The game had 4 riders and horses. It cam in a cardboard box that was somewhat like a pizza box.

description:
An expansion set of player cards only, published separately from the base game each year. Gamers who already owned the base game (which included a full set of cards for a year) could buy the new cards for the previous season, and play the teams without having to buy a base game again.

The package held a set of large perforated sheets for all the actual NFL teams and players, using revised statistical information based on the season passed (so the 1992 published set had the 1991 year stats). Some of the index cards included new rules or clarifications.

The packaging made it very clear that you needed the base game, so if you didn't know what it was about "it behooves you to gently place this package back on the shelf and purchase the complete game".

Basically this is a children's game which is a combination of Bingo and Old Maid. If he spins the Old Maid, then he takes possession of the Old Maid token. When the first player completes his card, then whoever is holding the old Maid marker loses.

Two ways to win. First on total points. the first player to reach 400 points wins.

Second way to win is score points in each turn for one of the four weapons ( anti-aircraft gun - 25 pts, gun truck - 10 pts, plane - 50 pts, and tank - 75 pts) to capture. First player to capture all four weapons wins.

In Bonkan, players are explorers traveling through ruins and looking for anything they can find. Players will discover both artifacts and traps, then must decide to go on exploring or escape.

Every round, each player decides to continue or exit the expedition. If you choose to continue, you draw a card. Each Anno Domini set consists of 336 cards, with a description of a historical event on one side of the card, and the year in which it happened on the other.
Each player receives nine of these cards, and may only look at the descriptions. Each player in turn puts a card on the table, trying to put them in chronological order. A player may, instead of adding a card, claim that the order in which the cards were put is incorrect. In this case all cards are turned over and the correct years revealed.
If the order was correct then the doubting player receives two cards and skips a turn. If the order was incorrect then the previous player, who accepted the order as correct, receives three cards. The first player that gets rid of all the cards wins.
Bluffing is as least as important as knowledge in this game. Most events are rather obscure and are hard to date.

boardgame: Bitter Heights: The Battle of Mitla Pass – October 29-31, 1956
description:
Bitter Heights: The Battle of Mitla Pass: October 29-31, 1956, is a low-complexity game on the battle for Mitla Pass during the 1956 Arab-Israeli War. The Israelis were to take and hold the Pass to prevent Egyptian forces from reinforcing their counterparts to the east, as well as establish a credible force that could threaten the Suez Canal. This would provide the pretext for Great Britain and France to intervene and take the crucial waterway back from Egyptian control.

The battle would come to represent the confused and tragic aspects of this war. The Israelis underestimated the strength of their opposition, the indecisive actions of their British and French allies upon which this operation depended, and the diplomatic victory Nasser and the Arab Allies would realize despite the military defeats they sustained. General Sharon’s controversial decision, to attack and take control of the pass in disobedience of direct orders, led to high losses in a fight that did nothing to further the Israeli cause.

As the Israeli player you will have to keep your losses low, while keeping hold of the roadway through the pass, as the Egyptian player you want the Israeli's to pay a high price.

Activation takes place by the use of a deck of cards, similar to the Pocket Battle Games by ATO. This creates the chaos of war that makes play unpredictable and interactive.

Game Scale:

Units: Battalion to Army

One 11x17" full color map
One dual-side printed un-mounted countersheet (90 1/2" counters)(mounted counters are optional)
One 4-page rulebook
One Player Aid Chart

Player Provided: One 6-sided die

Solitaire Playability: Medium
Complexity Level: Low
Players: 2 or more
Playing Time: 2 to 3 hours
