Wicked cards have scenarios and give a score (which you collect) and Willie cards have scenarios and move you back/forth so many squares or to a set place on the board. Humour is integral to the game both on the board and the cards. Instructions are on the bottom of the box.

The game components are a chicken coop (which has a timer mechanism), 36 multi-coloured chickens and a die.

Chickens are tossed into the game box or a bowl and mixed up then each player takes 9 of varying colours. Each player lines up three of the nine in front of themselves. (It's best if possible to use 3 different colours.)

Play begins by one player pressing the coop down, which starts the timer. The aim is then for that player to as quickly as possible throw the die and if it matches the colour of a chicken in his row, throw that chicken quickly into the coop. Grab another chicken for the row of three and roll again until the die doesn't match any chicken in your row. You then pass the die to the first player on your left who does have a matching chicken and their turn begins.

When the coop jumps up and chickens go flying the player with the die must gather them and collect three. The rest go out of play. Play continues until one player gets rid of all their chickens.

boardgame: Dahschur: Die Rote Pyramide – Die Privilegien des Pharao
description: Dahschur: Die Rote Pyramide - Die Privilegien des Pharao, a promotional item available at Spiel 2012, consists of four neutral marble tiles – one for each player. Players each have one tile and can use it during the game to extend the road or swap it for other items.

boardgame: Dahschur: Die Rote Pyramide – Der Felukenhändler
description: In the expansion Dahschur: Die Rote Pyramide - Der Felukenhändler, the Felukenhändler – feluccas merchant – is a  seventh market stall added to the end of the market street. Each time a player moves one of his traders to this location, he can swap one of his already acquired gems. He can also earn additional points through this location.

A crazy family/party-game. 
Its a game to play when you want to have a real fun time with your buddies. The game includes that you need to do charades, answer questions with only yes or no and you also may get forced to do very silly stuff, like jump on one leg and sound like chicken every time its your turn.
When you do all this stuff you move a piece on a scoringtrack, the more your do right the more points, if you fail you loose points. If your reach the finnishline first you win.

DragonClash is a game of strategic combat between mighty dragons of legend and is set in a war-torn, modern-day world. After centuries of abuse from mankind, the dying spirit of Earth has called out to the dragons with hopes they’d help better humanity and reshape the world in elemental harmony. However, the destruction and violence that gave rise to their birth also tainted the dragons, driving them mad with a lust for war. Now the dragons not only battle humanity, but themselves. Ruthlessly hunting for the dragon eggs to satiate their endless hunger for power, the dragons wage an eternal struggle for dominance and the very soul of Earth.

In DragonClash, you are the dragon, hatching your beast from a variety of decks geared towards a specific breed and play style. The core set of DragonClash includes the Red Breather, Blue Adept, Silver Channeler, and Brown Mauler decks. Each deck of 72-cards includes a dragon and a variety of attacks unique to that deck’s theme.

To claim victory, your dragon must win the opponent’s Dragon Egg Card. You can do this by either forcing your opponent to discard his Dragon Egg or by consuming your opponent’s hoard with a Feast card, while a Dragon Egg is hidden there. Players take turns feeding their dragon’s hoard, aging from hatchling to elder wyrm, training a variety of attacks, and engaging in combat. Executing attacks, playing effects, and taking damage depletes your dragon’s Essence (the playing deck) and when a player’s dragon has exhausted all of their Essence, they must flee and lose the game.

Objective: gather most victory points by racing on & cleaning a meadow
 - acting/moving by turn -
each player draws slides forming a meadow; according the number of blowballs your meeple may move forward - the further the higher the score; but if there isn't space enough to move players create environmental damage, too.
During the second phase players move back, cleaning up the waste they've generated. Due to mechanism removing all the trash they made (collecting garbage cards) you often have to choose an indirect way or even cooperate.

Aim of the game: The prince has been turned into a frog. Can you return him to his natural state by collecting kisses and the golden ball, and placing him on the right lily pad?

How it is played:

The central piece of the game is a clear plastic cylinder that has a disk inside. The disk shows the prince on one side, and a frog on the other. It is turned over via a magnetic mechanism: If the cylinder is put on the castle tile, it shows the frog. If it is put on the correct lily pad, the tile is turned over again and the prince reappears.

Players take turn spinning a spinner. The spinner has three different areas: One shows one "kiss" symbol (a pair of lips), the second shows two kisses, and the third one the golden ball. If the spinner points toward one of the kiss symbols, the active player gets the corresponding number of kiss tokens. If it points toward the golden ball, the active player gets the ball token (there is only one ball token, and it constantly changes its owner according to the spinner's results). A player can try rescuing the prince as soon as he/she has collected 3 kisses and the golden ball. To rescue the prince, the cylinder has to be put on the correct lily pad. But only one of the lily pads will make the prince reappear. If the player chooses the wrong lily pad, he/she loses their kiss tokens and has to start collecting new ones. If it is correct and the frog turns into a prince, that player wins the game.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Frog Prince

description:
Objective: be the first getting rid of all your puzzle pieces
 - acting by turns -
form a pile with hidden puzzle pieces;
yongest player or loser starts uncovering a piece, placing it upon the table & drawing another one in order to hand it over to left neighbour('s supply);
left neighbour acts in the same way; but if no tile can be placed it must be stored in his/her supply; in this case no tile may be handed over to left neighbour, too.
After pile is empty each play may place 2 tiles out of own supply - by turns.


remarks:
belongs to series: Mosaik "Raum und Grenze"

(English)

Hadak útja, or Crossroad of Peoples is a light wargame in the footsteps of the famous Britannia. The game covers the history of the Carpathian Basin from the Roman conquest (12 BC) to the arrival of the Hungarians (907 AD). Players control mighty invading armies - from the glorious Romans and the ferocius Germanic tribes to the dreaded Huns and the peaceful Slavs - that seek to carve a new home on the blood-soaked soil under the Carpathians.

The players move and fight battles with their units in order to occupy and keep given areas. Units include four types of combat units (normal and heavy infantry, normal and heavy cavalry), settlements (oppida and Roman cities) and leaders (e.g. Emperor Trajan, King Attila the Hun or Prince Svatopluk of Moravia). At the end of the turn each people on the map score VPs from their occupied areas.

The game includes a Simple and an Advanced Game. All files will be made available in English as well.

(magyar)

A Hadak útja egy történelmi társasjáték, amely a híres Britannia című játék nyomdokain a Kárpát-medence küzdelmes múltját mutatja be a római hódítástól (Kr. e. 12.) a magyar honfoglalásig (Kr. u. 907). A játékosok azokat a hódító népeket irányítják - a dicső rómaiaktól és a vad germánoktól a rettegett hunokig és a békés szlávokig -, akik új hazát kerestek a Kárpátok övezte véráztatta síkon.

A játékosok célja, hogy egységeikkel mozogva és csatázva bizonyos területeket foglaljanak el és tartsanak meg. A játékban négyféle harci egység (normál- és nehézgyalogos, normál- és nehézlovas), települések (oppidumok és római városok), illetve vezérek (pl. Traianus császár, Attila hun király vagy Szvatopluk morva fejedelem) kapott helyet. A térképen levő népek a fordulók végén pontokat kapnak az általuk elfoglalt területek után.

A játéknak egy Kezdő és egy Haladó változata is van. Minden szükséges kellék letölthető (lesz) innen.

boardgame: Fear & Greed: The Stock Market Card Game
description:
In Fear & Greed: The Stock Market Card Game, each player is given $10,000 to invest in eight different stock options. Twelve "Fear & Greed" cards are dealt out, simulating a year in the market. The first round is automatically a Buying rush. All players, starting with the Banker, buy stock – 100/shares Min 400/shares Maximum. Once all players have bought, the player to the Banker's left becomes the Broker. The Broker flips a new Stock Market Value card, picks a "Fear & Greed" card in hand and reads it; all other players Buy or Sell on the basis of the card. Each card is color-coded and if it matches stock in a player's portfolio, follow what the card says. Forced actions always take your turn, then the next player becomes The Broker.

When all cards have been played, flip over one last Stock Market Value card and cash out all remaining shares at the new rates. The player with the most equity wins!

A children's game themed on Jonathan Swifts "Gulliver's Travels", specifically on Gulliver's encounter with the Lilliputians.

Each player is head of a Lilliputian family clan, consisting of four members. The aim of the game is to tie down the sleeping Gulliver while gaining as few points as possible. Players take turns taking one of the Lilliputian figures, each of which has either a number from 0 to 5 or a rope symbol on its base (not visible when players choose which figure to take). The active player announces the figure's value and decides if they want to put it on their own family card or return it. If a rope symbol is drawn, the player must exchange two of their family members (they may choose to exchange for a member of a different family clan), announce the new values and tie Gulliver down on one arm or leg. A Lilliputian with a rope symbol is worth ten points.
The game ends when Gulliver has been tied down in all four places. The player whose family clan has the lowest total value (all family members added together) wins the game.

A simple die-based racing game for children.

A big walrus is trying to scare away penguins from their ice floes. Can your penguin still manage to reach the iceberg?

Each player has a penguin player's piece. Players move their penguin according to the roll of the die from one ice floe to the next. If a walrus symbol is rolled, the walrus comes down from the iceberg, pushes the closest penguin off the floe and makes it fall back a few spaces (penguins that have fallen off an ice floe are moved back to the next of the darker floes). The player who has rolled the walrus can choose where to place it.

The player whose penguin reaches the iceberg first wins the game.

A communicative card game in which quotations from the bible have to be put in relation to other texts. The idea behind the game is to show that the bible is still relevant today, to personal life as well as to society and politics.

The game consists of two different sets of cards. One contains quotations from the bible, the other descriptions and aspects of modern life in the form of short texts, messages, promotional slogans etc.

The players have to connect the quotations from the bible on the cards in their hands with the cards from the second set. This doesn't necessarily have to be a literal connection, it involves interpretation and translation into different contexts. 
Each player has a hand of five cards with a quotation from the bible, of which he/she has to choose one to go with the current card from the second set of cards. Each player then has one minute to explain why they have chosen a particular quotation, with the aim of "selling" their choice to the other players. The players then vote on the "best" (i.e. most fitting) quotation. Each round, a different player is the "buyer". He/she uses a set of blue chips numbered 1-5 to secretly vote which quotation they consider best, second best etc. The other players use white chips (also numbered 1-5) to guess the buyer's preferences. Players get points for choosing good (i.e. rated highly by the buyer) quotations as well as for guessing the buyer's preferences. 
The game ends either after a certain number of rounds, or after a player has reached a certain number of points. The player with the most points wins.

The object of the game is to go on a journey round and round any of the four circular routes and collect five picture cards from each of the wildlife, people, flowers, places and birds symbols.

Players roll the die and move, landing on a symbol and collecting a picture.  Sometimes the picture has instructions to move ahead or back, so a player's turn isn't over until no more movements are directed.  After five of one symbol have been collected, a player is penalized cowrie shells for every future landing on that same symbol.

The winner is the player who collects 25 cards and has the highest number of cowrie shells left (thus a player collecting 25 cards first is not necessarily the winner).

Donkey Derby uses something like a roulette wheel for players to win letters which they use to make words. There are four versions of the game based on the word list from which the players are trying to form the words. There is a "Beginers Game", a "Bird Game", an "Animal Game" and a "Capital City Game".

The color for each player is determined each game by lot. When 2 or 3 players are playinging each player gets 2 letter cards (or a special Donkey card) on each spin. If four players are playing there is a twist. The Yellow player gets one letter by spin and also gets to take any letter he wants from the bank. For five players the black player gets one letter plus he can "rob" any player of a letter not in a word.

The game is played for points. 400 points is recommended for 2-4 players, and 500 points for 5 players. The donkey cards are worth points or can be traded back to the bank for three letter cards.

boardgame: 100 Wacky Things You Have to Do While Playing This Game
description:
The game is based around 96 Wacky cards - each with a wacky thing to do, a point value 1-12 and some with either 'Always' or 'Whenever' written on. There are also 4 prop cards and 4 props - funny glasses, a feather boa, an echo microphone and an elephant nose - with detailed instruction on what to do if you have one of these cards.

The 4 youngest players randomly pick a prop card then take that prop. Wacky cards are shuffled and placed facedown within reach of all. On your turn take a Wacky card, place it face up in front of you and read it aloud to all players. You then do the wacky thing - and if it's marked as an Always card you repeat it every turn; if it's marked as a Whenever card it will be whenever someone else does a particular thing.

The object is to be the player who gets the most points for doing wacky things over an agreed number of rounds. Add up the points on each player's cards - highest points wins.

Szczekociny 1794 depicts one of the battles from the Kościuszko Uprising, an important event in Polish history. This particular battle historically was a Polish defeat suffered at the hands of the allied armies of Russia and Prussia, but players can now recreate this conflict to see if a different outcome would also be possible.

Szczekociny 1794 is a hex and counter wargame with a chit-pull mechanic of activating specific parts of a player's army. Game turn consists therefore of movement, ranged and close combat - but the sequence of activations isn't guaranteed. There are three main types of units available: infantry, cavalry and artillery but they are divided even further, so, for example, players can find in the game grenadiers, Russian Cossack cavalry and Polish irregular infantry armed with scythes, "kosynierzy".

The game comes with one introductory scenario, showing a preliminary fight for the village of Wywła, and a scenario for the main battle. Victory is decided by victory points, which themselves can be gained by elimination of enemy units, capturing enemy artillery or killing enemy leaders.

Szczekociny 1794 shares the rules of "Raclawice 1794" but due to the fact that bigger forces were involved in the battle of Szczekociny some rules of the game were simplified, for example conditions of withdrawal and victory. Szczekociny 1794 is an independent and complete game and knowledge of the game "Raclawice 1794" is not required.

Carcassone-like tile-laying game for two players.

Two players try to create a recreation park together. The finished park consists of all 36 tiles in a 6x6 square. There are water and land ways on the tiles (including bridges). Some tiles show black or white ducks.

Each player has a different goal: The "land" player tries to build a land way connecting all 4 edges of the park without building any islands and placing two black ducks next to each other. The "water" player tries the same with water ways (and avoiding lakes and placing two white ducks next to each other).

Like in "Carcassonne", on each turn a player draws a tile and places it next to an already played tile. At the end, there must be a 6x6 square. The players receive -1 point for not fulfilling parts of their goal (connecting edges, avoiding islands/lakes, placing black/white ducks together).

Note: The self published version features 36 cards, the Amigo version of Wege and The Legend of Landlock feature 40 tiles.

(English)

Eagle and Crescent is a 2 player card driven wargame set in 16th century Hungary. One player controls the Habsburg Empire, the other the Ottoman Turks as they vie for the control of Hungary in the titanic struggle known as Fifteen Years' War (1591-1606). Players move around with their generals, besiege forts and try to keep Transylvania and Wallachia on their side.

The game now has a fully playable VASSAL module! You can download it from https://drive.google.com/file/d/1DXyiUPG5PjOcV3QG_qUuJjzitWjhhPiR/view?usp=sharing.

All components of the game are downloadable in English, including a map, units and counters, standing generals, army sheets, 56 strategy cards and the rules.

(magyar)

A Sas és félhold egy kétszemélyes háborús játék (wargame), amely a 16. századi Magyarországon játszódik. Az egyik játékos a Habsburg, a másik a török seregeket irányítja a kor legnagyobb küzdelmében, a tizenöt éves háborúban (1591-1606). A játékosok a hadvezéreikkel manővereznek, várakat ostromolnak, miközben Erdélyt és Havasalföldet is meg kell tartaniuk a saját oldalukon.

A játék összes kelléke letölthető magyarul is: a térkép, az egységek és egyéb jelzők, a talpon álló hadvezérek, a sereglapok, az 56 stratégia kártya és a szabályok is.

A játék angol nyelvű VASSAL modulja letölthető erről a címről: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1DXyiUPG5PjOcV3QG_qUuJjzitWjhhPiR/view?usp=sharing.

Bill Johnson – You Wake Up To Marmageddon!

Set in the world of 'Bill Johnson - Zombie Issues Specialist' an e-book by Jon Brown, Marmageddon is a tile-placement game in which Players draw and place tiles, exploring a warehouse - trying to collect the most Marmite.

The tiles feature Marmite, Zombies and movement icons. They must be placed adjacent to tiles that have already been played, in such a way that a warehouse is formed. Having placed a tile, the Player moves his character around the warehouse (based on the movement icons on the tiles), collecting the precious Marmite and defeating the Zombies. Players must escape the warehouse with their collected Marmite before the tiles are exhausted or the warehouse is complete, else suffer the consequences!

Shinobi is a game for 2-4 player, players take turns being Ninja to submerge mazes of other players who need to find out hidden treasures.

Player can set their very own maze with walls, traps and treasures on the map, to keep other player not easily to find the treasures. And into mazes with other players' Ninja at the same time. By moving their own Ninja step by step to find the walls, traps and treasures, fight with other Ninja and samurai guardian.

Players use their own Ninja search other players maze which hide by the maze keeper. Players give the position they move to the keeper, the keeper give players their Ninjas' search result.
   
The goal of the game is find all the treasures, The fast one find all the treasures is the winner.

Spell Thief is a modular Dexterity game for 1-3 Players

Using the room and hallway tiles you build a floor of the Academy on which to attempt your great heist. You must carefully navigate the floor (by flicking your token) avoiding wizard guards and areas of light while sticking to shadows and stealing magic tomes. If you alert the guards you will have to deal with the mighty Iron Sentries that stalk your every move and the awesome power of the Hunters who cast Ice Walls, Snares and Light Traps to try and capture you! If you are brave enough to challenge the upper floors of the tower you might also have to be wary of the fearsome Matron of the Academy.

Everyone wants to see the first rainbow in the sky because of the satisfaction it brings.

In Rainbow Seven, you are an explorer looking for that "First Rainbow" – but you're not the only one, so you'll need to be quick to collect the seven color clues and find out where that first rainbow is before the rain stops. Watch your bearings, and don't let the North, South, East, or West lead you away from that first rainbow...

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Expansion 1: Illusions
description:
Escape: Illusions includes two new modules for the Escape base game that can be used individually or mixed with any other available modules.

The "Illusion Chambers" module consists of six chamber tiles that replace the tiles with only one magic gem in the basic game. After players return to the starting chamber during the game – something they must do twice in order to avoid losing a die – all illusion chambers in play are removed and returned to the bottom of the tile deck. Hopefully you already completed your business in any section of the temple now disconnected from the rest!

The "Special Chambers" module consists of six new chamber tiles, divided as follows:


    Three linked chamber tiles that allow you to activate magic gems, thereby making it easier for the adventurers to escape the temple – but the gems can be activated only if players are in two separate such chambers at the same time.
    Two double chamber tiles that consist of a front and back room, with access to the back room being available only if you add tiles to the temple and snake around to the back. Why do you want to reach this room? More opportunities to activate magic gems and ease your way out of the temple.
    One treasure chamber tile, containing a chalice that adventurers must bear to the exit tile. If they fail to do so, they lose the game – even if they otherwise would all escape!


Components for a sixth player are included, as well as two copies each of one new curse card ("Soul Exchange") and one new treasure tile ("Large Torch").

Codigo Cube is the play anywhere, anytime trivia game using QR Codes and the camera on your Smartphone. QR codes are those little pixilated squares you see everywhere. When you scan them they take you to a website. How Codigo Cube works is there is a QR Code embedded die. Each side of the die contains a different QR Code. Each QR Code represents a different trivia category. First person to get one question correct from all six sides wins!
publishers: Four Clowns Game & Toy Co, LLC

boardgame: Mr. Buckethead Adventure Game Series: Mr. Buckethead Goes To The Moon
description:
Mr. Buckethead gets to choose between visiting the candy store or the toy store; somehow, this eventually leads him to prehistoric jungles and the dangerous surface of the moon.

This is an interactive gamebook where the player constantly chooses options in an evolving comic-book-like story, collecting money, items, friends and Action Points (used to activate special powers). The book includes a scoresheet and many puzzles and mini-games throughout, as the player works to gain the challenging Victory Point (by defeating the Moon Monster). Like other books in the series, there is also a 2-player monster fight mini-game included.

ABOUT THE SERIES:
Based on the live action television and film character, this new series of interactive gamebooks is adventure-packed! 64 pages of intense gameplay action with an innovative story structure that allows continuous play across multiple issues. Each issue in this series contains a solo adventure plus a bonus multi-player monster fighting game! The appeal of an evolving RPG with the look and feel of a comic book, the Mr. Buckethead Adventure Game Series is challenging and entertaining fun for all ages!
family: TV Shows: Mr. Buckethead Adventure Game

description:
This is a set of 18 Exploration tiles offered with the Disaster Looms! Kickstarter campaign. They contain two additional events and four more region tiles per game phase.

These additional tiles can be used to add a bit more game length, or to add additional replay value, length, or mystery to the treasures - and dangers - that space has to offer.

Game given away in the first edition of the U.K. magazine, The Gamer.

The board represents a Space Invaders layout, broken up into squares. One player is the Planet Player (otherwise known as the PP) and the other is the Aliens Player (AP of course). The Aliens are represented by a single card that starts at the top and then will move across the board and down, just like the real thing. The PP gets to fire first and if he hits any aliens, they are marked off the card with a wax crayon. Then the AP gets to drop bombs.

On any one turn, the PP can fire four shots or he may move to any space along the baseline and get two shots only. A die is used to indicate successful hits.

The PP player wins if he destroys all the Aliens. The AP player wins if he can get just one alien to touch the baseline.

Variants are included for using two dice and for adapting the game for three players.

Another game by the company that specialised in making designs so that partially or totally colour blind people can play. They mainly made traditional games but this is an original game by the company owner.

The game is played on a 7 x 7 grid. Each player must try to get four pegs in a row, hence the name of the game. It is a refined version of Go-Moku or 5-in-a-Row. The limit on placing pegs is that each peg played must be adjacent to an opposition peg. First to get four in a row, wins.

boardgame: NHL Big League Manager: Platinum Edition
description: A hockey lover’s dream!  In this bilingual board game (French and English) with a Monopoly feel, players take on the role of General Manager of a hockey team.  Each player selects their starting line-up: a goalie, two defensemen and three forwards. Players must build and manage their teams, negotiate salaries and trades and win matches against other players' teams.  The game is quite complex and initially, regular references to the instructions are required; however, after a few times, children find the game straightforward.  The game has many realistic details and young hockey fans are thrilled with this game!

Originally named "Rosicrucian Chess," this is a four-handed chess variant that can be played as a game, or used as a divination tool. (The divination version requires the use of a die and an additional piece.) The game was invented in the Victorian era by the adepts of the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn in England. Despite the existence of extensive original instructions, no surviving manuscript gives perfectly unambiguous rules for play, so different modern authors have published instructions that do not always agree in all details. The first published version was in The Golden Dawn (1941) by Israel Regardie.

There are four different 8x8 boards, corresponding to the classical elements, of which only one is used in a given game session. Setup configuration of the pieces depends on which of the boards is being used, with four further variations possible within that decision. Ordinary game play involves partners seated across from one another, with their opponents at right angles to them: air and fire vs. water and earth. Setup is on the right-hand half of the nearest two rows, so that pawns generate a sort of spiral motion on the board as a whole.

Each player has nine chessmen (4 pawns, knight, queen, bishop, rook, and king), and piece movement varies slightly from standard modern chess, especially in the case of the queen. Pawns are promoted only to the corresponding piece of their native file. Each of the 36 chessmen has a correspondence to a given Egyptian god or goddess, and all of the board squares correspond to letters in the Angelic Tablets of Renaissance magicians John Dee and Edward Kelly.

HexPack: Mountains and Canyons is a flexible map system aid for the BattleTech board game. Designed for use in conjunction with other preprinted mapsheets (such as those from the Introductory Box Set), the system’s flexibility allows for easy modifications to existing mapsheets, increasing the enjoyment of a given scenario as players quickly change the terrain to bring new excitement to each game they play! A booklet contains two ready-to-play scenarios, which include expanded play options.

The BattleTech Introductory Box Set is suggested for use with HexPack: Mountains and Canyons.
 Contents:
 •One 18” x 22” full-color gameboard-quality mapsheet, with a different map printed on either side.
 •Four punch-out-and-ready-to-play gameboard-quality HexTile sheets.
 •Sixteen page booklet.

boardgame: Was ist  Was: Junior – Polizei Kartenspiel
description:
A card game that teaches children about some typical tasks police officers have to do and what equipment they need.

Whether the finale of an important soccer tournament or a state visit is taking place, whether there is a case of suspected arson or a suspicious suitcase is found - the police has to face many different challenges. What is to be done in each case and what means are needed is described on the challenge boards. The cards also show different resources that help the police to complete these tasks, such as handcuffs, a walkie-talkie, a police dog, a police horse, helicopter, motorbike etc. Players try to collect all the necessary resources for one of the three current challenges. If they are successful, they earn that challenge card and the points written on the card. The game ends when one player has collected 10 points or when the last card has been drawn. The player with the most points wins the game.

Components: 16 challenge boards, 53 cards, rules
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Golden Questions is an LDS game. The players are Mormon Missionaries who encounter various people they must try to convert with the Golden Question, "Would you like to know more about the Mormon Church?"

Players move around the board by spinning for results on a "Golden Spinner". Each street is filled with houses where the missionaries can seek for potential investigators. The game also makes use of Mormon trivia questions which players much answer in order to earn investigator cards.

The player who converted the most investigators to Mormonism wins, but there is also a kid edition where everyone wins.
family: Religious: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

description: An action game for children aged 4 an up. Players must flick plastic flies into a large plastic frog (with an gaping mouth) while the frog rotates under battery power. While a fly is in flight the other players can attempt to close the frog's mouth by activating their pump.

BLOODTHIRSTY BEASTS is the first expansion to the Clang! CORE ARMY PACK

Each player has one Beast Hunter card.  Instead of fighting or talking with their Troops (standard Clang! rules), players can instead choose to send out their Beast Hunters to search for Beasts that they can use to attack the enemy.  They can only do this if they are the player leading the battle, and only if they were also leading in the previous game turn.  Sending out the Beast Hunters means they surrender the lead (to the player on their left)

At the end of the turn that that player regains the lead, their Beast Hunters return with a Beast (top card from the pile).  They can use this Beast to fight the enemy, but only if they have their Beast Hunters with their army (not out beast hunting), as they need these to goad the Beast into battle

It is best to use Beasts to attack players who have no Beasts themselves, or whose Beast Hunters are out looking for monsters, as they will have to defend using their Troops rather than a Beast

Beasts can fight Beasts, using standard Clang! fighting rules, and Beasts can fight Troops, again using the standard rules, but only red attack bars can affect Beasts, so they are hard to defeat.  If a player has fewer than three red combat bars amongst the hand of four Troops they have to choose from, they are doomed to lose, so it is best to pick the three weakest Troops to send to their destruction

Some Beasts have Special Attacks - these could depend on Troops having a certain level of bar for a skill such as Bravery or they all run away from the beast, or could mean looking at the Troop pictures so for instance any Troops without shields cannot defend themselves against the Special Attack and are defeated

Beasts can help turn the tide of battle, intimidate other players, or add a final blow to win the war, but Beasts themselves do not count towards Troop totals so if your Troops are defeated you lose

Components:
BLOODTHIRSTY BEASTS gamebox
Beasts (1 deck of 16 cards)
Beast Hunters (1 deck of 6 cards)
Instructions (1 deck of 3 cards)

The object of the game is to be the first to fill one's tiara with all five of the gems.  There are four boards, one for each player.  The player spins the spinner and if it lands on a space with a gem that she (it's mostly a little girls' game) doesn't have in her tiara, she gets it.  There is a "lose a gem space" as well as a "spin again" space (although it has little effect on the game).

Components:
4 Game Boards
20 Gem pieces
Spinner
Instructions

boardgame: The Compleat Fondler: Eighteen Scenarios for Sharp Practice
description:
This superb Sharp Practice scenario supplement follows the career of Richard Fondler from his time as an enlisted man in India in 1799 through to Waterloo where he is a Lieutenant Colonel. In between he has an exciting and illustrious career in the Peninsula with the 95th Rifles and the Light Company of the West Sussex Regiment. Here we present eighteen scenarios, each with an introductory story, briefings for both sides and umpire's notes with a full colour map. The games are designed for between 30 and 100 or so figures per side, and are rip-roaring exciting games, packed with derring-do, larger than life characters and, best of all, real gaming challenges and fun. The scenarios included are as follows.

Fondler's Battle, Fondler's Enemy, Fondler's Rifles, Fondler's Allies, Fondler's Captain, Fondler's Pirate, Fondler's Colonel, Fondler's Gold, Fondler's Trap, Fondler's Escape, Fondler's Surprise, Fondler's Honour, Fondler's Siege, Fondler's Lady, Fondler's Triumph, Fondler's Breech, Fondler's Duke, and Fondler's Waterloo. All draw on the rich heritage of literary heroes and villains.

SET Junior uses the familiar SET gameplay, but scaled down in difficulty for younger players. In SET, players want to create sets of three cards, with each card in the set having the same feature as each other card in the set or a different feature. For example, all three cards have green symbols on them, but they have 1, 2 and 3 symbols and the symbols are all different shapes (oval, squiggle and diamond).

One side of the double-sided game board in SET Junior features three 3x3 squares, with each line in a square forming a set. Players take turns drawing a tile from the stack — with each tile featuring 1-3 symbols in one of three colors and in one of three shapes — and placing that tile on the game board. Each time a player completes a set by placing a tile, she claims a point chip and whoever collects the most chips wins.

On the other side of the game board, a dealer lays out up to ten tiles one by one and whenever any player sees a set among the face-up tiles, she shouts "Set!" and claims the tiles that form the set. The dealer then replaces these tiles and the game continues until no more sets can be claimed. Whoever claims the most sets wins.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: Rancheros is a casual strategy game for 2-4 players in which players take on the role of Mexican Ranchers that are trying to raise various animals to meet the demands of their local meat market. The player will acquire varying amounts of pesos for how they raise their animals while balancing the demands of the meat market. The player with the most pesos when the game ends is the winner.

This was published sometime between 1910 and 1930 in Germany.

On a 9 x 9 grid of black and white squares 2 players move their pawns diagonally from their own corner towards the centre of the board. Here, the central area of 9 squares is marked (the "black" squares are red).

Each player has 1 "main" and 8 "additional" pawns. Starting position is on the 9 spaces of one corner (creating another square), the main pawn in the middle of the 8 additional ones.

Before the game, players agree upon a goal to reach: One out of 7 ending positions to fulfill with their pawns. All of these positions are located in the central area, from 2 pawns sitting on two opposing corners of the area up to the full 9 spaces of the centre. If the goal includes the central space, the main piece has to be placed there to reach the goal.

On their turn, players move one of their pawns one space diagonally. You can "catch" an opposing pawn by having two of your own pawns on each side of it - again diagonally. You can then pick it up and place it on any unoccupied space of the same colour anywhere on the board.

The player who manages first to fulfill the goal wins the game.

From the publisher:

Fling your frogs onto the Lily Pad levels to score points. But, beware…someone else’s flung frog may push yours off and leave you scoreless! Challenge friends to a game, or play by yourself to top your best scores. The Lily Pad also acts as a convenient storage and travel case.

Contents: Lily Pad with 5 Levels, 12 Frogs (3 of Each Color), Rules. The lily pad also doubles as a convenient storage case.

Spurtle Turtle is one of the games in Patch Products 'Big Little Games' range.

From the publisher:
Game tokens have stickers on with pictures of different sea creatures. Mix the tokens and place 10 in each side of the turtle. Press the turtle’s back to spurt out tokens, and then try to find matching sets! The colorful tokens feature fun sea creatures that kids will love. The winner is the player who finds the most matches.

A twist would be to play the game by adding a timed play option. See which player can find the most matches in the shortest amount of time.

Contents: Turtle Double-Token Shooter, 20 Tokens, Rules. The turtle is also a convenient storage case.

This is one of the Patch Products Big Little Games series, designed to be travel games.

From the publisher:
Practice shooting hoops and gain mad skills! Just place the ball on the shooter, press down on the back lever, and your ball flies through the air! Simply aim the shooter where you think you will have a good chance to get the ball into the net, and press down. Includes rules for three games - Horse, Around the World and 3-Point Shootout - so you can challenge friends to different games.

Contents: Basketball hoop with fold-down backboard, attachable basketball shooter, 3 tiny basketballs, rules. The shooter attaches to the hoop for storage. The game takes a few minutes to assemble.

Banners of Betrayal is a lightly structured card game about feudal warfare. The players can freely attack each other, and the losing side of a fight joins the winner as a vassal. The ultimate objective is to eliminate every other contender for the crown.

Combat is based on a modified rock-paper-scissors mechanic. As cards that are played in a combat are discarded, extended fights weaken both players. The focus is on knowing when a player should yield and accept a lower rank, or risking losing in the final round.

There are three game modes called Honor, Tournament of Honor and Betrayal. The first two game modes use the principle described above. The tournament takes several rounds and the players are scored by their end rank. In the Betrayal game mode the King will award points for himself and his allies at the end of each round. The other players can opt to be contenders, who can fight with the king, or lords who can't fight by themselves, but who can sell their services for points.

In Foothold Enterprises, you are a young entrepreneur trying to get your startup company going. You have a limited amount of time to see a return on your investments. Buy advertising, win over clients, and put your company on the map!

This solitaire game uses a single deck of custom cards and can be played while standing in line.  It is mechanically inspired by such brilliant push-your-luck games as Zed Deck and Lord of the Rings: The Adventure Deck Game.  Each turn, you flip the top card of the deck and select one of the three aspects of the card on which to bid: advertising, money, or clients.  You then bid as little or as much money as you like.

The card then tells you how many cards to reveal from the top of the deck for the game's counter bid.  You add up the money scores from those revealed cards.  If you bid higher, you win the flipped card!  All revealed cards are placed face-up on the bottom of the deck.  The flipped card is also place face-up, but may be turned sideways to expose the advertising ability if you won the card, or turned upside-down to indicate that you won the clients.

Advertisements give you abilities that allow you to win certain kinds of bids more easily or otherwise manipulate the game rules.  Clients count as Victory Points at the end of the game.  You only gain $2 at the beginning of each turn, so keep an eye on your cash flow, or you'll lose out on important clients or key advertising abilities!

When you have gone through the entire deck (all cards are face-up), simply count up the client values of all the upside-down cards.  This is your score for the game.  Then try to beat that score next game!

Designed for the 2012 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.

Frog Skins was created for a Game Design Challenge, which charged its competitors to create a game that improved humanity in 60 seconds or less.  Its target: inflation.  The game gives new meaning to a CCG being a license to print money, because in this CCG, the "cards" are money — various denominations of bills from $1 to $100, to be more precise.

Each player creates a deck of five bills.  As in War, each player presents a bill, and the higher bill wins the round.  Ties go into a tie pool.  Whoever wins the most rounds wins the game and the money.  The hook, and the mechanism that combats inflation is that you may choose to rip a full bill into halves, which in the game, counts as two copies of that "card", but in the real world, causes a smidgen of deflation (the rules state that you must throw away torn bills after the game).

A quick 2-card gambling game.

The game is played with a standard deck of cards (without Jokers), divided into two deck -- one red and one black.

Each turn starts with a one coin ante, then each player is dealt one card from each deck, so that each player has one red and one black card.

Simultaneously each player reveals one card -- either red or black.  Then the player with the lowest card showing decides either to drop out or to force a showdown. Since it goes from lowest to highest, not every player will have the opportunity to drop out before the showdown occurs.

When the showdown occurs, everyone shows both of their cards.  The player with the highest black card wins.  Everyone else (except for those who dropped out) then pay the winning player a number of coins equal to the value of the red card (aces are worth 1 coin, and face cards are worth 10 coins). The winner also gets the antes.  If there is no showdown, the surviving player gets the antes.

This game is part of the Cheapass Games Poker Suite that was released on the Cheapass website.

Flames of War is a World War II tabletop game where you use armies represented by painted miniature tanks, soldiers, artillery, and aircraft against another similar opponent's force.

To begin all you need is the Flames of War book/rules and a starting force of miniatures depending on what type of army you want to collect.

The game uses 15mm scale (around 1:100) miniatures, allowing miniatures from other manufacturers to be used. The game aspires to provide a good balance between playability and simulation and has a typical game running under 2 hours.

Flames of War: Achtung! is a two-player starter set with the 3rd Edition Rules.

Achtung! has been carefully designed for someone who has never heard of Flames Of War before, or has seen the 296 page rulebook and not known where to start. Achtung! is not a simplified set of the Flames Of War rules, but rather a complete introductory box for a new player!

Paths of Destiny takes players into the world of fantasy arena. In this turn based strategy game two players will command a team of 5 Pathwalkers, selected from a lineup of 8 different Pathwalkers with individual unique abilities and pit their tactical skills against one another in an intense battle of life and death.

Players will take turns to maneuver their Pathwalkers on a hexagonal grid, and using cards that represent different body parts, players will engage in a game of chance and guesswork that simulates the exchanging of blows.

Players score points by injuring their opponent’s Pathwalkers, incapacitating their body parts and rendering them unconscious. The player that scores 20 points first wins the game.

Skystrike is a World War 2 Pacific Theater of Operations Nautical wargame  cited by the designer as very suitable for novices in that genre.

The game is based on the crucial battle for Midway Island on the 4th June 1942. To win, the Japanese player has to sink the United State's aircraft carriers and thus capturing Midway Island. The American player wins by preventing the Japanese taking the island and inflicting more losses on the Japanese forces than his own suffer.

Each player has their own board and the game thus uses a blind/search system. Only when the enemy is found can combat occur. Each turn represents 8 hours, so three turns per day are the limit. The searching rule owes a debt to dear old Battleships and the board is overlaid with squares so that movement is by square to square.

Once combat starts, results are determined by a die roll. I think there could be very quick combat rounds "Roll a 6 and the enemy carrier is sunk", that sort of thing (it is intended for beginners after all). When it comes to invading the island, the Japanese player gets only one attempt per game so the player must be pretty sure he has timed it correctly.

There are variants - a quick game and an advanced game, plus rules for designating your aircraft as fighters and/or bombers.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
There are 24 wooden tokens showing different lines/symbols.
This cards are placed on the table face up so everyone can see and reach all the tokens.
A pile of 24 task tokens with the same lines/symbols is prepared face down.
By turn, players draw a task token without showing it to other players.
Then thy use a chain to draw the line/symbol.
The other players have to find the matching wooden token.
The first player to get the right token scores one point (gets the token).
After the pile is exhausted, the game ends. The winner is the player who got the most tokens.

Suburbia: Essen Spiel consists of three new buildings (with two copies of each) for Suburbia:


     Grugapark: This increases your borough's reputation, with a bonus for being adjacent to other Essen Spiel buildings.
     U-Bahn: This increases your borough's income, with a bonus for being adjacent to other Essen Spiel buildings.
     Messe Essen: This increases both your borough's reputation and income, with a bonus for being adjacent to other Essen Spiel buildings.


To use these buildings in Suburbia, mix them into their appropriate stacks (A, B, or C) prior to starting a game, then follow the instructions on each tile after adding it to your city.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The fearsome dragon in charge of the Wedding Hoard has rallied the (uninvited) guests to its cause of getting the first bite of cake! The Wedding party must join forces to fight off this threat before the entire day is ruined by the dessert motivated forces of evil! Ultimate Chibi Defenders: Wedding Edition is a lite cooperative game of tactical cake defense.

On their turn players move and use an action (attack or special ability) to slow or defeat the advancing and growing hoard of wedding crashers. Once all players have acted, crashers advance - attacking any heroes in their path - and attempt to eat the wedding cake. The players win if the Dragon is defeated before the cake is completely eaten. The Wedding Crashers win if the cake is devoured.

The game was distributed as a wedding favour at the designer's wedding reception. It is now available for free as a print and play game!

Each player has pawn on the game board nad the object is to move it from start to finish.

In his turn player draws a card and chooses between a light, medium or heavy question (or statement) to answer. Then the card is turned over and who answered correctly andvances hi pawn 1 or 2 fields when light or medium question was chosen and as many fields as dice shows if
difficult question was chosen.

Player who reaches at finish first is the winner.

In this game Lady Rowena was kidnapped by the Black Knights and the players as a knight of the lion, the bear, the eagle and the unicorn sends his mercenary troops looking for the magic key. 
In his turn player rolls the dice, moves his character and follows the action event cards trying to get the key. Whoever gets to castle first with the key to open the door his mercenaries first is the winner.

Tom and Tamira's language game
Tamira and Tom are on the playground. They wait for their dog Robbie. But Robbie is lost.

The players can help Robbie to come to the playground. Tha question is drawn and Tom and Tamira give both answer that question. But who is right? Tom, Tamira or perhaps both.

Players who have given the correct answer may move Robbie to the next square. Choose the shortest route and watch out for all gates. Who is the first smart playing with Tom and Tamira  is the winner of the game.

The instructions/rules for the game are printed inside the lid. The game is for 2, 3 or 4 players. The required pieces are: one playing piece for each player, a spinning indicator and a package of checks. The checks are used for keeping account of the points won or lost, with white counting one, blue counting two and red counting three. Each player begins with a set number of scoring pieces

There are rewards and punishments for such things as falling from your horse, talking in ranks, smoking cigarettes, being proficient in study, having soldierly deportment, doing good recitations, etc.

Whoever has the highest score when all players have finished the game is the winner.

This vintage dexterity game features two foam rubber volleyball athletes inside a vertical playing surface. Each player controls a "flipper" dial that moves his athlete under a plaxiglass shield. The volleyball is a plastic disc that is tossed back-and-forth under the plexiglass.

The athletes are manipulated to catch or instantly return the white disc representing the ball. A dropped volleyball or a return that lands out of bounds scores a point for the other player. First player to five points is declared the winner.

Includes the plastic volleyball court assembly, two plastic legs, and two discs.

"This Hallowed Ground" is a set of rules for American Civil War 15mm military figures. These rules are designed for grand tactical play. The scale is set at six turns per hour and one inch for twenty-five yards. Movement, fire, and morale are based upon the brigade (consisting of one to six regiments for infantry and cavalry.) Artillery is structured around a battery of four to six guns (represented by a single unit.) Included is a sample (and partial) Order of Battle for Gettysburg and a supplement for adapting the rules to fictious battles against forces loyal to the Emperor Maxmillian I of Mexico.

 
These rules are quite comprehensive, including command control, weather and other random events (such as pre-battle disease), artillery crew casualties, the effects of unit training/experience, close action procedures, morale, sighting and line-of-fire, and much more. 
 Three appendices are included: A graphic description of formations; a brief description for conversion to 25mm figures; and a glossary.

Box description:

"Crossbows and Catapults... The action advendture game where skill and imagination win the battle."

"In the Dark Ages, enemy kingdoms battled using the powerful crossbow and the mighty catapult...

"The battlefield is now being readied for the clash between the Vikings and the Barbarians.  You are the commander of the Vikings and you opponent is the commander of the Barbarians.  Both commanders have great arsenals of weapons and brave warriors, but the one who possesses the greater skill and better strategy will conquer!"

TROJAN BATTLESET CONTENTS:
 2 towers
 2 courtyards
24 wallblocks
16 warriors (8 vikings, 8 barbarians)
32 projectiles (15 standard and 1 king carom each)
 2 crossbows
 2 catapults
 1 trojan horse
 1 battle shield
   Label sheets
   Rulebook

This rare Crossbows and Catapults set (not to be confused with the smaller and more common "Trojan Horse and Battle Shield" expansion) brings the additional power of two mighty weapons to the battlefield.

The set follows the rules of Crossbows and Catapults, but adds the Trojan Horse and Battle Shield to the players' arsenals.

To play, each player is given a set of castle parts, plastic warrior figures, flags, projectiles and the seige engines that fire them.  Before play, each player sets up their defenses to best withstand the oppenent's onslaught. They then take turns launching projectile caroms at the opposing defenses.

Opponents agree before play on any of the four official winning conditions. Alternatively players make up their own rules, or simply blast away at eachother's defenses until the enemy's force is annihilated.

The Trojan Horse (Vikings) and Battle Shield (Barbarians) are weapons used to propel caroms at the opposing forces, similar to the standard catapult and crossbow respectively.  But they also serve as tempting targets for the enemy. When struck, internal mechanisms spectacularly expel warrior figures or caroms.

“If Adventure has a Name, it must be Indiana Jones.”

It is the 1930s. The world is in the grip of the Great Depression. Across the globe war clouds gather. The Japanese have invaded China. The Italians are in Ethiopia. Adolf Hitler is planning to build an empire while Stalin butchers millions of his own people. Spain is engulfed in civil war. Britain, France, and America are too weak to stop the gathering tide of blood. In these chaotic times Dr. Henry Jones Jr., known by his nickname “Indiana,” quests after lost artifacts. In doing so he must battle gangsters, cultists, thugs, and worst of all Nazis, who are fanning out across the globe looking for items that will allow them to found a 1,000 year Reich.

“The return of the great adventure.”

In Indiana Jones: Fortune and Glory you play either as Indiana Jones or his adversaries, battling across Africa, Asia, and Europe as you try to recover some of the greatest artifacts of human history. As Indy you rely upon your wits, your weapons, and a small cadre of allies, including Marion, Sallah, Short Round, and Henry Jones Sr. The Villains rely more upon brute force, utilizing a host of henchmen. More importantly, they must use an asortment of talented and morally dubious individuals, including Belloq, Toht, Lao Che, Mola Ram, Elsa, and Vogel.

“You call this archaeology?”

Each player deploys some cards to locations, battling for either artifacts or an advantage. Some locations have a puzzle rating, which must also be overcome in order to gain artifacts, with each item being worth victory points. Special cards handle the events. While some cards are free, most are deployed by discarding cards from your hand. Each deck is asymmetrical. The Villains rely upon brute force, while Indy must often use event cards to get out of a jam. The game is meant to be played quickly. And of course, some of the greatest lines in the series will have cards…

“Indiana Jones. I always knew some day you'd come walking back through my door.” – Marion Ravenwood

“Fräulein Ravenwood, let me show you what I am used to...” – Toht

“Snakes. Why'd it have to be snakes?” – Indiana Jones

“Indiana, we are simply passing through history. This...this is history.” – Rene Belloq

“Fortune and glory, kid. Fortune and glory.” – Indiana Jones

“Drop them, Dr. Jones! They will be found! You won't!” – Mola Ram

“Prepare to meet Kali... in Hell!” – Indiana Jones

“I'm going home to Missouri where they never feed you snakes before ripping your heart out and lowering you into hot pits!” – Willie Scott

“You lost today, kid. But that doesn't mean you have to like it.” – Fedora

“And this is how we say goodbye in Germany, Dr. Jones.” – Colonel Vogel

“I suddenly remembered my Charlemagne. Let my armies be the rocks and the trees and the birds in the sky...” – Henry Jones Sr.

“He chose poorly.” – Grail Knight

In a post-apocalyptic world, a few months ago a zombie outbreak took charge of the largest cities on the planet. Urban areas are infested and the military are planning some reconnaissance missions on the major affected areas.

You and your team of soldiers were in one of those missions, flying a town overrun by zombies. But suddenly the unexpected happened! A problem with the helicopter engine causes the loss of altitude and the aircraft is going down. Luckily, (or not), you and the other members survived the crash.

It looks like the survivors crashed over some abandoned and unknown building. The problem is that, just like the whole city, there are zombies everywhere!

With low ammo, the only way to survive is to cross the interior of the building and search for exit.

This is a survival game, where characters are lost in an environment full of zombies.

The map, zombie encounters, weapons and ammo are randomly created and each game is never the same.

It is a print and play game. The PDF includes:
-24 map tiles
-Zombies and soldiers miniatures (cut, fold and glue)
-Modern weapons & ammo counters

Piłkarski Manager is an unique boardgame connecting football and economy. Each player becomes a football team manager. Their goal is to build the strongest team and win the league.

On the board we can:

     buy and sell players
     expand training school and draft junior players
     use airlines to move team between spots
     place bet at the bookies


Each player in our team has certain abilities with point values assigned. Our team strength depends on all its player abilities. The better the players, the more chance to win the match.

To play the match you roll the dice and compare the result to the game outcome table which takes into account different strengths of two teams.

From the publisher's and Toy Directory websites:
The board is used on a slight incline and the game pieces are magnetic. The Magical Rainbow is a magnetized game that takes children on a special adventure through 7 magical lands. Players race to a pot of gold, watching out for Paddy the Leprechaun, who on every turn travels from land to land protecting his gold. Throughout the journey players will try to collect gold coins, avoid the Leprechaun and his lucky trinkets, and use strategy to win the game. The first player to reach the pot of gold at the end of the Rainbow wins, however the game encourages sharing with others in need. This game is designed to teach children color recognition, sequencing, patterns, counting, strategy and sharing.

"Using a rainbow game theme lets kids imagine what it would be like living on a Magical Rainbow, and helping Paddy the leprechaun give out his gold to people in need.  The rainbow game combines colors and a magnetic 3d game set that keep kids entertained game after game.  The other goal for Magical Rainbow was to create a board game that could be played with one child.  There are a lot of one child families and what good is a board game if you don’t have anyone to play the game with?  Magical Rainbow can be used as a standalone make believe play set."  The game pieces double as flexible magnetic play pieces that have letters and numbers on the reverse side. They can be used on the front or back of the game board or on any metallic surface to assist children in developing recognition with the alphabet, numbers and words.

Awards: 2009 Family Choice Award, Creative Child Magazine's Game of the Year award, Dr Toy Best Picks, 2010

Form a gang and fight for dominance over the city. Seize the most valuable districts, search the ruins to gather the precious gambles, make use of your units' special abilities, which are straight from Neuroshima RPG.

Neuroshima Tactics is a miniature wargame in which the players - using 28mm figures - clash among the ruins of the Goddam United States. Outpost is the last regular army of humanity. Army that uses leftovers of pre-war equipment. This set contains 5 miniatures, tokens, cards and terrain elements.

Borgo Mutants are children of doom. Created by Moloch, led by their leader they were form into independent power. This set contains 5 miniatures, tokens, cards and terrain elements.

Neuroshima Tactics is a miniature wargame in which the players - using 28mm figures - clash among the ruins of the Goddam United States. Moloch. Machine empire. Mechano-electronic creation that takes area of several states. Uncountable cybernetic brains weave strategies and coordinate attacks. This set contains 5 miniatures, tokens, cards and terrain elements.

The quiz game for the television series on Pro 7.
Categories are: Purple: Jesus Christ, Green: Pirate, White: King Arthur, Red: Leonardo da Vinci, Orange: d'Artagnan, White: Various.
There are question cards, each with six questions and 3 possible answers per question. The players take turns to ask questions according to the color location of the other characters, all others respond with their cards. Who answers correctly, takes a step further on the point bar. For the first correct answer to a category you get a Mystery disc.
If all player finishes they scores according the finishing order and number of mystery discs.
Who has the most points is the winner.

The quiz game for the television series on Pro 7.
500 cards are divided into three stacks of three rounds. 
In the first round, players must answer 10 questions marking the correct answers with the electronic pen. Each correct answer takes 4 seconds. Unused time can be used in the second round after answering 12 questions. 
6 questions are in the final round. 
Points for correct answers are added up and the player with the most points wins.

Includes 2 mini expansions:

- Blind Monkey, suggested by Vlaada Chvátil.
Cover one of your tiles to avoid giving info about your tiles.
(and make it a bit harder to you)

- Interferences by Toni Serradesanferm.
Interferences are making the test more difficult, they interfere with the available tiles so you never know all of them for sure.

Available at Essen 2011.

Here are the official rules I received by mail from the publisher:

THE BLIND MONKEY
SETUP
Each player takes 1 “BLIND MONKEY” tile and places it in front of him, beside the “Hand replenish” tile.
PLAYING THE GAME
At any time, the players can place their “BLIND MONKEY” on any purchased tile (they cannot place it on the “Discard Hand” tile), so the “BLIND MONKEY” entirely covers it.

The covered tile by the “BLIND MONKEY”:
-Cannot be turned to take 3 more cards
-Cannot be checked.
SCORING PHASE
Before Pass 1, players with the “BLIND MONKEY” not over a purchased tile, place it aside as they do with the wrong combos.
Pass 1.
The players with the “BLIND MONKEY” over a purchased tile, show it to verify if there are repeated tiles.
If the tile under the “BLIND MONKEY” is repeated, the “BLIND MONKEY” is place aside with that tile.
If it’s not a repeated combo, leave the “BLIND MONKEY” on the tile cards, so the tile can be clearly seen.
Pass 3.
If the smartest monkey verifies the tile cards where there’s the “BLIND MONKEY” and finds that they are wrong, he places aside the “BLIND MONKEY” along with the wrong tile.
Pass 4.
If the “BLIND MONKEY” has been placed aside, it counts as -2 victory points as the other wrong tiles.

INTERFERENCES
SETUP
Once there are the combo stacks at the centre of the table, each player takes 1 ”INTERFERENCE” tile and put it above one of these stacks.
During setup, an ”INTERFERENCE” cannot be put above another ”INTERFERENCE”.
PLAYING THE GAME
At any time, the players can remove an ”INTERFERENCE” from above a combo stack and place it above another one. In this way, they can show the combos (on a starting stack or on a single tile that a player set apart during game).
During the game, an ”INTERFERENCE” can be put above another ”INTERFERENCE”.
If only 1 stack remains at the centre of the table (this one with all the ”INTERFERENCE” tiles on it), the players return all ”INTERFERENCE” to the box and the games continues.
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
From the Rulebook:

The Space Dwarf Defenders army book requires the Star Confrontations rules book in order to play.  The Space Dwarves are brave warriors that are always looking for a good fight.  These master engineers build powerful and deadly tanks, making them one of the most mechanized forces in the universe.

The Bible contains prophecies about the final days, describing how the armies of the World will fight the final battle and that without divine intervention, no life shall be saved (Matthew 24: 22). Certain Biblical scholars have described how closely these prophecies correlate to our modern times and put forward scenarios for this climactic battle.

The Battle of Armageddon is a 2 to 6 player game dealing with this final battle. The game brings together the forces of the World into the ultimate battle for control of the Earth.

The 17 x 33" game map represents the battleground, stretching from Egypt to Iran. The 216 5/8" counters (die-cut and back-printed) represent the armies of Israel, the Arabs, Magog (Russia), the Revived Roman Empire (Europe), USA and the Kings of the East (China). The 144 playing cards capture apocalyptic events (7 seals, 7 trumpets, 7 vials), special powers (e.g. the Anitchrist, Gog of Magog, etc.), nuclear weapons (tactical, theatre, and strategic) and homeland populations.  Battle dice are used to resolve all battles.

Players maneuver their armies across the map and resolve battles using the battle dice.  Event cards are drawn each turn, giving players special powers or weapons of mass destruction, but they can also bring on apocalyptic events.  Utlimately, the player who best manages the chaos and controls Jerusalem at the end of the game wins!

Note that this version of game The Battle of Armageddon is a significant departure from the original game published in 1999. In line with other main stream games, The Battle of Armageddon provides a simple but challenging game system suitable for both seasoned and casual players. Players are forced to adjust their strategy depending on rapidly changing conditions as dictated by the event cards. Combat is bloody, yet managed quickly and decisively through the battle dice. Players must manipulate allies and opponents to achieve the goal of capturing the Holy Land.
