description:
Derby Bingo is a combination of Bingo with a betting dice game. There are red wooden discs to cover the spaces on the bingo card and square paper chits (yellow/plain) with which to bet.

The 4x4 Bingo Cards represent a player's horse.  Betting on your horse is part of the game.  You can bet to WIN, PLACE, or SHOW at the top of the card.

There are two die. One die is the conventional d6 die with 1 to 6 spots. The other die is a special one with letters R, A, C, E, P, and T.

One your turn, roll the two die. When rolled, the letter die gives the column (R, A, C or E) on the bingo card and the number die the space. If you have a match, then you cover up that spot on your card. You finish by filling a bingo line.

The "POT" is started by players paying chits for their Bingo card. Also if a player rolls a P the player must pay into the POT.  Players rolling a T take money from the POT.

If more than three are playing then the game ends when first, second and third places have been decided and all bets are paid off. The "POT" is shared by the players owning the winning horses.

Spin-Tac-Toe combines Tic-Tac-Toe with a bit of luck. Instead of being able to mark a space directly you must use a spinner to decide if you can mark that square in the 3x3 field or not.

Each square has a half of the spinner circle colored red for "O" and blue for "X". On your turn, if you spin your color you can mark the square. If not, you can't.

The winner is the first to make a line of three. As in regular Tic-Tac-Toe it is possible for a tie.

This is a simple game where a wooden marble is "shot" for points. 300 points is suggested for a game, but any other score agreed upon by the players can be used.

The "marble" is placed on a "shooter" which is a disc with an indentation to hold the ball. The shooter is then flicked with a finger into the "garden wall" which causes the marble to jump the fence and roll around the other side of the board. There are holes in the board marked with various point values.

This installment in the "Ratz-Fatz" series revolves around the horse farm. Like the other Ratz Fatz games, this is a small collection of several educational games for children (from ages 3-12). Game play revolves about recognizing the different gaits, equipment like saddle, bridle and reins,  and different types of horse food on the cards and making connections between them as fast as possible, another version of the game is a fairly straightforward memory game; other game ideas include storytelling elements, riddles, poems and rhymes.

Components: 3 wooden horses, 1 wooden donkey, 12 cards, 1 colour die, rules

A construction-site themed educational children's game in the RatzFatz series.

The game consists of 16 picture tiles, three wooden pieces (excavator, road compactor, concrete mixing transport truck), a color die and a booklet with several game ideas for kids of various ages. The picture tiles show different tools (pincers, hammer, shovel, drill, ladder), materials (cement, bricks, stone, sand), pieces of clothing (helmet, boiler suit, glove, protective vest, shoe, tool belt) and screws.
The basic game idea is that an adult reads or tells a story or a poem, and the children have to grab tiles when they hear the names of the items on those tiles. The player with most tiles is the winner, but it is also playable with just one kid.

Other ideas presented in the booklet include memory games, sorting activities (sort tiles into tools, clothing, construction materials), and telling what an object is used for.

It was announced as a remake of "Panzer Division Kommandeur" from Ad-Technos in 1985.

But, it is not a remake. From different designer, with different component, with different combat system, with different scenarios.
Actually, it is just a different game.

This version covers only the Eastern front in 1944.  This is another important difference.
One player must play a game as a German Panzer Division Kommandeur.  The other player controls the enemy Soviet army leader.

The game uses a random scenario creation system. The map area and each force are determined by dice.  The most important point that the goal of each army is given by randomly drawn chits.  There are 6 mission chits in the game and each player draw 2 of them secretly.  So, each player now know his goal and some information that his opponent might have.  The missions, are breakthrough, conquer, defense and so on.  Each mission gives each player victory points. At the end of the game, victory points are calculated for each player and compared to determine the winner.

components:
one map, one countersheet, one rulebook

In this game, players attempt to collect potatoes of different sizes and types.

A number of cardboard potatoes are placed face-down on the table. The first player may now attempt to retrieve one or more potatoes using his index finger, following certain rules. After his turn ends, new potatoes are added to replace the ones he has taken away, and the next player takes his turn.

The game continues until no potatoes are left. Then, each players collection of potatoes are scored. Points are given for the majority of each type of potatoe, as well as for the number of potatoes.

Translated from the rules:

"Collect your carrots... but be aware of the angry farmer!"    
    
Players roll to advance on their own track. once they reach the crop field they roll another die, which allow them to look at 1 or 2 carrots or use the farmer to chase away another player back to their start field on their track.    
The first player to collect 3 carrots in his own colour wins the game.

De Magische Wereld van Pardoes is based upon a popular character of the Dutch theme park 'Efteling' and derived television series of the same name.

The game features a circular board, just like the previous games that have been released in the same series (Joris en de Draak, Raveleijn). In this case 74 spaces divide the starting square from the finishing square with an emerald.

The game is a typical roll-and-move game, combining some different genre elements. Before the game starts, players have to choose 1 of the 4 game's characters. Two of them (Pardoes, Pardijn) represent the heroes in the story depicted, while the two other characters (Rattar, Pantagor) are the villains in this game.

A basic turn consists of rolling 1D6 and moving your character the according number of spaces towards the finish.

The board contains a number 'white twinkles' and 'green lightnings' (4 of each), which work just like S'nakes and Ladders'. The white twinkles work as ladders for the heroes and as snakes for the villains; the green lightnings work the other way around.

The board also contains two sections with spinning arrows. If your move ends on a square in such a section, you spin that section's arrow and move to the square indicated by the arrow.

Other squares let you take an action card (some are to be executed immediately, others can be kept for later in the game) or invoke a duel between you and another player. Each of the duelling secretly chooses 1 card from a set of 3 (water, fire and wood) and reveal this card simultaneously. Water beats fire, fire burns wood and wood floats on water. The winner of the duel   gets a bonus move.

A duel is also fought when a player lands on a square with another player's piece. In this case the winner of the duel gets to stay on the square where as the loser goes backwards the number of squares indicated by 1D6.

This trivia game was released for the 60th aniversary of the Dutch theme park Efteling in 2012. The game contains 255 questions (most of them are multiple choice) in 5 different categories:

- Fairy tales
- Park's history
- Rides and attractions
- Shows and muic
- Interesting facts and figures

The game comes in tin box with a small game board (to determine the question categories) and score cards/tokens to keep track of  the score. The one wih the most positive scores when one player has a full score card, wins the game.

This game in the Ratz Fatz series of educational games for children makes the players run, hop and jump. Like the other Ratz Fatz games, this is a collection of games for kids from 3-12 years that can be played with the same material.

Components: 20 picture tiles (for use on the floor), 20 bracelets, 1 booklet with rules, stories, poems and tasks for different ages

The picture tiles show vehicles (bike, car, bus, train), furniture (chair, table, bed, closet), animals (dog, cat, horse, cow), toys (doll, ball, building blocks, picture books) and food (apple, bred, salad, pasta).

Games that can be played include:

1. Naming and grouping: The children say what's on the tiles, which pictures might go together, or they try to find something that fits a certain description (something made from wood, something that has four legs etc.)

2. Listen, run and win: The tiles are distributed on the floor. When the game master mentions one of the pictures while reading a story from the game booklet, they have to run to that tile as fast as possible. The player who is the first to stand on the correct tile earns a bracelet, and the player with the most bracelets wins the game. A variant includes jumping from tile to tile instead of running.

3. Running memory: Similar to 2., but the tiles are played face down and the children have to remember which tile is where.

4. Throw the tile: The players have to find something to match a description, but they only earn a bracelet if they manage to throw a fitting tile into the bottom of the box.

An educational game for children, focused on learning the numbers from 0-10. Like the other games in the Ratz Fatz series, this is a collection of games that can be played with the same material.

Central idea
A story or poem is being read, the kids listen, guess, rhyme and grab an object.

Components
10 wooden animals, 11 cards (showing a number one one side and the corresponding number of animals on the other side), one booklet with rules, stories, riddles and poems

Games that can be played:

1. Group and name: The players name the animals on the card and count them. Players are given different tasks (find a pet, all pets, a wild animal, the smallest, a black one, ...)

2. Sort by number: Cards are placed before/after another card according to the number of animals that are shown on it. The game can also be played with the number side.

3. Grab the animal: The animals or cards mentioned in one of the texts from the booklet have to be touched as quick as possible.

4. Memory game: Only six animals and/or cards are laid out on the table, the others stay in the box. When a number or animal is mentioned that is on the table, nothing happens. If one that is inside the box is mentioned, the players shout that word to gain one of the pieces from the table.

Like the other games in the Ratz Fatz series, this is a collection of games that can be played with the same material. This particular game focuses on fairytales.

Central idea: A story or poem is being read, the kids listen, guess, rhyme and grab an object.

Components: 3 wooden fairy tale figurines (frog prince, dwarf, puss in boots), 12 cards (showing objects from fairytales, like an enchanted mirror, a witch's broom, gingerbread house etc.), one symbol die, one booklet with rules, stories, riddles and poems

Games that can be played:

1. Group and name: The players name the figurines and things on the cards and explain what fairytales they are from, or who else is part of that fairytale. They also look for objects that fit a certain criterion (something valuable, something in which one can live...)

2. Grab it: The figurines or cards mentioned in one of the texts from the booklet have to be touched as quick as possible.

3. Memory game: Only six figurines and/or cards are laid out on the table, the others stay in the box. When a character or object is mentioned that is on the table, nothing happens. If one that is inside the box is mentioned, the players shout that word to gain one of the pieces from the table.

4. Memory and storytelling game: The players use the cards and figurines to tell a story. 4 cards are used for the first figurine, the next 4 for the second, and the last 4 for the third. Then the symbol die is rolled. The symbol decides which of the three stories the players have to re-tell.

5. What can you do with this: One player secretly decides on one of the cards. He/she then says what can be done with that object (e.g. "You can use it to make a rabbit appear from a hat" when they have chosen the magic wand). The other players try to guess the object. The first player to touch the correct card wins that card.

"Spies & Alibis is a Co-operative Mystery Game.

We are a team of Special Unit Detectives working together to discover which of the Suspects has taken the Government’s Top Secrets. We have to do this before the Spies board the Airplane and get away."
(from the game box)

Tiles are randomly selected and turned over by players to reveal "eye witness cards", each of which shows an enlarged part of a suspect's face.  The players must reveal enough tiles to properly rules out suspects and thereby deduce identity of the spies before the spies depart on the plane.  Each time a tile is turned over, there is a chance that it will be a "time flies card" which hastens the planes departure.  The game consists primarily of attempting to reveal as many eye witness cards as possible before revealing all 10 of the time flies cards, and then guessing as to the spies' identities.

A Card Game that brings competition that requires great speed and reflexes to win. The pictures cards are over the table and the players must be quick to find the same object in your hand. The quickest take the card, whoever take most cards is the winner.

There are two types of cards, objects cards and pictures cards and there are two play modes:
1- Play with all pictures cards face up. Reveal an object card. All players must collect all pictures cards that have the object on it, keeping that picture card. The player with most picture cards wins at the end.
2- Distribute evenly all picture cards among the players. Reveal an object card. The first player to show a picture card with a matching object win the object card. The player with most object cards wins the game.

Objective: Be the first moving up to field 132
- moving by turn -
throw the die and move according rolled number; ending on a red marked space follow instructions. Use 3 dice from out field '127';
remarks:
published ~ 1940's

The Quitting Game is a multiplayer take on the Prisoner Dilemma that challenges players to quit the game at the right time.

Players start with 30 points.  As the game goes on, these points decrease by a random amount (1-6, based on a six-sided die roll).  Each turn, players decide whether or not they will quit the game or stay in.  After each turn, the points decrease.  If a player quits they are out of the game (or round, if there are multiple rounds), and score zero points for the round, unless all players remaining quit together.  At this point, players evenly divide up the number of remaining points, times the number of players in the game.

Goal of the game is to score the most points.

Catholic Scattergories contains sixteen additional categories based on the Catholic faith!

How many Bible categories can you match before time runs out? Roll the letter die to determine the key letter for that round. You roll a "C". Now start the timer! How quickly can you think of a "Place Christ walked?..." Canaan!... "Things that separate us from each other?..." Carelessness!... Can't think of an answer? No problem. Just write down any answer that fits the category and you still score points if your answers are different from every other player. This game is entertaining, enhances faith and promotes learning.

Catholic edition and Bible edition use the same box. Catholic edition has a different barcode label attached.

boardgame: Rally Round the King:  Armies & Campaigns 3000 BC to 1500 AD
description:
With Rally Round the King, you can fight large fantasy battles by themselves or wrapped in an easy-to-use Campaign System. But what if you want to fight Roman Legions against Germanic invaders? Or Norman Knights versus Saxon Huscarls?
What about a good historical set of rules? Look no further!

Just drop the fantasy rules from Rally Round the King, add this supplement, and you have a historical mass-battles game. We've included over 145 historical armies from 3000 BC to 1500 AD, each with a Basic Army, and using Recruiting Rolls to work with the Campaign System from Rally Round the King. No Dragons, Trolls, or Elves, just Egyptian chariots, Roman legionaries, and 100 Years War longbowmen.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Unfortunates of Tozlandia and the Tablet of Doom
description:
You and 14 others have decided to create a new life on the peninsula of Tozlandia. The land is lush and plentiful, however after settling life, a dark, secret tablet is uncovered. After years of deciphering it is revealed that the land of the unfortunates will be ravaged by disasters predicted by the Tablet of Doom and that time when these things will unfold is drawing near. It dawns on you that you and your Tozlandianites are the unfortunates! You quickly re-read the tablet and realize that you only have time to build safe houses to survive the onslaught that’s about to happen. All of the other unfortunates are looking to you to help them get to the safe houses and avoid disaster.

Goal of the Game:
To have more than half of the unfortunates survive the events predicted by the Tablet of Doom. Unfortunates survive by making it to a safe house, however more than half of the unfortunates must make it to win.

Playing The Game:
Turns are broken down into a few parts, first you take your action phase and then you tempt fate and roll two 6-sided dice and do what happens on the Matrix of Doom. Finally you check to see if you have won the game.

Turns continue in the fashion until all unfortunates are in safe houses, all safe houses are destroyed, or no more unfortunates can move.  If you've gotten the required number of unfortunates to safe houses you've won the game, otherwise the game is lost.

This game was designed for the 2012 Solitaire Print and Play Contest placing 3rd in the Category of "Best Small Game" -- 2012 Solitaire Print and Play Contest Results Post

boardgame: Mighty Morphin Power Rangers: Gigantik Game
description:
Calling all Power Rangers! Lord Zedd has launched his assault. Its up to you to protect the planet by capturing Goldar, Squatt, Baboo, King Sphinx and the Putty Patrol.

Players play as their favorite Mighty Morphin Power Rangers on a gigantic plastic floor mat, as they spin the spinner and move around the board following the actions on the spaces where they land to protect the planet from evil Lord Zedd and his Evil Space Aliens!

Objective of the Game:
Be the first Power Ranger to collect four different bad guy disks and at least two Putty Patrol warriors to win the game!

A throwing game from an age of racist Americana.

The game features ten 6-1/2 inch heavy cardboard lithographed figures on wood bases. The figures are made to look like stereotypical african-american humans caricatures of the period. In the game, players roll balls at the figures, and whoever hits the most wins.

Sold as an novelty game, it was first published in 1914.

Objective: play the game several times - each time you win you may wear the sticker 'Nick Knatterton'; player who was wearing this sticker most is the winner;
 - moving by turns -
roll the die and move forward; each space (of 25) shows information to follow (see cover inside); player who reaches field 25 (precisely) wins this game...
remarks:
published during 1960's
The Players have to shoot black soldiers with a sling. The soldiers are dressed similar to valets. Made from Majora.

A ring toss game. Comes in a wooden box that has hinges on the front. The inside of the box has the instructions and more information about the game. The gamepieces are a wooden black man, a stand, a pipe and 4 red and black rubber rings.

The players toss rings and try to hook them on Sambos pipe which sticks out from the standup figure. The instructions are on the back of the figure.

boardgame: Fireball Forward: Panzer Lehr at Saint-Lo
description:
The American Army in Normandy is embroiled in the "Battle for the Hedgerows".  The key to breaking out lies in capturing the vital crossroads town of Saint-Lo.  The US 9th and 30th Divisions have created a bridgehead over the Vire river, the last major obstacle before the town, and are pushing on.  The Germans are determined to stop the drive and move the elite Panzer Lehr Division in position to launch a violent counterattack to crush the bridgehead.

In the dark night of July 10th, Panzer Grenadiers, Panthers and Mk. IVs move out to hit the unsuspecting dogfaces.

Follow the action in 15 action-packed historical scenarios!

SS Engineers Battlion Mini-Campaign: Three scenarios featuring elite SS assaulting GI using flamethrowers.

Panther Mini-Campaign:  Nine scenarios that follows a company of ten Panthers into the US rear areas battling M-10 Tank Destroyers.

Mk. IV Mini-Campaign:  Three scenarios highlighting the US counterattack on the morning of July 11. Hedgerow busting Sherman's battle Mk. IVs.

From the box:

"For the school room or home by Mrs. F. L. Crown"

"A game in which fifty-six music symbols are quickly and easily learned and the habit of concentration, alertness and fair play is encouraged among boys and girls.

Music supervisors and music teachers will find 'Note-O' invaluable in the teaching of the names and definitions of music symbols.

Rules for playing 'Note-O':

The game of 'Note-O' contains forty players' cards, fifty-six dealer's cards and pasteboard discs with which to cover the symbols on the players' cards.

As many players may participate in the game of 'Note-O' as there are players' cards.  Each player is provided with a 'Note-O' card and enough discs to cover all the symbols on his card.

At the beginning of each game, a leader is chosen.  This leader is provided with the deck of dealer's cards and from it calls the symbols, one by one.  As a symbol is called, the player having that symbol covers it with a disc.  The process is repeated until some player has covered all the symbols on his card at which time he calls out 'NOTE-O.'  The first player calling 'NOTE-O', whose symbols have been covered correctly, wins the game.

The game may be varied by having any player announce 'Note-O' when he has covered three symbols in a row on his card.

At the end of each game, cards should be exchanged and the leader's cards shuffled before starting a new game.

The game is made much more difficult and more interesting when each player is provided with two or more 'Note-O' cards."

From the box:

"Experience the fun of buying, selling, and trading equipment. Invest into audio Industries to increase your earnings. Follow the latest trends, and exchange insider gossip while you compete to build the HI-FI System of your dreams."

User summary:

Very similar to Monopoly, but in place of real estate, players move around the board (rolling dice to do so) and choosing to invest or not in whatever aspect of the hi-fi audio industry (circa 1970's) they land on, and paying royalties or fees to those already owned by other players, until all players but one are bankrupt.

Reguły Gry is fast, simple, card game licensed television station TVN7. It is based on the mechanism of exchange of cards and memory. Reguły Gry is also a Polish television series production of all-star cast (Julia Kaminska, Maciej Zakościelny, Paweł Wilczak, Katarzyna Kwiatkowska and Jan Jankowski).

The goal is to get 5 tokens with images of the main characters from Reguły Gry TV series. In turn, each player plays in front of him one of the four cards and must remember the name of this card. Then each player gives their cards to the left neighbor ang gains cards from the right neighbor (after that each player refills his hand up to 4 cards from the deck). Once the face-down in the stack to make the cards a player collects 4 cards with the image of the same characters from the show, to announce it. If he was right - achieved the first token. First collect five tokens wins the game.


Reguły Gry to szybka, nieskomplikowana, karciana gra imprezowa na licencji stacji telewizyjnej TVN7. Oparta jest na mechanizmie wymiany kart  i zapamiętywaniu. Reguły Gry to także polski serial produkcji telewizji TVN z gwiazdorską obsadą (Julia Kamińska, Maciej Zakościelny, Paweł Wilczak, Katarzyna Kwiatkowska oraz Jan Jankowski).

Celem gry jest zdobycie 5 żetonów z wizerunkami głównych bohaterów serialu. W turze każdy z graczy zagrywa przed siebie jedną z 4 kart i powinien zapamiętać jaka to karta. Następnie podaje swoje karty sąsiadowi i otrzymuje nowe od drugiego z sąsiadów (dobiera do nich jedną kartę z talii). Gdy tylko w zakrytym stosie do którego dokłada karty, któryś z graczy zbierze 4 karty z wizerunkiem tej samej postaci z serialu, ogłasza to. Jeśli nie pomylił się - uzyskuje pierwszy żeton. Kto pierwszy zbierze 5 żetonów wygrywa grę.

boardgame: The Game of Ten Little N*****rs
description:
NOTE: This game is racist in both name and presentation but is included here for historical interest.

A variation of Old Maid that features Black characters exclusively. The deck contains a pair of each of the ten "Little Niggers" plus one oddball to get "stuck" with.

boardgame: Strike Legion: Combined Arms Combat – Main Rulebook
description:
Strike Legion is a SciFi tabletop miniatures game dedicated to ground combat in the near and far future. The game uses a unique Electronic Warfare system that allows players to alter their units' attack and defense ratings each game turn.

The main rulebook is divided into Basic, Advanced, and optional rules, and teaches the rules through the use of 'programmed instruction' scenarios, wherein players learn to apply new rules through playing these scenarios.

A section dedicated to creating units is included, allowing players to create any troop or vehicle types form their favorite science fiction or alternate history backgrounds.

Combat is scaled at 1 piece = 1 tank, flier, gun, or infantry team; 1" on the tabletop = 250 meters. Miniatures from any manufacturer can be used, and the game is supporteed by dozens of army lists available for free download from the publisher's website.

Recommended miniatures scales range from 6mm to 15mm; the game can be played with smaller (2-3mm) scales, but is not recommended for figures larger than 15mm.

Rules and support for solitaire play are included in the Strike Legion: Skunk Works rules module.

Kuddelmuddel - wie bei Hempels unterm Sofa
(german pun, means roughly translated chaos in the living room)

From the box back: Chaos is nice, chaos is fun. At least family Hempel thinks that. But who looks for something in the mess sweats...The colour die and the sample card show, which kind of thing has to be found in this mess. The players look up their cards quickly to find what is missing and who finds the most gets many things from the living room to his/her victory.

Contents: rulebook, 12 sample cards, 4 * 12 picture cards, colour die.

Gameplay: The cards are sorted into piles. 1 pile = sample cards. Then 4 piles which different card backs picture cards. Each child picks one picture card pile, means they have 12 cards now. Each sample card shows 6 pictures with a different coloured frame (example: boat, book, cup, telephone, plane, whale). The die is rolled. The colour rolled is the picture or the thing that the people are looking for. The first child playing his/her card with matching picture wins.

From the box:

"NOISES IN THE DARK
or Touching Snap

Test your Fifth Sense--Touch--in this game of mirth and laughter.  Switch off the lights--'read' by touch the word embossed on the card--then imitate the noise.

SIX SETS OF TWELVE NOISES

A Eupax Game -- Copyright by Wm. Sessions Ltd.
The Ebor Press, York"

Each round a judge is declared. The judge then picks out one noise from the box. He gives an identical copy of the noise to each player. As the lights are out, the players cannot read the card. However, each card has the word written in raised bumps like braille. It is then a race to figure out the word and make that sound. Whomever makes the correct sound first wins the round. Additionally, the judge can pass out 2-3 words. Then the players must make all those sounds to win the round.

From the box:

"Code 3 Response! is the action oriented, strategy EMS trivia board game where you respond your ambulance along city streets to emergency scenes and transport your patients in a race to the hospital."

User Summary:

Game board is an overhead likeness of a city, with buildings, streets, hazards, and a hospital.  Players take turns moving their pieces according to rolling of die, and movement depends on negotiating map, hazards, and answering emergency procedure questions correctly.  Winner is the first to successfully negotiate all of this on the way to the hospital.  The game is intended as an educational aid for emergency training.

boardgame: Felix the Cat's Complete 3 Game Set for Boys & Girls
description:
User Summary:

Contains 3 games for children--Felix's Fishing Game, Felix's Road Race Game, Down on the Farm Game.

Each game is a race to reach the end of the board while overcoming obstacles.  Player movements are determined by a spinner.

A Throwing game In which Players try to hit black humans with colored coins. Published in France.

The three figures are attached to the cardboard of the box. There are three cardboard disks that are used to throw at the figures. Each one of the figures has a minor slight crease in the neck area.

From the game box : Get the game ready by placing the subjects upright throw the quoits while standing at a distance od 10 feet from them. Each in turn, the players may get threee shots at the black persons and the game is played in 10 Times The player adds his points and puts the fallen pices right again for the following competitor.

This expansion put the "Airborne" in "Airborne In My Pocket"!

It allows the players to play a mini-game, before the regular game, to simulate the parachute jump to the Drop Zone. All players have a set of Events and place their figuring on the special "Drop" Location. They start on the plane, then must decide when to jump, and how long their wish to stay airborne before landing. Of course, they must be careful not to miss the Drop Zone!

Contains 10 “Parachute Event” cards , 1 Special Operative (The Jumpmaster), and 1 big location (The Drop).

This expansion allows you to reach the Drop Zone by landing on the beach with the rest of the boys.

It allows the players to play a mini-game, before the regular game, to simulate the beach landings. All players have a set of Events and place their figurine on the special "Beach" Location. They start when their feet touch the cold water, then they must decide when to advance to the bluffs and reach the (relative) safety of the Drop Zone. All this time, they will be exposed to enemy fire and shelling. Who said landing was going to be easy?

Contains 10 “Beach Event” cards, 1 Special Operative (The Hero), and 1 big location (The Beach).

Taken from the description of the box

A new and very comical game for two or three people. Metal Figures of darkies with moveable arms, racing along three poles. Their comical actions are very funny. All start at the same time and it is exceedingly amusing to see the race.

This game from from Milton Bradley dates back to 1890 and features a lithograph map of an around the world race for two players. Each player in turn spins the spinner and moves the indicated number of spaces paying penalties for landing on an opponents playing piece. First player to circumnavigate the globe wins the game. There are safe areas where no penalty is called for if your opponent lands on your square.
A throwing game from 1925. Inside the box ten pins with minstrel faces and exotic costumes are to be found. The person who hits the most black persons, wins.

boardgame: Wall Street: The World of Financial Wizardry
description:
The fourth Jerry Lewis-endorsed, big box (26" square) Hasbro game. From the same series that brought us High Stakes, Power Broker, and Spellbound.

Players compete to be the first to either collect $100,000 or be the only remaining player that has not claimed bankruptcy.

Players take turns rolling the dice and moving onto board spaces (called "blocks"). Certain blocks permit players to buy and sell seven different stocks. each stock type pays different dividends. Payment can be affected by Bull or Bear cards as well as Insider Informatin cards.

A anti-semitic variant of Monopoly, self-published in 1997 in Germany by the neo-Nazis Beate Zschäpe, Uwe Mundlos and Uwe Böhnhardt, who later formed the underground terrorist group "NSU". Several copies were discovered in Zschäpe's garage in the course of a razzia against neo-Nazi activists in the city of Jena on January 28, 1998. According to newspaper articles referring to an official secret service report, it was sold by André Kapke to fellow neo-Nazis as a solidary fundraising for 100 German Marks after the trio's disappearance into illegality. Kapke, however, diverted those funds. In the framework of the NSU trial, the game is used by the prosecution as proof for Zschäpe's political committment and her alleged active involvement in the murder of 9 immigrants and 1 police officer, as well as other capital crimes supposedly carried out by the NSU.

The aim of the game is to "clean" cities (the "streets" in Monopoly) of Jews. Instead of 4 railroads, there are 4 different concentration camps. There are fields for SS and SA (chance and community chest), gasworks (waterworks), and a field depicting Hitler. A couple of dozens of copies are said to exist.

The sun rises on the solstice, and the tribes gather to the ancient battleground. Today, among these hallowed stones soaked with the blood of battles past, it shall be determined who shall rule the land in the year to come.
This is not the time for peace: it is the time for
RAGE!!!!!

A simple tactics game in which you control a group of barbarians, trying to wipe out all opposing barbarians on a battleground littered with druidic stones that can help or harm you. The main mechanic is the eponymous RAGE, which rises with the amount of damage your barbarians take, and makes them more powerful: of course if they take too much damage they still die.

Each barbarian is represented by a custom wood die, with a geomorphic cloth board styled after the designs of Elliot C. Evans.

boardgame: Ogre: BGG Exclusive Overlays Sponsored Counter Sheets
description:
This Ogre terrain promo pack is a result of the Ogre Kickstarter project, where BoardGameGeek pledged $9,000 for the creation and distribution of two sets of promos, one of which is this terrain promo (the other promo set is a set of 3D units!).  Many ideas came from BoardGameGeek users, many others were devised by Steve Jackson, and all were finalized by Steve.

The result is a scenario designer's dream come true, as there is terrain in this set (the log-filled water, for instance) that is not in the rules but is left for the scenario designer.  There are also many things that aren't standard Ogre canon.  Various pieces are intended to work together.  Here are some ideas that Steve Jackson suggests, for example:


        - Dams, broken dams, downstream log-jams, and flooded towns are all designed to support destroy-the-dam scenarios.



        - BGGsaurus from the 3-D sheet, and the terrain he has stomped through. Is it merely damaged, or has he left it radioactive or worse? And does he have some connection with the flying saucer?



        - The big comm tower can just be a target, or it can be related to the flying saucer. Does it call the saucer? Does the saucer BUILD it? Maybe it's not actually a comm tower - those dishes could be super lasers!



        - For that matter, is the giant glowing crater an exploded reactor, or is it what happens when the flying saucer takes too much damage?


Sheet 1 contains (front/back):


        - Giant flying saucer / Giant CRASHED flying saucer



        - Missile base (center control building, nine launch silos) / Shot-up missile base



        - Flooded city (white buildings in blue water) / City devastated by BGGsaurus (footprints)



        - Repair facility (road going through buildings, robot arms, and so on) / Wrecked version of repair facility



        - Swamp shape with multiple streams (this would drastically slow anything but infantry) / Flopped on reverse



        - Town hex completely surrounded by forest / Humongous crater



        - Bank of radar dishes / Shot-up radars.


Sheet 2 contains (front/back):


        - Two dams, one with a road over it and one not / Each has a broken version on the other side



        - Airfield with road passing near / Shot-up airfield



        - Coastal shape with forest / Coastal shape with burning rubble.



        - Drowned city / Stomped city



        - Three different-shaped overlays of flooded terrain choked with debris and logs (to use downstream from a broken dam) / Clear terrain with BGGsaurus footprints



        - Humongous crater, but old and with plants regrowing on the inside / Humongous crater, full of water. The terrain inside the crater is NOT defined in the regular rules - it looks like shallow water filled with junk, but make of it what you will. Steve says he would not swim in it.


Both sheets also have some "dogleg" shapes for changing streams and ridges.

Cracoviana takes you for the walk throgh Cracow old streets, where you find traces of historical events, rulers and artists. We learn about the city, its climate, history and tradition. Great way to make you children and grandchildren learn about the city, while having great time

Game goal is to gather as many "Wall Blanks" as possible in shortest time. Whoever achieves this and reaches Wawel, wins.
Players move through fields that symbolize streets of Old Town.

On street crossings there are questions from 10 sets - Legends, Artists, Tratition, Monuments, Events and so on, whic you must answer in order to get "City Blanks".

Two player Deduction game.
Both player try to guess the position of a criminal, by asking their opponent Yes/No-Questions and deducing the taken escape route through Germany. 
Part of the small "Black Criminal Card game" Series.

In the first part of the game the players lay out the route that one criminal took: For that he always combines citys with transportations (car, train, bus, plane, boat) that he uses for his getaway. 4 days of travelling is layed-out in this manner.
In the secon part the players take turns to ask Yes/no-Questions to find out the route taken. The players can ask anything about the route taken, but not about the final destination. So they can ask "Did you use the plane in those five days?" or "Did you pass through Austria on wendesday or thursday?" but not "Did you end up in Vienna?"
Once a player knows where his opponent was on each of those 4 days he can make a guess to win the game. If he guesses wrong he looses.

Murder! A body has been found in the idyllic woods of the Allgäu, one of Germany's most popular holiday areas. Side by side with commissioner Kluftinger (famous from a series of German crime novels), the players in Jagdrevier start their investigations to reveal the murderer. Each turn, the gamemaster places hints and clues in different places located in the Allgäu (e.g., Schloss Neuschwanstein). Each player travels from place to place, putting the clues together to deduce the murderer and his or her motive – but time is running and not every clue will contribute to the solution. Only the most quick-witted player can win this game!

boardgame: Babar und die Abenteuer von Badou: Das Kartenspiel
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Badou, the grandson of the elephant king Babar, loves to go on adventures with his friends – but it's time to leave on the next outing and he can't find them! In Babar und die Abenteuer von Badou: Das Kartenspiel, the players need to help Badou find his missing friends so that they can take off!

boardgame: Babar und die Abenteuer von Badou: Das spannende Suchspiel
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Badou, Babar, and their friends want to use colored chalk to bring children into their jungle school – but alas, their friends have taken off running down the wrong roads. Can the players go after this motley army of youngsters and help them find the right path?

boardgame: 宝探しアドベンチャーTORE!魔宮攻略GAME (Treasure Hunt Adventure TORE! Mamiya Kōryaku Game)
description:
Based on the popular Japanese game show, TORE! is a game where 2 to 4 players compete against each other in challenges and quizzes to try to earn the most golden Pharaoh Statues and win the game.  The expansive board is built up as a three-dimensional play space representing the various chambers of the cursed temple from the TV show.

In this board game version, the players move together through five of the popular rooms of the TORE! Temple.  First they try to avoid being eaten by the stone monster in the "Stone Idol Chamber" then they must race to avoid being crushed by the closing walls in the "Wall Chamber".  After that, their task is to not get wrapped up in the "Mummy Chamber" before next trying to escape from the "Floor Chamber" where the ground slowly rises until the hapless player slides off to the their doom.  Finally, in the "Cliff Chamber", the players must use their wits as they are quizzed one-by-one while portions of the walls begin to protrude, threatening to push them over into the bottomless abyss.

Interspersed between these five rooms are additional challenges including quizzes, gambling to win more Golden Pharaohs, and even the ultra-popular "Spring of the Goddess" metal-ball challenge of trying to control and drop a ball into a goal using two ramp-like bars.

TORE! is a game of both chance and skill as your brain and ability to stay cool under pressure will help you as much as a lucky die roll. All the while the game maintains the same light-hearted humor from the TV show.

From the game rules (translated):
This game is a three-dimensional board game of trying to find the "Golden Pharaoh Statues" hidden in the cursed temple! Try to escape from the Pharaoh's many challenges while collecting more Pharaoh Statues than your opponents!

The seventh scenario pack produced by Le Franc Tireur.

The ten scenarios included are:

- The Road to Juniville-   French vs. German.   La Neuville, France                      10 June 1940
- Getting your bell rung-  British vs. German.  Sanfatucchio Italy,                      21 June 1944
- Clearing the LZ-         American vs. German. Grosbeak Heights, Nijmegen Holland.      18 September 1944
- A misstep in Lorraine-   American vs. German  Near Vaudrecourt, France.                20 September 1944
- Green berets-            British vs. German.  Flushing Walcheren Island, Holland,      1 November 1944
- Dover bunker-            British vs German.   Flushing Walcheren Island, Holland,      2 November 1944
- Inter-allied attack-     British vs German.   Domburg, Walcheren Island, Holland       3 November 1944
- Bloodier than D-Day-     Allies vs German.    Vrouwenpolder, Walcheren Island, Holland 7 November 1944
- Niederburg Farmhouse-    American vs German.  Near Bettendorf, Luxembourg              18 December 1944
- Landstorm over Arnhem-   British vs German.   Near Arnhem Holland.                     14 April 1945

All are ETO actions. Scenario length estimates range from 1 hour 45mins to 5 hours.

Game description from the publisher:

To celebrate the 10th anniversary of the best-selling BANG!, dV Giochi will present this limited edition version. BANG! 10th Anniversary is packaged in a metal box and includes all the BANG! playing cards, plus a selection of popular characters – the "Most Wanted", if you will – from all the released expansions of BANG!. In addition, the game will feature deluxe components such as wooden bullets and maxi-sized player boards. To top it all off, this edition will include three previously unpublished characters and a brand new player board!

"The Outlaws hunt the Sheriff. The Sheriff hunts the Outlaws. The Renegade plots secretly, ready to take one side or the other. Bullets fly. Who among the gunmen is a Deputy, ready to sacrifice himself for the Sheriff? And who is a merciless Outlaw, willing to kill him? If you want to find out, just draw (your cards)!" (From back of box)

This card game recreates an old-fashioned spaghetti western shoot-out, with each player randomly receiving a Character card to determine special abilities, and a secret Role card to determine their goal.

Four different Roles are available, each with a unique victory condition:
Sheriff - Kill all Outlaws and the Renegade
Deputy - Protect the Sheriff and kill any Outlaws
Outlaw - Kill the Sheriff
Renegade - Be the last person standing
A player's Role is kept secret, except for the Sheriff. Character cards are placed face-up on table, and also track strength (hand limit) in addition to special ability.

There are 22 different types of cards in the draw deck. Most common are the BANG! cards, which let you shoot at another player, assuming the target is within "range" of your current gun. The target player can play a "MISSED!" card to dodge the shot. Other cards can provide temporary boosts while in play (for example, different guns to improve your firing range) and special one-time effects to help you or hinder your opponents (such as Beer to restore health, or Barrels to hide behind during a shootout). A horse is useful for keeping your distance from unruly neighbors, while the Winchester can hit a target at range 5. The Gatling is a deadly exception where range doesn't matter - it can only be used once, but targets all other players at the table!

Information on the cards is displayed using language-independent symbols, and 7 summary/reference cards are included.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

boardgame: Banditos: Hippies and an Armored Truck
description:
Go on a joyride through the 1980s. Your destination? Robbing the Banco de Mexico!

Now there are even more ways to make your millions with Banditos: Hippies and an Armored Truck, a 30-card expansion for Banditos that introduces four new characters, new cards for the action card deck, and inside information for the armored truck.

In the board game Console Romano, players are Roman citizens who try to expand their power in the city of Rome, while resources are stored in preparation for war with other countries. When war occurs, if you can supply resources for the military services, you receive honor points.

Console Romano has an action-selection system in which players will sometimes become "magistrate" to propose particular political tactics – i.e., actions – to the other players. Whoever with the most honor points wins.

Collection of 6 boardgames based on the use of magnetic marbles (clickets) as game pieces.

The box contains six games. Each of the games is a combination of the nature of the magnetic marbles (e.g. magnetism) and strategic or random placement of the marbles on the board.

The object is to place or remove those magnetic marbles without moving or disturbing the marbles still occupying holes on the gamebord.

CLACTO HARLEQUIN is a game of dice, not unlike the traditional game, but combined with the magnetic force of the 'clickets' the game gets a somewhat wider perspective. Apart from the placement of the clickets of the board as the throw of the dice indicates, the players have tot take the magnetism in account.

The same principle goes for CLACTO DICE.

CLACTO FISH is about removing clickets from the gameboard without disturbing the other clickets.

CLACTO 4 AND 4, CLACTO TACTIC and CLACTO SNAKES all have the placement of clickets on the board as subject, without moving or disturbing the clickets allready placed.

Game based on the use of magnetic marbles (clickets). The object is to place all the clickets on the games board (in the shape of a snake), without moving them as a result of magnetic attraction.The player who succeeds in placing his last clicket without disturbing the other marbles on the board wins the game.

Fridge is heavy combat brain. It commands other machines, thinks for them, optimizes tactics. Fridge constantly scans battlefield for any changes that could affect Moloch actions.

This set contains 1 Fridge mini, accessories set, random location card, additional game counters and rules booklet.

boardgame: Neuroshima Tactics: The Outpost – Assault Troopers
description:
Assault troopers. Equipped with assault carbines and kevlar body suits are elite among Outpost forces. They are assigned to hardest tasks, as no one else can defend positions as fiercely as they.

Assault troopers set contains 2 troopers, accessories set, random location card, additional game counters and rules booklet.

Punks believe in motto "Leave and let die". They like to party, their lives are intense but short. They act in large groups, so their machine pistols can spit out rain of bullets

Punks set contains 2 punks, accessories set, random location card, additional game counters and rules booklet.

Cokes look like a bear. To get to that point you need a lot of bulls cojones and a lot of coke. He is hard as rock and just as stupid. Andrenaline makes him even more dangerous - when injured he can send whole squads to the grave.

Cokes set contains 1 miniature, accessories set, random location card, additional game counters and rules booklet.

Mesmerite is made for special tasks. As he approaches his enemies he attacks their brainwaves with mental noise, making them see invisible flame walls and feel pain which is not there. In the meantime other Borgo units can safely accomplish their tasks.

Mesmerite set contains 1 miniature, accessories set, random location card, additional game counters and rules booklet.

boardgame: Iron Curtain: Patton's Nightmare – A Panzer Grenadier Scenario Book
description:
Iron Curtain - Patton’s Nightmare is a brand-new book that kicks off a new alternate-history arc for Panzer Grenadier, similar in scope and concept to the Second Great War arc just launched for Second World War at Sea. This will provide a common background for late-1940’s tactical combat with Pershings, JS III’s and Centurions in a world where the Cold War never happened – it turned hot almost right away.

Patton’s Nightmare, the book, has 77 die-cut-and-mounted American counters, with 20 completely new scenarios and a campaign game featuring the exploits of the 2nd “Hell on Wheels” Armored Division as it smashes the Red hordes.

A mathematical card game. The cards feature somewhere interesting in Sunderland, which is in the northeast of England.

Each card has a number between 0 and 9, except for the Double cards. Each player gets 6 cards and must look to see if a pair can be formed. A pair is made up of two numbers that, when multiplied together, make a total that is also on one of the two cards. For instance, you play a 5 and an 8 which makes 40. Thus the number 40 must also be on one of the two cards played. If successful, a pair is formed and can be laid face-up in front of the player. The first player to lay three pairs (6 cards) wins the round.

When a round is one, the other players add up the value of the cards in their hand and that is their score. After 12 rounds, the lowest score is the winner.

There are special rules for the Double Cards which you need to play because if you are caught with them in your hand when a player goes out, you get a nasty negative score.

This is an expanded game (but more subtle and a better design of the cards) that first appeared as Multitu in the 1960's.

A mathematical card game which first appeared in 1964. The cards feature numbers and you must form pairs. A pair consist of a total on one of the two cards that is equalled when the two cards played are multiplied together.

If you use all your cards first, the round ends and the player going out scores nothing but the other players take a negative score based on the cards still in their hands.

To spice up play, there are Problem Cards which are nasty if you get caught with them in your hand when another player goues out. One of them is Mr Whacker, a school teacher who seems to like caning his pupils.

Educational card game published by the British Diabetic Association. It is an attempt to make people think about what they eat, especially for them to know the calorie count of their meals.

In it's basic form, players can just ask each other about a card, for instance: " Find me another card with the same calorie count as X".
But two more interactive games are possible. In 'Shop Shut' (for which you need two copies of the game) each player gets a hand of cards. One player is nominated as the Caller. Everyone plays their cards face up in front of them. The Caller draws a card and if a player has the same card, he shouts "Sold" and turns the card over. This continues until one player turns over his last card and then shouts "Shop Shut". That player scores nothing but the other players score their cards still 'open' by totalling the Carbohydrates on the card (or the Calorie count if decided before the game begins). After a pre-agrred time limit expires, the player with the lowest score wins.

In 'Happy Food Families, it plays exactly the same as a Happy Families/Quartet/Authors game. A set of four cards must all be the same food value as indicated on the card. The player who has the most sets when the cards run out, wins.

Far in the future, war has been relegated to a spectator sport. As humanity has spread throughout the solar system, advances in material sciences have left us wanting for nothing yet craving new forms of entertainment.

3D printers ranging in size from minuscule to mammoth process material known as Nano-Goo (or just “Goo”) into virtually anything a person could need, from food and clothing to massive, space-faring ships.  Using these technologies, a few enterprising individuals capitalized on humanity’s need for entertainment and have brought back to life the ancient custom of gladiatorial combat -- done safely by remotely operating (teleoperating) vastly powerful machines of war known as War Bots.

Large 3D printing structures are dropped onto remote asteroids and other small, celestial bodies. These come with a small complement of devices which begin to process the raw materials of these asteroids into Goo, which these massive printers can use to create War Bots. Printers use a series of programs called .WAR Files, which give them the specifics of War Bots which they can then use Goo to print.

War Bots are printed out of Goo, which is programmed to take a specific form with specific functions. Because they are made of self organizing nanomaterials, War Bots can be modified by simple programs called .UPGrade Files – or upgrades. These upgrades can be applied to War Bots while they are in the field.

Governed by a strict set of rules, and limited by the programs made available to them, contestants manufacture and then fight with these giant machines, for the delight of audiences throughout the Solar System.

Upgrade Wars is a deck building, tactical combat game. Players begin the game with a starter deck of 10 resources and two War Bots (the giant robots that do the actual fighting).

Through the course of the game, they may purchase new programs to deploy as War Bots or to Upgrade existing War Bots that have already been deployed.

The battle field is divided into three zones - the Printer zone (where the magic happens), the Defensive Ring and the Front Line - a zone shared by all players.

Your bots traverse the battlefield under your command in an attempt to reach other player's printers and destroy them.

Complete Victory: The last player with a Printer still capable of creating War Bots wins the game!
Tactical Victory: Each enemy War Bot you destroy is worth 1 Victory Point. Each enemy Printer is worth 5 Victory Points. Once three card piles are depleted, the player with the most Victory Points wins!

Featuring strategic and tactical play - players must decide where and when to spend their resources, which can go to either purchasing new program files or deploying War Bots into the field of battle. At the same time, you must manage your existing War Bots to defend your Printer and attack the enemy.

In Quarriors! Quest of the Qladiator, the third expansion for Quarriors!, you have been selected to compete in the Qladitorial Games! Quadrennially, Empress Quiana calls together the mightiest Quarriors across the land to compete in the great Quolosseum. Will you defeat all other competitors for the greatest Glory?

Quarriors! Quest of the Qladiator adds forty new custom dice powering six new creatures and two new spells, in addition to a new "Lock" mechanism that allows some creatures to have long-lasting game effects.

--description from the publisher:

description:
Game description from the publisher:

Leviathans simulates combat between warships that have taken to the air in an alternate history/steampunk 1910. The king leviathans, battleships, are the largest vessels. Maneuvering in support are light cruisers, destroyers and other vessels. Will you captain your ﬂeet for king and country, expanding your nation's power and becoming legend? Or will you fall from the sky, forgotten? You determine the outcome!

Leviathans: British Fleet Box contains the British battleship, light cruiser, and two destroyer high-quality plastic ship miniatures from the Leviathans Core Box Set, but with four new, unique variant Ship Cards. Players can instantly expand their faction fleet after purchasing the Core Box Set for larger games.

Game description from the publisher:

Leviathans simulates combat between warships that have taken to the air in an alternate history/steampunk 1910. The king leviathans, battleships, are the largest vessels. Maneuvering in support are light cruisers, destroyers and other vessels. Will you captain your ﬂeet for king and country, expanding your nation's power and becoming legend? Or will you fall from the sky, forgotten? You determine the outcome!

Leviathans: French Fleet Box contains the French battleship, light cruiser, and two destroyer high-quality plastic ship miniatures from the Leviathans Core Box Set, but with four new, unique variant Ship Cards. Players can instantly expand their faction fleet after purchasing the Core Box Set for larger games.

After millennia of shifting dominant civilizations, wars and industrialization, Homeworld became a planet that couldn’t sustain its population... Off-world migration was the last hope. When your Homeworld is nearing its demise, it is time to saddle up your rocket ship and explore the neighborhood in nearby space.

PARSEC is a tile placement game of discovery and exploration in outer space for 2 to 4 players. Featuring easy mechanics and a nimble pace, Parsec is a truly teachable game for everyone. It boasts rich, compelling Euro-style competitive gameplay as each player races to improve their positions to garner the most points and technology progress between scoring rounds. Careful placement of drawn tiles featuring sections of planets, nebulae, asteroid fields, and more is half the game, while maneuvering your rocket and carefully assigning its complement of robots for control of these key locations is equally important. Asteroids and a presence on opponent-dominated Planets garner Credits used to purchase additional actions. After the last Planet is completed, a final scoring round takes place and the winner is determined.

This is your chance to save the denizens of Homeworld; explore PARSEC today!

Note: PARSEC DELUXE includes PARSEC and its first two expansions: Moons and Monsters (#1), and Nebulas and Nanotechs (#2).

Game Data:
Complexity: 2.5 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 3 on a 9 scale
Scale: Each player commands a ship full of robots exploring the space around their declining homeworld

boardgame: Bump N Run the Stock Car Racing Board Game
description:
Bump N Run is a roll and move style game played on an oval race track/game board with 3 racing lanes. each player rolls the dice to determine the starting order. Highest roller is 1st, next highest 2nd, and so on up to 8 players. Each player starts the game with 10 fuel tokens, 4 tire tokens,and 4 Bump n Run cards. Then you decided on how many laps to run(5,10,20,etc). 
  
Each player, in turn rolls the dice and moves there car/pawn around the board space by space, lane to lane wherever they choose. Every time you roll the dice it cost you 1 fuel token, every time you change lanes it cost you 1 tire token. when you come up on another car/player you have the choice to race around him, or race to through him by playing a Bump R Run card, which knocks him back to where your turn started.
  
When you get low on fuel or tires you race in to the pits to spin the pit wheel to earn money to buy more fuel, tires, and Bump N Run cards.  You spin the wheel once per turn and stay as many turns as you need to. 
  
Every time you complete a lap of the track/board you collect a lap counter card to keep score.  The first player to complete a pre-determined number of laps is the winner.

In this trivia game, on your turn you will answer your question with 1 to 3 possible answers. The other players then vote using their cards whether the correct answer is #1, #2, #3, or "None of the Above." The active player gets 3-5 points if he's right, more points if he wrote fewer answers. The other players get 2 points if they guess correctly.

If players don't feel they can accurately guess, they can "cheat." They can look at the answer, get only 1 point that round, and must include the correct answer in the three they write on the board.

boardgame: Revolver: By the Gun They Died
description:
Revolver: By the Gun They Died, is a promotional set of cards available at Spiel 2012, that can be used to customise the two different decks in Revolver. Between matches, the owner of a deck can draft new cards in for old, leaving each deck at 62 cards. The cards included in the set are:

Colonel McReady's deck:


    Apache Warrior (5 copies)
    "Set the train ablaze!"
    Pony Express Relay Station
    Ruined Chapel at El Ray
    Herschel Papazian - Pinkerton Sharpshooter
    Sefton Redshaw - Bounty Hunter
    Clear Skies
    Brady Logan
    Elias Hooker
    "Wriggler" Hooley and the Swain Boys


Jack Colty's deck:


    Austin Granby - Railroad Engineer
    "She's carryin' your grandchild!"
    Banditos (5 copies)
    Turkey Joe
    Bullet: Vicious Mutt (alternate bandit)
    Uncouple the Caboose
    Buttermilk – Colty's Mustang
    Robber's Roost (2 copies)
