description:
With Border Bed, instead of having the bed completely stolen by your partner during night, you will play bed space through series of questions and actions. The most successful player will enjoy more bed space - to the detriment of the other - for the following night.

At the beginning, the bed is equally split on the “bed space” tracker. Then, each player moves one after the other on a simple board using a D6. Depending on where the player ends up, he or she will need to either answer a question or perform an action - on a successful answer, he/she will gain 5 cm on the bed (the other losing an equal amount) and will be allow to play again.
A few squares on the board have extra bonuses/maluses beside the basic question or action.

Questions are taken from a trivial pursuit style deck, with 5 categories: sport/entertainement/musik, general knowledge, day-to-day life, erotism/sexuality, and “personal”. The last one typically consist of question asking you to know something about your partner - favorite dish, name of first pet and more personal things. Actions come from an action card deck. They usually involves some personal things - kissing your partner for more than 10s, doing a massage and so on.

The game ends when one of the player arrives at the last position on the board. The loser - player who is going to have less space on the bed - can still decide to perform a few extra actions to regain a bit of space.

6600 Questions in six fields of knowledge are waiting for you to be answered in this special Austrian Edition.
The categories are: general knowledge, Austria, natural sciences, geography, art and history.
The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

Build up a full meal using recipes from all French Regions by answering trivia questions about French gastronomy. The game comes with a full cookbook of the 264 dishes mentioned in the game.

Questions are the main part of the game - they come from a deck of cards, each card corresponding to one region local dish and containing 4 different questions - e.g., what composes a traditional dish, where the name of specialty comes from and so on.

Each player is randomly assigned a french region at the beginning of the game and tries to build a full menu - first course, main dish, cheese, desert, drink and a speciality - preferably with recipes from the region. First player to have a complete menu win the game.

Every time a player answers correctly a question, he/she will be able to either add it to his/her own menu if the dish (written on the question card) was from the same region and that the menu slot is still empty. Otherwise, a token mechanism still allows the player to eventually fill in its menu slots.

Close Quarters is a fast-paced print 'n play boardgame of futuristic man-to-man combat, played on maps created from multiple room tiles. Using easy-to-learn game mechanics, The Prometheus Incident places players in the roles of the defending crewmembers of the Earth Protectorate Ship Prometheus or the troopers of an assaulting rebel faction intent on capturing the vessel for their own purposes, with battles fought within the confines of the key areas of the ship.

Close Quarters: The Prometheus Incident includes:


    Complete game rules for playing Close Quarters, including rules that will be used in future combat modules;
    Over 30 unique room and corridor map tiles, rendered at 300dpi for best printing results. Use them in the provided scenarios, those of your own design, or to spice up the combat layouts of your favorite SciFi RPG.
    Five highly replayable scenarios that allow players to re-create the more intense segments of the action aboard the Prometheus.
    Background fiction describing the basis of the overall conflict and each of the scenarios.
    All of the counters, markers, and reference sheets necessary to play - everything but dice!


Close Quarters can also be played on any RPG-compatible map overprinted with 1" squares, allowing players to use self-created or commercially available maps or deck plans from their collection when playing this game.

Objective: gain 3 victory chips
 - acting by turn -
place meeples on starting position; try to 'climb' goalwards 
using 3 dice in the beginning; choose a target - if your dice show a matching colour => move upon; if you want to do a second/third move, use 2 dice or 1 die respectively.
If you fail you are going to loose all your moves during that turn. Scoring a goal => gain 1 chip
family: Creatures: Die Olchis (designed by Erhard Dietl)

description:
Objective: be the first getting rid of all your cards
 - acting by turn/reacting simultaneously -
all cards are distributed among players; staples are formed & active player unvovers top card:
Person or the ape => each player must shout the correct title simultaneously. Correct - discard your card into the centre of the table; wrong - take all the cards out of the centre;
if the card shows a bag - put your hand as fast as you can into the centre - the very last one has to take all the cards.

More than 1000 questions for Wissens-Spektrum about fairy tales.

The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

More than 1000 questions for Wissens-Spektrum about Motor World.

The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

More than 1000 questions for Wissens-Spektrum about Bible.

The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

More than 1000 questions for Wissens-Spektrum about Olympics.

The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

More than 1000 questions for Wissens-Spektrum about Traveling.

The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

Players compete to grow long extinct plants, while trying to enlist the aid of magical creatures and repel the attacks of terrifying ogres and repulsive urgs.

Each turn players draw cards and either discard unwanted cards or plant crops. At the beginning of the turn all planted crops grow and are worth points at the end of the game. Lodges and mystical helpers can also be played to game changing effect!

More than 1000 questions for Wissens-Spektrum about Rock and Pop Music.

The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

More than 1000 questions for Wissens-Spektrum about Germany.

The first, who has answered three questions of each category and an additional question of one category chosen by the opponents, wins.

"The world of animals" in English
On 32 cards there are 2 questions with three answers each, on the back with information and illustration on the subject. Players receive questions cards, in case of 3 and 4 players also betting cards.

The active player is asked by the quizmaster, if the first answer is right, he is asked the second question. If the answer is again correct, the player receives the card and places it with his betting cards. In case the first answer was wrong the left neighbour of the player can risk an answer and use one of his betting cards. If he answers wrong, he loses he card. When no one has questions cards left, the player with the most betting cards wins.

Updated August 2013 after more playtesting and streamlining.

On another Earth, in another Universe, the Magic Maelstrom sits, surrounded by the frozen northlands warped by the gravity-like pull of the mana.  When the Magic Maelstrom spins, it can destroy all within its reach; only those with inherent immunity can resist its power.
 Fire, ice and darkness dominate the northland seasons; brief glimpses of light intervene to lift the doom and gloom.

Mana is power; mana is all; those who control the mana can control the northlands...

Standard Game: 4 rounds.
Playing Time:  2-3 hours for experienced players, longer for newer players
Number of Players:  2-4, best with 4
Winning: At the end of the agreed number of rounds, the player with the most mana points on the victory track wins.

KEY FEATURES
Alternate actions to limit player downtime - rounds are highly interactive.
Potential use of card colour and/or function to give a player both options and a dilemma.

See the Two-page Rules and Introduction documents below for more details.

Each round consists of two communal actions, then alternate actions until all available options are either used or turn declared complete by all players.

Players use units on a board to control their home region and to take over new areas. Areas can generate resource cards.

Resource cards are used to purchase units, activate areas of their card colour to take actions in or are traded in for mana. Resource cards are generated by a dice roll once per round. At the end of the round, you must trade in any unused resource cards for mana points.

Fate cards have a variety of effects, such as creatures, devastating events and strange occurrences that may help or hinder your cause. They may also be used to activate areas of their card colour to take actions in. Fate cards are drawn at the start of each round and can be retained in your hand until used or, perhaps, stolen by your enemies.

All discarded Fate cards are placed face up and some discarded Fate cards may be affected by those held by other players.

At the end of the game, the winner is decided by adding up a combination of mana points for areas, units and special places, then adding these to the victory track to see has the most mana in total.

St. George's Gauntlet is a "push your luck" dice game. As a knight of the Order of St. George, you must collect gems from a cavern guarded by a gauntlet of dragons. The roll of the dice determines whether you collect gems or get killed by the dragons. Certain rolls will allow you to kill the dragons, if you are willing to take the risk of rolling a special "Dragon Slayer Die". Collecting the last gem in a level of the cavern results in a big bonus, but failure gives your opponents an easier shot! Do you leave the cavern to earn glory, or push forward for greater rewards?

The game can be played two ways: In the Basic Game, all players compete until the sixth  level of the cavern is explored. In the challenging "Video Game" variant, each player begins with a set number of lives and is eliminated when all lives are lost.

boardgame: Pettersson und Findus  Wie Findus zu Pettersson kam
description:
Objective: be the first one gaining 6 cards in front of you
 - acting by turns/drawing simultaneously -
2 x 15 cards (with 6 different images)forming a circle upon the table, visible are only 15; in the centre of the circle 6 cards (numbered 0 - 5) are placed, too.
Active player throws the die; the symbol shown on the die must be combined with a matching no. card (if 3 cards of with that kind of image are visible 00> take no. 3);
the first successful player gets 1 card; wrong => an already achieved card must be dropped.

boardgame: The Road to St. Die: France, October 1944
description:
The Road to St Die is a company/battalion level simulation of those battles in October 1944.

Players control the various German and American forces which took part. The American player must coordinate their three divisions to penetrate the front lines of the Germans and drive towards St. Die. The German player must coordinate their forces to slow the American drive and maintain the integrity of their front line, while preserving their forces and preventing the American from achieving their geographical objectives.

The system is an evolution of the "Korps" system with variable turn order and more tactical differentiation  to deal with late war units and equipment.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Be the first to get from start to goal with 3 game-pieces by rolling dice, hit special areas and do exercises that are drawn from a deck of exercise-cards along the way.

Every player gets 3 pieces in the same colour that are placed on the matching start-fields. The youngest player starts the game, roll a die and move one piece at a time. When you reach an exercise- spot, draw a card an do what it says. Then draw a "grading" card to see the verdict on your "performance" and get a bonus or a set back. Along the route there are also arrows that you want to hit/not hit in order to get to goal faster.

This is a turn-based strategic world domination game with economic/industrial, resource and military planning components.  It is currently a proposed Kickstarter project.

There are three main layers to WW3.  The first is industrial/economic production.  Control the centers of industry, and you earn their production points.  However, such control can be gained either through economic investment OR military adventurism.  To invade or to invest, that is the question!

The second layer is fuel and resource production.  Fuel helps your armies move faster, and your fleets to move MUCH faster.  But use up all your fuel on movement, and you may not have much left over for battle.  Remember that an army fighting without fuel will be struggling for its very survival.

The final layer to game play is, of course, the military dimension.  The basic version of the game will have two types of military units: armies and fleets.  Unlike in Risk, where ridiculous numbers of armies start crowding the board very quickly, WW3 forces you to make tough choices with limited military forces.  The loss of just one critical army at a key moment could prove lethal; every strategic choice in this game is critical.

B-Movie Hero is a push your luck dice and card game with a little bit of strategy thrown in.  
 Your goal, as a lowly B-Movie hero, is to kill as many monsters as you can to survive. This means you will have to barricade yourself in whatever relatively safe place you can find, scavenge around for supplies, and of course, duct tape multiple weapons together to create one massive weapon of destruction that by all means should not work, but does.

How do you accomplish this goal is simple. Each turn a card is flipped over from the Situation Deck. These cards contain global effects that help/hinder your dice rolling, or supply locations for you to clear out and possibly barricade yourself in for added protection.

Once this is done, 3 dice are grabbed at random from a group of 12. The dice contain everything from weapons to barricades to scavenging abilities and killing sprees. The dice are rolled and placed aside for points, barricades, weapons, etc... But if you roll 3 "Bite" symbols before voluntarily ending your turn, you lose any points you might have gained. Play continues until somebody reaches the agreed upon victory points to win the game.

Expands the original game for up to 7 players; includes new equipment cards; includes new dictator cards.

Cool Factors:
Adds three extra players to the base game.
Introduces new dictator chaos including the assassin!
Doubles the equipment over the base game.
Description:
MERC is a strategy game where each player hires and controls a team of mercenaries working co-operatively against an evil dictator.

MERC: The Expansion is an expansion for MERC that allows you to play with up to 6 players plus a dictator. It doubles the amount of available equipment, nearly doubles the size of the map, doubles the encounters, nearly doubles the amount of MERCs, and increases the amount of chaos the dictator can unleash dramatically.

This expansion requires the base game of MERC

Objective: get rid of most 'Olchi'-chips, evenly distributed
 - acting by turns -
a garbage dump is prepared by placing cards = 'Olchi' items and oil drums (fire supplies for the dragon) vertically into board & box; next to the box 11 digging chips have to be placed.
Active player rolls the coloured die and turns the box in a way, looking at the matching colour (=digger) or oil drum; using the 'Olchi' tongs a card has to be drawn: an 'Olchi' item? => drop one of your chips; an oil drum? remove a digging chip;
if the die showed an oil drum symbol the dragon requires supply: now the player should draw an 'oil drum' card instead...
Game ends if all digging chips are gone; in case that a player dropped all his 'Olchi' chips game ends at once.
family: Creatures: Die Olchis (designed by Erhard Dietl)

description:
Children's motorized action game. Part of a series of games entitled T.H.I.N.G.S. or Totally Hilarious Incredibly Neat Games of Skill. This is "Godzilla" or "Kaiju" themed game . The goal is to cover Eggzilla in a plastic egg before he jumps off the base. The timer is started by pushing Eggzilla down, firmly, onto the base. The game is won when the top of the egg is pushed down onto Eggzillas head, stopping the timer.

From the box: "Piece the egg together before the monster hatches!"

Ages 5 and up for 1 or more players.

Children's motorized action game. Part of a series of games entitled T.H.I.N.G.S. or Totally Hilarious Incredibly Neat Games of Skill. This is a hippopotamus themed game with a pop-a-shot mechanic. The goal is to flip all 8 plastic marbles into the hippos mouth before the time stops. The hippos mouth opens and closes while the player attempts to shoot in all the marbles.

From the box: "Flip the hippo a mouthful of marbles!"

Ages 5 and up for 1 or more players.

Children's motorized action game. Part of a series of games entitled T.H.I.N.G.S. or Totally Hilarious Incredibly Neat Games of Skill. Amphibian themed game that uses a rotating tree to spin mosquitos towards the frog. The goal is to make the frog grab up all 6 bugs before the timer stops. When the time stops the tree pops off the base.

From the box: "Grab the bugs before the time stops and the tree pops!"

Ages 5 and up for 1 or more players.

Issue game of Wargame Japanese History #14.

Multi-players game for vassals of Oda Nobunaga in Sengoku period of Japan. Oda Nobunaga was most powerful daimyo(feudal lord) in Sengoku period.
"Yuke! Nobunaga Kashindan" means Go! Nobunaga's Vassals.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oda_Nobunaga

Each player control one major vassals in Oda clan.
Standard game can play from three to five players. The game also include rule for one(solitaire) and two players.

Each counter represents a vassal to serve Oda clan. Each vassal have three ability, military, castellation and politic. During in the game, players can recruit vassals to own faction.

Each card represents a mission be ordered from Oda Nobunaga. Each mission has victory points, necessary abilities and penalty of failure.

Elect chief vassal at the beginning of each turn. Then, chief vassal is draw mission cards and he assigns missions to each player. Players can negotiate freely for achieve mission.
Repeat until the mission is exhausted.

Winner is determined by victory points.

boardgame: Die Olchis sind da  card game
description:
Objective: be the first getting rid of your cards
 - acting by turns/simultaneously -
there are 9 different type of 'Olchi' cards: each card requires a specific action; 
each player gets the same amount of cards (9), shuffles them and forms a hidden staple; active player places his top card upon the centre of the table and flips it => all players should perform required action, e.g. yawning, grunting...
if all perform correctly: next player's turn; a player who fails must take all the cards out of the centre.
Special card 'garbage': all players try - as fast as possible -
to cover that card with their hand; the 'top' hand takes all cards...
family: Creatures: Die Olchis (designed by Erhard Dietl)

boardgame: Shadows Upon Lassadar: Siege at Dalnish
description:
The wars with the Grayking have taken their toll. Lassadar's defenses are not as they once were, the Wardens are greatly reduced in number and magic is seemingly drained from the land.

From the South now arises a new threat. Twelve dread barons have formed a pact and pledged to take Lassadar for their own. They have gathered their armies and new allies - the Trulls, hulking brutes that hurl stones like mobile catapults - and have come north to Dalnish, a border city and first line of defense against invasion, to lay siege.

The Tower of Dalnish stands ready to repel the invasion but supplies and soldiers are limited. As the Captain of the Guard, it is your responsibility to hold out long enough for reinforcements to arrive. Rally your men, ready your weapons. They are upon you.....

A solo tower siege game lasting about 20 minutes and is the first game in the 2nd Lassadar game trilogy.

You, and the forces you command, must hold out long enough until Reinforcements arrive (drawn from the Event deck). If you can succeed in this you will drive the Barons and their armies back across the Southern border. If all of your Tower’s battlements are destroyed by Enemy Units or all of your Tower Units are slain or destroyed you lose the game and Lassadar is invaded.

42 cards, divided into Defensive Units, Enemy Units and Events are payed on a 2 section game board. Each Unit has a set of attack and defensive values. Using Action Points units played by the player can be deployed, engaged and repaired.

Unique to this game are Damage Pattern footprints that players must use effectively to combat Enemy Units.

A series of Event cards act as an AI in the game changing game play as the rounds progress. These cards are drawn when sufficient damage is done to the Enemy Units

Dice counters have been provided along with the card files/player board. Players may substitute 8mm dice if they prefer.

1st Place - Best Science Fiction/Fantasy Themed Game - 2012 Solitaire PNP Contest
2st Place - Best Artwork for an Entered Game Category - 2012 Solitaire PNP Contest
Grand Prize - 2012 Solitaire PNP Contest

boardgame: Shadows Upon Lassadar: Siege at Nem
description:
The Barons continue their invasion of Lassadar, striking out at the port city of Nem. Raise the call, they attack by sea!

Siege at Nem is a postcard style wargame for 1 or 2 players lasting 25 minutes and is the second game in the 2nd Lassadar game trilogy.

This hex and counter style wargame uses a deck of cards (standard or Tarot) + 1d6 for action point (AP) allotment. Action points may be spent to move units, for combat, for spell casting, bombardment of the city or deploying additional units.

The game last 7 rounds with the Baron player winning if they can occupy the Citadel Hex in the City of Nem with 1 unit until the end of any turn or if they are able to kill all Lassadar units within the city.

The Lassadar player wins if they can sink both of the Baron's Ship units or kill the Baron unit before game end.

Solo rules provided.

Simple Game for boys and girls 8-12. Object of the game is to get from your Pirate Ship into the cove with as much treasure as you can while avoiding bad things that could happen to you. Roll and move type game, turn over hidden pieces of treasure or turn over skull and crossbones and find nothing.

From the Kickstarter Page

Morphology Junior takes all the fun of the "original" game and adapts it to younger players (target age: 8+.) In order to move their frog-pieces from the "Start" lilypad to the "Finish," Morphologists must put on their creative thinking-caps to build words like "bike" and "snow" using the 35+ game pieces provided (see below for details). The game mixes some "original" Morphology rules like "Everybody Play" and "Pick 5" with some new adaptations, including "Morph" (where the Morphologist becomes a piece in the game) and "Ribbit, Ribbit" (where the Morphologist can make noises to help his/her teammates guess the right word).

....

In Morphology Junior, players form into up to 4 teams.  Each team gets a delightful plastic team frog marker to mark their place along the lilypad themed game board.

After deciding if the players will use the easy or hard sides of the clue cards (or perhaps kids use easy and adults use hard?) the first team to play chooses their first Morphologist who will create the word on the card from a collection of bits including colored cubes, a black string, glass baubles, wooden planks and little wooden 'people'.

In basic play, the Morphologist is a puppeteer and cannot spell out words or symbols, make any sounds other than a small amount of grunting and encouraging for guess feedback, and cannot interact directly with the pieces.  The rulebook gives the example of 'toothbrush' and how the Morphologist can't pretend to brush their own teeth but they can brush the teeth of the little wooden figure.

Some of the lily pad spaces can change this though.  Everyone Plays allows both teams to guess what the current Morphologist is building.  Interception allows an opposing team to guess if the current team doesn't guess correctly by the time the timer runs down. Morph! allows the Morphologist to be a piece as well, so go ahead and brush YOUR teeth!  Pick 5 forces the Morphologist to choose just 5 props to use after looking at the word. Pick 5 + 1 however allows the opposing teams to choose the 5 props and the Morphologist gets to add just one more prop after reading the word.  And finally there is Ribbit! Ribbit! where the Morphologist can make noises!

Correct guesses allow a team to move their frog to the next lily pad.  First team to get their frog to the final lily pad wins!
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
A World War II era card game where each player tries to be the first to complete a "Flight" and win the game by playing a Terminal card on his/her turn. A completed Flight consists of an Aeroplane card, A Pilot card, Map cards and a Terminal card all of the same color. The Air Hostess card (acting as a wild card) may substitute for any Flight card to assist in completing a Flight. Players first draw a terminal card to determine which Flight they need to complete. These are kept face down. Six cards from the deck are then drawn for each player and play continues with each player attempting to complete the Flight color that matches their Terminal card. All flight cards played are placed face up and used communally.

A point variation for this game my be played where players not completing a Flight total the points from the cards remaining in their hands and the lowest cumulative score over four completed games is declared the winner. In this version all cards except the Air Hostess (50 points) have an assigned value of 10 points. In this version penalty points (100) for poor play may be assessed.

The game consists of 60 cards divided as follows: 4 Aeroplane cards. 4 Pilot cards, 36 Map cards (9 for each of 4 flights numbered 2 through 10), 4 Storm cards, 4 Storm Buster cards, 1 Air Hostess (wild) card, 7 Terminal cards. Cards are colored white, yellow, blue and pink for each of the 4 flights. The Air Hostess (wild) card is green.

From publisher's website: 
Gather some friends to play as an anthropomorphized sled dog rescue team, overcoming cannibalistic strays and a landscape as capricious as it is lethal. Only your wits and your gear stand between you and certain disaster. Quick-reference instructions provide the rules in minutes, while a scalable difficulty curve provides a challenge for everyone from pups to even the most veteran sled dogs. A randomized map ensures no two missions will ever be the same.

Based on the award-winning graphic novel Nordguard: Across Thin Ice, this set comes packed with over 120 illustrations by the artists Tess Garman & Teagan Gavet, as well as four custom-etched dice.

Each game starts by shuffling a deck of Location cards and laying them out to form a different map for each play through. The team then studies this map while selecting their Character and Item cards. Each character has unique skills and abilities to give the team an edge in various areas. Various Item cards equip the team against the dangers of the landscape and its inhabitants. The team travels in a group and must make it from their start point at Frontier to the Destination card within a limited number of turns. Each turn, the team draws an Event card — which may help or hinder them. Your team wins or loses depending on whether you all reach the destination in time.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: uSpy is a WWII espionage deck-building game for two to five players. In the game, each player acts as the Director of Espionage of a WWII major power and the goal is to infiltrate enemy Headquarters, place moles inside enemy spy Networks, and steal money/technologies. The game ends after seven rounds or when one player has three or more enemy moles in place. Players tally their victory points are tallied, and whoever has the most points wins!

This is a game played with 6 tops. The 6 tops are different colors corresponding to 6 colors on the game board. You get all 6 tops spinning and wait for them to stop. A top with its tip resting on its color scores 1 point. Any other color scores nothing. The game is to 50 points.

The board is a square divided into 4 quadrants. In each quadrant the board is broken up into circular arcs that look like 6 concentric circles that were cut horizontally and vertically through the center, then randomized.

For some odd reason the box top has the name "Rainbow Spinning Game" but the rules have the name "The Game of Rainbow Tops".

In addition to adding a fifth player to the game, Magnum Sal: Muria brings something new to extract from the mines in Magnum Sal: brine (which in Latin is "muria"). Yes, players will now be able to get salt from brine, in addition to deciding where shafts in the mine are going to be built, something that will allow each game of Magnum Sal to play out a little bit differently. Magnum Sal: Muria also contains:


    A game board with two new buildings.
    Mine foremen in each of the player colors.
    New royal orders that require brine to fulfill.
    New mine tiles
    Two new tools, including the capstan – the winning tool from a game design competition.

The game consists of 30 mystery cards, 40 black cards with taboo words and a grim reaper card. The players try to guess the stories behind the scene outlined on the mystery cards without using the taboo words.

Like the other Game: Black Stories games, the central element are the mystery cards. They contain an illustration and a short statement about a spooky or crime event. On the back of the card, the story is presented in more detail. Only one of the players reads the back of the card. The others try to reconstruct how the crime happened by asking yes/no questions.

New about "Black Stories: Die Erweiterung" is that there are cards with taboo words. At the beginning of each rounds, three of these are selected. If one of the players mentions one of these words, the others try to slap the grim reaper card as quickly as possible. The player who correctly slaps the grim reaper card the fastest, wins the taboo card. The person who has contributed most to finding the solution gets the card. The player with the most cards wins the game.

The game can be combined with other games from the series. You can either just use the taboo word cards with other Black Stories instalments, or mix all the cards together.

boardgame: Lost Squadrons: A Black Cross/Blue Sky Supplement
description:
publisher's description

From its conception, the Blue Sky Game Series was designed to be an expandable system that would allow players to simulate and explore the challenges of aerial warfare throughout the 20th century. With this supplement players can now field Polish, French, and Dutch aircraft.

All the maps, info counters, charts, dice and Flight Stands from Black Cross / Blue Sky are used with this supplement.  Players do not have to continually re-purchase these essential components. Some of the aircraft counters from Black Cross / Blue Sky are also used. All of the scenarios can be expanded with additional aircraft counters.

So put on your parachute, and remember to bring your foreign language dictionaries, for there is a battle to be won over the skies of Europe!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Dice Farmer is a very fast-playing game of chance, with just enough strategy to make it and interesting.  The game was developed by Tailsteak of Leftover Soup.

There's a pool of dice from four siders to twenty siders in a pool between the players, including d14s, d16s, and d18s. Players have a "hand" of dice they throw every turn. Any 1s rolled are returned to the pool. Other dice that singly or in groups total the size of a die available in the pool can add that die to the players hand (ie a rolled 4 and 8 could claim a d12).

Last player remaining or first with three d20s in their hand wins!

The historic Lincoln Highway, the first coast-to-coast highway in 1913, stretched from New York City to San Francisco. So children can learn more about the highway in a fun way, the nonprofit organization Lincoln Highway Heritage Corridor created a board game. The Lincoln Highway Road Trip comes with game pieces of a vintage gas pump, antique autos, compass, tourist cabin, postcard and suitcase as well as 200 trivia cards and fortune cards.

The game board reflects the regional part of the highway -- known as Route 30 -- in the six-county area from North Huntingdon at the Allegheny and Westmoreland County border to beyond Gettysburg. A grant from the Sprout Fund in Pittsburgh enabled the organization to develop the game and distribute it to upper elementary and middle schools along the region's 200-mile corridor. It was created by Alex Grab of Ligoni

Using pewter-like game pieces (vintage gas tank, suitcase, compass, tourist cabin, postcard and antique auto), players move along the highway beginning in Irwin. The goal is to be the first to travel the historic route through Westmoreland, Somerset, Bedford, Fulton, Franklin and Adams counties to Abbottstown in eastern Pa.

One of the 200 question cards reads: “A volunteer highway crew is picking up debris along the side of the road near Buckstown. How long does it take for a Styrofoam cup to decompose?” A. 20 years; B. 90 years; C. 150 years; or D. 400 years?” The correct answer is 400 years.

Another is: “The Mountain Playhouse and Green Gables Restaurant originally started as a sandwich stand. What was the dessert that became such a hit with Lincoln Highway travelers?” A. Ice Cream Sandwich; B. Custard; C. Angel Food Cake; or D. Torte? C is the correct answer.

A birthday-themed educational children's game in the Ratz Fatz series.

The game consists of 20 wooden figures (a rabbit, a pirate, a candle, a cake, a present,...), 10 wooden tokens and a booklet with several game ideas for kids of various ages. 
The basic game idea is that an adult reads or tells a story or a poem from the booklet, and the children have to grab the figures representing items/persons from the text. For each correct item, they gain a token, and the player with the most tokens wins the game.

Other ideas presented in the booklet include a memory game and finding figures that fit a certain description (all animals, everything that is edible, things you can play with etc.).

The Immigration game is an adaptation of John Conway's abstract zero-player game of Life to a two-player game.  It was first described in Lifeline, a newsletter dedicated to Conway's Life, in 1971.

In the Immigration game, players setup the game by taking turns and placing 5 pieces each of their color on a 25x25 rectangular grid.  Each position in the 25x25 grid can be either alive or dead, the living positions (or “cells”) are those that have a counter placed in them. Once setup is complete, the pieces are not moved and board positions evolve without player intervention according to the rules  of Conway’s Game of Life, as listed below:

At each turn (or “generation”) the state of a cell is determined by the following rules:

1.     If a live cell has less than two neighbors in the eight neighboring cells, it dies in the next generation (“loneliness”)

2.     If a live cell has at two or three neighbors in the eight neighboring cells, it stays alive in the next generation.

3.     If a live cell has more than three neighbors in the eight neighboring cells, it dies in the next generation (“overcrowding”).

4.     If a dead cell has exactly three neighbors in the eight neighboring cells, it becomes alive in the next generation (“reproduction”).

New living cells, which come into being by having exactly 3 neighbors, take on the color of the majority of those three neighbors.  Note that all cells in the 25 x 25 board have 8 neighbors each as the board is considered to wrap around the edges to each opposite side.

After the 10th generation, the player with the most living cells is allowed to place a counter thus adding an additional live cell of his or her color, followed by a turn by the other player.  If both players have the same amount of live cells, generations continue to develop until one player has a majority, at which time the generations stop so players can place their new live cells and the process begins again.

Play continues this way until one of the players has no live cells left and the winning player is the only one with live cells on the board.

Afterworld: Earth Reborn captures the earth's apocalyptic fall into darkness slightly after a decade-long golden period in which people lived longer, explored their minds both intellectually and spiritually more than ever, created grandiose machines and robots, and lived in relative peace.

The actual game takes place in an opportunity-deprived environment (making nods to the steampunk genre) that is dominated by several characters--both good and evil in nature.  There's grown and spliced creatures; sinister, calculated robots; demented magical incantations; and fervent warriors/scholars from assorted historical and fantasy sources.

The goal of the game is to acquire victory points, which are gained through several different types of actions.  Gameplay involves several cards that create a gameboard; are drawn and played by players; and dictate what players do (through an avatar).  Players take turns gaining resources, playing cards, and fighting creatures as they interact with the board and opponents.

Objective: be the first reaching target zone
 - acting by turns -
cards (pairs) are shuffled & placed - hidden - upon the table;
each player places a chip (same colour like meeple), too; a (diving) larger chip is added.
Active player uncovers 2 cards - matching pair? Take them out & move your meeple 1 step ahead; if a meeple's move ends on a red marked space a player must take all chips into both hands (hidden) by splitting them and let the active player make a guess: uncovered chips let correspndent meeples move ahead, even two spaces far, if diving chip is present.

Theme:
Play one of four deities attempting to build a new world. Score the most points to become the chief of the pantheon.

Game Play:
Each World Card has 12 symbols in 4 shapes. The symbols are colored in or black. Each player is trying to accumulate 1 of the 4 shapes. Colored in shapes count for points unless the Polarity special action card is played, then black shapes count for points. Each player gets to play 1 card per turn unless a special action card is used to change this.

Objective: be the first completing your equipment => gaining a saddle, riding helmet, carrot & caring box for your pony
 - acting by turns -
memory like gaming approach
18 pony cards (6 different names => 3 coloured cards forming a complete set) are shuffled and split into 3 piles; 
6 tiles (with images of 0,1,2 children) are placed upon the table, hidden. 
Active player draws a pony card: symbol on the back shows required tile => draw the matching tile: successful - keep the pony card and drop & mix the tiles; wrong => discard the pony card and place tile back upon the table.

This is a small expansion for Exposaurus.

It consists of a small ceramics dinosaur and a sheet of rules.
The first player to include all 6 types of fossils in their museum gets the dinosaur. At the end of the game, the dinosaur is worth 4 additional victory points.

Objective: gain most question cards finding correct answers
- acting by turns -
draw a card and give the correct answer;
if you are right keep card as bonus point

64 question and answer cards, informative and curious about geography, politics, celebrities, recreational activities and history of Baden-Württemberg, state of Germany.

Dice Dale is a resource management, worker placement euro style dice game with heavy strategy and serious gameplay in a small dice bag.  Players compete for victory points as they build up the thriving fictional city of Dice Dale.

Players roll six-sided dice for resources each round, collect workers in inverse proportion to the number of resources they gained, and then spend those resources and workers to build new buildings and gain victory points.  A large collection of buildings with differing effects provides deep strategy and interesting decisions as the game unfolds.

Deck building game inpired by games like MTG & Ether Lords.

Each player controls a clan and tries to defeat the opponent by using spells, creatures and enchantments.

Mana is accumulated each round so no pestering mana cards in your hand later in the game.

Each Player can specialize in an area making certain cards more powerful but your opponent can also specialize in rendering your costly skills useless.

A fast paced game for 2 or 4 players.

Infinity Campaign: Paradiso is second, after Human Sphere, book that expands Infinity Skirmish Game. Paradiso delivers few new features to Infinity:
- Completely new race: Tohaa
- Complete campaign set on planet Paradiso and depicting fight between Humanity and Combined Army
- Rules to make other campaigns and among them: Spec Ops and experience
- New troops to existing Infiity factions

Campaign Paradiso is meant for 4 players, 300 points armies and contains 14 different scenarios divided into 4 different chapters. Thanks to the unique campaign map players play different missions depending on their wins or loses. Thanks to this, and experience system campaign is different for each player.

Tohaa is new faction introduced in Infinity Campaign: Paradiso book. It is highly developed alien faction of scientists. It depends on symbiotic armor and three man squads (number 3 is their sacred number). It is currently in war with Combined Army and wants to form alliance with Humanity.

Objective: be the first reaching target zone
 - acting by turns/simultaneously -
Active player draws a card & decides, which question (1 out of 2) should be answered (multiple choice A-C);
all players switch simultaneously chosen letter & red foil is used to make the correct answer visible:
correct? Move one space ahead.
At the time leading player steps on a marked (tossing) space  last players have to use their letters in a 'rock-paper-scissors'
game... winner - or tied - may step ahead.

Learn about animals and their environments as you follow the stone trail through the world's habitats. Draw a "Wisdom & Consequence" card to learn about environmental hazards and to discover ways in which you can help improve the environment. With every correct answer to an animal or habitat question on the "Knowledge" cards, you collect one "Habitat" card. Collect two cards from each habitat to become an "Earth Steward" and win the game. It's a fun expedition around the world for family and friends when you "HIT THE HABITAT TRAIL!"

A children's game about counting and additions.

Basic idea: The hamsters are on a frantic shopping spree - they'll buy whatever they find. The only thing that counts is that they can collect as many nuts as possible because they get a certain number of nuts per purchase. This isn't as easy as it sounds, though, as they have to calculate the exact total for their purchase, and if they make a mistake, another hamster will be happy.

Aim of the game and how it is played: The aim of the game is to collect item cards with as many nuts printed on them as possible. Each item card also has a price printed on it. There is always one item card fewer at the center of the table than there are players. The players have to use some of their bank notes to exactly reach the price of one of the items in the middle of the table (e.g. if there is an item that costs 837 and another that costs 1200, they can choose the 800 and the 37 banknote, or the 800 and 400). Some items are worth more nuts than others. All players do the calculating simultaneously. If a player thinks they have put together a correct set of banknotes, they put the banknotes face down in front of him/herself and puts his/her hamster on the item card to reserve it. Once all the items have been reserved, the hamsters proceed to the checkout: all players check if the cards are correct (i.e. make up the exact price of the item). If they are, the player earns that item. If they aren't, this hamster doesn't get any card this round. The player who hasn't managed to reserve an item (there is always one item fewer than there are players) gets lucky if another player has miscalculated: he/she gets the item that player has reserved.

The game ends when there are no item cards left. The player with the most nuts wins the game.

There is also an "expert" variant of the game, which includes a memory element.

Blitzschnell! is a jigsaw game for children, with an important speed element.

Each player gets one of the picture cards. The 36 jigsaw pieces are shuffled and placed in the box bottom. The first player rolls the die. If it shows a cross, the die is passed on to the next player. If it shows a jigsaw piece, he takes the box bottom and starts looking for pieces that belong to his jigsaw. At the same time, the next player rolls the die. As soon as another player has rolled a jigsaw piece, the box bottom is passed to that player.

As soon as a player thinks his jigsaw is finished, he shouts "Stop!". The other players stop and check whether the jigsaw is correct. If it isn't, the player returns all the pieces collected to the box and must start again; if it is, the player earns a racing car, all pieces are returned to the box, players pass their picture cards to the next player, and a new round begins.

When one player has collected two cars, he wins the game!

Variant: All the jigsaw pieces are randomized and put face down in the middle of the table. All players are simultaneously turning over a piece. If it fits your own jigsaw puzzle, you take the piece. If not, put it back face down. The first player to complete his jigsaw wins the round.

A simple, turtle-themed racing game for children.

The board shows a racing track made up of tomatoes and salad. Each player has two turtles and a peg in the same colour (so they don't forget what colour they are). The aim of the game is to get one's turtles to the end of the track first. Players take turns rolling the colour die. Turtles are moved according to the die roll. If blue is rolled, a blue turtle is moved - even if it isn't one's own turtle. Green allows the active player to put one of his/her turtles on the next salad space, red on the next tomato field. If a turtle lands on the same space as another, it is put on that turtle's back. If a turtle in a stack gets to move, the others that are on its back ride along. Turtles can only leave the track at the end if the player rolls red, green or his/her own colour. If there is another turtle on its back, they also reach the finish.
The first player who manages to get both his/her turtles to the finish wins the game.

Temporum is a strategy game about the seasons. Each players embody one of the seasons and try to spread his territory the biggest he can, but the entire concept of the game is based on the succession of seasons, in this game you have to accept that your territory will be taken by the season that follow you and you'll do the same to the season that precede you. So your territories are always changing. The deal about this game is to calculate when you have to spread wide and when you have to spread wise.

Game take place over 12 years (12 turns), by using limited action points players place pawn on the board to expand their season on this particular territory (island). They can also spread from existing controlled island to expand more their territory, but they can grow only on the season that preceed them (eg. winter spread on fall). Each player also have a mega-bonus that they will use over the game. It's important to calculate when it's the moment to use a lot of APs and when it's important to stay low profile. Players score at the beginning of each year, their score egal the number of island they control.

It's a fast poetic semi-competitve strategy game.

Young children's game. Each child has a hand of four cards and the top card of the stack is revealed. If the player has an identical type of vehicle and the number of such vehicles are the same plus 1, then he can discard the card. If not, another card is revealed and the next player tries, and so on. The first child to use all their cards, wins.

Young childrens game. Each card features a clown in different costumes. Each player gets a hand of cards and the top card is revealed. In your turn, if you can match one feature on the exposed card you can play your card on top of it. If you don't or cannot spot it, the turn passes to the next player. The first player to use all their cards, wins.

boardgame: Die drei ??? Kids: Im Reich der Rätsel
description:
Objective: be the first gaining three cards => winning 3 games
 - acting simultaneously -
each game requires a game master, who draws a (hidden) card with 3 (out of 7 possible) suspects first; 
further cards are drawn with 3 images each; after the game master has valuated (0, 1 or 2 hits) the card is placed upon the table & next card is drawn; as soon as a player knows the correct combination card is won; if guess was wrong player is out of game.

Fanmade translation:
How well do you know actually the Czech Republic?
In this game you will find 2400 interesting questions concerning the Czech Republic and its people. This game follows the great success of the game "Česko, otázky a odpovědi" that many of you enjoyed. In "Golden Czech" you will find brand new category, which examines your knowledge more thoroughly! The questions are divided into 6 areas: persons, culture, at school, homeland study, Czech BEST and various .

Two-sided game board allows you to play a conquest version of this game in which you conquer castle Karlštejn. During the siege of the castle you will acquire gold for your knowledge. The most successful of you at the end of the game conquers famous castle. During the game you not enjoy great fun but also learn a lot of interesting information about Czech republic. To  win the game you might need a little luck except the knowledge.


From the publisher:
Zlaté Česko, otázky a odpovědi
Zábavná hra pro zvídavé Čechy

Jak dobře vlastně znáte Českou republiku?
V této vědomostní hře najdete 2 400 zajímavých otázek týkajících se České republiky a jejích obyvatel. Tato hra navazuje na velký úspěch vědomostní hry Česko Otázky a odpovědi, kterou si mnozí z Vás velmi oblíbili. Ve Zlatém Česku ale najdete zbrusu nové kategorie, které vaše znalosti prověří ještě důkladněji! Otázky jsou rozděleny do 6 okruhů: osobnosti, kultura, ze školních lavic, vlastivěda, česká NEJ a různé.

Oboustranný herní plán umožňuje hrát i dobyvatelskou verzi této hry, ve které budete dobývat hrad Karlštejn. Během obléhání hradu budete za svoje znalosti získávat zlaťáky. Ten nejúspěšnější z vás na konci hry dobude tento slavný hrad. Během hry si nejen užijete spoustu zábavy, ale také se o své vlasti dozvíte mnoho zajímavých informací. K tomu, abyste ve hře zvítězili, budete kromě znalostí potřebovat i trochu štěstí.

boardgame: Die drei ??? Kids: In Letzter Sekunde
description:
Objective: get most of those cards building an investigation path showing the cleverest detective 
 - acting by turns -
Active player draws a card (with images of one, two or three different detectives); now a path for each detective/image (max. 3 during the game) may be formed, starting from an own tableau; a player may draw additional cards (step by step), but will loose his/her turn if there is'nt - at least - the same image on each card. If cards are linked to the own tableau (safe): Next player's turn. 
Special symbols like '+', '=>' force the player drawing once more or giving away the card. 
At a certain point a detective is disqualified and a new round starts; after the second detective is gone winning cards are counted.

On the edges of known space, newly discovered planets beckon to your great galactic empire. An accurate guidance system would cost more than a jillion rockets. So you send lots of ships.

Each player gets five six-sided dice with faces representing rockets, landers, colonists, and the warp void. Players take turns lobbing their dice toward the board. Colonists in orbit rely on rockets to supply them, and colonists on the planet rely on landers to help them get settled.

There is dexterity and some skill involved, because a good launch can knock out around other your opponents' pieces while putting what you need on the board. Yet the basic action is simple enough that kids and adults can all play, and the element of luck means that even a skilled shark can't always run the table.

Hijinks. In space.
The DOR is you’re only way out!

The Planit game was included with Omni Magazine Volume 14 #1, October 1991 edition.  From the publisher's site:
"The Omni Evolution Game Life’s not one big crapshoot. Instead it’s a board game that lets each player be Gaia."

"In the first phase, players must fill the atmosphere up with oxygen. In the second phase, they must evolve higher animals in sequence. The first to evolve 'Evolutionary Humans" wins."

Players play cards from their hand to affect the environment and evolution of life on their individual planets, which are represented on the game map.

A fire-fighting-themed educational children's game in the Ratz Fatz series.

The game consists of 3 wooden vehicles (fire engine, ambulance, police car), 13 picture tiles, a colour die and a booklet with several game ideas for kids of various ages.
The basic game idea is that an adult reads or tells a story or a poem from the booklet, and the children have to grab the figures or tiles representing items/situations from the text.

Other ideas presented in the booklet include describing what the pictures show, naming the objects, a memory game, finding tiles/figures that fit a certain description (a vehicle, a situation in which an ambulance can help,...), and a simple die rolling game.

Each player is dealt 6 cards. — The card following next will be placed on the table face up, being the “starting-card” for the journey. The remaining cards will be left in the pile, face down.

The first player takes the top card of the pile and must consider how to lay out as many cards as possible on to the starting card, according to the following rules:
The player may continue laying out only cards showing countries with common borders (a map of Europe is provided). He is also allowed to lay out two cards of the same country at the same time;

Any country may be reached by aeroplane, i.e. including any country not adjoining the country shown on the card laid out last. However, the rule is that a plane may be used only once in each turn. Flying will be indicated by leaving the previously laid out card uncovered in such a way that the aeroplane will remain visible. (With neighbouring countries the cards will be placed partly on top of each other in the form of scales, the aeroplane remaining covered.) It is left to the discretion of the player himself whether or not and when he wishes to make use of a card as air ticket.

The first player having finished laying out as many cards as possible next to the starting card and in conformity with the above rules, each additional player will follow his turn in corresponding manner, by laying out his cards next to the card laid out by the previous player.

The winner of a round will be the player who first succeeds in laying out all his cards. The game should be played to a pre-agreed number of rounds.

Scoring the points: the figure in the left hand corner of each card shows the number of neighbouring countries. The figures shown on the cards remaining with the players will be counted minus points for those players.
Special instructions: in addition to countries with common borders those countries connected with each other by ferry boat (railway ferries) will be considered neighbouring countries too. (See the map of Europe with the game).
In this game Turkey is considered a neighbouring country also of the Soviet Union, although that frontier is in Asia. There is no neighbouring country to Iceland.

boardgame: Thomas & Friends: Windy Day Rescue Game
description:
This is a children's game in which each player moves around the board collecting items as they land on them. Movement is decided by rolling a dice which has three different colours on its sides, and moving to the next available space of the colour shown. The object is to collect four items and return to the start/finish space.

Publisher's Description:

Join Thomas & his friends in a race around Sodor collecting stranded cargo before delivering them safely to the station!

It's a very windy day in Sodor and all the cargo has been blown around the village. Help Thomas and his friends collect the stranded cargo and deliver it safely back to the station. A colour recognition and collection game for children from 3+ years.

From the back of the book;
Introduction to Star Fleet Battles is a complete game, but is also the gateway to all the excitement which is Game: Star Fleet Battles.

User Description
The aim of this book is to introduce a new player to the complex game system of Game: Star Fleet Battles, but it can be used as a complete game in itself.

It takes the reader slowly through the rules by small scenarios and adding more rules to the game in each scenario played.

The game is played on a fold-out A1 hex map with die-cut playing starship pieces.  Each scenario has its own goal for each player to win the game.  The scenarios in the book start off with solo player games like piloting a starship through an asteroid belt, then later with two-player battle game scenarios.

Included with Introduction to Star Fleet Battles;

     Step-by-Step Instructions.
     54 Die-Cut Playing Pieces.
     Game Map.


Re-implemented by Star Fleet Battles Cadet Training Manual

The game ego pictures consists mainly of photo cards, task cards, word cards and number chips. Six photo cards are placed on the table and an active player is selected. She draws a task card and follows the instructions on it. These can be:

1. Match two pictures: The player draws a number chip, marks the respective photo card, then everyone picks a number chip which corresponds to the photo that matches the selected photo card best.

2. A word on a word card is randomly selected. Players now mark which photo matches that word best.

3. A photo is randomly selected. Players now mark which word on a word card matches the photo best.

4. Players choose which of the photo cards matches the active player best.

Afterwards, points are scored: The active player gets a point for each other player making the same choice as s/he. The other players who guessed correctly also score one point each. If nobody guessed correctly, the players get a point for each other player making the same choice as them.

Points are marked on a score track. The first player to reach 16 points is the winner.

The Danish (Original) version plays slightly different:

There are only 3 pictures shown at start of the game.

Each player gets 10 tokens, and 10 tokens are placed in the common area.

Players bet any number of tokens on their answer. Wrong and you loose your tokens to the common area. Right and you gain a number of tokens from the common area equal to your bet. The active player gains a number of tokens equal to the players who guessed correctly.

Game ends when there are no more tokens in the common area.

The player with the most tokens is the winner.

Blokkerende Botsauto's (Blocking Dodgems) is a print and play game for two players.

Each player is the driver of a car. The cars drive on a 4 by 4 grid of streets. Each player in turn passes one intersection. Each intersection is a double sided tile. One side has an intersection where no right turn is allowed. The other side shows an intersection where no left turn allowed. Driving straight ahead is always allowed. Each time a car has passed an intersection, its tile is flipped. The first player to pass all 16 intersections wins. The cars cannot be both on the same tile at the same time. A player who has nowhere to go (his car can only drive off the grid, or on a blocked tile) loses the game. During the setup phase of the game, each player determines the initial state of half of the grid.

This game was published as a prize question in the Dutch magazine Pythagoras (issue 51/3, Jun 2012). It's a magazine about mathematics for high school students. Readers had to find a winning strategy (no answer was found).

Some people don't realize that the Dragonfly is a friend. It eats up a lot of Mosquitoes and in the summer when I see armies of dragonflies arriving, I give a cheer. Go, Team Dragonfly, get those nasty mosquitoes before they get us and our pets.  The object of the game is to capture the mosquitoes with the dragonflies so people and pets don't get bitten. The game is over when all the mosquitoes are accounted for, either all caught or some caught and some (ouch) biting people and pets.

Adults enjoy the strategies as well, especially in a family setting. Play solo or with as many as 4.

The Verräter Söldnervariante (mercenary variant) was available at Spiel 2003 celebrating the 5 year anniversary of Verräter. The Spiel 2003 edition was printed on a postcard along with advertisements for games by Marcel-André Casasola Merkle: Verräter, Meuterer, Attika, and Attribut. The Söldnervariante was also made available as downloadable file by Lookout Games.

In this variant, the mercenary can be hired when playing conflict cards (Phase 3). The player hiring the mercenary immediately loses 3 victory points but in conflict resolution (Phase 5), the mercenary counts as 5 conflict points.

The Best of Butler is a real estate game unique to Butler County, Pennsylvania. Players buy properties representing local businesses or institutions from which they collect rent from game opponents. A player may expand their property by adding Investments to collect higher rents or resell the properties at a profit.

The object of the game is to become the wealthiest player and retire all other opponents from the game.

The Best of Bulter was a fund-raising project produced by friends and members of the Association for Retarded Citizens of Butler County. The proceeds enables the association to pay off the state required elevator that was installed at the workshop. This list price on the box top was $9.50 (including tax).

You and your friends must shop against each other for the finest in designer items: Shoes, Dresses, Make-up, Handbags, and Jewelry. But along the way you will have to avoid credit card declines, maxing out, and store security.

GoShop is a round-the-table dice game. Players take turns rolling the dice to make as many purchases as they can, aiming for the finest designer items. The shopper with the most purchases at the end wins.

boardgame: Die drei ??? und das leere Grab
description:
Objective: Gain most victory points gaining sets (3 matching cards) and making corroct guess es about (3) hidden cards 
 - acting by turns -
Out of each three category groups (person, place, tool) a card is placed upon the table, uncovered. All other cards are distributed among players. Active player may drop a matching set (3 cards = 1 victory point) and/or ask another player to hand out a special card;
successful? - keep asking; wrong? - next player's turn; if player wants to make a guess regarding a hidden card: successful? +3 VP; wrong? -3VP

version 'Bilder-Orakel': - entering the game easily -

form a 6 x 10 grid;
