version Bilder-Rallye': - take out 'consumer products' -
 - acting simultaneously/game master alternates -
60 images are placed randomly upon the table; game master reads a history card: all other must place a chip upon an image
(only one per image); correct or wrong - you got rid of a chip; - winner: most correct chips
 version Bild-Reporter: - take out 'consumer products' -
 - acting simultaneously/game master alternates -
60 images are placed randomly upon the table;
game master describes a quality of an image - all others must place their chips - if a chip is matching: 1 victory point, wrong chips are removed

boardgame: Die drei ???: Im Bann des Voodoo
description:
Objective: get 5 victory points forming a continuous line on a 
number line from 1 - 10
 - acting by turns -
active player rolls the die and moves; ending on a question mark a card may be drawn from another player - one out of 3 questions is read - 3 possible answers, too. Correct? Take top (hidden) numbered chip from that place and drop it upon your number line. Wrong or space already occupied? Place chip at lowest position.
Special space (!!!) in the centre: May be entered by one player only => search and use a chip of your choice;

Earth has been connected to the fantasy world of Laris. The result of the mixture of technology and magic is the Cataclysm, an event that tore holes in the fabric of time and space. Five heroes have been pulled together on Laris, where they embark on a journey to mend both worlds.

Consequential is a co-operative board game that lets players live the tale of this adventure.  Over the course of four acts, the story will be told.  The base game box contains the board, and all the cards for Act I.  Acts II, III, and IV are packaged and sold separately. They contain additional cards, new locations, and new mechanics!

The unique aspect of this game is that it is not solely a tabletop experience.  A free companion app for your iOS/Android device or laptop is required for play. It will display voice-acted events that are triggered by actions in the physical game, and tell more of the story.  The app also is responsible for keeping the timer for the real-time portion of each episode.  One episode of Consequential contains a turn-based phase (about 45 minutes) and a real-time phase (about 10 minutes) to conclude the session.  The real-time phase can be played without a timer, if players prefer.

boardgame: The Emerald City (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
It's 1962, and the Steam Era is over. The monorail is the future, and that future is starting in Seattle, Washington. There’s a reward to find the best routes in the city to build the template for tomorrow.  Will you go it alone or team up for a chance at victory? The choice is yours...

Ticket to Ride™: The Emerald City is a fan-made expansion to the Days of Wonder, Inc. Ticket to Ride™ series created by Alan R. Moon. A tribute both to Ticket to Ride™ and the Pacific Northwest, this expansion introduces new rules and is designed for players who are already well familiar with Ticket to Ride™’s standard gameplay and mechanics.

Ticket to Ride™: The Emerald City is also the first expansion map to be designed from the ground up to be played either individually (for up to 4 players) OR in teams (for up to 6 players).

Published by permission for non-commercial use. All rights to the Ticket to Ride™ name, gameplay, mechanics, and imagery are reserved by Days of Wonder, Inc., who have not endorsed or playtested this expansion. Everything else © Rainshine Graphics, 2013.

boardgame: Combat 3001 (or A Fist Full of Credits): A Combat 3000 Supplement
description:
Combat 3001 (or A Fist Full of Credits) is a rules supplment for the SF wargames rules; Combat 3000: Infantry Combat in the Year 3000.

It adds the following rules to Combat 3000.

    Advanced Rules on;
        Weapon Profiles
        Close Combat
        Movement
        Visibility - Awareness
        Fire Modes
        Gravity
        Close Combat Training
        Vehicles
    
    New Lists on;
        Creatures
        More Weapons
        Equipment
    


Additional Credits; 
Richard Prieastly, Anthony Epworth, Steve Tiffin, Paul Elsey, Pete Harris, Gavin Denton, Andy Szazepankiewicz (Pank), Martin Parker (Perce), Andrew Phillips and Simon Elliot.

Golf-O-Matic puts you right in the action, at two of the most challenging courses in the world. Japan's famous KAWANA COUNTRY CLUB, and the BIG G at Grossingers, New York.

From the moment you "tee off" you're involved. Keeping score. Positioning your ball marker in the authentic-scale diagram of each hole you play. Deciding which "club" to use next. Each time you press one of the club levers, the wheel behind the window spins, stops at random, and tells you how far you've gone, whether your ball went true, or if you've hooked or sliced and whether you're in the rough, in a trap or on the fairway.

Play against a single opponent, as part of a foursome or by yourself. You'll find the Golf-O-Matic to be the next best thing, to actually being out there! Ideal for those frustrating bad weather days, on a trip, in the clubhouse or at parties. Place the same bets you make on the golf course: nassaus, presses, medal or match. PGA rules apply. Fore!

Golf-O-Matic is a great equalizer...everybody starts evenly matched. On each stroke, the wheel behind the window spins...stops at random...and tells youhow you did.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Zombies, ninjas, perverts, nerds, dwarves, elves (and weretigers), evil erasers, very manly bunnies and Cthulhu Ninja Zombie attack each other in a deck-building and battling card game.

Based on the webcomic Commissioned, 3v3: The Commissioned Comic Card Game is a rapid-paced game in which players build a thirty-card deck and battle by drawing three cards a hand. Each card has an attack value, a defense value, and a special ability. The twist is that you play one card for attack, one for defense, and one for special ability.

For every point your opponent scores on you, you must remove a card from your deck and send it to the scoreboard. The first person to 10 points wins the game.

Game description from the publisher:

In Magic Feather, you play as one of eight neighborhood animals and move around a magical neighborhood by doing things like pretending to be a cat that swims, telling a story about your breakfast, or making another player laugh without talking – but you'll have to stay one step ahead of those pesky Crows to win!

Described on the box as "The Card Game for Pony Lovers".

Each player is dealt 7 cards and the game-play is similar to Rummy. You can draw a card of the deck or take the top card in the discard pile. The aim is to be the first to complete a 'set'. A set must contain a pony, a bridle, a saddle, a pair of stirrups and 3 other cards that a rider might need or desire (a rosette is good and is worth 9 points whereas a grooming kit is not so nice and only scores 3 points).

Each card in the set has a points value. The winning player scores those points and a new hand is played. The one with the most points after an agreed number of hands, wins.

Objective: be the first reaching target zone 
 - acting by turns -
active player rolls the die: ending upon a 'Hully-Gully' space =>
draw a card & perform requested activity; successful => roll the die (exception: 'grey' space: next player's turn);
ending upon a 'grips' space => answer question correctly:
successful? roll again.. wrong? next player's turn.
Last 6 spaces are 'do or die' ones, no die is used;

Thousands of years ago, this galaxy was in turmoil. Many civilizations discovered the power of Grotons as an energy source, and used it to master interplanetary travel. Unfortunately, they found their neighbors very difficult to relate to. The one thing they all seemed to have in common was their pride in their ability to travel through space, and they all liked to show off. Before long they began having periodical competitions to see who had the better spaceships, and this eventually grew into this regular race.

Players choose an alien race to represent in the race, and they determine the shape of the course. Planets along the course can be chosen at random, and each racer is assigned a Home world and a Rival world, which provide benefits and disadvantages during the race. Who will demonstrate their superior technology and racing skills by crossing the finish line first and win the Groton Cup?

Play Speed cards to move your Rocket along the track and to break orbit from the planets along the way. Status cards played against you by other players will slow you down, but can be repaired using matching Remedy cards. Equipping your ship will protect you from Status cards, but if the status is powerful enough it could destroy your equipment!

The game also features a unique 'Last Place' rule, where the player at the back of the pack gets an extra turn each round to try and catch up to the rest, or blow past them if the circumstances permit. Last Place can change hands multiple times each race, and it's really not a bad place to be!

From the back of the rules book;

STARGRUNT is a rules system for Small-Unit miniatures battles in a Science-Fiction environment.  It is usable for Any background storyliune, and with almost any range of 25mm (or 15mm) miniture figures.

Rules are included for Infantry, Combat Vehicles, Air Support, Inderect Fire and lots more.

User Description;
Stargrunt is a generic set of rules for Squard level SF wargames, which means each side have few units of about ten figures in each with a few vehicles.  The rules use poly dice, from d4 to d12 to repersents the experence level of each troop type and inchies for movement and range.

The rules are usable with any ranges of miniatues and thier are some recommedations in the rules since GZG miniatures and vehicle ranges was not that big when the rules was publishied in 1990.

Re-implemented by:

    Stargrunt II

From the back of the rules book;

This Manual is a supplement for the "Stargrunt" 25mm Squard-Level Combat System from Ground Zero Games.

This booklet Contains:


    Over eighty different weapons and systems, with full game stats and details!
    Additional and optional rules for "Stargrunt"!
    Games stats for many of the vehicles available from GZG Daemonscape.

Exchange Square is a stock market simulation board game where players compete to become the first to reach Master Trader.

Buy and sell assets and ride on the economic highs to make the most money. Beware of the bubble bursting as other players back-stab you to get ahead in the rat race!

This turn based roll and move game involves both team work and individual insight. Players can buy four assets (Gold, Oil, Technology and Property) and then manipulate the prices of their assets by using Insider Knowledge cards. Fortune may smile on some lucky traders with the Tax & Benefits cards, however beware of the Event cards which can change everything in one move for all the players!

Shoot-A-Loop is a tin loop with a spring that launches marbles to the top where they will drop down into scoring holes. There are seven scoring holes with values of  25, 0, 50, 100, 75, 0 and 250 points. Included are three marbles in different colors.

The instruction sheet includes five games:

Triple Play - Each player shoots all three marbles, one at a time. The player then adds up the points for the round. The player with the highest total after three rounds wins.

Lucky Triple Play - Played the same as Triple Play, except a player picks one marble to be "lucky". That marble is worth double points.

1500 or Bust - Each player shoots all three marbles, one at a time. The first player to score a total of exactly 1500 points wins. If a player exceeds 1500 points, they bust back to 1250 and play continues.

Shoot Ten - Each player takes a turn shooting one marble ten times. After all players have taken a turn, the scores are added up. The player with the highest total wins.

Call Your Shot - Played like Shoot Ten, except that a player gets a chance to double their score by calling their shot.

In Pyramyz players will be tranported to a silent arena of minimalist appearence. In this strange place we see portals of energy that seem to connect with another reality and give rise to mysterious colored pyramidal shapes that float. These pyramids move controlled by an invisible force to dominate areas of this strange and unusual place of combat and strategy.

Pyramyz is an abstract game of area control. It is for 2 players and use D4 like pieces. In each round, the players must put new dices in board OR move dices that are already in play; The idea is let dices of same color orthogonally connected because that way they "lock"  and the players earn points.

One player plays with the black color and the other with the white color. When the dice lock orthogonally to bind the points are distributed according to the number of the dice and the color of space it occupies. A movement based in "jumps" by rows of dices and the special areas of launch granted many strategies and new high gameplay in Pyramyz.

This is a solitaire card game which takes its inspiration from the classic game of Patience.

The object is to ride a clear round with your horse.

The cards have two types, Stride cards and Jump cards. First you have to try to make your horse take the required strides to the fence, this might be 8 for example. Then you turn over cards three at a time, like in Patience, and you can add the third exposed card to the table. If you do, the second card will become exposed and that can be used. Once you have a total of eight (in our example) from 2 or more cards, you switch to the Jump cards which are dealt with in a similar way. Eventually you ride over the 10 Jumps required to finish the course.

When you are blocked in your attempts to jump (a combination of 1, 2 and 3 required in that order) then you can choose a card that is not required. This will result in four faults or worse.

Extra rules include racing against the clock and playing with more players who each take turns to get their horse around the course whilst the other riders watch.

from inside box lid:
"George Blanda's Monday Nite Touchdown is a fun game for ages seven through adult. It is also educational as it will help those who are learning to add, subtract, multiply and those who are trying to learn the fundamentals of football. It is played like real football with actual rules and techniques applied."

There are twelve dice total, six each of two colors. Each player has five dice of one color and one of the opposite color, which is his "captain". The board is a cardboard stylized football field with plastic sliders moving back and forth in slots to track the ball position on the field, the first down position, and the current down. Real time is used as the game clock.

In general, the game follows the play of a regular NFL match, with kickoffs (including touchbacks), rushing, passing, touchdowns, PATs, field goals, and punts. Players switch ends of the “field” at the half. Fumbles, interceptions, and even safeties are also provided for. Play consists of both players rolling their dice and comparing the results, with the higher total prevailing.

If a player mis-adds his dice, a 15-yard penalty is assessed.

boardgame: The Donner Party: the Card Game
description:
Disclaimer:  This game in no way trivializes the tragedy that befell the Donner Party expedition in 1846-1847 but rather is an attempt to recreate the challenges it faced to better appreciate the difficult choices that were made.

In May of 1846 a wagon train left Missouri bound for California, lured by dreams of a new life of wealth and prosperity.  The pioneers chose George Donner as their leader, and in an effort to shave 350 miles off the trip they took an alternate route through Utah known as Hastings Cutoff. This turned out to be a mistake, and subsequently the wagon train became trapped by early winter snow blocking the Frémont Pass in the Sierra Nevada Mountains.  The pioneers made camp to wait out the winter at Truckee Lake and Alder Creek.  Starvation, illness and even cannibalism took their toll, and out of the 87 original members of the Donner Party only 48 made it out alive.

Players each control a family of pioneers with the goal of reaching California.  The game starts in November of 1846 with food supplies dwindling and more winter storms on the horizon.  Each player receives a cabin and six pioneer dice that represent your pioneers' conditions: Healthy, Lean, Starving, Sick (Dying) or Dead.  Each round starts with an event after which players take turns placing their pioneers on action spaces until the Starvation Phase when they need to feed their families with food tokens and heat their cabins with wood tokens.

Player interaction is maximized by the Beg action which takes food or wood from another player and the Desperation action which is how to draw and play Desperation cards that are either beneficial for the player or punish opponents.  Some card effects and actions to gain food earn player cannibalism tokens, which penalize a player's score at the end of the game. Periodically during the game a Rescue event occurs and players must choose whether to gamble some of their pioneers on a risky rescue attempt over Frémont Pass or wait until the thirteenth turn of the game when all pioneers left in their cabins are automatically scored.

The object of the game is to deliver as many of your six pioneers as you can to Sutter's Fort alive and minimize your cannibalism token penalty.
  
  "The needs of the many outweigh the needs of the few or one." -Spock, The Wrath of Kahn.

"It's fun to get together and have something good to eat at least once a day." -Julia Child

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – Reach of the Kraken
description:
Game description from the publisher:

As the Great Houses of Westeros play their game of thrones, their battles and bids for power necessitate they maintain strong fleets of warships, lest their opponents find their shores ripe for the picking. Naval superiority is the call of the day, especially while the krakens of House Greyjoy sail the coastlines in their longships.

The Chapter Packs from the A Song of the Sea cycle introduce the challenge enhancement mechanism to A Game of Thrones, and Reach of the Kraken allows players to make good use of the new naval enhancement. No fewer than ten characters from Reach of the Kraken feature the enhancement, which allows players to have them join a challenge as an action – even after the challenge's attackers and defenders have been declared!
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
User Summary

The Master Blaster Promo Card is a weapon card that attaches to the Doom Buggy. When bodged it requires one other player (of the owner's choice) to discard a card. If a Doom Buggy with a Master Blaster attached wins a rush, then the owner may wreck two pieces of equipment belonging to any other player(s) in the game.

When played during a rush the card can defend any color, but then attacks any color of the player's choice.

From the publisher's site:

The second release in the Wormhole series, brings two entirely different races into the conflict. The Verus Republic, a vast and sprawling nation with many different worlds, races and cultures has a long history of conflict. External foes both many and varied have assailed and been assaulted by the Republic, and internal conflicts with the Nomad Cotaries and various Pirate fleets have kept the legions hard at work.

Nothing quite compares, though, to the sudden appearance of the displaced Sathrican Empire appearing on and within their borders, making claim to larger portions of their space in a bid to reestablish themselves. Petty differences will need to be set aside, and less pressing matters put on hold, while this new and grave threat is dealt with.

Mondo Micro is a 2 player Expansion for Mondo Sapiens, and is also compatible with Mondo.

The new player board lays between both players and will be played with the "first-time" rules, except the timer will be set at 3 only minutes.

The board has two different player boards (double-sided).

It also contains a small variant: Before a round choose 2 identical tiles (various animals are allowed). Place one with random direction on one of the fields which differs to the colors of the other fields. The second player has to use the 2nd tile in the same orientation.

The fast-paced pig race game for 2, 3, or 4 players. Shuffle the cards and each player draws one to find out which pig he will use in the game. With the hogs in the starting places, each player begins "hollering" to get his piggy moving down the trail toward the pen. The first hog into the pen is the winner and gets an award. Win the majority of the awards and you're the grand prize hog hollerer.

(Players holler into long tubes and the vibrations of their voice makes the hogs move down the tracks.)

You and your investors have worked long and hard to scrounge together enough cash and loans to purchase the rights to three drugs (one in research, one in development and one in production) to develop and produce at your small pharmaceutical company.  With a little luck and a lot of hard work you hope to grow your company into an international conglomerate and take your place among your peers in Big Pharma.

Big Pharma is a game of balance.  You must balance your cash flow, efficiency, research, development and production, and manage the flow of your drugs from the research lab all the way to the production facility.  To complicate matters, the FDA is constantly looking over your shoulder to make sure you’re doing things right.  If they find you out of compliance, you could be paying some heavy fines or even face plant shutdown!

R&D Cards are available on ArtsCow here: http://www.artscow.com/gallery/playing-cards/big-pharma-revised-kywqcg1kv23k

This is a strategy game for 2-8 racers.  The game aims to tell the "story" of Le Mans in 4 sets of races (rounds).  There are 246 cards that tell the store of the race and allow the players to move their cars along the track.  Each racer also has an endurance and various penalties can show up that will reduce their endurance.

You collect cards on your turn (6 cards max in your hand) and try to collect 3-5 cards from the same family; this will give you a bonus in your move or give you additional endurance to last longer.

You can also play on teams, with drivers on the same team, taking a team counter.

Rules are in both French and English.

The games was published in conjunction with the Automobile Club de l'Ouest (ACO).

In Iron Reign, you play the role of a king competing to expand your kingdom while battling to keep opponent's kingdoms in chaos. Glory is obtained by raining down Iron munitions on your enemies' castle walls. This turn based strategy game allows players to experience new adventures and tactics in every campaign.

Iron Reign is played in a series of phases that make up each round. Kings must first manage their kingdom's Gold. Purchase an army of troops, a fleet of ships, an arsenal of cannons, kingdom specialists, and most importantly new castle walls to expand your kingdom. Move your troops and fleet into battle. Take aim and let the Iron fly; this is a key part to setting back and crippling your enemies expansion. Hopefully you purchased enough new wall segments to repair your castle and expand into new areas on the map. If you can't repair at least one of your castles your reign has ended and your enemy has claimed victory. After a tough round of battle deploy new units on the board and prepare for the next round of battle to begin.

Some struggles will end in a kingdom falling to an enemy while others will result in one king expanding the kingdom and taking over so much territory that victory is claimed. So many strategy combinations can be put together for victory. Eight unique kingdom specialists (occupations) can be hired to change your kingdom's strategy. These range from markets that yield extra gold to workshops that convert troops into massive land roving siege weapons. What will be your strategy, quick expansion or maybe an arsenal of cannons?

boardgame: Combat Commander: Battle Pack #5 – Fall of the West

description:
Ukraine 1941 shows the very first days of Fall Barbarossa - a German attack on Soviet Union. 
Even though the Red army seemed very powerful its front line was almost annihilated during the first days of the campaign. But Stalin did also had tremendous reserves heading on the front...

This is the second game of K-2 system presenting southern front of German assault through steppes of Ukraine towards Kiev. Soviets seem to be hampered at first but as games progresses the problems for Germans arise.

Game allows to play first month of Fall Barbarossa in Southern part of Soviet Union. Map covers Eastern Poland, middle and Western Ukrainian territory and provide German, Romanian, Hungarian, Slovakian and Soviet forces that were involved in the region. Both Players must control as much strategic cities as they can at the end of the game.

There are 2 scenarios in the game: German initial assault on June 22nd and full battle which started when Romanian forces joined on July 1st.

A car racing game through Finland for 2 to 6 players. Players start the race from Helsinki and must visit Rovaniemi, before heading back to Helsinki.

Each player chooses a route order card he must obey, when driving from control point to another control point. The game includes also chance cards for unexpected events that may slow down or advance his race. When a player gets back to Helsinki, he must drive additional acceleration and brake test before the race is over for him.

A player with least penalty points is a winner of the Suomi-Rally.

Objective: collect as many star chips as possible
 - acting by turns -
cards (=items) are dealt - 5 per person:
5 places: (bookshelf, chimney, table, broom cabinet, herbal rack) must be equipped with items out of players' hands.
Active player may drop a matching card at that place Bibi is present; dropped card shows next place Bibi must be moved to; if active player has a matching card it may be dropped, too...
for each dropped item player receives a starchip; your turn ends at a space you can't drop an item; draw as many cards as you've dropped.
Game ends if you can't take new cards or if one of the 5 places is fully equipped (7 items).

Two player trumps game. Divide the cards evenly and stack them face-down in front of you. Both players draw a card and reveal it.
There are a number of possible results:

Predator card (P) On the bottom of the card are silhouettes of creatures it will eat. If the card revealed by your opponent features that silhouette, the card is 'eaten'. Both cards are taken by the Predator and put on the bottom of his stack.

Food card is a creature that could be eaten so it is vulnerable. A Food card cannot beat anything.

Wildlife Rescue Card will beat a Predator.

Man Hunter card beats all cards except Wildlife Rescue cards.

Neutral (N) cards are just that. They cannot eat any card and they cannot be eaten.

If a situation arises where the exposed cards cannot beat each other then reveal two more cards and check again. This is repeated until one card takes the other and the winner of that duel takes all the revealed cards.

The main cards are in four colours but the colours only link to a region of the world. The colour itself has no other relevance.

The game is endorsed by the World Wildlife Fund (WWF).
First player to capture all the cards, wins.

Objective: gain most horse cards
 - acting simultaneously -
all horse cards are placed in the middle of the table - uncovered. Each player gets a playing board with images of these horses.
Active player flips a card: first player who shouts 'Stop' gets the card; wrong? an already gained card must be discarded.
Game ends after all cards are caught.

An Onkey is something that gets whacked by a Bonkey. For example, a Bonkey (Hammer) whacks an Onkey (Nail).

Shuffle all the Onkey cards and place them in a face-down stack. The first player turns up the top card. The other players look at their hand of nine cards and if they have the correct Bonkey, they should try to be first to place it on top of the Onkey and shout out "Onkey Bonkey". If first, that player takes the Onkey Bonkey (a matching pair is called an Onkey Bonkey). If you have a Blank Bonkey card you can also try to be first and cover the Onkey card. If you do, you don't get an Onkey Bonkey but you do stop another player from doing so.

The game is played over two rounds and the one with the most Onkey Bonkey's, wins.

There are a couple of errors on the box. It says it is for two to four players but clearly two players cannot play it (the player turning over the Onkey card cannot compete, only the other players, so in a two player game this would be pointless). And the publisher claims it is an educational game! That is stretching the game description to say the least.

Munchkin Apocalypse: Mars Attacks! is a 15-card booster for Munchkin Apocalypse (or any Munchkin core game) that uses art from the original 1962 Mars Attacks! trading card series. In this booster, you'll battle "Monster Bugs" and the "Heat Ray Saucer" on your way to Level 10, with the help of new goodies like the "Martian Zap Rifle" and "Giant Spiky Feet". But watch out for the new Seals – you could end the world with "Reality TV"! (It makes total sense to us.)

This game includes 13 Munchkin Apocalypse cards (8 Treasures, 5 Doors) and 2 Seals. All of the artwork is from the 1962 Topps trading card set, Mars Attacks!

Put down your cell phone and reach for Cut the Rope, the Mattel game that brings the excitement of the video game to a board game. The monster Om Nom waits at the top; you need to cut ropes to bring him down. Along the way, you face obstacles and dangers that keep you from getting the best score. Each rope has a variety of stars; the winner is the person who collects the most.

Om-nom, munch! Based on the award winning Cut the Rope video game and top selling iPhone and Android application for children, this Cut the Rope Game lets you get hands-on while feeding the hungry monster, Om-nom, by cutting the ropes that hold the candy he wants. Ages 5 and up.

From the company website:

DYSTOPIAN LEGIONS is a fast-paced, action-packed 28mm scale tabletop game set in the exciting world of Dystopian Wars, where Victorian super science fiction has created a fascinating and brutal arena for a deadly world war. The game is scalable from a small number of models right the way up to a full blown company of men with armoured support. A modular approach to army building guarantees the game embraces further new releases and game expansions.

Mennonite Manners is a simple fun and crazy dice party game. All you need is a sheet of paper per player, one pencil, and one six sided die. The first player to write out the numbers 1-100 wins.

Place the pencil in the middle of the playing area. Players each roll the die in turn until someone rolls a six. That player takes the pencil and starts writing out the numbers 1-100 as fast as they can. The other players continue rolling the die in turn. When someone else gets a six they gently grab the pencil from the player currently writing and start writing 1-100 on their sheet. Continue until someone gets to 100. Numbers may be checked for legibility.

The game doesn't sound like much, but usually generates a lot of laughter, especially when someone is just beginning to write and the pencil is grabbed away. If grabbing pencils sounds dangerous then you can try ringing a bell to stop play in order for the writing player to give the pencil.

Oikodomos is a civilization game that focuses on resource management, and that is characterized by flexibility and simplicity. Any unit can perform various roles – explorer, worker, priest, scientist, soldier – at different times during the game, and the modular game board further contributes to the game’s flexibility. Moreover, Oikodomos can be played with any number of players for which components are available: one, two, six, or even twelve if necessary. (Playing time depends on the number of players, however: for experienced players approximately 90 minutes per player; for new players a bit more.)

As in any civilization game, players start with a small tribe (represented by a single unit), build their first city, and start developing technology. More cities are built, and the land around those cities are cultivated to feed the growing population. With population growth, however, dissent rises, and leaving that unchecked results in revolt.

Throughout the game, players should balance growth, development, and the 'happiness' of their citizens. Losing that balance may turn out disastrous later. Too much focus on technological development, for example, may hamper population growth, or leave insufficient means to counter growing dissent; while too much focus on economic growth may hurt scientific progress.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Tribal Wars are over and the land is now divided into many kingdoms, reigned by iconic, mighty Lords, Kings and Queens who have emerged from the realm of myth and lore. Deep inside of the cold, snowy Northern Mountains, Isengrimm resides in his Golden Halls to rule over the Dwarfs, who delve deep into the rock to fill their lord's treasuries with gold, silver and precious gems. In Darkwood Forest, Xilvox reigns supreme over his countless Alben minions. Known as the King of the Clouds, Goldfeather is the lord of all Griffins and ruler of the Skies. On the southern islands Lord Zephiros, the Master-Mage, has his domain and guards many arcane secrets which are the key to his enormous magical powers.

The fourth expansion to Elemental Clash – Unicum – brings you 72 exciting, all-new cards. Command mighty "Unique" Creatures or cast powerful "Unique" Spells (cards which are so powerful your Spellbook can only have one copy of them). Also, a new ability – Immunity – is introduced and four new, exciting races make their debut: Albs, Griffins, Fish and Dwarfs.

The aim of the game is to reduce the cards in your opponent’s card-deck (or Spellbook) to zero by using the Spells and Creatures in your Spellbook. Players take turns that always follow the same structure. For each point of damage a Player receives through Creatures or Spells, he or she puts the top card of his or her deck on his or her Discard Pile (or Archive). If a player unable to draw a card, he or she loses immediately.

Card game made as a promotion by the biscuit people, McVitie's.

The game has 34 cards: 15 sets of 2 cards each and 4 Joker Cards. Each set of 2 cards represents one "It's a Knock Out" game. It's a Knockout was a hugely successful TV show that featured people representing a  were invited to play very silly games. See the illustration where a man must ring a bell but he has to climb a greased slope to get to the bell. This would be a very typical It's a Knockout game.

The cards must be well shuffled and the complete pack dealt between the players. If some players get more than others, this does not matter in the normal course of the game. Every player looks at his cards and tries to find groups whose words and pictures fit together. Two fitting cards will be called a game, and the player who has one can lay it down when it is his turn to play. (If a player retains a 'game' in his hand he runs the risk of having the cards taken by his opponents.)

Each player holds his cards in his hand in such a way that the picture side cannot be seen by the other players. A nominated player starts and draws one card from his neighbour. If with this card he can form a game he can lay it down. Then his right neighbour draws one of his cards, and so on anti-clockwise. The first player to complete all his games shouts ’Knockout’ and places all his cards on the table.

The players then total up the number of points scored by each competitor on the basis of 5 points for each completed game, with the exception of the 'marathon' game (denoted by letter M) which qualifies for 10 points.

The Joker can be used at any stage of the game to complete the other half of a two card 'game', but this particular game only qualifies for standard points, i.e.5 If the Joker card is laid on top of a matching 2 card game this entitles the player to double points for the one 'game', i.e.10 points. The player with the highest number of points is declared the winner.

In Indigenous people of Taiwan, players are tour guides who plan a trip around the beautiful island of Taiwan. You will also reach deep in the mountains to experience the cultural variety of different tribes. The player who manages to spend the least on travel will become the best tour guide this journey!

Indigenous people of Taiwan is both a standalone game and an expansion for Hello Taiwan! that allows for up to six players to enjoy a fruitful trip in Taiwan.

玩家扮演領隊導遊角色，帶各位來趟臺灣環島旅行，東南西北共四個方向，並且深入原住民部落，體驗原住民祭典文化。在這場旅程中花費最少的旅費的玩家將成為最專業的旅遊達人！

Integrates with


    Hello! Taiwan

Goal Chance is a simple, quick-result soccer replay/simulation game which allows you to complete entire seasons very quickly and accurately with goal scorers and times of goals.  Gameplay consists of rolling the dice and referring to the player/team statistics on the team set cards and the game charts to determine which team won the match.

Goal Chance can be used with the expansion team sets from the following of Terry Goodchild's other soccer games:
Full Time, Soccer Replay or Final Score

although it is a much faster and simpler than those games, mostly used to get quick match results.

It comes with a rulebook and some charts.  Two 6-sided and 1 10-sided dice are also needed to play.

It can be purchased alone or with Full Time.

description:
This is the brutal ground-pounding side of combat using massive forces of tanks, ground attack craft, mechanical walkers and heavily armoured infantry. This is the part of the invasion when you use your major assets to smash your enemy, this isn't about pretending you are taking a planet with 10 tanks - this is the part when you tactically deploy waves of armour to clear the way before you, pulverising any fool who stands in your way. We call this PLANETFALL and it will be presented using 10mm scale miniatures.

Source: Publisher blurb

Planetfall is a miniature tabletop wargame, set in Firestorm Armada universe. Game uses 10mm miniatures and dice to reproduce future combat during plant invasion. Planetfall if one of three game systems (second is Conquest and third Spec Ops), and focuses on big battles between tanks, and heavy mechs.

Firestorm Invasion: Planetfall is designed to be a fast and furious game of massed armored combat. The game mechanics have been kept simple and easy to learn, allowing players to quickly get to grips with the tactical use and deployment of their key military assets.

Dice are an integral part of FI:P, adding an element of chance to represent the many varied factors present on the battlegrounds of the far-future. Generally, players will be asked to roll a number of D6 and add up the total number of ‘hits’ or ‘successes’. In most circumstances each roll of 4, 5 or 6 will be a success, although various effects can cause this ‘to hit’ number to change.

Every Squadron in FI:P has an Activation Card. These cards are used to add an extra level of tactical game play. These are supplied as blank cards, so that you can mark them to distinguish between your Squadrons. Activation Cards have two purposes in the game; they are used to control a player’s Order of March and are also used to plan a player’s reserves.

Each Turn during a game of FI:P each player will have a number of Command Points. The number available is calculated at the beginning of each Turn. These are then used throughout the Turn to improve Command Tests, play Game Cards and to allow Squadrons to perform more complex actions.

Game Cards in FI:P are used to represent a wide range of unplanned events, electronic warfare systems, direct command intervention and sheer luck! They add an
additional level of tactics and learning when and how to use them brings a dynamic element to the game.

A game of FI:P consists of a number of Turns, with each Turn broken down into the following main phases:

1. Command Point Phase
2. Order of March Phase
3. Turn Card Phase
4. Initiative Phase
5. Squadron Activation Phase
6. End Phase

END OF GAME

At this point, players should check to see if the Scenario’s Victory Conditions have been satisfied by either player, if the Time or Turn Limit has expired or if the roll for Variable game length should be made. If the game has ended, then Victory Points should be calculated for both sides, depending on the Scenario played, and the winner should be determined.

The winner of a game of FI:P is normally decided by comparing Victory Point Totals after the final Turn has been played, unless a Scenario is being played that gives alternative Victory Conditions.

MULTI-PLAYER GAMES

Although it is preferable for everyone to have their own deck of Game Cards, it is possible share a deck. Each player will need to draw from a shared deck and discard to a shared Discard Pile.

Important Note: If a deck is used by more than one player, where possible, allies, rather than opponents, should use a shared deck.

You may not play cards on your allies’ models unless the card text specifically allows you to. In games with more than two players per side you may ONLY play cards to benefit models you control or to hinder enemy models.

A soccer replay/simulation/management game in which dice rolls and charts are used to simulate the management and gameplay results of soccer teams.

Paraphrased from the Owzat site:

Team Squad Ratings, attack and defence are used separately, reflecting the squad strengths of the teams. These are constructed on a goals scored and conceded basis using the final league table to ensure an accurate statistical base. Individual team ratings are used for Injuries and Suspensions, for Injuries reflecting the effect of an injury to a key player of players on the Squad strength of the team, thus ensuring that the teams with smaller and less flexible squads will suffer more severely from injury absentees, for Suspensions penalising those teams that have proved over the season that they are rarely able to field a full strength side because of this reason (e.g. Arsenal, Leeds). Cash is allocated to each club, allowing the rich to enter the transfer market more freely than their less wealthy rivals, provided the right player comes along of course. Transfers in and out, injuries and suspensions are dealt with in a fairly abstract way, as is necessary in a game that is about teams and not individuals. Opportunities will arise to strengthen the Attack/ Defensive Squad Values, players may have to be sold which have the reverse affect, all without names but all having an immediate effect on team strengths and performances.

A day's fixtures, whether it be just a couple of mid-week matches or a full Saturday/Sunday programme, are valued as a complete package before any match is resolved. This involves checking for Injuries and Suspensions on a team-by-team basis and then assessing which Goal Card to use for each time by deducting one team's Defence Squad value from the opponent's Attack Squad value and rounding the result down to a figure divisible by ten. When all matches have been valued, a roll of two pairs of coloured dice per match will produce a half time score line, a second roll for each will give the full time result. Book keeping consists of marking up the result on the Results Matrix and up dating both the Cumulative Points and Goals For and Against Charts.

Wild Patterns  - A game of fast perception

Wild Patterns  is a family board game offering a mixture of fast perception, problem solving, and tactics.

The aim is to find patterns shown on your cards from the different colored wooden tiles constantly moving around the game board. The trickier the pattern, the more points they give. Constant changes within patterns cause sudden and surprisingly tricky situations.

You better be fast, because patterns can be scored at any time, even during other players' turn.

Keep focused, wild patterns are on the move!

Game duration is less than half an hour. The Game can be played by 2-6 players from ages 8 and up. Rules are simple and quick to comprehend.
Wild Patterns can be played as a short & easy or a long & tricky version.

---

Villit Kuviot  - nopean hahmottamisen peli

Villit Kuviot on nopeaa hahmottamista, ongelmanratkaisua ja taktiikkaa yhdistävä hauska koko perheen lautapeli. Yhden pelin kesto on noin puoli tuntia ja peli sopii sekä lapsille, että aikuisille. Pelin säännöt ovat helpot ja pelin oppii välittömästi.

Pelissä yritetään laudalla olevia värillisiä puukuutioita liikuttelemalla muodostaa kädessä oleviin kortteihin merkittyjä kuvioita. Pelin voittaa pelaaja, joka saa valmiiksi eniten korttien mukaisia kuvioita.

Pelissä pelilaattojen muutokset aiheuttavat hauskoja ja yllättäviä tilanteita. Pisteitä voi jopa tehdä vastustajien pelivuorojen aikana. Nopea visuaalinen hahmottaminen on tärkeää. Peli sopii kaikenlaisille pelaajille iästä riippumatta.

Pelaaja voi valita oman pelitaktiikkansa. Rakentamalla pieniä helppoja kuvioita saa vähemmän pisteitä, mutta enemmän kuvioita valmiiksi. Rakentamalla vaikeampia isoja kuvioita, pelaaja saa enemmän pisteitä, mutta ottaa riskin, että kuviota ei saakaan valmiiksi.

Nuoremmat lapset voivat pelata peliä ilman vaikeimpia kuvioita, kun taas haastetta kaipaavat voivat jättää pelistä helpot kuviot pois. Kun kokemus kertyy voi mukaan ottaa vaikeimmat erikoiskuviokortit, jotka antavat lisähaastetta.

Objective: Gain as many cards (=witches' brew) as possible
 - acting by turns -
flip top 'brew' card:
active player chooses one of the ingredients (sticks) out of the witches' vessel; if it matches you may draw another (if needed); wrong? Place stick(s) back into the vessel, next players turn.
If you finished a brew: keep the card. 
Drawing a spell? Turn the vessel at 180° 
Game ends after all cards are gone.

The game can be played with questioning and answering by points or by time. Who is the first to score predetermined points or has the most points after a certain time is the winner..

+6 Bag o' Radioactive Munchkin d6 contains six Munchkin-head dice and four new Munchkin Apocalypse cards. Three of the dice are fluorescent green with orange pips, and three are fluorescent orange with green pips. The cards all require the use of the dice, although you could substitute regular dice if necessary.

The cards are as follows:

     Classified - (Treasure) An item enhancer that gives a variable bonus based on rolling a radioactive d6.
     Carpet Bomb - (Treasure) An item that gives combat bonuses to the players and monsters, based on rolling all of the radioactive d6s.
     The Beast - (Door) A level 20 monster that has an additional effect based on rolling all of the radioactive d6s.
     Lots of Luck! - (Door) Based on the roll of all of the radioactive d6s, either open a Seal, close a Seal, or go up a level.


Part of the Munchkin series.

"Beir & Bones" is the 3rd Game Book in a series for the worlds foremost Black Powder Fantasy game; Flintloque. Contains full rules for Character driven Section level skirmish gaming along with full background for the Kartoffelbreg theatre of the Mordredian Wars. Plus Army information for the Nations located thier.

Character creation and Section rules, magicke, skills, traits, flaws both generic and racial specific.

Also Solo play rules and much more.

Flintloque is a tabletop miniature game which takes place in a parody fantasy setting loosely based upon early 19th century Europe and the Napoleonic wars. Features many standard fantasy races such as dwarves, elves, and orcs but also has some unusual races like bunnies, otters, and rats.

Fast playing with minimal bookkeeping, it is a radical departure from previous versions of the game. It is sure to appeal to more casual gamers as well as Flintloque veterans.

Fanny is a nice and helpful instructor. She tells you all about riding and horse care, how you have to hold the reins, and how you scrape the horse's hoof etc.
 Players choose their horse and starts racing. In his turn player rolls the dice and draws a card collecting various stuff he needs. If he must jump over the hurdles he needs horseshoes etc. or continues his ride until he collects things he needs

Who finish the race first is the winner.

Great fun for two little racers.

Player rolls the color dice in his turn and it shows the color of the next space player may advance with his car.
Who finishes first is the winner.
A simple roll and then read the results of the play, football game from the early 1940's.

Players are on a trek around the world, trying to take photographs of the things they see, but somehow all the vowels are missing!  Complete the words by finding the missing vowels and you will move ahead on the Game Board.

Each player receives three Word Cards, and places them face up in front of them.  On your turn, draw a Vowel Tile and check whether it can be added to any of your own Word Cards.  If not, check whether it can be added to another player's Word Cards.  If it can, and it has no prior vowels added to it, you may exchange one of your empty cards for theirs and play your Vowels Tile.

If a player completes a word, they move ahead on the Game Board as many spaces as the length of that word (3 letter words move 3 spaces, etc).  The player must spell the word aloud for everyone to hear as they move their pawn on the board, then discard their used Word Cards and Vowel Tiles, and draw a new card.

The first time any player crosses either of the wooden bridges, recycle all the used Vowel Tiles from the Discard Tray back into the draw area, and rearrange them.  The first player to reach the Finish space wins!

WHAT THEY'LL LEARN:  The Vowels, Using Vowels to Spell, Word/Picture Recognition

A complete campaign system for operating as Free Traders.

Once you've smashed your enemy with a lightning strike, it's time to take, and then hold, those vital enemy hotspots. This could be fighting over a military base, entering the ruins of a devastated city, or some other strategic objective. This is where your highly trained infantry, with armoured support, come into their own. We call this CONQUEST and it will be presented using 15mm scale miniatures.

Source: Publisher Blurb

Conquest is second tabletop wargame in Firestorm Invasion family. This game uses 15mm and dice to recreate fights during planet invasion in Firestorm Armada universe.

Each player controls his forces made of 15mm infantry and vehicles and tries to decimate opponent or fulfil scenario objective.

Lastly, but by no means least, comes the gritty art of daring special operations. Small teams of highly trained troops going man-to-man against the best your enemy can throw at you. Capture a bunker, rescue hostages, eliminate a target or destroy a shield generator - this is what your elite troops trained for. We call this SPECIAL OPS and it will be presented using 28mm scale miniatures.

Source: Publisher blurb

Special Ops is third, and final tabletop wargame in Firestorm Invasion family. It uses dice and 28mm to recreate small scale special operations during final phase of planet invasion in Firestorm Armada universe.

Each player control small force made from 28mm miniatures and tries to fulfil scenario objective or smash his opponent.

Race around the board to get your animals to the safety of the reserve, but be careful to avoid the hazards as you go.  Will help build awareness of animal conservation.  Each player takes a turn throwing their dice and moves one of their animals the appropriate number of spaces.  When you land on a space, follow the instructions written on the space.  The first player to get all their family of animals to safety is the winner.

description:
Childrens card game. There are eight full sets of different Teddy cards. A set consists of five cards, a full-size Teddy and four other cards, each a quarter of the related Teddy.

Deal 7 cards to each player. The first player must play a full size Teddy card if he has one. If he doesn't, he draws a card. Once a full size Teddy card is on the table, the next player can play either another full size Teddy card or can add a quarter card to any matching Teddy currently on the table. You must play a card if you can but you may only ever play one card per turn.

When a set is finished, the player playing the last quarter card on it, scores 1 point. The first player to use all their cards scores 5 points. The winner is the first player to score 11 points.

A single pawn travels on a wall the number of spaces indicated by the dice, but in the direction chosen by the player. The space where the hen stops indicates what action is possible to do - draw, give, receive or exchange card etc. 
The goal is to collect five cards that make up the rhyme.

description:
Pentanque on the table.

There are discs instead of balls and players throw them as close as possible to jack. Player (or team) who has his ball closer to jack is the winner.

Bacchanalia is a narrative game for 3-6 people. Following the rules, each player creates a fictional character – a person accused (justly or not) of a crime against the empire and on the run with her lover. The game starts when the protagonists find themselves passing through Bertinoro, a settlement on the hills of Romagna by the Via Aemilia and close to the Ravenna port.

Unfortunately for them, in that torrid summer, Bacchus and his followers came down to this village surrounded by vineyards. Attracted by the excesses brought forth by intoxication, other gods manifest themselves; cruel Pluto, severe Minerva and the sensuous Venus will play with the destiny of mortals with all the thoughtlessness only gods can have. The player's objective is to reunite with her beloved and run away. Each player in turn draws a hand of cards and, based on the results of the draw, describes a scene from the story for her protagonists. Elements of the scene vary according to which god was more present in the draw. For instance, a scene dominated by Pluto will feature a violent crime in which the protagonist ends up somehow involved. Benevolent Venus reunites lovers. Bacchus brings thrill and excitement, and so on. There is a clear winning condition – reuniting with the beloved and running away before your accuser catches you – but one player reaching it doesn't prevent other players from doing the same; multiple winners are possible.

In the chaos of the bacchanal, will the fugitives be able to reunite with their beloved and get to safety? Or will they meet a harsher end? Will they be caught by their accuser – or will they be lost forever in the rapture of the bacchanal?

Brrr! Scrat shivers on the ice floe. While holding his precious nut, he throws ice cubes from the ice floe. Whoever grabs all the ice-pieces of his color fastest wins the game. All wrong cubes must be put back.

From the packaging:

"An addictive game of strategy versus risk for any number of players. Roll the dice and build your score... but learn to judge when to stop!"

The game comes in a plastic blister pack. It contains a "velveteen" dice pouch, six dice with roman numerals, a black-and-white rule book with historical notes, and a black-and-white score pad.

A turn consists of the player rolling all six dice. If he scores any points, according to a list of scoring combinations (e.g. three "D"s score 500 points), he may then set aside these and roll again, getting the chance to accumulate more points. Alternatively, he can stop rolling and keep his accumulated score. If he rolls a no-scoring throw, then he  scores zero for that turn, even if he had set aside scoring dice from previous rolls. His points are then recorded, and play passes on to the next player. The game ends when someone scores 5000 points; everyone else "with a turn left" then gets a chance to better this player's score.

The historical notes claim that this is a real ancient Roman game, originating from the late republic.

As of writing, this game is being sold in museum shops in the UK, at historic sites related to ancient Rome (e.g. sites on Hadrian's Wall).

This expansion consists of a files providing a new map and station cards for On The Underground, as well as rules document describing the differences to the base On The Underground game required to play it.

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – Lair of the Wyrm
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) - Lair of the Wyrm introduces five new quests that can be played individually or used to supplement a larger campaign. With inventive objectives like rescuing survivors from a burning inn and protecting valuable ore from a greedy ettin, the Quest Guide in Lair of the Wyrm delivers fresh new challenges to your adventures.

What's more, Lair of the Wyrm brings two new heroes to the fight against the forces of evil. Reynhart the Worthy is a fearless warrior whose prowess in combat means he rarely makes mistakes. He can reroll misses once per attack, helping to ensure that every strike is righteous and true. Meanwhile, High Mage Quellen is a master of body and mind, and is able to quickly recover from exertions that would devastate lesser men. Quellen has developed the ability to feed off the fatigue of those around him, gaining strength where his fellows fail.

But even as the heroes of Terrinoth gain new allies and tactics, their great enemy silently grows in sinister power. In addition to the Wyrm Queen herself, a fearsome Dragon Lord lieutenant seemingly formed from solidified flame, the Overlord gains two new monsters: mischievous and destructive Fire Imps, and flying Hybrid Sentinels...half-dragon monstrosities with a cruel penchant for preying on the weak. The Overlord's new advantages are not limited to his minions, however. His new available class, Punisher, specializes in making heroes pay for every inch they advance. With abilities like Trading Pains and Exploit Weakness, the Punisher class will make the hero players flinch with fear every time something seems to go their way.

Also integrates with Road to Legend app (Android, Apple iOS, and Steam).
