description:
Translated box black blurb

School Order Rep has come to investigate any personal items student have brought to school. As a student, you must treat the Order rep correctly so as to avoid punishment.

How?

Well, by seeing to that your BL fanzines aren't "unnecessary" items, of course!

User summary
A simple card game consisting of different types of attractive boys, guys and men (anime style). Players take turns being the Order rep, who decides what preferences are OK, and the remaining players (students) try to play their cards so that they match them as much as possible. A game of deduction and creative guessing. And laughs.

The game also comes with identical rules called "Model Men", where the players try to guess what combination of model will sell the next fashion mag.

The aim of the game is to remove the traffic sign cards from the game board. The player who has gathered most cards is the winner.

Before starting to play, shuffle the 24 cards well and place them on the fields with the blue side upwards and with the poles of the sign posts pointing all in the same direction. No card should be placed on the field with the traffic lights. This field is used to place the pieces. Now agree on a player who starts, and then alternate clockwise. The player whose turn it is throws the dice and forwards his piece on the game board by the number of pips. After starting the game, the field with the traffic lights must not be entered again. You may move in all directions but not diagonally. During a move you may change the direction as often as you want. However, you have to keep two things in mind: you must not move on to a field twice during a move, and at the end of the move you must not arrive at the starting field. Bad luck, if you throw a six, because you will have to miss this turn. If a move ends on a field with a card, you have to guess which of the two signs shown on the blue side is displayed on the yellow side of the card. Subsequently look whether the traffic sign you think is shown on the yellow side. If the guess was correct, the player may take the card. The player places this card with the blue side upwards in front of himself. If the guess was wrong, the player has to return the card to the same place on the game board. If one card only is left on the game board, the player whose turn it is may throw the dice twice and move. If this card has been removed as well, every player counts his cards. The winner is who has most cards.

boardgame: ASL Comp: Orders For The Major – The Battle Of Normandy 1944
description:
A game in the ASLComp series by Critical Hit.

Subject: the Allied Operation Epsom (aka First Battle of the Odon), Normandy, France 1944. Centered around battles near the town of Gavrus.

This is a remake of Scotland the Brave I. As such it contains the same original 9 scenarios, and 1 campaign game, with some minor alterations. The game features new map and counter art.

This game allows you to recreate firefights between British and German troops in Normandy 1944, on a tactical scale: individual vehicles, artillery pieces, support weapons, squads and leaders.
The terrain lends itself for a mixture of close quarter battles around villages and bridges, coupled with long range tank duels, due to the relatively flat, open area with little or no hedges (bocage).

Because of the designer's research and availability of detailed sources on the depicted actions, the scenarios have a highly historically informative value, backed by information and pictures of some of the landmarks in the designer's notes booklet.

boardgame: ASL Comp: Shout For PIATS – The Battle Of Normandy 1944
description:
A game in the ASLComp series by Critical Hit.

Subject: the Allied Operation Epsom (aka First Battle of the Odon), Normandy, France 1944. Centered around battles near the town of Grainville-sur-Odon.

This is a remake of Scotland the Brave II. It contains 12 scenarios, and 2 campaign games, mostly the same as in the original, but with additions (most notably scenarios #21 and #22) and some minor alterations. The game features new map and counter art.

This game allows you to recreate firefights between British and German troops in Normandy 1944, on a tactical scale: individual vehicles, artillery pieces, support weapons, squads and leaders. Combining this game with Orders For The Major, you can play on a large area (campaign games), like in the original combination of Scotland the Brave I + II.

The terrain lends itself for a mixture of close quarter battles around villages and bridges, coupled with long range tank duels, due to the relatively flat, open area with little or no hedges (bocage).

Because of the designer's research and availability of detailed sources on the depicted actions, the scenarios have a highly historically informative value, backed by information and pictures of some of the landmarks in the designer's notes booklet.

Crystal Clash is an easy-to-pick-up yet tremendously strategic card game for two players. One player picks the Light Deck and the other plays the Darkness Deck.

The goal of the game is to bring your opponent down to zero Crystals in their Crystal-Pool.

Each player starts with 10 Crystals (represented by 10-sided dice) and plays Characters, equips them with magical Relics and has them perform powerful Abilities. Your Crystals double as "Life Points" and resources.

At the beginning of your turn you draw 3 cards from your deck, then you may discard a card from your hand and add a number of Crystals to your Crystal-Pool equal to the Crystal Value of the discarded card (which doubles as cost to play the card from your hand). Then, in Main Phase, you may play Characters and Relics and have your Characters attack the opponent. Each unblocked attack results in 2 Crystals being removed from your opponent's Crystal Pool. If at any point a player has no more Crystals left in their Pool, they lose the game immediately.

Crystal Clash consists of two preconstructed, well balanced decks with 40 UNIQUE cards each. All the cards are illustrated in Anime-Style by Manchen Yang (the Darkness Deck) and Laurie Thomas (the Light Deck).


This game is a cooperative effort by Andreas Propst and Nora Propst, Andreas' younger sister.

If you want to take a look at all 40 different Darkness Cards please visit

http://ap.o-nan.org/2012/11/crystal-clash-darkness-deck-all-cards/

If you want to take a look at all 40 different Light Cards please visit

http://ap.o-nan.org/2012/11/crystal-clash-light-deck-all-cards/

description:
Children's game that is very much in the camp of a favourite German game called Malefiz ('Barricade in English').

Each player gets two identical playing pieces. The board features paths from the bottom up to Pride Rock at the top. But the paths get closer together as they go up the board which results in playing pieces getting a little tight to move. Thus, if you land exactly on another playing piece, that piece must return to the bottom row and start again.

Movement is by a die (that includes a spot symbol on two faces). Should you roll a spot you can place a jungle maker on to the board. Or, you can move one on the board to a new location. These jungle markers are blockers which cannot be jumped.

The winner is the first player to get one of his pieces on to the Pride Rock square by an exact throw of the die.

The board game, Moneture, illustrates operation of the National Bank of Serbia  under inflation targeting regime. Players take the role of the Governor and apply appropriate measures to influence the actual situation, win or lose the number of points and maintain inflation within the target range. The points to be won in the game represent success features: inflation value, dinar value and public trust. In addition, for membership in the EU monetary system, three conditions have to be met (Price Stability, Financial Stability and Independence of the National Bank). The winner of the game is the player who first completes all the specified tasks and reaches the end of  the inner circle of the board - average inflation of the European Union.

boardgame: The Amazing Adventures of Fibber McGee
description:
Like the Wistful Vista Mystery versions, this is a word substitution tale and plays proceeds the same, though this is a different book. This edition provides a four-part adventure titled "The Amazing Adventures of Fibber McGee"

The back page of this book features ads for G-Men and Easy Money. Both games of the day also published by Milton Bradley.


"A hilarious party game for any number of players. A riot of fun for everyone - take it from me! - Fibber McGee

...by Arrangement with the National Broadcasting Co. Inc."

A large deck of yellow cards are placed face down on a table in a loose spread out pile. Each card has a few words that make up a partial sentence on them.

One player reads a story out of a booklet and when the reader encounters a blank line ("_______") in the story, they pause and another player quickly draws a card and reads what is on it, then the reader continues.

For example, "My first encounter came after a series of lessons from _A_perforated_fire_hose_ who had slain nine thousand bulls."

Some cards have points on them, so the player who drew that cards would get some points. When all cards have been drawn, the points are tallied and a winner determined.

A push your luck dice rolling game.

Object: "All players try to accumulate the highest number of points per turn, which uhen added to their score, gives them the necessary 10,000 points to win."

Equipment: box, instructions, scoresheets, dice cup and six dice (marked blank, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5).

Scoring: blank = 50, 1=100, 3 of a kind = varies (2-5 = die x 100, blank = 500, 1 = 1000), straight (blank, 1-5) = 1000, and 6 of a kind = 3 of a kind x 3.

A turn-based roll-and-move car racing game based on the zany videogame kart racing mania (Mario Kart, Crash Team Racing, etc.) Each player tries to be first to cross the finish line, but not before equipping weapons cards or playing sneaky tricks and trying to blow the competition out of their way!

The core mechanic of this game is a trade-off between going fast and equipping weapons (which reduces the speed and sturdiness of your vehicle.) Eventually, a player's car will be destroyed, at which point the player will return to the nearest checkpoint and continue racing (or just blowing up their enemies for the fun of it!)

boardgame: Mobile Suit Gundam ZZ: Side One
description:
Tactical combat game based on Japanese mecha animation series Mobile Suit Gundam ZZ. The game covers combat in and around space colonies.

Each unit represent one humanoid weapon called mobile suit (MS). Unit ability is written on separate data cards.

Each player control one or few MS and aims at the destruction of the opponent MS.

Players writes down the plan of the movement and the battle in the log. Then, that is disclose and resolve at simultaneous.

boardgame: Mobile Suit Z Gundam: Sign of Z
description:
Tactical level wargame based on Japanese robotech animation series Mobile Suit Z Gundam. This game covering all battle field and weapons in story.

Each unit represent a mobile suit (giant robot). Unit ability is written on each unit.

The turn sequence is simple, repeat of movement and simultaneous attacking. Some special weapons covered by special rules, such as Mega Bazooka Launcher or Funnel.

Victory condition is stated for each scenario.

Tactical space combat game based on Japanese robotech animation series "Blue Comet SPT Layzner".

Each unit represent a SPT(Super Powered Tracer, robotech name in this universe). Unit ability is written on the data cards.

Each player control one or few SPT and aims at the destruction of the opponent SPT.

Players writes down the plan of the movement and the battle in the log. Then, that is disclose and resolve at simultaneous. The law of the inertia acts on the movement in the outer space. Feature of this game is a rule of search operation using the dual map.

Tactical land combat game based on Japanese robotech animation series "Blue Comet SPT Layzner". The second game for Layzner.

Each unit represent a SPT(Super Powered Tracer, robotech name in this universe). Unit ability is written on the data cards.

Each player control one or few SPT and aims at the destruction of the opponent SPT.

Players writes down the plan of the movement and the battle in the log. Then, that is disclose and resolve at simultaneous.  Feature of this game is a rule of V-Max system, add a six to eight times faster and immune to ranged weapons.

From the objective in the rule book:  "The object of the game is to depart from home, arrive at the hunting camp, go on a gobbler hunt and be the first to "Bag" your gobbler."  Players roll a die to move along the paths in the woods.  Spaces include such activities as "Gas Stop", "Forgot Flashlight", and "Stuck in Swamp", all of which result in losing a turn.  There are also 3 decks of cards: Road Chance, Hunt Chance, and Phone Chance.  Also from the rule book, to win the game, "A player must roll the EXACT NUMBER ON THE DIE to get his gobbler or be lucky enough to draw the 'WINNER CARD' from the green chance pile."

Monster Match is a fast and fun card game where you are trying to reassemble body parts from different monsters. The game suits between 2 to 7 players, for ages 8 and up.

The game consists of one deck that contains all the monster parts. Players are dealt out cards and take turn trying to improve upon there monster(s).

Taking its mechanical roots in games like gin rummy but with a new theme and abilities for cards makes this anything but what your grandmother use to play.

boardgame: Das kleine Ich bin ich: Würfelpuzzle
description:
Objective: be the first completing your puzzle
 - acting by turns -
arrange puzzle tile by colour and build a pile for each player,
symbols on top.
Each player rolls the die:
There is a matching symbol on top of your pile - take it and try to complete your puzzle; if your die shows 'Das kleine Ich bin Ich = joker', take the tile, too, but the 'moon' symbol let you sleep - lose one turn.

A game for foreign learners of the English language.

The game contains more than 1.000 questions in 5 different levels, from beginner to advanced. There are also 5 different categories: vocabulary, grammar, phrases, general knowledge, and miscellaneous.

A die determines which category a question is asked from. It shows five colours or a star (let's the player decide if he/she wants a grammar or vocabulary question). If the player answers the question correctly, he/she wins the card. Cards are collected until one player is ready for the grand finale: The player who is first to answer correctly here wins the game.

The game can also be played in teams.

Grow is a solitaire game where the player guides a growing forest towards safety from the lumber industry.

The Player builds his/her tree through a series of rounds using hex tiles representing branches and leaves. The leaf tiles provides energy (used as Action point) for the player to spend growing his tree further or expanding his tree population.

As the player's tree grows bigger, it also becomes more profitable to cut down. The lumberjack token will become more active and more powerful as the game progresses. The player loses the game when there's no more trees for the lumberjack to cut down!

The game is a free Print-and-play game available to anyone! Counters are provided but standard wooden cube are suggested for convenience.

Introduction
They say that the mythical King Tuu Ku Ihu and the god Make Make possessed the “mana” or divine power to command the Moai to walk. Question is, have you got the mana to move them?
 Moai is a territorial game for 2 players.
 Played on a hex board
 You will also need a Black and a White pawn and a supply of counters.
 Play
Players start by placing their Moai on a space anywhere on the board.
 White moves first, players then alternate turns.
 On a turn
 1) Place two counters on empty spaces in line with your Moai on the board.
 2) Move your Moai towards or away along an empty path from either counter, you have placed.
 When placing counters, you must have empty spaces between those counters and your Moai. Once placed counters are fixed.
A Moai must move a minimum of 1 space toward or away from the counters just placed.
The player unable to take her turn loses the game.

The HomeVesting playing board has two six-sided playing courses: the outer, purple “Rehabilitation” course, and the inner, orange “Selling” course. Each course represents a different stage of the investing process.

The outer “Rehabilitation” course is where we initially buy, carry, and fix up our properties. The game always starts on this outer course.

The inner “Selling” course is where we seek deals to sell our investments and cash out. Players individually move onto the inner Selling path as they complete their Rehabilitation process.

Summary of the Game

Players select investment properties, begin on their individual colored Start spot, and take turns moving their pawn around the outer Rehabilitation path to develop their properties.

Eventually, each player will complete their Rehabilitation phase, progress to their colored Start spot on the inner Selling course, then proceed around the Selling course to seek a deal for cashing out and selling their investments.

Play continues until only one player remains which has not settled all debts and cashed out of the game. All players have the option of short-selling and cashing out of the game at any time before completing their full rehabilitation and selling process, which establishes their final cash position. But a final remaining player is always forced into a short-sell to end the game. All players then evaluate their final cash position. The player finishing with the most money wins.

Top-A-Top Plus is a simple and fast-playing party game in which players take turns placing their cards in the middle of the table and reacting appropriately to the funny pictures on the cards played, e.g., by making special gestures or saying something.

Your reflexes and the ability to make associations are crucial. Your reaction has to be quick and appropriate or else you lose a turn and take all the cards on the table into your pile. The first player to get rid of all of his cards wins.

Top-A-Top Plus is both a standalone game and an expansion for the Spiel 2011 release Top-A-Top, which was game of the year in Romania in the category of children's games.

Integrates with:

    Top-A-Top

UNRELEASED GAME

    
    
         Note that all details for this game are preliminary. If you have information about this game, you are invited to edit this section or submit corrections.

    


Chez Guild blends the characters from the web series The Guild with the Chez Geek series of card games, with characters trying to collect slack while annoying their roommates and keeping them from achieving their goals.

Game by S. Zdorovtsov depicts Napoleon's invasion of Russia on a strategic level. Players command Corps and their goal is to destroy their opponent. This could be done by reducing enemy to 3 or below Corps. French start with 14 Corps and the Russians with 13, but one French Corps leaves play on Turn 9, to simulate Reynier's help to the Austrians. Game mechanic also includes reinforcements, partisans, morale,  and generals.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: IS-48 Super-Heavy Tank – "Lavrentiy Beria / Karl Marx"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The IS-48 Super-Heavy Tank is perhaps the most powerful ground vehicle in the world. Armed with the brand new Heavy Tesla Gun, this tank is a dire threat to infantry and vehicle units alike. Designed by the SSU, the tank's deadly Tesla cannon ignores cover, wipes out out enemy squads in a single burst, and "explodes" for tremendous damage against enemy vehicles. When the IS-48 Super-Heavy Tank takes the battlefield, it instantly becomes the centerpiece of any army.

This versatile expansion allows for two builds of a single tank: the IS-48 Karl Marx or the IS-48 Lavrentiy Beria.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Red Guards Assault Squad – "Red Storm"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The core of the SSU's famous Red Guards Infantry Regiments, the Red Guards Assault Squad consists of some of the best soldiers on the planet. Joining a unit of the Red Guards is an honor for any SSU soldier; it means that he has made it through the selection process and survived one of the world's toughest training programs.
 With their PPSh-48, DPM MG, UGLs, and Molotov cocktails, these Guards are capable of dealing critical damage to unwary foes.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: British Commandos Kill Squad – "Devil's Own / Legio Patria Nostra"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The British Commandos Kill Squad provides players with three carefully sculpted miniatures that they can configure as either a British Commandos Kill Squad or a French Foreign Legion Kill Squad. Either of these Allied Kill Squads can rain death upon enemy infantry with its 50W Phaser Rifles. The British Commandos back up their firepower with the Scout skill while the French Foreign Legion Kill Squad can use its Assault skill to execute a vital tactical maneuver once per game.

Dust Tactics: British Commandos Kill Squad contains three soldiers with swappable heads for two possible unit configurations.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Heavy Antitank Grenadiers – "Jagdgrenadiere / Laser-Jagdgrenadiere"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The development of heavy body armor allowed the Axis to deploy its soldiers into the deadliest of combat zones at lightning speed, often well before they could mobilize long-range support. Axis engineers worked tirelessly to develop long-range weaponry for these elite soldiers, and the result was the Caseless PaK 7.5 cm. With this formidable weapon, the Axis Heavy Antitank Grenadiers are deadlier than ever.

The Heavy Antitank Grenadiers share a single base and come with a special card with three health boxes, like on a hero card. This single expansion comes with options for two separate unit configurations.

Matching card game with topic "World"

There are info or picture on the middle of the card and multiple patterns on the edges. Two or more cards match if at least one pattern fits on all cards.

In the beginning of round multiple cards are drawn from the table. Players who first declare the matching cards (based by their knowlidge or seeing the matching pattern) get the cards.
If there are no matched cards found the new cards are drawn.

Player with the most cards at the end of the game is the winner.

description:
Reverse Charades: Holiday Edition, an expansion pack for Reverse Charades, consists of 220 cards that feature holiday-themed words.

Game play remains the same as in the base game, with players divided into two teams. On a turn, all but one player on a team give charades-style clues to that one person left out, and that person guesses as many words as possible in sixty seconds. After each player on both teams has had a shot at guessing (and both teams have had the same number of turns), the team with the most words guessed wins.

Reverse Charades: Sports Edition, an expansion pack for Reverse Charades, consists of 220 cards that feature sports-themed words.

Game play remains the same as in the base game, with players divided into two teams. On a turn, all but one player on a team give charades-style clues to that one person left out, and that person guesses as many words as possible in sixty seconds. After each player on both teams has had a shot at guessing (and both teams have had the same number of turns), the team with the most words guessed wins.

boardgame: Reverse Charades: Girls' Night In Edition
description:
Reverse Charades: Girls' Night In Edition, an expansion pack for Reverse Charades, consists of 220 cards that feature women-themed words.

Game play remains the same as in the base game, with players divided into two teams. On a turn, all but one player on a team give charades-style clues to that one person left out, and that person guesses as many words as possible in sixty seconds. After each player on both teams has had a shot at guessing (and both teams have had the same number of turns), the team with the most words guessed wins.

THE EPISHELL WARS  Squads of humanoid mecha face off in varying terrains over ever changing goals,commonly resulting in the destruction of the other team.  The game uses a system of 3d12 rolls, with modifiers to the minimum and maximum that may be rolled.  Each roll will have a modifier of +X/-Y (EG. +1/-1, +4/-2, +0/-3).  The +X portion determines the minimum result of each of the d12, and the -Y determines the maximum.  A modifier of +2/-2 would mean that each die can result in the range of 3-10 (1+2, 12-2), the total roll being anywhere between 9 and 30.

The game setup involves a map with 1" hexes of a size predetermined by either a provided scenario or popular decision.  Terrain involves transparent overlays called terrain Plates that have visual cues as to which form(s) of terrain are included (Obscure, Hostile , Hostile , Resistant).  Terrain Plates can overlap, and each terrain type present in a given hex applies in a "yes/no" fashion.

Players agree on the number of units and of which types that each will bring into play.  Given a default game of 1v1 death match, each player will have four Epishell (humanoid mech) units.  For each unit they will decide the variety from Scout, Assault, Marksman, and Escort and apply modular designed equipment based on the carrying capacity of that unit.  Pilots are also assigned to the units with a zero sum trait set.  Typically, each player will have a side of the map to treat as their starting area.  Time and objectives will be determined by popular decision before the terrain is placed and the units outfitted.

During game play, each player has a set number of "MOVE" and "FIRE" actions equal to half of their total unit count, rounded up and may only allot one to each unit at the start of the "Circuit".  A countdown procedure occurs using the traits of the units, giving priority to "FIRE" actions.  Every action is given a small amount of Movement points, with an extra bonus from a "MOVE" action.  Movements points can be used to change facing or location.

From the publisher: Characters from the hit movie, Star Trek, beam down to your HeroClix game! Completely compatible with the core HeroClix Rules system, The Star Trek HeroClix: Tactics Away Team 7-Pack includes pre-painted figures of Kirk, Spock, McCoy, Uhura, Scotty, Sulu, and Chekov, each incorporating a dual-dial design to provide 14 different playable HeroClix dials.

Which number is supposed to bring bad luck in China? When were the Beatles founded? And what is the diameter of the biggest igloo?

In Voll ins Schwarze, all players are guessing these answers and others at the same time – but only the player who gets closest to the right answer scores the point! That is not as easy as it sounds: All players have number cards to use for their guesses, but sometimes those cards don't match the question being asked. Use your cards wisely to win the game!

In Scheibenkleister one player tries to describe a word, term or person that the other players then need to guess – well, most of the other players. One or two opponents are trap makers who also know the word to be described; they try to foresee how the explainer will go about describing the word and lay down traps by secretly writing down two "trap" words each. If either the explainer or one of the guessers uses one of these words, the trap maker who wrote the word scores.

If anyone says a trap word, all trap words are revealed, and the guessers now have one final guess at the solution. Now a correct guess will score points for the trap makers rather than the explainer. Thus, the explainer's challenge is trying to avoid using "obvious words" – which often results in an explanation that is severely cryptical and not very to the point.

Topics: language - count - History - Society - Geography - Earth, space and environment - physics and technology - nature - traffic - English

The questions are on the left flaps, the answers on the right flaps. Turn the right flap to the answer.

Who answers correctly get a point. Winner is the player with the most point.

Objective: gain as little as possible sacrificing points 
 - acting by turns - each player starts with 5 - two-player game, alternative 4 - (coloured) meeples, on top a white (=happy), below a black (=sad) dog; move according die roll (forward or backward); ending on a white space a meeple may be kicked by another one (back to start) and flipped to 'sad' dog. A 'flower' space is safe. Game ends after one player moved all own meeples into target zone (field 25). Depending on position and status (happy/sad) penalty points are assigned to each player. remarks: published ~ 1940

In this card-game you lay down your cards to create words like in scrabble but there are syllables instead of letters on the cards. 
Also, like scrabble you can add your cards to existing cards making words horizontal and vertical. Each card has a value and adding the values of your cards gives your score.

"Can Superboy Escape The Deadly Green Kryptonite?" Board has great action scene showing Superboy with spaceship as missiles fly toward him, and Krypto rides a rocket at the corner. The game ends on a "Kryptonite" square. There is a spinner attached to the cardboard insert and from the board it appears to be a simple spin and move game.

This was one of three games published by Hasbro in 1965 - one each for Batman, Superman and Superboy. SUPERBOY appears to be quite rare and is clearly valued by collectors.

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Team Manager – The Card Game: Sudden Death
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Sudden Death, a bone-breaking, pulse-pounding expansion for Blood Bowl: Team Manager - The Card Game, brings a host of options to the pitch, including three new teams, new Contract payouts, new enchanted balls, and more.

The Dark Sorcery Syndicate union features dabblers in forbidden magics who just don't know when to stay down. The undead Champions of Death tear up the pitch – sometimes literally as they raise new zombies and skeletons from the corpses of former Blood Bowl players. The vampires of the Black Fangs are always thirsty for victory (and blood). And the Dark Elves of the Naggaroth Nightmares are happy to give both teams plenty of fresh corpses with which to play.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Players in QBQ start with four cubes of their color in one corner of the game board, and to win they must move across the board, which is a 6x6 grid, to create in the opposite corner a square of four cubes, a cube of eight cubes, or a square of nine cubes. All other pieces owned by that player must be out of play. On a turn, a player takes one of three possible actions:


     Moving a cube already on the board to a space orthogonally adjacent to the pieces it was taking previously (so the shape "oozes" across the board); you can choose to place the cube on top of another cube of your color.
     Tilting a stack of cubes of your color to make them spill across the board. If you land on an opponent's stack that was shorter than your stack, then the opponent's pieces are removed from play.
     Adding a cube from your stock to an existing stack of yours, whether on the lowest level or above.


Who will reach the other side first?

This wine-themed trivia/party game can be played in two teams or with at least two players.
The object of the game is to win as many cards as possible, by answering questions about wine and performing tasks from the activity cards. Each card is worth 1 point. The first player to reach the finish space on the board wins the game.

Activity cards come in two types: "Picture Memo" and "Sparkling Letters". "Picture Memo" means the active player has to memorize the picture on the card. After 30 seconds, the player on the left makes up a question about the picture, which the active player has to answer. In the "Sparkling Letters" activity, there are three words on the card, with their letters jumbled. The active player has to guess the words while the timer is running.

Doctor Faustus made a pact with the demon, Mephistopheles, to have power over all demons and so become the most powerful magician of all time. You, too, have entered into such a pact, but can you really control what you have unleashed or will they take control of you?

Secrets are power and temptation! Battle with other magicians to ensure that you, and only you, escape the price for such power: Damnation!

Faustus is a game of secrets and temptation. In this game for 3 to 6 players, you take on the role of a magician summoning demons onto yourself and casting them at other magicians whilst tempting them with your hidden secrets. But only one can escape damnation. Only one! One goes free else all are damned!

With two versions of the game and optional rules, this game can be played by both novice and experienced gamers. In the Basic version of the game all that matters on the demons are the numbers; keep your numbers low and doom the rest. In the Advanced game you experience the full power of the demons under your command with each of the 40 Demons offering a unique power that is yours to command while they are in bound to your Pact.

Duell der Formen / Duel of Forms is a 2 player abstract strategy game. The aim of the game is to position six tokens to make a line, triangle or hexagon. The player who manages to do this first wins the game.

Each player gets 12 tokens and takes it in turns to place one of their tokens on the empty board.

Each player attempts to use 6 of the tokens of their colour to make an unbroken line, a hexagon with six equal edges or a triangle with three even edges (including placing a token in the middle of each of edges of the triangle).

If all the tokens have been set out and neither player has won, the players take it in turns to move their tokens to empty spaces. Players can only move one space and cannot jump. Each player must move a token to an empty space during their go and cannot choose to wait.

Variant: Once all the tokens have been set out, players cannot only move but also jump to any place.

boardgame: The Big Bang Theory: For Your Consideration
description:
Object: The object of the game is to travel from Pasedena, through the many challenges of life as a genius, and ultimately arrive in the Nomination Zone, where you might just win as Emmy.

To Begin: Play "Rock, paper Scissors, Lizard, Spock" to determine who goes first (scissors cut paper, paper covers rock, rock crushes lizard, lizard poisons spock, spock vaporizes rock, rock crushes scissors). Each player takes a turnspinning the wheel and moving forward the number of spaces the spinner lands on.

Winning The Game: The first player to reach the Emmy nomination zone wins the game and has bragging rights for one year. They must attend all parties and pose for red-carpet photos. There are no guarantees that the funniest player will win the game, or that the winner will win again next time.
family: TV Shows: The Big Bang Theory

description:
This is a kid game for learning the letters and writing in French.

There is lot of pieces in the form of different objects where the first letter of the word is in-script.  There is also letter pieces.

The game have 4 levels: 
 1) The kid take the objects pieces and name them. The kid who name the more pieces win.
  2) The kid associate the object whit the first letter piece. The kid who associate the more objects win.
  3) Make pair of 2 objects starting by the same letter. The kid who make the more pairs win.
 4) The kid take letters to make words write on a card.  The kid who make the most words win.

This game must be play by a adult helping one or multiple kids.

Sea Titans is the 4th installment to the Arena Assault games.  Battle in an arena of the future as either Iron Beard the Pirate or Megalotron.  Arena Assault is a MASSIVE MULTIPLAYER CARD GAME, meaning there is no maximum number of players.  You are only limited by the number of cards you have.
Despite the large number of players the game can handle, gameplay is always fast, fun and furious.  
Draw your cards, choose who you want to attack and what attacks cards to play.  Defenders may try to defend with defense cards, if available.  All Arena Assault games include 20 gear cards.  Cards that are available to all that do any number of things.

Triovision Master challenges players to move quickly and create certain combinations of shapes.

To start, the nine tokens – three cubes, three cylinders and three pawns, with red, blue and yellow versions of each – are placed randomly on the playing area: a field of 17 squares in a rough cross. Ten cards are revealed from the deck, with each card showing a pattern of colors and shapes in a particular arrangement. The backside of the card on top of the deck shows one of the nine tokens.

Everyone plays simultaneously. Players attempt to form one of the patterns shown on the cards by moving the specific piece indicated on the card back to any empty space on the game board. If the player does this correctly, she claims the card; if not, she's out for the round. Once someone claims a card, the top card of the deck is flipped, revealing a new pattern and the piece that now must be moved.

The first player to collect twelve cards wins.

Translated from the box back
The circle of fate for the girls is starting to turn…

Lylicalion is a double hand management game, where the players compete for the attention of six characters from Lylican Nanoha. The players first draft themselves through the draw deck consisting of magic cards and friendship cards. Once the deck is depleted, cards drafted to a certain face down pile will be auctioned out with the cards players have in their hands. At the end of the game, for each character, the player with the highest total of friendship gains the points that character is worth.

Re-implements:

    Biblios

A game in the Mancala family adapted from Tschuba with a novel starting position. Associated 1891 US patent: number 448,574.

Description following provided by Milton Bradley prior to 1897.

Chuba is an adaptation from a rude game of Eastern Africa which is greatly enjoyed by the natives, who squat on the ground, and play in holes scooped out of the sand, using shells, young coconuts, etc., for counters, which they move from hole to hole.
As now presented to the civilized world for its diversion, Chuba is a game of skill for two players. It is made up of a board with 4 parallel rows or pockets, 11 in each row, and 60 small beads used as men or counters.

...

The winner is the player who captures all his opponent's men."

BRAAAAAINS! is a VERY fun and fast paced card game where you take on the role of a zombie amongst a zombie horde. You are simply trying to survive by gathering enough sustenance to satisfy your insatiable hunger, and the only food you crave is BRAAAAAINS!  Be wary though, Zombie hunters, and other players are waiting to disrupt your feeding frenzy!

BRAAAAAINS! has been developed for between 2 to 4 zombies ages 12+, and has an average game time between 5 and 15 minutes.  It’s a great game for casual players or others who want a hilariously fun way to slaughter a little "time.

BRAAAAAINS! boasts a total of 52 cards with a lot of attention to detail and fantastic art, and even quirky tongue-in-cheek references and homages to the zombie subculture. It really does show that this has been almost a year in the making!

The deck is opened and the rules card and credits card are removed and the cards are to be shuffled.

Once the cards are shuffled well, the deck is placed center on the table, face down, and scattered around into a heap.

Once the group has decided who will go first, that person will select a card from the heap. If that card is a Brain Card, they will set that card in front of them. They can choose to pass to the next player, or keep drawing.

If the player draws an Incident Card, the player must do what the Incident Card instructs. They must also place the card back into the heap and mix the heap thoroughly.

The play continues this way for each player until a player reaches 10 brains, then that player wins!

Welcome to the world of Diggin’ Deep Tennis. Diggin’ Deep Tennis is a multifaceted game, which brings the world of professional tennis right onto your tabletop. You play at the pace that suits you with Diggin’ Deep Tennis. The game engine offers a quick play mechanic in which you can resolve the action game-by-game, set-by-set, or you can even simulate an entire match in as little as one roll of the dice.

Diggin’ Deep Tennis also features a point-by-point mechanic which factors in every possible skill for the player rated. Whether it’s the big server like Andy Roddick, the great return of service game of Novak Djokovic, or fantastic all-around play of Nadal and Federer, every player will perform as they did in real life.

The point-by-point game also offers an amazing element of strategy with the Diggin’ Deep System, which allows players to dig deep for a little something extra in certain circumstances. Do you Dig Deep do attempt to break serve in the first set? Or do you wait for an opportunity later in the match? The Diggin’ Deep System brings tabletop tennis games to an entirely new level of play. The Diggin’ Deep System also makes for outstanding head-to-head play. Not to fear though, we didn’t leave solitaire players out in the cold, as the game also has a solitaire system for when to Dig Deep.

It’s match point, and time for you to Dig Deep!

Welcome to the world of Glory on the Pitch, brought to you by Diggin Deep Sports.  With Glory on the Pitch, you can re-create a full soccer match in about ten minutes, complete with the following:
Complete list of goal scorers, time of goals, and assists
Complete set of stats for selected players (Goals, Assists, Shots, Shots on Goal, Yellow Cards, Red Cards)
Complete keeper stats
In game strategies (Who to start? / When to sub? / Who to sub?)
Team Formations (Heavy offense / Heavy Defense / Balanced Attack)
For players that only play one half...Which half to play?
Individual cards means you can have draft leagues, and not be locked into stock teams.
 Within five minutes, you’ll have a final score complete with total shots on goal (per half).  You also have the option of tracking the stats of up to three players from each squad.  An entire match along with stats for certain players can be played in about ten to fifteen minutes.

Objective: be the first reaching target space (Waldhaus = field 60)
- simple 'Roll & Move' game/ moving by turns -
each player moves his/her meeple according die roll
ending on a numbered space (1-20) follow instructions printed cover inside.
Remarks:
Printed in the GDR during 1950's

Basically a "trouble style" game with cards.  Classic Moves was developed in the early 1990's by Moore and Moore Industries of London, Ontario.  Players begin with 4 marbles in a "safe" zone, and they must be moved around the board to the player's "Home Base".

Movement is accomplished with a 56 card deck, with numbers 1-12, and a number of "wild" cards.

There is a centre "shortcut" that can also be utilized.

Players can also play with a partner, assisting them once their marbles are "home"

boardgame: Khet 2.0: Eye of Horus Beam Splitter
description:
Eye of Horus Beam Splitter
You've had fun with The Laser Game Khet 2.0 using one laser beam, now can you up the ante and handle three? Each Eye of Horus game piece splits one laser beam into two! Half of the beam hitting the Eye of Horus reflects, and the other half passes through, for twice the strategy and excitement. With both game pieces in play, the beam can be split twice, blasting three lasers around the field.

Includes:
- 2x Eye of Horus Beam Splitter pieces (one silver & one red)
- Full color booklet including 5 new starting setups

Objective: be the first reaching target space (field 100)
- simple 'Roll & Move' game/ moving by turns -
each player moves his/her meeple according die roll
ending on a red space follow instructions.
Remarks:
published during 1930's in bavaria or Austria
cute pewter meeples

The game contains 500 history cards from five categories which explore the "people, events, documents, principles and faith that have contributed to the founding of the United States".  Each card provides 'content-rich' answers (and pictures) that are sourced from over 100 books and 100s of online resources.

The game not only teaches America's history, but the humor in the game board and "social justice" cards draw a clear contrast between the founding roots of this country and the direction the nation has taken over the past 100 years.  The game was added to Glenn Beck's Marketplace in August, 2013. http://marketplace.theblaze.com/we-the-people-games-home

Players advance their pawns along the outside "semi-capitalist" square track until they lose their starting cash.  They are then required to mortgage their home and move to the inside circle or "path of social justice."  Stars are earned by answering the history cards correctly and lost through porkulus taxes.  The objective of the game is to be the player with the most stars when the first player goes broke on the inside track. The game takes about 1.5 to 2 hours to play.

boardgame: Reinforcements: A Supplement for Operation Squad World War II
description:
Publisher Blurb:

Reinforcements is the latest supplement for the Operation Squad WWII game. This supplement contains details on new support sections and their weapons as well as many new squads to use in the 5 exciting scenarios which have been especially designed to let you play the game on bigger tables.

From Russian Penal Squads (Shtrafniye Roti) to the infamous Hitlerjugend Volunteers (defenders of Berlin), from American Recon squads (in jeeps) to British Scout squads (and their Universal Carriers)... your games will never be the same again. The Reinforcements supplement is the same price (£18) as the other two Operation Squad books (Operation Squad and Vehicles!) and includes: -
New rules for Mortar and Machine Gun Sections
5 new scenarios – designed for playing on larger tables
New Characteristics
Card Weapon references
New vehicles - including the Churchill & Kettenkrad!
17 New Units:
American Army Recon squad
'All American' Paratrooper squad
US Glider Riders squad
British Army scout patrol
New Zealand 2nd Division rifle section
Scottish rifle section
Royal Marine Commando section
Russian Penal troops squad
Russian Sniper squad
Russian 'Black Death' Naval Brigade squad
Russian Scout squad
German Schnell squad
German Volksgrenadier squad
Hitlerjugend Volunteers squad
Waffen-SS squad
Das Reich squad
German Jager squad
You will need to have Operation Squad WWII and a copy of Vehicles! to use this 50 page, full-colour, softback supplement

boardgame: Prinzessin Lillifee: Ein Fest für das Einhorn
description:
In this Princess-Lillifee-themed children's game players try to build garlands as long as possible for the unicorn's birthday party. To do so, players have to collect cards. The 36 cards consist of two parts of different colours (some of them have the same colour on both ends). Players take turns rolling the four colour dice. Dice are then grouped into pairs of two to determine which two cards the player is allowed to take (e.g. if a player rolls orange, yellow, green and green, he/she could take an orange-yellow card and a green-green one). If a player can't use a combination to get a card, he/she removes one card from the game instead. 
When all the cards have been distributed, players put together their own cardss like they would put together domino tiles, i.e. with matching ends next to one another. This part of the game is basically a solo puzzle activity.
The player who manages to put together the longest "garland" (row of cards) wins the game.

A Princess-Lillifee themed travel game for two players.

The game comes in a metal box, whose lid also functions as the game board. It shows a grid of 4x7 squares and two starting spaces. On the grid are placed 28 magnetic flower pieces. Players take turns rolling a die. If the active player rolls a flower colour, he/she may move his/her player piece (also magnetic) until it reaches a flower of the corresponding colour. Pieces may only be moved either vertically or horizontally, and it may not jump over the other player's piece or the rose piece. If it reaches a flower of the colour on the die roll, the player wins that flower. If he can't reach a flower of the current colour, he/she may move their piece to an empty space. If a player rolls the rose symbol, he gets to move the rose piece. The rose can only be plucked once all the other flowers have been taken. In this phase of the game, there are no more die rolls. Each player gets to move once per round. While the other flowers are worth one point, the rose is worth three points.
The game ends when all flowers have been plucked. The player with the most points wins the game.
"Math Geek" is a Kickstarter exclusive expansion set for "Got It!" consisting of 12 cards with exponentiation, concatenation, factorial, and modulo operators, as well as the numbers 15-20

The game is set up by inserting one of the two double sided game boards into the frame in 1 of possible 4 orientations (=16 possible configurations).  Pictures will be visible through holes in the top of the frame.  However, when the game begins, each of these holes is covered by a pawn.

Players take turns pulling off two pawns in an effort to find a match.  Matching pictures do not match exactly in I Spy games.  For example, a match might be two similar airplanes that are different colors.  Or it might be an elephant toy photographed from 2 different angles.

If a match is made, the player keeps the pawns and takes another turn.  If not match is made, the player puts the pawns back and the next player gets their turn.

Once all matches are revealed, the player with the most pawns wins.

There is also a set of short riddles to answer after the game for each of the 16 board configurations.

Contents: Game tray, 2 double-sided picture boards (16 game variations), reveal sheet with 16 riddles, 36 pawns

In Frontier Apocalypse, the world famous gunfight at the O.K Corral never happens. The night before, the unspeakable happens and the world was forever changed. Some blamed the South, some blamed the North, some blamed England, some blamed God or the Devil, but no one really knew what happened or why. But somehow humanity was able to survive the pulverizing chunks of moon rock that struck the planet; somehow humanity survived the various super volcanoes, massive radioactive solar flares and other terrible apocalyptic events. Frontier Apocalypse takes place over 100 years after that unspeakable event. In Frontier Apocalypse the Wild West never ended, it just got Wilder!

Frontier Apocalypse: The Hermit's Expedition is a solo Role Playing board/card game in the vein of games like Dungeon, Talisman, Chainsaw Warrior, and Dungeoneer. The player creates a character rolling dice, and then explores a post apocalyptic wild west by flipping cards that either reveal territories, critters, contraptions or various other events or points of interest. There are also boards to help with organization of character stats, gear and the game world. The game has several randomly chosen Expeditions that the player will need to complete with their character in order to win.Combat versus enemies is resolved by the character stats and contraptions and dice rolling. The player wins by completing their Expedition before running out of supplies or getting defeated the by various strange and weird critters who inhabit the Frontier Apocalypse world.

Objective: Eliminate or block all opponent meeples
 - acting/moving by turns -
First each player is positioning troops (20) and obstacles (trees (17),houses & rivers (15)) upon own side; obstacles block movement.

Movement (transversal/horizontal/vertical) one step like Halma, but own and opposing meeples can be skipped; skipped enemies are removed.

Published during WW II
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Realism is a money-based board game which is easy to play at the basic level with a subtle form of competition where, unlike other money games, winners do not succeed at the expense of other players, so everyone can enjoy the game.  A realistic game which makes players differentiate between needs and wants and encourages them to analyze their risk and limit their exposure.

The object of the game is to try to become a virtual millionaire by buying property, shares & other assets. The players have jobs, can invest, take out loans and buy insurance.  During play there are personal and market events to cope with.

This game is particularly relevant in these difficult financial times, as it encourages individuals to think more deeply about the financial decisions they make.

The game is aimed at adults and the family and helps the players learn about investment and spot the difference between "good" debt and "bad" debt. While playing they can build up their income with good investments and try to avoid buying costly luxuries.

The winner is the player with the most net assets when play finishes.

boardgame: Mighty Morphin Power Rangers: Save Tommy Game
description:
Power Rangers, The evil Rita and Lord Zedd have imprisoned Tommy! It’s up to you to save him. Fight your way through the Evil Space Aliens and Putty Patrol warriors as your travel along the path of the Moon Palace to save Tommy and defeat Rita and Lord Zedd.

Players choose their favorite Mighty Morphin Power Ranger to play as. Each player's ranger has 5 health chips. If they lose battles against the Evil Space Aliens or Putty Patrol warriors, they'll lose one health chip. They'll need land on a Power Ranger space or give up a turn in order to regain health back.

Game play consists of rolling the die and moving your Power Ranger in any direction on the board within the level the players Power Ranger is on. There are four levels to Moon Palace Rangers need to advance through to make it to top to battle Rita and Lord Zedd to save Tommy.

Board spaces include; Power Ranger spaces (regain one health chip back), Putty Patrol warrior spaces (have battle a Putty; roll two dice, higher die wins battle), Evil Space Aliens spaces (they are blocking your Power Rangers path to next level, need acquire the Ready for Action chip in order fight them), and Ready for Action spaces (players need to get each of these within the level they are in, in order to fight the Evil Space Alien that is blocking them from advancing to next level) and blank spaces.

Players Power Rangers will need perform certain dice rolls to obtain a Ready for Action chip and when battling the Evil Space Aliens. Example; Roll either 1, 2 or 3. As your Power Ranger advances through the levels of the game board, they will become be challenging. Players need to obtain at least 4 Ready for Action chips in order to battle the last Evil Space Alien blocking them from reaching top level of the Moon Palace to battle Rita and Lord Zedd to save Tommy.

Snoopy Card Game "With Your Favorite Peanuts Characters" consists of character cards from Charles Schulz's Peanuts comic strips: Charlie Brown, Lucy, Linus, Woodstock and, of course, Snoopy; each character card has a different color background. Players are dealt four cards, which they then play in turn, one at a time, on the gameboard, a 5 x 5 grid. At different points in the game, players claim ownership of a row and a column and, when all spaces are filled, score for their color combinations.
Except for the title and artist (Schulz), this game has no connection to the Milton Bradley game of the same name from 1974.

Two fantasy wargames in one box, based on Japanese heroic fantasy novel Guin saga. These games is covering the war between Parros and Mongauli.

Both games use standard hex and counter mechanism, so repeat of movement and combat.

Nospherus is small size operational level wargame based on story from book 1 to 5. This game covering for pursuit battles in Nospherus. Contains special rules for leaders, wasteland environment and Mongauli's colored legions. Winner determined by capture or escape of crown prince and princess of Parros.

"Parros" is medium size strategic level wargame based on story of book 1 to 16. This game covering entire war between Parros and Mongauli. Contains special rules for recruit, diplomacy, rebellion, capture and escape. Winner determined by collapse of either nation.

French Rails is a Print and Play map in the crayon rails series of games.  Players receive demand cards where cities want different goods.  These goods and cities are scattered across the map, and the players must construct track by drawing on the board to connect the cities with the goods they demand.  Each demand card has three different deliveries on it, and players much decide which demands work best together so they can optimize both movement and track building.  Money is very tight at the beginning of the game as the payouts do not completely cover the cost of track, so players must slowly build a network that they can use repeatedly to generate enough cash to win the game.

This game is the 1st expansion to Dominion Nitroplus Card Masters.
It features 152 cards with characters from Phantom PHANTOM OF INFERNO, "Hello, world.", Demonbane, Jingai Makyo, Hanachirasu, Gekko no Carnevale, Zoku Satsuriku no Jango (TRE DONNE CRUDELI) -Jigoku no Shoukinkubi-, CHAOS;HEAD, Sumaga, STEINS;GATE, Full Metal Daemon Muramasa, AXANAEL, Guilty Crown Lost Christmas, etc.
This expansions also adds coins to the gameplay with a design featuring Kyubey from Puella Magi Madoka Magica.

This is an "action Bible game" with a Christian trivia theme.

The educational design features basic doctrine and trivia in a question and answer format. Players use a spinner to move along a rainbow-shaped pathway. A correct response allows the player to advance to the a square matching the color shown on the dial.

Grace cards allow a player to take a bonus move. Sin cards force a player to lose a turn. These cards are mixed into the trivia deck. The first player to land on the "Abundant Life" space is declared the winner.

Includes the board with a spinner, the cards, and tokens.

boardgame: Strike Dice & The Portkey of Chaos
description:
NEW EXPANSION RULES: PORTKEY OF CHAOS

The Basic Strike Dice Game consists of 8 Basic Triangle Cards which carry the Attributes of the Creatures you will meet in the Expansion. These Attributes are also part of the Triangle Bases of the Arena (Gameboard). Next, you will find 32 Pyramid Dice of different color, 8 per player. In the same basic pack you will find and the Strike Dice Expansion: Portkey of Chaos consisting of the 9 Creatures plus the New Rules.


THE GAME

The game here consists of 3 Phases: In each Phase you draw in order 3 Creature Cards. Place the cards on the triangle bases of the board according to which attribute they represent. The creatures carry 2 different numbers: The left number indicates the strength and the right number the Bonus you receive when you kill a Creature. To destroy a Creature you must roll a number with your Pawndie higher from the one which represents his Strength and place it on the opposite Tribase where this Creature is located. When you do that, you draw as many Tricards as the Bonus number this Creature caries on his right side. You keep these cards and you may use them as you wish. Note: The Rules of the Basic Game Apply here too, the only difference is that the Creatures add new elements in the game. You also keep and the Creature you killed since it counts as part of the collected cards. The player with the most cards in the end of 3rd round is the winner.

Object of the Game: To be the player with the most markers on the board at the end of the game.

Game Play: Two (2) players need a pair of dice and fifteen (15) markers each. Player with the dark markers throws the dice first. The numbers as shown on the dice (either individual numbers or combination of the two dice) are then covered on the board. When all twenty-one (21) spaces on the board are covered, the game is over.

The game board contains 21 squares each containing a picture of 2 dice rolls (i.e. one square has a picture of 2 smaller squares, one with three (3) dots, the other with two (2), representing a possible roll) each square contains a different possible roll.

Objective: flip balls into holes of different values in the box base & sum up values
 - acting by turns -
each player shoots 6 balls summing up gained values; 
compared with Flying Hats 
balls instead of cones are used - you can't hit the same hole; players are challenged summing up.

objective: journey around the world - be the first ending your round trip in Berlin
 - acting by turns -
each player draws 4 (hidden) travel cards; in order to move forward you must play a matching (colour/symbol) card regarding next space; you even may step further if you play another matching one; if you possess a card with a plane (image) you even move three spaces ahead (if it matches).
After end of your turn fill up to 4 cards; 
remarks: published end 1950's/early 60's

Objective: be the first reaching target zone
 - acting/moving by turns -
intermediate goal cards are shuffled & distributed; each player moves own car according die roll; move your meeple to the intermediate goals first before heading to target zone; due to die results stop lights (spinner) have to be changed.

remarks:
7th player acts as game master

Tony Blewitt's The Feely Game

The board game that will drive you crazy!

He could be holding a mushroom or an ironing board, she could have a stick of liquorice or a roll of toilet paper. It's up to you to find out in 60 seconds. Sounds easy? Try it out!

Teams have to identify mystery objects that players imagine they are holding in their hands. They ask questions about it ... "Is it big?" ... "Does it move?" ... "Can you eat it?"

This game will bring out powers of quick thinking, memory and also hilarious interpretations of other players' descriptions of everyday objects.

The game is ready to play. Take it home, open it up, gather your friends around and get down to some "FEELY FUN".

Crush Monster Rampage! is the sequel to the 2010 game, Smash Monster Rampage!. This expansion uses the same playing board, buildings, military units and dice but introduces a deadly new monster with new Monster Hit cards.

Concept and Objective
A huge multi-tentacled monster is attacking your city. From below!
Mobilize your troops and exterminate the beast before every building becomes a heap of rubble. The victorious task force will score more hits and rescue more survivors than their opponent.

Game Overview
Two players compete to rescue survivors and win the most ‘Monster Hit’ cards.

The ‘Monster Die’ generates random actions for the Monster making the beast unpredictable and dangerous. It destroys everything in its path, moving effortlessly around the city on a tangle of amorphous tentacles. As it moves, the monster pushes tentacles into the ground, creating a network of tunnels beneath the city.
Tentacles burst up through the ground grabbing tanks and helicopters and crushing whole buildings.

Taking turns to move their Tank and/or Helicopter Units, players get into position to attack the Monster and its tentacles. Win ‘Monster Hit’ cards by successfully firing at the Monster or tentacles and fulfilling the conditions on the cards. Act fast, If the city is flattened before the Monster is killed, the Monster wins!

Objective: starting from Karlsruhe be the first ending on space 110 (Stuttgart)
- moving by turn -
simple 'Roll & Move' game - roll the die, move & follow instructions printed on cover inside if your meeple end upon a red marked space.

remarks:
belongs to series: "Scholz' künstlerische Spiele";
meeples: pewter figures

This expansion to the Bulrup core game adds the effects of powerful Weather cards, as well as adds 4 new crops and 1 new Magical Creature.  2 Lodges are also added to the game, allowing the maximum number of players to go up to 8.

A game of travel in a large city with the goal to save as much money possible while having a good time visiting different sights in a large city.

One travels through a designed course on the board landing and visiting  different sights to stop on in their vacation travel.

While spending along the way, one tries to save in their travels as well.  Whoever gets home earliest gets an 'savings' award and winner is not determined till everyone playing reaches home.

boardgame: Avgvstvs to Avrelian: Wargaming the Roman Principate
description:
A miniatures game, centered around the Roman Empire.  The rules cover the period between the time when Augustus seized power (25 BC), right through the period of the Empire’s height, down through the crisis of the third century, and ending with the reign of Aurelian (275 AD).

Shooting and combat are determined using a number of ten-sided dice.  The number depending on the size of the units involved. The dice rolls are modified by a few factors, and the result looked up on a simple chart. Units which take more than a specific number of casualties become "Shaken".  Commanders have a limited ability to rally Shaken troops, returning them, at least temporarily, to more or less full effectiveness.

Games are scenario based rather than the familiar points based systems.

The turn sequence is based on a unique card based system which emphasizes command control and battlefield friction.

A simple toy racing game for toddlers using dominoes and 6 sided dice.

Players race their toys around a domino track by rolling 2d6 and moving to their choice of matching domino with _one_ of the two dice.

Designed to teach the following Little Geek Skills Syllabus items.
- Rolling Dice
- Counting Pips
- Moving a token
- Matching Numbers
- Taking Turns
- Winning/Losing gracefully
- Tactical Choice

description:
One player hides the stuffed chipmunk and then selects one or more picture clue tiles, at least one of which should give a hint as to the animal's location.  Players then seek out the chipmunk, with the finder earning the right to hide him the next time.

From the publisher:

Hide him INSIDE or OUT! Clue cards point the way. Can you find the chipmunk? Three ways to play! Each time you find him, an acorn you'll earn. First player to get three will win, and all will learn!   
No reading required!   
3 ways to play! Includes Rules for Beginner, Advanced & Team Play!

To Glory Rise is a high speed, no holds barred airship race set in a steampunk world. Players take command of rival airships out to win at any cost. Roll the dice and assign power to your engines and cannons. Head for the gates and the win, but beware your opponents they're bound to have a cannon with your name on it.

The goal of the game is to be the first airship to pass through all of the numbered gates and complete the race, or be the last airship still flying. Players will select rival airships and compete with speed and deadly cannon fire to win the challenge and prove themselves as the world's greatest airship captain.

This tabletop game is designed to be played anywhere you have the time to play. All that is required are the game pieces and a ruler or two, and you can setup a race track and start playing. The game scales for up to six players.

The ease of interaction and rich combination of strategies, coupled with the profound Chinese cuisine, this is the "Chef Masters" ! "Chef Masters" is the 2-6 player strategy game, with simple rules, accessories, that is rich, interesting, and playable. The various restaurants compete with street food in the capital. Players as restaurant owners use auctions to entice guests on the rivers and lakes. The players want to beat opponents in bringing unique national dishes to the market. By winning the praise of diners, the winner will be the unanimous choice to become this generation's Kitchen king.

The skills of the twenty people in the game is fun and interactive, has strategic part of auction and cash flow management. A variety of scoring mechanism, and allows players to develop according to their own situation unique win strategy. Both newcomers and old players of the board game can find their own fun.

description: A simple bingo game for young children.  Put all the clothing cards into the laundry bag and shake.  When it's your turn pull one out.  If you have the same picture on your bingo card then you made a match!  The first player to match three in a row wins.

Unfortunately the game needs a ko rule, and even then it isn't really satisfactory. My friends and family really like it though, so it still might be fun for casuals...

Unfortunately the game needs a ko rule, and even then it isn't really satisfactory. My friends and family really like it though, so it still might be fun for casuals...
