The player who receives the least negative points (there are no positive points in a world where Goobers are involved) by the time of blast off is the one who will be most responsible for the destruction of the next world the Goobers land on (also known as the winner of this game).

boardgame: Flames of War: Grey Wolf – Axis Forces on the Eastern Front, January 1944-February 1945
description:
Publisher blurb:

Grey Wolf contains all the Germans, Hungarians and Finnish forces you will be familiar with from Stalin’s Onslaught, Hammer and Sickle, River of Heroes and Stalin’s Europe. We’ve also added our most popular PDF Intelligence Briefings as well as some new Briefings and some forces from Fortress Europe.

boardgame: Flames of War: Red Bear – Allied Forces on the Eastern Front, January 1944-February 1945
description:
Publisher Blurb:

Red Bear contains all the Soviet, Polish and Romanians forces you will be familiar with from Stalin's Onslaught, Hammer and Sickle, River of Heroes and Stalin's Europe. We've also added our most popular PDF Intelligence Briefings as well as some new Briefings and some forces from Fortress Europe.

boardgame: Flames of War: Blood, Guts, & Glory – Tank Battles in the Lorraine, September 1944-January 1945
description:
Publisher Blurb:

Hello, and welcome to Patton's Third Army! The road to victory lies straight through the Lorraine, a stretch of land perfectly suited to mobile tank warfare. The American army and its allies have been steamrolling through France and the Low Countries. The end of the war seems so near with the Germans barely able to put up a fight. But the Allied advance has slowed as supplies and fuel become scarce. Furthermore, the Allied strategic and logistical focus has shifted to the north as Montgomery pushes for a bold attack to Arnhem. However, Patton won't play second fiddle to Monty's offensive and has stored up enough supplies to launch a small attack of his own along the Moselle River in the Lorraine. As an American company commander you are part of this offensive when you take your force from Blood, Guts, & Glory into combat!

The skinniest and the weirdest race begins right now! You – yes, you – have received an invitation to participate in the "skinnies" race, a race in which only skinny people can compete. Problem is, the stadium that holds the race has incredibly strong winds that can blow skinny people like you in directions you don't want to go. Can you win this race against the competitors and the devil winds?

In Wind Runner, players have a runner and a hand of seven action cards. Each turn in a round, the first player rolls the wind direction and speed dice to determine whether the wind is blowing north or south and how quickly. Players then each secretly choose an action card in hand, then reveal them and resolve them, starting with the player farthest down the track. The cards let players move forward, move sideways, swap places with another runner, reverse the wind (!), ignore the wind, or move a barricade. Afterwards, players then move forward or backward according to the wind – but if a player or a barricade blocks the wind, then you receive no benefit (or penalty) from it.

After six turns, players pick up all their cards and start a new round. The game ends after 3-5 rounds (depending on the number of players) or as soon as a runner reaches the finish line.

boardgame: Flames of War: Know Your Enemy – Late War
description:
Publisher Blurb:

Complete late war arsenals, warriors, Divisional and National rules for German, British, US, Soviet, Finnish, Hungarian, Polish, and Romanian forces with rules clarifications.

boardgame: Flames of War: Devil's Charge – The German Offensive: Battle of the Bulge, December 1944
description:
Publisher Blurb:

Welcome to the Ardennes, soldier! Well, we're finally here. In the past few years we've seen the US Army fight its way through the rocky hills of Tunisia and Sicily, painfully crawl up the boot of Italy, storm bloody Omaha Beach, and sweep across the France in what has been described as an 'American Blitzkrieg'. Its now the end of 1944 and that same army, for all of its hard-earned victories, is at the end of its tether. Fuel and supplies are only just arriving in force, the men are exhausted, and the raw recruits are fresher than ever. For the US Army, December was a time to pause briefly to rebuild their stocks and prepare for the final drive into Germany. This opened a very small window of opportunity for the Germans to launch an ambitious offensive with the best troops they could muster. The plan called for an all-out attack through the Ardennes to capture Antwerp, cutting off and capturing several Allied armies, and forcing a peace settlement in Germany's favour. No one, including Hitler himself, was under any delusions about the cost of failure in the up coming operation. For the Germans, it was all or nothing.

A promotional card given out at Gateway 2012 (Strategicon Conventions) by Antoine Bauza. Esteban is Antoine's baby son. The card was also available at Essen 2012.
Like all other 7 Wonders Promos this card can be ordered directly from the publisher Repos Production (see BGG forum).

The effect allows the player to use Esteban's ability once per game to force all players to pick a card from the same hand again and play it as normal.

This game could be described as Battleship with words.  The board is a bi-fold, with a ribbon connecting the two sides to provide support for tenting on a table.  Each side of the board has two areas - the lower portion contains a thin ledge on which to place the letter tiles and a coordinate system.  The upper portion has the coordinates as well as holes which can be filled with either light or dark pegs, depending on on the result of a query.  The letter tiles, which come in two shades, are imprinted on a quadrille rule themselves, for better alignment with the coordinate system.

The game plays by each person placing a word made from their color of tiles, in secret, on their side of the board, and then the players take turns calling out coordinates. A player records a "hit" with a light peg, and a "miss" with a darker one.  The first player to guess the other's word is the winner.
publishers: KMS Industries Inc. Scientific Games Div.

description:
Homestead is a game of old west dueling with an added element of unit purchase. There are two different boards each with similar yet unique game-play. Side one is a town where each player is trying to  either reach the other players 'overran' space or trying to kill off the other player. The game continues in a reverse tog-of-war pushing one another's units back and forth.

Side two of the board is a desert area with an open field, this side the only way to win is by killing the other players main player token. There are many more options to strategy than on the town side.

A simple family action game .
6 coloured balls should be placed in holes by a spinning top.
Under the lucent platter are colours. For each ball of the same colour than printed below, the player will get a chip of the  corresponding colour.

Goal is, to collect 3 chips of each colour.
Reimplemented in 1975 as Blumengarten. Game mechanism is the same. Karussell is abstract and has no theme, Blumengarten uses flowers tiles instead of ordinary plastic chips. Victory conditions are slightly different.

Card game that has nothing to do with teaching children safety first (as most similar titled games do) but the cards do make use of roadside imagery even if the theme is not followed through into the rules.

VALUE OF CENTRE CARDS
Beacon    (Highest card)    scores    5    Points
Stop    (Second    card)    loses    4    Points
Caution    (Third    card)    loses    3    Points
Go    (Fourth    card)    scores    2    Points
Bobbie    (Fifth    card)    scores    1    Point
Each pack consists of 20 centre cards, and 36 other cards numbered 2 to 9 in orange, red, green and black.

To commence playing, all the above centre cards must be placed face downwards in the centre of the table. Deal out nine to each player of the remainder of the cards. If more than four players, deal round until all the playing cards have been dealt. Dealer commences playing by leading his smallest card in any suit. Should he lead a red two and be holds the three in his own hand, he can follow immediately with it, and the four also, if he holds it. Should he lead the two, not holding the three, the next player on his left follows. He must either play the three of the same suit, or pick up a card from the centre of the table.
Should a Beacon card be picked up, the player registers on the score card 5 points, and continues to play with his lowest card in any suit. Next player then either follows suit or picks up a centre card.
Should a Stop card be picked up, the player deducts four points on his score card, and loses his turn to play. The next player then either following suit with the three or picking up a centre card. Should a Caution card be picked up, the player loses three points on his score card, but continues to play his lowest card (in any suit), the next player again following suit or picking up a centre card.
Should a Go card be picked up, he scores two points and continues playing his lowest card in any suit.
Should a Bobbie card be picked up, the player registers one point, and continues to play any low card.
Any player playing the ten in any suit is allowed to go again with any low card.
The player who chips first (i.e., uses all his cards up), adds 20 points to his score.

The player, who, after deducting his losses, scores 50 points is the winner.

Should there be insufficient centre cards, all scores to be added or deducted, and the cards collected, shuffled, and replaced face downwards in the centre of the table. All centre cards must be kept separate in play, and it is in the interest of each player to keep all cards, except the last one played, covered up.
Monopoly set in Schweinfurt, Germany. All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Wismar, in Germany.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of the island Norderney, in Germany.

An early Victorian board game, probably inspired by games such as Fox & Geese where it pits one player against the rest.

One player is the Ghost and the rest of the players are hunters. The Ghost is placed in the central diamond on the board and the playing pieces of the other players in the five diamonds nearest the Ferry (but not across the water). The movements are diamond to diamond through the lines joining them and you can cross from one circular line to another when there is a line linking them. The cards can not cross water anywhere but the Ghost may do so by following the dotted lines.

Ghost wins the game if he succeeds in crossing the water to the part of the country at the top of the board. The Hunters win if they surround the Ghost and force him on to the diamond at either of the two houses with diamonds, or on to the either of the two diamonds at the Cave, or on to the centre diamond so that he cannot escape.

The Ghost moves first and then the Hunters, then the Ghost again, and so on. Neither the Ghost or Hunters can be taken. Everyone can move backwards or forwards, but only one move each turn. (I assume that you can only move into empty diamonds and not occupied ones.

Abstract game for two players. One takes the Pole playing pieces, the other the Russians. The Poles are lighter in appearance, the Russians darker. Each player puts his men on the spaces marked with an X on his side of the board.

The object is to capture all your opponent's pieces.The men move forwards (or sideways I suspect but the rules are not clear on this) one space at a time. The Captains can move backwards or forwards. No Captains are in play at the start. You capture men by jumping over them, as in Draughts or Checkers. When either player gets a man into the Battlefield (up the centre of the board) that man is changed into a Captain and is placed at the uppermost spot on the outside of the board. Now he can move backwards or forwards but still only one space at a time (unless jumping to capture). Capturing must be done if you are in place to do so.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of the Baltic Sea, in Germany.

boardgame: West Point A Game For The Nation
description:
The game is a rather simple roll and move game. It has 43 spaces arranged in a triangle. Some spaces are marked with a cannon ball or a star. The cannon ball marked spaces are penalty spaces. Lose a turn, go back spaces and so on. the star marked spaces predict good fortune for the player but bestow no advantage in the game itself.

The game uses one six sided die (1-6) for determining the number of spaces to move. The game sample has two dice cups so there may have been more than one die originally. The markers are red, black, yellow, and green.

The game box is mostly red with the title in yellow. A West Point Cadet in full dress is standing at attention with a bayoneted musket.

Designer Sergey Machin has created a game in a completely new genre – the active party game – with the name coming from a traditional Russian new year's entertainment for kids and adults alike: Confetti.

The point of the game is simple. We toss half the deck on the table, sit down, then while the hourglass runs try to create different card combinations that bring us victory points (VPs).

One side of a Confetti card shows three differently sized and colored circles; by collecting cards with a small, medium and large circle of the same color, the player receives 1 VP. The other side has different shapes, but the idea is the same; a small, medium and large shape of the same color is worth 1 VP.

What makes the game tough is that you can take cards from the table only by using the fingers on your right hand and sliding them across the table.  You'll also need to use your left hand to hang on to cards already taken or else other players can steal them from you. Finally, any cards you collect that aren't used in a combination with others cost you 1 VP at the end of the game. (Bear in mind that any shape on a card can be used in only one combo!)

Confetti lasts three rounds. During the first and second rounds you can collect and keep six cards in your play area; during the third round nine cards. In between rounds, you can keep some previously collected cards, specifically a number of cards equal to the number of VPs you scored in the previous round.

Calmness and concentration.
Just one right motion and the card will be fixed in the needed position.
Step by step, card by card – in front of you a fragile and elegant tower of a Japanese castle appears.

Japanese castle is a three-dimensional meccano and a competition of skill and accuracy. Laconic fascination of traditional Japanese architecture, ancient European card-building passion and a native Russian talent of constructing magnificent edifices without any nail are united in this game.

The game concept is familiar to everyone who has built a card castle at least once: vertical piers are leaned together and horizontal cards are placed at their top becoming a base for the new floor, and it continues till there are no cards left or one clumsy movement ruins all the construction. Moreover standard cards called heki which are used only for walls there are some other building elements: gamen screens (twice bigger than heki) and yanegae overlaps with the special fixed stops for heki. Vertical cards stay at their places because of it so castle levels can be built as a tower one right above another, not only like a pyramid. Yanegae cards are of two sizes – small ones with the space for one leaned pair of heki (this construction is called karigoya) and big for two pairs which can be placed transversely to each other as well as parallel. The last variety of big yanegae with two perpendicular pairs is called antei and works as the basic unit for the most of constructions.

Rules offer many varieties of the game: for one player or for the company of 2-4 builders. There can be up to 8 players if two game kits are used. Solo gameplay is simple – you need to build a castle as high as possible. There are no strict rules – you can use any element conditionally to your imagination. Competing versions usually offer to build a tower of exact height in a pelter or make the most solid building – in this case players jolt the table trying to ruin opponent’s castle and not to damage own. Especially amusing version tenders to take your castle and move it to another table keeping it all-of-a-piece, of cause. There is also some kind of gameplay resembling Jenga – players put some cards into a heap, place a few karigoya at the top and try to pull the cards out of the heap without busting the buildings.

Building castles with special elements is easier than with common cards but still the same interesting. Japanese castle can be even built by a child nevertheless nobody suggests to play it in the after-party morning. 
The game differs favorably from the common cards by design – building elements show off the authentical Japanese pictures and interiors from the rich collection of the “Yugen” antiques gallery. Japanese castle will give you hours of creativity and excitement and maybe can become a first step to a high-altitude card building champion title for someone. Theoretically 5 Japanese castle game sets will be enough to build an eight-meter tower – the last registered record doesn’t haul a bit to this point.

boardgame: Bull's Eye: The Square Way to Success
description: Bull's Eye is a simple roll-and-move played on a 7x7 grid printed on cardstock.  The cells are numbered 1-48 and the very center one is labelled "BULL'S EYE".  Players use the included pawns and single die to race around the spiral to the center.  Four numbers have special instructions - lose a turn, go back four spaces, free throw and go ahead five spaces.  An exact roll is required to enter the winning space.  If one player arrives at a space that is already occupied, both players roll the die.  The player with the highest number advances his pawn the number of spaces rolled.

123 Dice Game by Collective Wisdom

This small-box game has eight 1" dice with large numbers ranging from 1-72.  Rules for 14 games and a few variations are included.  All are math games designed for preschool through fourth grade.  They are:

________________________________________
Preschool - 1st Grade:

Number Identification Game - Roll the dice and name the numbers, earning a point for each number correctly identified.

Order of Numbers Game - Roll all dice, re-rolling duplicates.  Put the dice in numerical order.  Earn one point per roll and play to five.

Counting Game - Roll all dice except the "81" die.  Select any two dice and say aloud all the numbers between them.  Earn one point per roll.
________________________________________
Games and Activities for 1st and 2nd Grades:

Another More Challenging Counting Game - Plays as "Counting Game" above except that a point is earned for each number named, encouraging players to select dice that are further apart.

Blind Toss Number Value Game - A moderator rolls all the dice and gives one to each player whose eyes are shut.  Each player then goes around the circle, saying whether their number is greater than or less than each other player's die.  If his is less than any player, he is eliminated.  Eventually, one player will remain in the round and earn one point.  This is repeated except that the lowest number will win the second round.  Continue until a single player scores six.

Odd Even Game - Roll all dice and divide them into even and odd piles.  Earn one point for each roll.

Addition Game (Adding 2 numbers) - Roll all dice.  Select two, add them and announce the sum.  One point is given for each correct answer.  Play to ten points.

Addition and Subtraction -  As above for Addition Game, but continue, subtracting the lesser die from the greater die and announcing the difference.  One point is given for each correct answer.  Play to ten points.

Addition Game (Adding 3 Numbers) - Roll all dice and choose three.  Add them together and announce the sum.

Challenging Variation of Additiona & Subtraction Games - Instead of granting one point per correct answer, players earn the answer as the number of points, thus encouraging players to select harder dice.

Place Value Game - Roll dice until all show single-digit numbers.  Select any three and name the three-digit number they form.  Earn one point for each roll and play to 28.  Optionally, offer an extra point for each number that is written out long-hand.

________________________________________
Games and Activities for 3rd and 4th Grades:

Multiplication Game I: Each player writes down a set of five numbers.  Before rolling, each player will announce which of those numbers they will be using.  After rolling all the dice, select three and multiply each by the number chosen before the roll, scoring one point per correct answer.  On his next turn, the player must choose a new multiplicand.

Multiplication Game II: Roll six dice.  Divide the rolled dice into three pairs and multiply each pair, announcing the product.  Earn one point per correct answer.

Challenging Variation to Multiplication Games I and II: Play as above except that players add the products to their scores instead of one point, thus challenging them to select higher-yielding combinations.

Division Game: Roll all dice, removing any duplicate numbers.  Identify any pairs of dice where one can be evenly divided into the other.  Die may be used more than once.  Earn one point per pair identified.  Before the next player rolls the dice, they may review the pairs and earn two points for any missed pairs they identify.  Play to 30 points.

Equation Game: Roll all dice.  Identify any valid equations - addition, subtraction, multiplication or division.  Each equation will require at least three dice, but may use more.   Earn one point for each die used.  Continue making equations, re-using dice as needed.  Play to 25 points.

________________________________________
The dice are of good quality and have the following faces:

A:  1 -  2 -  3 -  4 -  5 -  6
B:  2 -  3 -  4 -  5 -  8 -  9
C:  2 -  9 - 10 - 10 - 11 - 12
D:  3 -  4 -  5 -  6 -  7 -  8
E:  3 -  5 -  6 -  7 -  9 - 10
F:  8 -  8 - 16 - 24 - 32 - 40
G:  9 - 18 - 36 - 54 - 72 - 81 
H: 10 - 15 - 20 - 25 - 30 - 40

The arm of the Empire is expanding and its next colony will be on the lunar surface. The only thing in the way of progress is the pesky natives. New rockets are being assembled and the call has gone out for brave leaders to captain these metal flying machines and annex the moon.

Players bid for parts, build their rockets, and then blast their way into space. They can battle lunar natives, found colonies, and construct a flying fortress. All the while they are competing against other players for prestige and the title of Grand Admiral of Her Majesty's Rocket Corps.

Her Majesty's Rocket Corps is a steampunk themed board game based on lunar conquest. Players acquire space ship parts from the auction, build their their rockets, combat the lunar natives, and start lunar colonies.

Join the Rocket Corps and see the solar system!

Description:
The zombie hordes have swept the world, humanity is barely hanging on, now is your chance to rule!!
All you have to do is survive, horde your resources and then find a nice mall or small town that you can rule with an iron fist. Fight zombies, sabotage your fellow survivors and ensure that you are the on top when all the undead dust settles.
Apocalypse is an exciting new collectable card game, where 2 or more players take turns battling zombies, gathering resources, and stabbing each other in the back. Find items like food and weapons, use zombie bait on your opponents, refresh yourself with the world's most aggressive energy drink, Prison Rape brand energy drinks. Expand your Apocalypse with the upcoming set Going Nuclear! The zombies are only the beginning.

2-4 players recommended for a 60 card deck, for more players just add more cards.

From Kickstarter Web Site
Monster Crabs is a card game.  It combines war with rock, paper, scissors.  First you deal all the cards out.  There will be 16 different crabs in three different elements.  Each player says offense or defense and then flips up the card after they said offense or defense at the same time.  The person whose card has the highest value wins that round and gets the cards.  If it is a tie, you see if it's air, fire or water type.  Air beats water, water beats fire, and fire beats air.  Those are the basic rules.  A full rule set will be included in each box.

Even a racing super-star has time to sit down to a game of Super Dungeon Explore.  Princess Malya brings her love of speed into the Last Galaxy's favorite online game, zipping through the dungeon at a breakneck pace.  Malya slays monsters with glee, but is always willing to lend fellow adventurers a hand with the help of some creation magic.

Contents:
Princess Malya Hero Model
25mm Base
Game Card

Explosions! Calico Kate bursts into Super Dungeon Explore to the boom of her trademark love of mass destruction.  Her pirating skill ensures that she can plunder the best loot from even the stingiest dungeon minions.

Contents:
Calico Kate Hero Model
20mm Base
Game Card
Slavika: Zmora, a promotional card for Slavika that's available at Spiel 2012, features Zmora – "Bane" in English – a monster that does not count against the monster limit in a location.
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Wangerooge, an East Frisian island in Germany.
Monopoly set in Speyer, one of the oldest cities of Germany. All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.
Monopoly set in Passau,a city in south-east-Germany. All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

Monopoly set of the Sauerland, a German region. Due to good catering, winter sport region and short distance to the Netherlands menu cards are often written in Dutch, too (rare exception). All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

After the humans went and got themselves all Zombified, the Four Horsemen are left with lots and lots of free time. Following failed attempts at other leisure activities, they are giving mini-golf a try. Unfortunately, sportsmanship is a concept Death, War, Pestilence, and Famine have a difficult time grasping...

You and your fellow horsemen start the game on the first hole. Each turn you roll your bones and move by matching the symbols on the board. But, winning isn't easy against cheaters like the horsemen! Use your "FORE! stones" whenever you want to instantly knock another horseman's ball back to the last hole.

Win the game by being the first horseman to complete the 9th hole!

Franklin is the worse lab assistant EVER! Of course HE would be hit by lightning! How are you suppose to fire someone when he's just been fried!?!? The first step is putting him back together, and quickly too - you're going to need that lightning to restart the bone-head once you sew him back together... You did go to med-school right? Pre-med? Took home-ec? Oh no this isn't good at all... Man, did he just get hit AGAIN? Come on everyone, quickly now....

The game starts with that bone-headed lab assistant Frankie laying on the ground. The storm is raging and you're racing to put him back together before it passes. On your turn, pick a symbol, pick a partner to work with you, and roll your bones! If your combined roll shows the right combination, you have successfully mended one of Frankie's parts. Yay! If you don't, the storm keeps coming and the lightning rains down! But be careful, if more lightning hits Frankie before you're ready, some of your hard work will come undone!

Are your fellow scientists and co-workers missing in action? Well then it's up to you to fix Frankie all by yourself - roll all of the bones and with just a pinch of luck, you alone can put him back together!

Frankie is a co-operative game with all of the players working together against the storm tracker (timer). Win the game by successfully repairing all of Frankie's parts before the storm passes!

Moving into a new house is so exciting, especially one in a brand new neighborhood! People are moving in from all over the world... and so are the ghosts. Unfortunately, this town isn't big enough to contain so many spirits! The solution? Only the most scary, sheety ghost can stay. That's right, a real-life, astral scare-off! Thankfully, most of the new houses have full linen closets!

In Scared Sheetless, scaring is the name of the game! But let's face it, there's nothing scary about a naked ghost. The game starts out with you and your ghostly competitors alone in the new neighborhood, a neighborhood only big enough for one ghost. On your turn, roll your bones. Doubles reveal new sheets and the rest of your roll is movement. Match the symbols to move from house to house, picking up sheets and scaring your fellow ghosts as you go!

Win the game by being the last sheet-ed ghost!

Exploring a newly discovered tomb is so exciting! The hieroglyphs, the architecture, the sarcophagus... And... and the Mummy! Oh no, no no! Run! A brilliant idea, only foiled by the echoing thud of a dozen stone doors slamming shut! Need some keys. Oh man, keys, keys, keys! Quick!

The game starts off with you and your friends in the central chamber of a massive tomb - with the Mummy you just woke up! Oh no! Roll your bones and move as quick as you can because once he gets his bearings, the Mummy will be after you! You'll need to roll a key to pass through the locks and escape, but the mummy takes part of your rolls making those keys even harder to find!

The first person out of the tomb wins the game!

It's not easy being a werewolf, but you've learned how to make a life. Keep a low profile, don't chase the neighbors, etc... Unfortunately, it seems somewolf else hasn't learned that yet. And now the town's in an uproar over the mess left in the middle of town, and something must be done! The plan: head out into the woods on the next full moon and catch the wolf! The problem, everyone has to be there - including you! So you need to catch the other wolf before someone catches you. But what you don't know: your lovely town of Wolfeboro is inhabited entirely by werewolves and all of your neighbors are facing your same dilemma - catch or be caught!

Before the game begins, you and your friends must work together to create the Wolfeboro woods - every player gets a say in how many trees you use and where they go! Yay! And then its time to hunt down one of your friends before they can hunt you! Each turn, roll your bones to move, but be careful, the wrong roll means you get hit by moonlight and become the prey.

The first player to catch a w(h)erewolf wins the game!

Zombified is a 4-10 player elimination game.

Let's face it, there's no good explanation. You and your friends are locked in a silo with a broken jar of Zombie-Maker-243. The end result is a no-brainer: it's only a matter of time until you're all infected. But hey, why not make your last moments fun? After all, when it comes to tag, zombies really bite!

You and your friends start the game locked inside an abondoned military silo contaminated with Zombie-Maker-243. It's inevitable that you'll become a zombie, but that's not exactly the life choice that you prefer! Each turn, you roll your bones to run away from your (former) friends, but you'd better roll lucky: getting the wrong symbols means you've been contaminated and, BAM!, are instantly Zombified!

Zombified can also be played by groups even larger than 10 people! The central logo-staircase allows movement between the silo's underground floors. While there is no limit to the number of floors you can play on, a minimum of 5 people per floor is recommended.

The last player to become Zombified is the winner!

The Stake-Out is a 2 player unequal-forces strategy game.

That darn Vampire has gone too far! With his flowing locks, eye-liner, and accent, he's setting the bar too high and the women-folk aren't willing to marry a simple guy anymore. They want poetry and compliments, charm and *ugh* romance! *shudder* Enough is enough! It's time for us villagers to stand together, put him in his place (and make sure he stays this time!)! He can't kill us all... right?

The Stake-Out starts with the Villagers pouring into the Vampire's lair and the unaware Vampire napping peacefully in his coffin. Moving slowly and staying out of the shadows, the Villagers need to surround the Vampire in order to stake their claim. The Vampire flies in shadows, taking out the villagers that stray too far from the group.

The Villagers win by surrounding the Vampire with at least 3 pieces. The Vampire wins by not being staked (and hopefully eating all of the Villagers!)!

Them Bones is a 4 player perfect-information strategy game.

While the bone yard isn't a lively place, it's most definitely an active one, especially the underground scene! And all that activity has come to a head now that it's Halloween night. The Necromancer has returned to this unhallowed yard with his once-yearly promise to revive ONE skeleton. Factions have been established, lines drawn, and the fight is on! And you'll be damned if he picks somebony else AGAIN!

Them Bones starts off like any normal graveyard - with all of the skeletons in their graves. But the necromancer is coming and only ONE skeleton will get a second chance at life. You'll have to work together with your competitors to eliminate other skeletons, but it'll be up to you alone to take and hold the central Masoleum while the necormancer casts his spell. Don't forget to use of the graveyard's underground tunnel network to move between graves and get the jump on your competition!

Skeletons are captured by being surrounded by any two opposing skeletons (mixed-force captures are allowed!). The first player to capture and hold the center space for one full round wins the game!

Monopoly set of the soccer team TSG 1899 Hoffenheim, located at a village in southern Germany. Compared with other home bases of German teams like Hamburg, Munich... the size of the village is incredible small. 
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

A game that defies explanation, the ultimate goal being whatever you make it to be. Teams of 2-players take part in this party-style game by moving across a board and performing one of four actions from a specified deck of cards:

Mime Medium: help your partner guess the clues on the card by miming it to them

North Korean Trivia: guess the correct answer to a collection of erroneous and admittedly falsified questions

Illustrative Efforts: using drawing only, get your partner to guess the clues

Thespian Affronts: using any means necessary, except for saying the exact clues themselves, help your partner guess the clues on the card

Each team also has a collection of dominoes, which can be played after each card action is performed. The game being played with these dominoes should be agreed upon before the game starts (the rules recommend playing "double sixes"), and the scores should be kept track of on a piece of paper.

Whether the result of each card was successful or not, a word from the bottom of the card is written down, and at the end of the game the group tries to come up with a poem using these words.

The end of the game should be agreed upon by all players before starting, or by setting up a house rule to that effect.

Monopoly promotional set of Ringhotels, a German hotel chain of 19 participants. 
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

boardgame: Run for Your Life, Candyman!: Extra Special Treats
description:
A Publisher-issued promo item for Run for Your Life, Candyman!

Includes the following 11 cards and a cover/ad card labeled
Run for Your Life, Candyman! Extra Special Treats:

     100Gs Bar
     B.B. Ruthless
     Bite-Me-Honey
     Frosting
     Necrowafer
     Nurds
     Penny Candy
     Sour Patchwork Kids
     Spear Mint
     2 blank Make Your Own Treat cards


All have Special Treat on the back.

boardgame: From Log Cabin To White House
description: Each player takes one piece, and puts it on the Log Cabin. At the beginning of the game each player must spin a one to advance to the first square. All plays are made in turn, moving the number of squares spun with the dial. Each square represents an office, and a progress to the White House. If a player stops on a square indicating a promotion or a setback, the player moves forward or backward to the square named. If sent to the Log Cabin, the player must begin over again. If sent to Court the player removes his or her piece to the Jury corner, and spins in turn the Jury dial to get the verdict of the Jury. The player must abide by this verdict, doing as the dial directs. The player first reaching the White House at the centre, moving there exactly for his or her spin, will be President, and win the game.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Niederbayern, a region in southern Germany.
remarks:
The city 'Straubing' (a pearl) belongs to Niederbayern and was busy - at that time - publishing its own city edition, a bit competitive to the region approach. The discussions around rose up in some (local) newspapers.
Monopoly set of Oberpfalz, a region in southern Germany. All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.
Monopoly set in Oberursel, a city north of Frankfurt, Germany. All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

IMPLEMENTS:
Two differently numbered yachts
An indicator with an arrow

Each player takes a yacht and the plays in turn, according to the rules.
Each player must spin a 5 to enter the yacht at space 1 but if later on he is compelled to start over again by stopping on either space 4 or 15 the player may enter on any spin.

If a yacht stops on a gold space it gains two points, going to the next gold space.

If a yacht stops on a red space the player loses his/her turn.

If two yachts wind up on the same space, the first yacht is "blanketed" and goes back 4 spaces.

If a yacht becomes disabled by landing on space 27 it moves forward at 1/2 spinner speed rounded up for the remainder of the game unless a 6 is rolled which repairs the yacht and then on the next turn the yacht returns to normal speed. Players stopping on spaces containing directions must always move according to those directions except for those landing on space 27 (disabled)

First player to reach space 40 wins the race

Game submission should be credited to KEVIN Kluvon KG who sent me the info on the game

Simulates the dog show circuit life.  Players roll a die and move, encountering various expenses and bonuses.  At several points, you may participate in regional Dog Shows, possibly acquiring points (the rules are rather sketchy).  Once you have 15 points, you may attempt to enter the Best Minster K.C. show in the center of the board, where you win the game if you correctly guess the [breed] Group which has won 'Best In Show'; if you do not win, you collect all the money on the board (how it gets there is not mentioned) and return to the circuit.

Everybody needs a hug now and again! In Hugs, by designer John Poniske, you are a member of a big family and you love everyone. So how do you show your love to the members of your family? By giving them a lot of hugs!

Each time you hug a family member, you earn points, and the objective is to earn the most HUG points possible. Of course, some members enjoy more hugs than others, and then there are SOME things you want to avoid hugging altogether. But will you be able to tell the difference?

Each turn, a player has one of four actions to choose per turn: To place one of their colored HUG counters, remove a family token from the board, peek at other tokens on the board, or draw a token from the "Token Pool" and place it face-down on the board.
Not all tokens are good to hug, and some family members are worth more points than others! When all pieces from each player have been placed, each counter is flipped over and the points are counted where their HUG counter connects with any character tokens.

A great little kids game that the whole family will love, give some HUGS to you and yours today!

Man of The Match is a soccer card game that can be played by 2-4 players. Each player becomes a coach, manages a soccer team and competes against the other coaches' teams in order to win the Championship and Cup.

At the beginning of the game and after each match, the coaches buy and sell soccer players in order to create a strong and competitive team. The coach that handles better his economics, will sign in the new soccer star!

During the match the home team coach gets 6 Attack and 6 Defence cards and the away team coach gets 5 Attack and 5 Defence cards. The home team coach attacks first using one of his Attack cards and the away team coach tries to defend his team using one of his Defence cards. Then the away team coach makes his move using one of his Attack cards and the home team coach uses his Defence cards. Each Attack card describes an event, for example "Injury" or "Goal" or "Penalty" etc. and the Defence cards deactivate the Attack cards. When all attacks have ended, the coach who has scored more goals is the winner.

The game can be played for as many seasons as you like and never ends. Every match is different than the previous and this is what makes this game really exciting! One season lasts about 1 hour.

Man of The Match is a very entertaining, easy-to-play, fast and funny game which can be played by people of all ages. It is very addictive to those who love soccer, but people who don't even watch soccer show a lot of excitement playing it!

Players take turns spinning a spinner and moving the number of spaces shown.  When they spin 'Return to Start', they do, and take the number of points shown on the space they're leaving, or lose 10 points if they were already on 'Start'.  A few other spaces have other effects, and a player landing on an occupied space moves back two spaces instead.  First to 250 points wins.
publishers: Tee Pee Toys, Jessup Paper Box Co.

description:
Phytocats is a card game where players nurture and hatch eggs into adorable cat-plant hybrids. Depending how the player cares for their eggs, they will hatch into different breeds of Phytocat. These creatures assist their owners and hinder their rivals.

Phytocats focuses on interactive, but not destructive, gameplay. Each player’s decisions are constantly influenced by the players around them, and a “first to achieve” system encourages players to diversify their strategies. This leads to rich, dynamic play where each player is striving to succeed using a wide variety of Phytocats.

While some game elements allow players to interfere with another player’s strategy, a player’s accomplishments cannot be taken away from them. The majority of play elements involve positive, helpful interactions.

Phytocats is looking for a publisher! If you are interested please contact phytocats@gmail.com!

Diagonal Blocks is an abstract strategy game modeled after Blokus, and playable with a Blokus set.  The pieces, (same as Blokus) consist of a complete set of all polyominoes up to size 5.  The board is 20x20 (smaller in 2 or 3 player variants) with the six squares nearest each corner removed.  Play begins at a starting square for each player, and each players subsequent move must contact his previously placed pieces only at a diagonal points.   The game ends when no further pieces can be played.  The winner is the player who covered the most squares with his pieces.

Vis-a-vis Blokus, the differences are:
(1) The corners are removed from the board so there are barely more squares on the board than pieces to be played.
(2) There are no special scoring bonuses. The count of squares covered is the entire score.
(3) In two player variants, A "swap" rule applies to the second move.   This removes the first player advantage.

Variations:
 Diagonal Blocks (3 player, intermediate size board)
 Diagonal Blocks Duo (2 player)
 Diagonal Blocks Classic (2 player, 4 color)

Online Play

     Boardspace.net real-time, AI option


Mobile Apps

     Boardspace.net local multiplayer or online, IOS
     Boardspace.net local multiplayer or online, Android

Aerochamp deals with a trans-Atlantic air race. Four players travel from the USA to Europe by balloon. The critical factors in the game are wind and gas.

The wind direction can be determined by the players using tactical cards. The quantity of gas needs to be managed carefully, as otherwise the balloon won't reach Europe and will plunge into the Atlantic Ocean.

Depending on the power and direction of the wind the players choose the optimal flying course. The players who is first to reach the destined port (in Europe) wins the game.

Expansion to the game Koekie Loeren (Cookie Disco). Het kruipkoekie (the crawl cookie) is played on top of the cookies of the basis game. The crawl cookie itself has no point value, therefore a cookie that’s connected to the crawl cookie only, cannot be moved. A cookie that’s underneath the crawl cookie, has no point value as long as the crawl cookie is on top of it and no points are scored for a cookie that’s underneath the crawl cookie at the end of the game.

This small expansion gives the basis game even more tactical options and depth.

Monopoly set of Garmisch-Partenkirchen, a German city at the northern edge of the Bavarian Alps.
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

boardgame: Austria (fan expansion of Ticket to Ride)
description:
Unofficial map of Austria for use with Ticket to Ride / Zug um Zug. The map was designed to resemble the Austrian railroad network to a high degree. The game is completely self-designed and includes a map with real tracks and holding areas for destination tickets and wagon cards.

Some special rules apply. Touristic routes - indicated by a small zig-zag icon along the route - have to be built like a tunnel. These routes end in a dead-end and are covered by only 1 destination ticket. Players who attempt to build these routes need to complete the route and hold the appropriate destination ticket. Otherwise, they will lose a number of victory points as indicated on the ticket.

boardgame: Ticket to Finland (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Beautiful map of Finland. The game is set in the year 1938 and the map makes use of the Ticket to Ride Europe rules. This includes tunnels, ferries as well as different long and short route destination tickets. In addition to a regular set of tickets, the author provides a second extended set of tickets. This certainly increases re-playability.
In addition to the European rules, the map also contains connections to cities in other countries, such as Leningrad, Tallinn, etc.

boardgame: Italy (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description: To play, you either need to have the original Ticket to Ride or Ticket to Ride Europe. The Italy map can be played with either rule set and components. However, there are some modifications. For both variants players receive only 42 instead of the usual 45 trains. The Europe variant starts with an initial destination ticket draw of three short and one long ticket. At least two tickets have to be kept.

boardgame: Formula D: Circuits 4 – Grand Prix of Baltimore & Buddh
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Formula D: Circuits 4 - Baltimore & India is an expansion board for the Formula D board game with two new tracks on a double-sided game board.

Designed to be one of the fastest circuits of the championship, the Formula 1 circuit of Buddh, India stretches over three miles. Beware of your opponents as the circuit has improved overtaking possibilities, adding interesting twists and turns to the game! And if you've got nerves of steel, you'll need them as you push the pedal to the metal on the Baltimore street racing circuit. Have fun managing your timing between braking and pushing your car to its limits so you can take-off like a rocket as you come out of corners!

boardgame: Gnomes: The Great Sweeping of Ammowan
description:
Gnomes: The Great Sweeping of Ammowan is a storytelling game that is designed to bring a shared adventure to life for age 3 to adult.

The game includes a storyteller's guide and letters from the gnomes, which provides the backstory and step by step instruction on how to take your kids on a memorable adventure to save Ammowan, a gnome kingdom that happens to exist in your yard or home!

The storyteller(s) will weave this magical tale about the gnomes, and the gnome’s arch enemy; trolls.

Every 365 days, the great sweeping hits the gnome kingdom. This is a time when trolls sweep through the area looking to capture gnomes. And they need your help.

This is an adventure meant to be led by an adult and experienced by children.

During the game, one player acts as the storyteller who sets the scene by placing evidence of a gnome civilization as outlined in the storyteller's guide and then leads players on a quest. As a cooperative experience, players must find clues and solve riddles placed during the set up phase by the storyteller (and included in with game). Players win if they can successfully discover all hidden items by solving the problems presented by the storyteller. They will lose if all items cannot be found through cooperation and deduction within a set period of time.

This official Mayfair promo expansion was included as a convention and retail give-away on a tile sheet titled: Game Variants & Mini Expansions Set #1. The expansion is also included in Game Trade Magazine #185 (July 2015).

This expansion adds ways to enter a city without being closed out.

Stalag 18 is the expansion of Stalag 17. This new version takes the basic game, focused in a familiar public, to become it to a more tactic game, deeper, for a more demanding players.
The expansion is ready to play with all or just few of the new rules, so the complex grade is decided by the players.
New actions are added to the basic ones. Now all the cards, even the no useful for the jailbreak plan in a turn, can help with the tactics.
The new warehouse cards can be taken during the game to help, in a critic moment, to the players to get all the objects. But the guardians will keep an eye on them, if they realize who is dealing with these new cards.
And finally, now the player can choose among 6 prisoner tokens which prisoners will attempt to escape in each jailbreak phase. Each prisoner has two special skills and different prisoner values, what gives to the game a deeper theme.
And finally the expansion bring all components for a six player game.


Stalag 18 es la ampliación para el juego Stalag 17 que transforma el juego básico, enfocado a un público familiar, en un juego más táctico y mucho más profundo para jugadores más exigentes.  
La ampliación está preparada para que los jugadores puedan añadir al juego básico todas o algunas de las nuevas reglas, por lo que el grado de complejidad lo ajustan los jugadores a su gusto.
Se añaden nuevas acciones a las básicas, que harán que todas las cartas, incluso los objetos que no sean útiles para el plan de fuga de ese turno, sean útiles para las tácticas de los jugadores.
Las nuevas cartas de almacén pueden ser elegidas en medio de la partida para ayudar a los jugadores a tener el objeto necesario en el momento crítico, aunque los guardias los vigilarán más al verlos negociar con ellos.
Finalmente los jugadores podran, cada turno de fuga, elegir los prisioneros que serán los que intenten escapar entre su grupo de soldados, cada uno con sus habilidades especiales y sus puntuaciones de prisioneros diferente, lo que da una profundidad temática muy grande.
Finalmente se añaden componentes para un sexto jugador.

Funstir, an expansion for Frigiti, plays much like the base game: In a round, the active player rolls all ten dice – seven of which have consonants on all sides, and three of which have only vowels – then creates a word by using at least three dice. (This word cannot be real, and if anyone claims it is, reroll the dice and try again.)

What Funstir adds to the game are marketing slogans, and players are now trying to create brand names to match those slogans, along with an explanatory phrase. Once players read off their phrases, everyone will then each distribute three tokens to the answers they thought best, with one of those tokens going to the player's own answer. (This self-congratulatory vote aids in the scorekeeping process.)

After each person has been the active player once, the game ends and the player with the most tokens wins.

A Family Card Game for Creative Minds

Compete or use teamwork to construct incredible machines and contraptions out of loads of scrap! Five game modes offer unique challenges – Easy, Standard, Crazy, Co-Op, and Sandbox.

Scrapyard Warriors is centered around the Scrap Cards. These are what you use to build your amazing machines and contraptions. Each Scrap Card has color and corresponding point value, located in the lower-right corner of the card: Red = 1 Point, Light Blue = 2 Points, Yellow = 3 Points, Green = 4 Points, Blue = 5 Points, Purple = 6 Points, and Orange = 1-6 Bonus Points.

Bonus Cards cannot be used to construct your machines. They simply add extra points, ranging from 1 to 6. They have a green plus sign in the lower-right corner.

Soccer is the sport that probably has more followers in the world, so it's a great challenge for all board game designers to reproduce the emotion of the goal. Futbolmate is an innovative option in which the player develops his own winning plays, so the amusement is not lost in any play.

The soccer strategy game Futbolmate has a rectangular board in 108 green and yellow blocks; on these blocks are placed 18 round pieces in two colors to represent the two teams. The two goalkeepers move similar to the Queen in Chess – that is, as many spaces as desired in any straight direction. The other pieces can move only one diagonal space each turn. Another smaller round piece represents the ball. The objective of the game is to move all the pieces over the rectangular board to score the most goals in an established time. The game ends with a winner or a tie, similar to professional soccer.

The movement of the ball makes the difference. It does not follow a predetermined path, as its movement responds to the logic mazes of Ságaze, rules that allow the ball to rebound on the pieces and edges board, with players always trying to recover the ball or score a goal. Futbolmate uses no dice, no cards, and no other random elements – only the skill of the two players.

Over several centuries, four medieval kingdoms disputed the conquest of the Sagario castle, fighting in successive medieval battles. The cycle began every time a kingdom beat the other remaining three in a battle. This winning kingdom enjoyed only a short stay in the castle before the neighboring kingdoms attacked once again. This endless cycle repeated itself constantly — until one day a monk known in the four kingdoms for his wisdom and pursuit of peace, while observing this absurd dynamic of endless battles, decided to end them by creating a board game with the following decree: "The kingdom that manages to capture all the knights of the neighboring kingdoms will be the owner of the Sagario."

Sagario is played on a cross-form board made up of 212 boxes. The Sagario castle is placed in the center of the board game and is surrounded by four towers, which are the landmarks that delimit the territories of the four kingdoms: Sazio, Galia, Risko, and Orbis. Each of these kingdoms has an army of five soldiers and four knights as well as four assault towers to siege the castle. Each player must try to capture all the knights of the bordering kingdoms, at which point he now owns the castle and wins the game.

boardgame: Great War at Sea: Confederate States Navy (Second Edition)
description:
In a different summer of 1862, following a series of disastrous Union defeats and internal frustrations with the course of this accursed Civil War, the Confederate States of America wins its independence, and from that time on, the North American continent is divided between two nations: the CSA and the USA.

Confederate States Navy is a 64-page book supplement for the Great War at Sea series that examines this very unusual question of alternate history: hypothesizing how the Confederate States of America would have seen to its naval needs in future decades, had the South successfully broken away from the Union and become an independent nation.  Greatly outclassed by the neighboring Union in terms of manpower and raw economics, yet spurred by a desire to project power into the Gulf of Mexico and Caribbean, a fleet built around a moderate contingent of capital ships (of British design) capable of facing down South American rivals, and backed up by numerous small craft, submarines and airplanes intended to offset any Union fleet numerical advantage, is projected for the new Confederacy.

Confederate States Navy includes 35 long and 70 short laser-cut cardboard playing pieces, depicting CSN ships, aircraft, fleets and submarine flotillas as well as tropical cyclone markers.  The supplement is not a complete game; the use of maps and pieces from Plan Gold, Jutland, Black Waters, Sea of Troubles and Pacific Crossroads are needed to play the majority of the twelve battle and eighteen operational scenarios, while those from Cone of Fire, Mediterranean and Zeppelins! are needed to play them all.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: Great War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
Gem Tactics is a free to play Final Fantasy Tactics inspired board game. Players customize their army then use them to destroy their rivals. Players can discover many unique tactics, even after several games. The game can be played in 1-off skirmishes or in a larger campaign setting.

The first phase of play is the character creation team building. Players choose which classes they want to use. Some characters will have two classes. Each class has four skills; the player may unlock and use any one skill. An item shop adds further depth, as players have starting gold to spend on equipment and items for their characters.

The second phase of play is the battle on a grid map. Players take turns moving their units. The map may have desirable areas to move your units to, such as treasure or a tactical position. Some maps have campaign specific features like special victory conditions.

boardgame: Great Art Close Up: The Detroit Institute of Arts
description:
From the publisher:
Discover art of the great museums of the world as you play 5 fun card games, including "Memory", "Old Maid", and 2 new matching games for children of different ages. Turn your next visit to the art museum into a treasure hunt with this deck of 48 cards masterpieces from the Detroit Institute of Arts. This game spans centuries of great art from old masters, such as Breughel and Bellini, to modern artists, such as Diego Rivera, Roy Lichtenstein, and Pablo Picasso.

Discover secret details in great works of art. Have fun pairing each full work of art with a close-up that shows every brushstroke.


     The publication year has not been found but all other card games in this series were published in 2006.
family: Series: Great Art Close Up (Birdcage Press)
Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

boardgame: China (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description: In this installation we get to visit Shanghai, Beijing, Hong Kong and other fast growing cities. The game follows the original Ticket to Ride rules. While the destination ticket layout does not follow the original Ticket to Ride design the map is amazingly beautiful: it features a huge dragon on one side and inherits the usual TtR design. I am looking forward to playing this in conjunction with a quick game of Mah-Jongg maybe...

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Chemnitz, in Germany/Saxony.

boardgame: North-Africa (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description: This time we can heat the engines in North Africa connecting cities like Cairo, Baghdad, Tripoli, Tunis, Addis-Ababa, Marrakech and Monrovia (to name a few). This new inofficial expansion is played with the original Ticket to Ride rules.

boardgame: Scandinavia (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Scandinavian map and several new action cards for Ticket to Ride like

     Veto: Card owner may stop other players building a route
     Public Line: One route is build by a public company. This route is available for all players
     Ghost Line: At the end of the game, an unused route can be claimed in exchange for four victory points
     Jackpot: The player can pick up all 5 open train / waggon cards


etc.

boardgame: Portugal (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map was designed to be 100% compatible with "Ticket to Ride: Märklin" rules, including merchandise tokens, passengers, long and short destination tickets.
But it can be used with any other Ticket to Ride edition, just ignore the city dot colors.

The board is available in two versions:

     The light version is recommended for 2-3 players. Few double routes make it difficult to reach some areas if you play it with 4-5 players (which can be nice if you like cut-throat games). During play-testing, the light version makes it difficult to complete some routes in the end-game.
     The advanced version is recommended for 4-5 players and contains more double routes. This version is better for those who don't like others to block them.


To get the map files, contact the author by Geekmail:
https://www.boardgamegeek.com/geekmail/compose?touser=helioa

boardgame: New destination tickets (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride Europe)
description: 18 new destination tickets for Ticket to Ride Europe. These tickets include 6 new long routes in blue color and 12 green tickets with points between 14 and 17. The rules need to be changed as follows: At the beginning of the game a player takes 1 blue, 1 green and 2 brown destination cards (from the base game). This happens instead of drawing 1 blue and three brown destination cards. It is the only change to the rules.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Gera, in Germany/Thuringia.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Cottbus, in East Germany.

boardgame: Bretagne (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Brittany (French: Bretagne) is a cultural region in the north-west of France.

The map contains ferries as well as several long routes making use of some of the extended rules as seen in Ticket to Ride Europe.

boardgame: Quebec Province (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description: The Québec Province map is played with the original Ticket to Ride rules and components. This includes the final 10-point score for the longest route. The map is the first to ever include a route of length 9. What a challenge! Such a long route will be difficult to complete without locomotive wildcards, as there are only 12 cards of one color in a standard TtR game. To compensate, the rewards are high: the lucky player finishing this route scores a heavy 24 points -and that's even more than the 20 points from Vancouver to Montreal in the original game.

Monopoly set of Ludwigshafen, a city located at Southwest Germany.
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

Monopoly set of Bremerhaven, a German city at the North Sea.
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

Monopoly set of Herne, a German city (very close to Essen) with quite a lot enthusiastic board gamers.
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

Monopoly set of Mallorca, a Spanish island in the Mediterranean Sea. 
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.
remarks:
a German(!) edition, customised for German tourists/inhabitants.

Very simple board game for the under fives (with supervision). The board is very large and made of some sort of synthetic plastic. It folds out like a piece of cloth to give it it's size. The board is a track, running from the Start to end at Winnie the Pooh's house. Dice controls movement and special squares (there aren't many as it is intended for the very young) must be obeyed. The first to get to Winnie's house, wins.

The poor quality box lets down the nice presentation inside. A lot of thought has gone into the board, both physically and artisticly. The four playing pieces are like large round half-discs so easy for children to use.

Monopoly set of Bad Homburg, a German city - north of Frankfurt.
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

Publisher Blurb:

You've grasped the tactics involved with the additional units and weapons from Total Warfare to defeat your opponents. Now you own Technical Readout: 3055 Upgrade and want to deploy some of those 'Mechs, vehicles, and battle armor on your gaming table. Grab your dice and start rolling, because these sheets are for you! Record Sheets: 3055 Upgrade is a companion to Technical Readout: 3055 Upgrade, containing 80 preprinted 'Mech-and 12 Clan aerospace fighter-record sheets that will have players firing autocannons, missiles and PPCs at each other in no time. The extensive Rules Addendum of this record sheet brings Solaris: The Gaming World to your table tops with rules for playing as a member of a MechWarrior stable, running arena scenarios, and even how to modify any map sheet into several of the most famous gladitorial arenas! The Solaris VII BattleMechs are constructed using select equipment found in Tactical Operations. To use those designs, players will need that book.

Monopoly set of FC Bayern München, a German soccer team.
All important events, the club's own stadium investments, special meeples...
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

Monopoly set of 1. FC Köln, a German soccer team. 
Important events, people, locations out of the history with special meeples.
All the traditional elements of rolling, moving, purchasing, earning, and spending.

A game of planetary collection!

The objective is to obtain the most planets/points in his or her collection by the time the deck runs out!

When planets are drawn they are immediately placed on the table. Using your probe cards on the planets themselves, you must either complete a sequence of three "like" probes over any number of turns or drop three dissimilar probes in a single turn to obtain that planet.

Use galactic catastrophes against your opponent such as meteor showers, solar flares or radiation!

Once the deck has run out, each player tallies up there points. 1 point for each planet + 1 additional point for each planet in similar systems (example: if you have two planets in the same system and another set of three in the same system, that's 5 additional points).

Quick and easy to play. Get the family in on it!
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Hamm, a German city on the northern edge of the Ruhr area.

The aim of the game is to travel around Europe for a vacation with a budget set at the beginning, and bring maximum points (no money, whew!) At the end of the game.

The game board represent the Old Continent with countries, cities and capitals. The counters are four animals (rabbit, duck, sheep, pig) probably because the animals know no borders!

At start each player is dealt 7 cards with "cities" they must to visit during the game and then return to their capital. The game begins with a preparatory phase to travel, with a maximum of 5 rounds, for which players can interact with each other or with the deck, one card per turn to plan their journey.

In his turn player move on the board visiting cities, playing special cards, buying souvenirs.

The holiday ends when first player has visited all 7 cities (he also wins a bonus of 30 points).
The winner is whoever has the most points for his "Souvenir" cards and "Cities" cards.

Junior version of Euromind. The first who reached European Parlament changed its national currency against the euro and won 3 flags during the game wins.

The board represents the European continent with many different spaces. Each player receives 1 contract-card, 3 national currencies and a money in euros.
A player may move his pawn forward paying his visit, he may choose to check back on a bank to earn 50 Euros per square crossed.
Boxes can earn Euros (or make!) 100 € per turn. On the Bank  player may exchange its currency against the Euro. 
Player can acquire the flags of his contract-card in answering questions about Europe.
 The first to join the European Parliament in its possession three flags (and hold more than 100 €) becomes citizen of Europe and win the game.

The goal is to reach the end of the path (the European Parliament) by being stripped of its national currency and providing three flags won during the game.

In turn, the player chooses to move forwards or backwards
on the game board.
Moving forward player must pay euros, moving backwards player may earn euros from bank.
Reaching the space "Chance", player draws a card and follows the instructions. On "Change", the player exchange its currency against the Euro. On "Flags", the player must answer 2 or 3 questions (depending on age) to win the banner.

The first player who reach the Euro Parlament with three flags is the winner.

The object of the game is to be the first player to collect two monster tokens and the Control Panel card, then reach the control room.

On each player's turn they spin the spinner and advance the number of spaces as indicated by the arrow.  If the player lands on one of several special spaces like a skull space, follow the directions.  It could instruct the player to take the control panel card even if another player already has gotten it.

If the player lands on a corner space with a monster, both the monster and the player movers are put on a special race path and a "race" ensues by the use of the spinner.  Another player spins for the monster.  If you win the race, you collect the token that matches the monster.

After a player collects two monster tokens and the control panel card the player moves towards the control room.  If the player reaches the control room without losing the control panel card, the player wins the game.

The goal is to become the first EUREXPERT answering knowledge questions on various European states.

The board represents Europe with its capitals, and color paths joining the big cities. Each country has five areas of knowledge: Art (literature, music, painting, cinema), Company (history, sports, science, institutions), Environment (geography, crafts and industry), Special (specialty, folklore, anecdotes), European Community (history, general, relations between states). Initially each player secretly sets its goal to answer 3 questions in five areas, or answer 6 questions in three areas, or answer 12 questions in one single domain.
Each player takes his departure from the capital.

In his turn player rolls the dice with colored faces and moves his pawn to space with this color. Player may answer the question in any space during the moving. If he answers correctly he may continue moving, if the answer is incorrect his turn is over.
When a player has reached his goal, he announces it and wins the game.
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Tübingen, a South-German city - university with important history.
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of Ingolstadt, city/home base of 'Audi' in Germany.

This official Mayfair promo expansion was included as a convention and retail give-away on a tile sheet titled: Game Variants & Mini Expansions Set #1.

This expansion adds a movable obstacle into play, the Cranky Beaver, and gives options for a Sudden Death Ending and a Rough Rapids variant.

This official Mayfair promo expansion was included as a convention and retail give-away on a tile sheet titled: Game Variants & Mini Expansions Set #1.

This tile adds five track access into one town.

This is included in Steam: Map Expansion #5

boardgame: Nuns on the Run: The Anchorite
description:
This official Mayfair promo expansion was included as a convention and retail give-away on a tile sheet titled: Game Variants & Mini Expansions Set #1.

This token is used in a game with only 1 or 2 Novices to effectively shrink the size of the board. In a 4 player game it can be used to change the landscape a bit.

This official Mayfair promo expansion was included as a convention and retail give-away on a tile sheet titled: Game Variants & Mini Expansions Set #1. It was also included in the Mayfair Games' Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #18.

This gives players the opportunity to potentially push two sleds on their turn.

"He sled, she sled"

Setup:
Take a Fast Sled token and place it behind your screen.

Nominate Another Sled:
On your turn, after a sled is pushed or pyramid stone is lifted, you may use and discard your fast sled token to nominate and push an additional sled. This token may only be used to push a sled, it may not be used to lift. If another player has used his/her sled token this round, then you can not use your token until the next round.

Game End:
If the game ends without the completion of the pyramid and you still have your Fast Sled token, you are eliminated from the game.

boardgame: Lords of Vegas: Skybridge / Building Boom / Card Sharp
description:
This official Mayfair promo expansion was included as a convention and retail give-away on a tile sheet titled: Game Variants & Mini Expansions Set #2.

This adds a bridge to connect two regions together.  The Sky Bridge ruleset begins the game with a connection, whereas the Building Boom allows for players to add Sky Bridges as an action.

Scrapbots is a mashup of Mechs, with a dash of Battlebots, and a twist of Junkyard Wars. The idea is that you build your bots from the scrap heap and then send them into the arena, where you earn points for smashing each other and knocking parts off the other robots. If you die, you just grab some more parts and send in another bot — it’s all about pleasing the crowds.  The goal is to have the most points at the end of the game which has a set number of rounds.

It is set in a televised sporting events arena, complete with Sponsors who provide reliable equipment upgrades to the contestants and commercial breaks where players get to retool their bots.

Since the bots are made from scrap, lots of things will go wrong.  Players can affect this tactically.  There is also an Economy of Failure that allows players to turn their failures into tactical advantages.

In SKIRMISH: Modern Card Warfare the goal is simply to bust through your opponents defenses and capture their flag. To accomplish this, you will SKIRMISH against your opponent constantly on the field of battle using the cards that you’ve drawn into your hand.
